articles.headline: Chloroquine and pyronaridine-artesunate equally effective against vivax malaria
articles.title_original: Efficacy of chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine and pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine against Plasmodium vivax malaria in adults and transmission to mosquitoes in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.
articles.authors: Bezabih MK, Sisay S, Behaksera SW, Ramjith J, Teka H, Girma S, Shimelash A, Ejigu LA, Kassa FA, Hailegiorgis B, Tadesse M, Misganaw T, Gizat A, Gashaw A, Habtamu G, Legesse NS, Hundera NL, Milki DW, Abdo M, Chali W, Tongren JE, Hwang J, Bousema T, Tadesse FG
articles.details: Phase 2/3 RCT in Ethiopia comparing chloroquine or pyronaridine-artesunate, each with 14-day low-dose primaquine, in 176 adults with microscopy-confirmed P. vivax. Day-28 ACPR was 98.7% (78/79, chloroquine) vs 98.8% (82/83, pyronaridine); day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine was 96.4% (80/83). Both regimens well-tolerated with mild adverse events and no serious events. In transmission substudy, 92% of gametocyte carriers remained infectious to mosquitoes after treatment.

articles.headline: Wealth, education, bed nets reduce malaria in African children
articles.title_original: Determinants of malaria prevalence among children under 5 years of age in 17 sub-Saharan African countries: insights from Malaria Indicator Surveys.
articles.authors: Kassie AT, Bazezew AM, Tamir TT, Tekeba B, Zeleke GA, Mekonen EG, Endalew HL, Wassie M, Zegeye AF
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 51,457 children <5 from 17 sub-Saharan African countries using Malaria Indicator Survey data (2016-2022). Multilevel logistic regression identified key risk factors: age 1-2 years (AOR 1.46), low household wealth (AOR 1.94), rural residence (AOR 2.69), high community illiteracy (AOR 1.76). Protective factors were maternal primary education (AOR 0.84) and ITN use (AOR 0.80). Over one-third of children were infected with malaria.

articles.headline: Severe malaria leaves neurologic deficits; caretakers struggle with support
articles.title_original: Perceptions and experiences of caretakers of children with neurologic deficits after severe malaria in Uganda.
articles.authors: Okullo AE, Kisakye S, Idro R, Boele van Hensbroek M, John CC, Kaawa-Mafigiri D
articles.journal: Int J Qual Stud Health Well-being
articles.details: Qualitative study in Uganda with 23 caretakers interviewed about children's neurologic deficits post-severe malaria, including motor, cognitive, behavioral, sensory impairments and epilepsy. Thematic analysis identified eight major themes: mixed perceptions, difficulty educating impaired children, community mistreatment, mental/emotional distress, financial strain, misconceptions, limited access to specialized care, and adaptation. Caretakers held misconceptions about deficit causes but gradually adapted to supporting their children's needs.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine and pyronaridine-artesunate effective against resistant falciparum
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the effectiveness of artemether-lumefantrine and artesunate-pyronaridine each combined with single low-dose primaquine in adults with Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.
articles.authors: Bezabih MK, Misganaw T, Ejigu LA, Rosillo S, Teka H, Alemayehu DH, Adnew B, Hailegiorgis B, Girma S, Tadesse M, Kassa FA, Gebremichael M, Tefera DA, Chali W, Behaksera SW, Alemayehu L, Deressa JD, Abdo M, Habtamu G, Gizat A, Yalew WG, Jember TH, Bitew M, Mumba P, Plucinski MM, Souza S, Patel DS, Talundzic E, Taffese HS, Geleta KE, Mehari D, Bayissa GA, Bousema T, Tongren JE, Hwang J, Tadesse FG
articles.details: Phase 2/3 RCT in Ethiopia comparing artemether-lumefantrine or pyronaridine-artesunate (each with primaquine) in 199 adults with uncomplicated P. falciparum from an area with high pfkelch13 R622I artemisinin-resistance mutations and pfhrp2/3 deletions. Day-28 PCR-corrected ACPR was 96.8% (artemether-lumefantrine) vs 98.9% (pyronaridine); day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine was 96.5%. No early treatment failures; mild adverse events predominated.

articles.headline: Respiratory distress and neurologic complications predict AKI malaria mortality
articles.title_original: Prognostic factors in malaria patients with acute kidney injury: a systematic review and meta analysis.
articles.authors: Tear AA, Fauziah N, Riswari SF, Purba AR, Akhlaq MM, Susanto NH
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 16 studies (1,104 malaria-AKI patients) identified strong mortality predictors: respiratory distress syndrome (OR 18.50, p<0.001), neurological involvement (OR 12.83, p<0.001), disseminated intravascular coagulation (OR 12.00, p<0.001), hypotension (OR 18.23, p<0.001), ventilator requirement (OR 30.35, p<0.001), leukocytosis (OR 4.87, p=0.003), acidosis (OR 4.88, p<0.001), and jaundice (OR 5.03, p=0.001). Findings highlight need for vigilant management of severe complications.

articles.headline: Blood donors show higher malaria risk if family replacement donation
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted infections among blood donors in Saudi Arabia: demographic and malaria risk differences by donation purpose.
articles.authors: Alshalani A, Alzamil L, Alrezaihi A, Alsughayyir J, Bin Muhannaa NI, Alshehri FS, Almuqrin AM
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 49,590 blood donors in Saudi Arabia screened for transfusion-transmitted infections. Malaria prevalence was significantly higher in family replacement donors (5.3%) versus voluntary donors (2.9%), particularly among non-Saudi nationals. No significant differences for HBV, HCV, HIV, or syphilis. Family replacement donation was more common in younger, male, and non-Saudi donors.

articles.headline: Matrix-M-adjuvanted malaria vaccine shows favorable safety profile
articles.title_original: Safety of Matrix-M-adjuvanted COVID-19, seasonal influenza, combination influenza-COVID-19, and malaria vaccines: a review of the evidence.
articles.authors: Fix J, Lee S, Nachbar J, Sadadia P, Lövgren Bengtsson K, Stertman L, Palm AE, Walker R, Draghia Akli R, Sellers S
articles.details: Comprehensive review of 66 clinical trials and post-marketing studies evaluating safety of Matrix-M-adjuvanted vaccines, including R21/Matrix-M (P. falciparum malaria). Among 64,101 participants receiving 143,170 total doses, 4,263 received malaria vaccine doses. All authorized and candidate vaccines were well-tolerated across diverse populations including immunocompromised individuals and children, with favorable reactogenicity profiles compared to licensed vaccines.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr nets combat pyrethroid resistance but emerging resistance threatens gains
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets for pyrethroid-resistant malaria vectors: efficacy, resistance risks, and policy implications.
articles.authors: John Mwang'onde B
articles.details: Systematic review of 31 studies (2010-2024) evaluating chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets versus pyrethroid-only nets against resistant Anopheles. Nets demonstrated 1.8-fold increased mosquito mortality (95% CI 1.5-2.1) and 40-60% reductions in malaria incidence and entomological inoculation rates in community trials. Early chlorfenapyr resistance detected in A. gambiae (RR 2.4, p=0.01, linked to CYP6P4 overexpression); significant correlation between agricultural pesticide use and vector resistance (r=0.62, p<0.05).

articles.headline: Asymptomatic carriers harbor artemisinin-resistant parasites in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic malaria: a hidden reservoir for the transmission and circulation of artemisinin partial-resistant Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Mazigo E, Lee WJ, Jun H, Fitriana F, Syahada JH, Louis JM, Muh F, Lu F, Lee KJ, Nam JW, Han JH, Kwon TH, Lee SJ, Na S, Chun W, Park WS, Han ET, Todd J, Manjurano A, Kidima W, Han JH
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 3,489 Tanzanian participants (Dec 2022–July 2023) detected overall malaria positivity of 26.2% (qPCR), with 802 isolates sequenced. In high-transmission regions, 3.0% carried pfk13 partial-resistance markers (Y493H 0.2%, R561H 2.0%, A675V 0.7%), while low-transmission areas were wild-type. NFD haplotype (reduced lumefantrine susceptibility) found in 48.1-48.4% of isolates. Artemether-lumefantrine use (64.7% in high-transmission) correlated with increased pfmdr1 mutations.

articles.headline: Cold stress impairs malaria parasite invasion and growth
articles.title_original: Cold stress restricts malaria parasite growth by affecting erythrocyte invasion.
articles.authors: Odagawa T, Saiki E, Kanuka H
articles.details: In vitro and in vivo studies examined cold stress effects (27-33°C in culture, 4°C in mice) on P. falciparum development. Cold stress suppressed parasite growth and differentiation in temperature-dependent manner and enhanced suppression combined with low-dose artesunate. Cold stress decreased erythrocyte invasion efficiency, accompanied by downregulation of invasion-associated genes including ama1 at schizont and ring stages.

articles.headline: P. falciparum employs stage-specific RNA editing throughout development
articles.title_original: Genome-wide detection of conserved stage-specific RNA editing events for nuclear genes in the Plasmodium falciparum 3D7 malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Azad MTA, Sugi T, Qulsum U, Kato K
articles.details: Genome-wide analysis of RNA editing in synchronized P. falciparum 3D7 at developmental stages 16-40 hours detected extensive A-to-G, G-to-A, and C-to-T editing events in stage-specific manner. RNA editing peaked during 32-hour trophozoite stage and declined at 40-hour schizont stage. G-to-A and C-to-T events predominated; RNA binding and catalytic pathways downregulated at 40 hours, suggesting robust RNA editing machinery facilitates rapid base conversions during development.

articles.headline: Avian malaria pathogen shows cryptic speciation and introgression globally
articles.title_original: Phylogenomic analyses reveal introgression and cryptic speciation in the globally distributed, vector-transmitted pathogen Plasmodium relictum.
articles.authors: Weyer E, Theodosopoulos AN, Palinauskas V, Bensch S, Westerdahl H, Duc M, Ciloglu A, Huang X, Kim KS, Tsuda Y, Atkinson CT, Zehtindjiev P, Fleischer RC, VanTassel NM, Taylor SA, Hellgren O, Ellis VA
articles.details: Phylogenomic analysis of 52 P. relictum (avian malaria) infections from multiple continents revealed two cryptic species with evidence of genetic introgression. GRW4 clade, which devastated endemic Hawaiian bird populations after introduction approximately 100 years ago, showed no positive selection despite release from host selective pressures. Findings underscore genomic analysis importance for establishing species limits and understanding pathogen evolution in vector-transmitted pathogens.

articles.headline: Indonesian plants yield potent antimalarial compounds
articles.title_original: Harnessing Indonesian natural products against neglected tropical diseases: Malaria.
articles.authors: Nugraha AS, Nawiyanto, Muhyidin A, Qonitah ZN, Anggraeni BA, Triatmoko B, Pratama ANW, Keller PA, Siregar JE, Khasanah U, Wangchuk P
articles.details: Comprehensive review of 208 Indonesian medicinal plants with reported antimalarial activity. 99 extracts and fractions from 61 species were evaluated against P. falciparum strains. 17 plant species underwent detailed chemical isolation, yielding 146 structurally diverse compounds. Several showed potent anti-plasmodial activity at nanomolar concentrations: samaradine X (IC50 14 nM), norcaesalpinins E (IC50 90 nM), methyl 15-acetoxylansiolate (IC50 170 nM), and dehatrine (IC50 170 nM), compared to chloroquine IC50 of 290 nM.

articles.headline: Housing quality protects children from malaria across sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Housing and malaria risk: Evidence across ecological and climatic gradients in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: AlAbbas M
articles.details: Pooled analysis of DHS and MIS data from 17 sub-Saharan African countries (2006–2022, n=206,723) using logistic regression to predict positive malaria RDT results. Modern housing was associated with lower odds of RDT positivity (β=−0.543, p<0.001), independent of socioeconomic status, WASH conditions, net usage, and ecological-climatic controls. This protective effect persisted across diverse ecological contexts and climatic gradients, highlighting housing quality as a durable, non-insecticidal correlate of child malaria vulnerability.

articles.headline: Simplified malaria control model preserves key dynamics
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.
articles.authors: Korsah MA, Johnston ST, Tiedje K, Day K, Walker CR, Flegg JA
articles.details: Study developed a reduced mathematical model for malaria control that preserves essential dynamics of a comprehensive model. Accuracy was validated using Bayesian MCMC; comparative analysis through simulation studies, parameter identifiability analysis, and sensitivity analysis demonstrated that the reduced system exhibits similar transmission characteristics as the full model. The reduced model provides improved flexibility and computational efficiency for evaluating and implementing malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Comment on malaria surveillance in non-endemic Brazil
articles.title_original: Comment on "Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024)".
articles.authors: V VKK, Kshirsagar P, Mishra M, Panwar S
articles.details: Commentary article on comparative analysis of national malaria surveillance data in Brazil from 2007–2024.

articles.headline: PvMSP-1 genetic diversity in Vietnamese P. vivax isolates
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of merozoite surface protein 1 C-terminal 42 kDa in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.
articles.authors: Nguyễn ĐTD, Nguyễn TH, Võ TC, Lê HG, Van Khanh C, Trinh NTM, Hanh NTL, Cho M, Goo YK, Quang HH, Na BK
articles.details: Analysis of genetic polymorphism and natural selection in PvMSP-142 from 88 Vietnamese P. vivax isolates collected from four malaria-endemic provinces. PvMSP-142 exhibited genetic polymorphisms from nucleotide substitutions and meiotic recombination, with most polymorphic sites in PvMSP-133 while PvMSP-119 was relatively well-conserved. Balancing selection appeared to preserve gene diversity. PvMSP-119 was well-conserved across global populations, with implications for vaccine candidate development.

articles.headline: Pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations persist but decline in Gabon
articles.title_original: Status of pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations in Plasmodium falciparum isolates in Gabon in 2019 after chloroquine withdrawal.
articles.authors: Dinzouna-Boutamba SD, Iroungou BA, Akombi FL, Yacka-Mouele L, Moon Z, Lee S, Na BK, Hong Y, Goo YK
articles.details: Study monitored pfcrt and pfmdr1 drug-resistance markers in 70 P. falciparum isolates from Libreville, Gabon using archived dried blood spots from 2019. SNPs identified in 25.71% of pfcrt and 20% of pfmdr1 samples. Most frequent pfcrt mutations: N75(E/K) (24.3%), M74I (22.86%), K76T (17.14%); wild-type CVMNK haplotype dominated at 74.29% versus mutant CVIET at 17.13%. Wild-type pfmdr1 NEYND haplotype in 80% of population; declining mutant frequencies suggest progressive restoration of chloroquine susceptibility.

articles.headline: Critical care management for HIV, TB, and malaria globally
articles.title_original: The Global Intensive Care Unit: Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Tuberculosis, and Malaria.
articles.authors: Riunga F, Wambua GM, Lee BW
articles.details: Review of burden of disease, clinical presentation, diagnostic strategies, and management for HIV, tuberculosis, and malaria in critically ill patients. Over 80% of world population lives in low-income and middle-income countries with limited critical care access; these infectious diseases are leading causes of critical care admissions with high mortality. Early recognition and timely initiation of appropriate therapies are important for improving outcomes in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Anacardic acid modulates inflammatory response in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Saturated anacardic acid shows promise as adjuvant therapy for preventing severe malaria via attenuation of inflammatory response by modulation of the NF-κB pathway.
articles.authors: Gontijo ACGC, Corazza D, Albuquerque LFF, Ciarlini ACL, de Oliveira AS, Monteiro NC, de Oliveira MS, Couto SCP, Romeiro LAS, Borges TKDS, Muniz-Junqueira MI
articles.details: Study evaluated saturated anacardic acid (SAA) as adjunct therapy for severe malaria by assessing effects on inflammatory mediators in BV-2 microglia and THP-1 monocytes incubated with P. falciparum or P. berghei ANKA, and in cerebral malaria-susceptible CBA mice infected with P. berghei ANKA. SAA increased survival in infected mice and decreased TNF-α, IL-8, MCP-1, ROS, NO, and COX-2 expression while modulating NF-κB pathway components RelA and RelB. Findings suggest SAA as promising adjunct to prevent progression to severe malaria.

articles.headline: Corrigendum: urbanization, malaria, and avian microbiome
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to 'Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome' [Environ. Res. 297 (2026) 124073].
articles.authors: Garrigós M, Jiménez-Peñuela J, Saavedra I, Veiga J, García-López MJ, Garrido M, Ruiz-López MJ, Figuerola J, Moreno-Indias I, Martínez-de la Puente J
articles.details: Corrigendum to prior publication on interactions between urbanization, malaria infection, and avian cloacal microbiome.

articles.headline: Multi-epitope Plasmodium vaccine design shows immunogenic promise
articles.title_original: Designing a next-generation multi-stage vaccine for malaria prevention.
articles.authors: Arya H, Tripathi H, Bhatt TK
articles.details: Study designed three multi-epitope subunit vaccine constructs targeting five key Plasmodium proteins using immunoinformatic approaches, incorporating adjuvants, cytotoxic T-lymphocyte epitopes, helper T-lymphocyte epitopes, and B-cell epitopes. Constructs assessed as antigenic, non-allergenic, and non-toxic. Molecular docking with TLR-4 showed construct 1 had most stable binding; molecular dynamics simulations confirmed strong interactions. Immune simulations predicted promising immunogenic profile; in silico cloning indicated potential for efficient E. coli expression. In vitro and in vivo validation remain necessary.

articles.headline: P. vivax merozoite surface protein diversity in Pakistan
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein 3α and β field isolates.
articles.authors: Shaheen N, Ashraf A, Malik N, Naeem M, Zeb I, Nabi G, Alwethaynani MS, Mohamed RAEH, Elkhadragy MF, Al-Hoshani N
articles.details: Analysis of genetic diversity of Pvmsp-3α and 3β in field isolates from Azad Jammu and Kashmir, Pakistan using blood samples (n=207) collected March–August 2024. P. vivax detected in 188/207 samples (90.82%); Pvmsp-3α amplified in 98/188 showing three allelic variants (type A 66.33%, type B 11.22%, type C 13.27%); Pvmsp-3β amplified in 90/188 with two variants (type A 76.67%, type B 23.33%). Findings contribute to understanding molecular epidemiology and genotypic complexity of P. vivax for control strategies.

articles.headline: Black cumin-acha cookies reduce parasitemia in diabetic mice
articles.title_original: Functional-Diet Potential of Black Cumin-Enriched Acha-Based Cookies on Plasmodium berghei-Infected Diabetic Mice.
articles.authors: Nwanna EE, Olanrewaju BT, Oyeleye IS, Ojueromi OO, Oboh G
articles.details: Study evaluated black cumin-enriched acha-based cookies in Plasmodium berghei-infected type 2 diabetic mice. Mice induced with streptozotocin (35 mg/kg) and infected with NK65 strain P. berghei, then fed formulated cookies for 14 days with parasitemia and blood glucose measured at 3-day intervals. Diet affected pancreatic enzymes (α-amylase, α-glucosidase), antioxidant activities (superoxide dismutase, catalase, glutathione peroxidase), and inflammatory markers (NF-κB, IL-10), with results indicating reversal of damage in malaria-infected diabetic mice versus negative controls.

articles.headline: Multi-antigen vaccine blocks Plasmodium transmission in mice
articles.title_original: Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi.
articles.authors: Pourhashem Z, Siasi S, Nourani L, Yousefi H, Sani JJ, Vand-Rajabpour H, Raz A, Pirahmadi S, Zargar M, Djadid ND, Zakeri S, Mehrizi AA
articles.details: In BALB/c mice, a vaccine combining antigens APN-1, CelTOS, and cd-HAP with adjuvants CPG, MPL, and QS-21 elicited high-avidity IgG antibodies and robust Th1 responses (elevated IFN-γ and TNF). Standard membrane feeding assay with P. falciparum NF54 gametocytes and Anopheles stephensi mosquitoes showed the antibodies substantially reduced oocyst load, supporting further development of multi-component transmission-blocking vaccine strategies.

articles.headline: Copper complex shows activity against P. falciparum in vitro
articles.title_original: Synthesis, Characterization, and Anti-Plasmodium falciparum Activity of a New Copper(II) Complex Containing 2-(Tert-Butoxy)-6-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)pyridine.
articles.authors: Patiño-Cubides A, Velasco-Pareja MC, Macías MA, Yasnot-Acosta MF, Rodriguez A, Valderrama-Aguirre A, Hurtado JJ
articles.details: A copper(II) complex with imidazole-based ligand was tested in vitro against chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum (3D7 strain). The compound showed low hemocytotoxicity (≤20% at ≥500 µg/mL) and exhibited significantly higher activity than the free ligand, suggesting that metal coordination enhances antiparasitic activity and warrants further investigation of metal complexes as antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: Antimicrobial peptides: promising anti-malarial agents from systematic review
articles.title_original: Antimicrobial peptides in malaria and tuberculosis management: a systematic review of emerging evidence.
articles.authors: Walter NS, Gorki V, Medhi B, Avti PK
articles.details: A systematic review of antimicrobial peptides against Plasmodium and Mycobacterium (from APD3 database and PubMed, January 1965 to June 2025) found natural anti-malarial AMPs are defensins and cecropins, while synthetic AMPs are designed to enhance activity and selectivity while reducing toxicity. AMPs possess α-helical, β-sheet, or random-coil structures and are cationic, amphipathic, and hydrophobic; artificial intelligence and machine learning could advance AMP therapy as an alternative to conventional antimalarials.

articles.headline: Retraction: seaweed nanoparticles and mosquito control
articles.title_original: Retraction notice to 'Seaweed Sargassum wightii mediated preparation of TiO(2) nanoparticles, larvicidal activity against malaria and filariasis vectors, and its effect on non-target organisms' [Environ. Res. 225 (2023) 115569].
articles.authors: Mathivanan D, Kamaraj C, Suseem SR, Gandhi PR, Malafaia G
articles.details: This is a retraction notice; the original article described TiO2 nanoparticles prepared using seaweed Sargassum wightii, with claimed larvicidal activity against malaria and filariasis vectors. The retraction removes previous claims from the literature.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic P. falciparum in Yemen: environmental and household risk factors
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic malaria and its associated factors in the Al Zuhrah district, Al-Hodeidah Government, Yemen, 2022.
articles.authors: Hajjam MA, Al Amad MA, Nasher SS
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 422 participants in Al-Zuhra District, Yemen (mean age 35.7 years) found 5% prevalence of asymptomatic P. falciparum by rapid diagnostic test. Education was protective (adjusted prevalence odds ratio 0.35, 95% CI 0.13–0.97, p=0.043), while absence of window screens (APOR 9.56, 95% CI 2.15–42.51, p=0.003) and proximity to stagnant water (APOR 4.93, 95% CI 1.50–16.23, p=0.009) were strongly associated with infection.

articles.headline: Pf12 and Pf41: conserved vaccine-candidate antigens in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Conserved genetic and antigenic profiles of Pf12 and Pf41 in asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum carriers in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Fitriana F, Lee WJ, Sim HY, Jun H, Syahada JH, Muh F, Lee SJ, Na S, Lee KJ, Nam JW, Han ET, Todd J, Manjurano A, Kidima W, Mazigo E, Han JH
articles.details: In 172 P. falciparum isolates from asymptomatic carriers in Tanzania, genetic analysis revealed low nucleotide diversity for pf12 (π = 0.00042 ± 0.00007) and pf41 (π = 0.00061 ± 0.00004), with negative Tajima's D values indicating purifying selection. Both antigens elicited high IgG seropositivity (Pf12 97.7%, Pf41 98.3%) with strongly correlated antibody levels (ρ = 0.721, p < 0.001), supporting their potential as conserved vaccine candidates with robust exposure-driven immunity.

articles.headline: Parasite-host interactions: molecular basis for malaria vaccines and therapeutics
articles.title_original: Host-Pathogen Interactions in Malaria: Invasion, Neutralization, and Evasion.
articles.authors: Dickey TH, Tolia NH
articles.details: A review of receptor-ligand interactions between Plasmodium parasites and human hosts, focusing on clinically relevant antigens critical for parasite motility, cell traversal, and invasion. The authors examine antibody-antigen interactions, parasite immune evasion mechanisms, and how understanding this evolutionary interplay has inspired vaccine and therapeutic development, providing a framework for designing improved next-generation interventions.

articles.headline: ITN utilization in Southeast Asia: geography and SES drive non-use
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated nets utilization and determinants in Southeast Asia toward malaria elimination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Soe BK, Huertas-López A, Nugraheni YR, Adisakwattana P
articles.details: A meta-analysis of 28 studies (20 with quantitative data; 122,258 individuals across 14,556 households) published 2003–2023 examined insecticide-treated net utilization in Southeast Asia. Geographic region was the strongest determinant of non-use (coastal versus delta areas: pooled odds ratio 4.73, 95% CI 1.59–14.06), followed by socioeconomic status (poorest versus richest: pooled OR 1.70, 95% CI 1.01–2.86), indicating structural and contextual factors are more important than demographics for ITN use.

articles.headline: RIPR antibodies synergize to block P. falciparum invasion via EGF domains
articles.title_original: Analysis of monoclonal antibodies against the malaria invasion complex protein RIPR reveals the structural basis for synergistic antibody protection.
articles.authors: Williams BG, Barrett JR, Scott JB, Rigby CA, Cagiada M, Quinkert D, McHugh K, Huhn A, Burnap SA, Gourjault C, Byrne F, Raghavan SSR, Rodrigues A, Bergamaschi L, Balzarotti B, Watson S, Miller N, King LDW, Donnellan FR, Gladstone CA, Paterson J, Scalabrino S, Silk SE, Salkeld J, Minassian AM, Skinner K, Struwe WB, Deane CM, Reece ST, Ward AB, Draper SJ
articles.details: Analysis of 83 human IgG monoclonal antibodies from RIPR-vaccinated mice showed single antibodies had minimal neutralizing activity, but pools targeting the RIPR-tail region, particularly antibodies against epidermal growth factor domains 6–8, synergized to constrain RIPR conformation. Combining these antibodies with sera from humans vaccinated with R78C (RIPREGF(7-8)-CyRPA) candidate vaccine enhanced parasite growth inhibition, supporting next-generation blood-stage vaccine design.

articles.headline: Digital surveillance system enables rapid malaria case response in Cambodia
articles.title_original: Digital Health Center App for Community and National Malaria Surveillance in Cambodia: Implementation Case Study.
articles.authors: Ngor P, Rekol H, Thyda E, Rith R, Kimleng S, Rattana Y, Vunsokserey O, Vanna H, Pumeoung C, Ringwald P, Maude RJ, White L, Sovannaroth S
articles.details: Cambodia's Android-based Malaria Information System app achieved 99% data completeness with real-time, geo-located case reporting across 761 health centers in 21 provinces. Between January–July 2025, 69 national malaria cases were reported; 95.7% (66/69) were notified and classified within 1 day, 100% of 21 reactive case detection investigations completed within 3 days, and 84.2% of eligible foci responses within 7 days, supporting sustainable surveillance and decentralized decision-making.

articles.headline: Ginsenoside Rg1 targets glucose metabolism to block P. falciparum growth
articles.title_original: Selective targeting of Plasmodium falciparum hexose transporter by phytochemical Ginsenoside Rg1 disrupts glucose metabolism and blocks development of parasite.
articles.authors: Shafi S, Maurya P, Bansal R, Sharma J, Mishra SK, Najmi AK, Singh S
articles.details: Ginsenoside Rg1, a natural compound, was identified as a PfHT (P. falciparum hexose transporter) inhibitor and validated in yeast. In vitro studies showed Ginsenoside Rg1 suppressed growth of both artemisinin-sensitive and artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum and potentiated dihydroartemisinin efficacy; in vivo (P. berghei ANKA-infected mice) it reduced parasite multiplication and increased mean survival time, supporting its development for combating drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: DHA-PPQ for malaria IPTp does not interact with antiretrovirals
articles.title_original: Effects of malaria intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine on antiretroviral drug concentrations in African pregnant women living with HIV.
articles.authors: Stöger L, Mischlinger J, Nhampossa T, Mombo-Ngoma G, Esen M, Tchouatieu A-M, Brunet M, Mendes A, Dimessa Mbadinga LB, Gamberini S, Ndzebe Ndoumba W, Figueroa-Romero A, Lell B, Lagler H, Zoleko-Manego R, El Gaaloul M, Méndez S, Botella N, Sanz S, Piqueras M, Sevene E, Saúte F, Wicha SG, Soy D, Menéndez C, Ramharter M, González R
articles.details: A clinical trial (NCT03671109) of 105 pregnant women living with HIV in Mozambique and Gabon (January–November 2021) evaluated monthly intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) on antiretroviral concentrations. At end of pregnancy, dolutegravir (median 835.1 ng/mL maternal, 1,204.2 ng/mL cord) and lamivudine (median 108.3 ng/mL maternal, 186.5 ng/mL cord) showed no significant differences between DHA-PPQ and placebo arms, indicating no clinically relevant drug interaction.

articles.headline: Iron homeostasis targets mosquito transmission potential
articles.title_original: Targeting Iron Homeostasis Curtails Mosquito Fertility and Plasmodium Transmission Potential.
articles.authors: Tupe C, Pasupareddy R, Bhatt S, Goyal B, Kumar A, Gupta A, Lalwani A, Das RN, Sunil S, Singh S, Pandey KC, Chakraborti S
articles.details: Computational and functional characterization of mosquito ferritin revealed a unique chimeric structure with high heme-binding affinity. Functional knockdown of ferritin heavy-chain in Anopheles stephensi females caused profound reproductive tissue defects. The iron chelator β-thujaplicin significantly suppressed Plasmodium oocyst development in vivo, establishing iron homeostasis as a target for transmission-blocking interventions.

articles.headline: IL-33 SNPs influence P. falciparum infection susceptibility
articles.title_original: Association of interleukin-33 gene polymorphisms with susceptibility to Plasmodium falciparum infection.
articles.authors: Aljarba NH, Alghnnam F, Al-Qahtani AA, Al-Hazani TM, Al Marzan A, Bohol MFF, Alhamlan F, Shafeai MI, Rudiny FH, Motaen AM, Alkahtani S, Al-Qahtani AA
articles.details: Case-control study in Saudi Arabia (250 active P. falciparum cases, 200 healthy controls) genotyped 13 IL-33 SNPs. rs10118795 CT increased malaria susceptibility (OR=1.584, 95% CI 1.068-2.350; p=0.022); rs55726619 CT was protective (OR=0.042, 95% CI 0.002-0.725; p<0.001). rs55726619 and rs10815393 showed strong age-dependent effects, with particularly high enrichment in children aged 1-5 years (OR=650.46, p=7.12×10⁻¹¹ and OR=172.69, p=5.91×10⁻⁸). Haplotype set 4 associated with susceptibility (p=2.76×10⁻⁶).

articles.headline: Precipitation timing predicts malaria risk across Mozambique regions
articles.title_original: Exploratory analysis of the correlation between precipitation and storms and malaria cases from national surveillance in Mozambique.
articles.authors: Zhu H, Zhang L, Khan E, Srivastava J, Croal M, Candrinho B, da Silva M, Searle KM
articles.details: Analysis of district-level malaria surveillance data from Mozambique correlated with satellite precipitation data using Poisson generalized linear mixed models. Found strong spatial variation in malaria cases, hospitalizations, and deaths. Nationally, increased relative risk for malaria at 8-week lag after precipitation; regional variation: Northern Region 6-8 weeks, Central Region 10-12 weeks, Southern Region 10-13 weeks. Regional heterogeneity suggests need for location-specific response strategies.

articles.headline: p38-MAPK degradation reduces P. falciparum infection in vitro
articles.title_original: Chemical degradation of Human p38-MAPK reveals it as a potential host target to combat parasitic infections by Leishmania donovani and Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Singhal J, Rawat N, Bansal R, Gupta A, Srivastava P, Madan E, Jha N, Ghouse Peer GD, Pandey RP, Ranganathan A, Singh S
articles.details: Used NR-7h PROTAC to degrade human p38-MAPK in Leishmania donovani and P. falciparum infected cells. Degradation was time- and dose-dependent; parasite load was reduced dose-dependently. For P. falciparum, degradation impaired merozoite growth and invasion. Combined treatment with Amphotericin B enhanced efficacy against Leishmania. Suggests p38-MAPK signaling is essential for parasite survival and potential host-directed therapy target.

articles.headline: Rapid extraction-free CRISPR detection of P. falciparum and P. vivax
articles.title_original: Extract-free one-pot ambient RPA-CRISPR detection of Plasmodium in whole blood.
articles.authors: Liao J, Su Y, Jiang F
articles.details: Developed extraction-free RPA-CRISPR assay for detecting Plasmodium directly from crude blood lysates with lateral flow detection. Analytical sensitivity 100 copies/μL (~12-20 parasites/μL). Clinical evaluation in 116 malaria-positive and 109 malaria-negative specimens showed 93.1% overall sensitivity, 100% specificity vs qPCR. Sensitivity varied by parasite density: 97.8% high-density, 95.1% moderate-density, 82.8% low-density. Workflow completed in 40 minutes at room temperature without specialized equipment.

articles.headline: Novel KRS inhibitors show multistage antimalarial efficacy
articles.title_original: Structure-Guided Optimization of Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Lysyl-tRNA Synthetase with Multistage Activity against Malaria Parasites.
articles.authors: Forte B, Bellany F, Campbell PS, Chemi G, Dawson A, Anderson M, Aniweh Y, Burkhard AY, Aguiar ACC, Churchyard A, Cooper CA, Dos Santos Ferreira A, Famodimu MT, Fang FG, Hu X, Huijs T, Baud D, Jansen C, Jiménez Díaz MB, Bonnert R, Boyd S, Crespo-Fernández B, Mitasev B, Montagna S, Mok S, Murugesan D, Narwal SK, Norcross NR, Okombo J, Park H, Peet C, Pereira DB, Post JM, Reader J, Riley J, Robinson DA, Shinkyo R, Simeons FRC, Simpson L, Smith A, Smith D, Striepen J, Teles CBG, van der Laak R, Uhlemann AC, Vantaux A, Wilson C, Witkowski B, Wood G, Yeo T, Zuccotto F, Angulo-Barturen I, Baum J, Bolscher JM, Guido RVC, Birkholtz LM, Delves MJ, Dembele L, Fidock DA, Gamo FJ, Kyle DE, Maher SP, Popovici J, Walpole C, Gusovsky F, Willis PA, Read KD, Gilbert IH, Baragaña B
articles.details: Structure-guided optimization of lysyl-tRNA synthetase (KRS) inhibitors from GHCDL screening identified compound 30 with excellent selectivity (>1000-fold over human enzyme). Compound 30 showed potent activity against P. falciparum including drug-resistant strains and field isolates, and other Plasmodium species. Achieved single-dose cure in SCID mouse model with favorable safety profile and long half-life. Unfortunately lacked oral bioavailability despite chemical modifications.

articles.headline: Low education and ITN non-use drive malaria in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Assessment of malaria prevalence and determinants among clinically suspected individuals attending Dejen Primary Hospital, East Gojjam Zone, northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Emnetu A, Habtamu A, Lemlemu A, Yeshanew S
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study of 385 symptomatic individuals in Ethiopia (April-November 2024) found 46.2% positive by microscopy, predominantly P. falciparum (56.1% of malaria cases). Lack of formal education (AOR=2.16, 95% CI 0.46-3.28; p=0.001), proximity to breeding sites (AOR=2.15, 95% CI 0.76-3.25; p=0.020), no insecticide-treated net use (AOR=1.72, 95% CI 0.33-1.84; p=0.001), and no indoor residual spraying (AOR=1.02, 95% CI 0.27-2.05; p=0.035) were significant risk factors.

articles.headline: ddPCR assay for P. vivax drug-resistance marker detection
articles.title_original: Droplet digital PCR assays for Pvmdr1 and Pvcrt-o gene copy number variation determination in Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Srisutham S, Boonpeng K, Pitchayatanakorn P, Kijprasong K, Nosten FH, Day NPJ, Dondorp AM, Imwong M
articles.details: Developed duplex digital PCR assay for absolute quantification of P. vivax pvmdr1 and pvcrt-o gene copy number variation using pvtubulin as single-copy reference. Quantification limits: 22.4±3.5 copies/μL (pvmdr1) and 21.5±2.6 copies/μL (pvcrt-o). Field samples from Thailand (N=122): Tak Province (2008) showed 16% with multiple pvmdr1 copies, all with single pvcrt-o copy. Kanchanaburi Province (2023-2025) showed no evidence of multiple copies of either gene.

articles.headline: SNP-rich markers improve P. falciparum recrudescence detection
articles.title_original: Comparison of techniques and markers to distinguish Plasmodium falciparum recrudescence from new infection in Rwanda.
articles.authors: Cantoreggi SL, Golumbeanu M, Zwyer M, Uwimana A, Niyonzima JD, Mbituyumuremyi A, Lucchi NW, Plucinski MM, Nsanzabana C
articles.details: Compared genotyping techniques for distinguishing P. falciparum recrudescence from new infection in Rwanda 2018 therapeutic efficacy study samples. Tested capillary electrophoresis (microsatellites, msp1/2, glurp) vs amplicon deep sequencing (SNP-rich markers cpmp, ama1, cpp, csp). WHO, 2/3, and Bayesian correction algorithms gave different recrudescence classifications. Amplicon sequencing with SNP-rich markers provided most consistent results regardless of decision algorithm, suggesting superior utility for PCR correction in drug efficacy assessment.

articles.headline: Flood exposure drives malaria burden in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of malaria risk in flood-prone zones of Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Eudaric J, de Ruiter MC, Sairam N, Camero A, Shahi KR, Smith MW, Zhu XX, Kreibich H
articles.details: Geospatial analysis integrated high-resolution flood maps (2000-2018), malaria incidence data, and population data across 492 flood-prone zones in 38 sub-Saharan African countries. Found elevated relative risk for malaria in flood zones vs national baselines in East and West Africa. Approximately 12 million individuals diagnosed with P. falciparum were exposed to flooding events, representing one-third of flood-affected population. Flood exposure identified as a main driver of malaria burden in these high-risk zones.

articles.headline: MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage P. falciparum in adults
articles.title_original: The pyrrolidinamide antimalarial drug MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage Plasmodium falciparum in healthy adults with experimental malaria.
articles.authors: Barber BE, Denhardt-Eriksson R, Guirou EA, Flynn J, Sahai N, Leelasena I, Mathison S, Moller C, Lim I, Evangelista C, Webster R, Potter AJ, Llewellyn S, Amante FH, Gower J, Peters J, Khoury DS, Thathy V, Narwal SK, Yeo T, Fidock DA, Marx MW, Gamo FJ, Sharma R, Gibson RA, Sanz L, Cahn A, Bestgen B, Chalon S
articles.details: Volunteer infection study in 12 healthy malaria-naïve adults inoculated with P. falciparum 3D7, receiving single oral MMV367 dose (range 3-1500 mg) on day 8. Maximum plasma concentrations at 2-4 hours; elimination half-life 12.9-18 hours. For doses ≥20 mg, parasite clearance half-life 2.2-4.3 hours with viable parasite elimination half-life 1.1-2.7 hours. Model-derived MIC 6.3 ng/ml (95% CI 5.2-7.8); minimum parasiticidal concentration 54 ng/ml (95% CI 43-68). No serious adverse events or in vitro resistance development.

articles.headline: MSCs modulate macrophage response in experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Macrophage markers and gene signature profiling reveals mesenchymal stem cells mediated immune modulation in Plasmodium berghei ANKA infection.
articles.authors: Gupta A, Sharan Thakur R, Ojha RK, Khan T, Kalkal M, Das J
articles.details: In Pb.ANKA-infected C57BL/6 mice, intravenous mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) administration reduced parasitemia, improved survival, preserved blood-brain barrier integrity (Evans blue extravasation), and attenuated brain pathology. Flow cytometry of splenic macrophages showed reduced M1 markers (CD86, CD38) and maintained/enhanced M2 markers (CD163, CD206, EGR-2). Gene expression analysis revealed downregulation of iNOS-2 and upregulation of Arginase-1, EGR-2, GPR-18, FPR-1, PTGES-2, indicating macrophage reprogramming toward balanced M1/M2 response.

articles.headline: IMC55 protein dispensable for P. falciparum asexual replication
articles.title_original: The inner membrane complex protein, IMC55, is dispensable for intraerythrocytic development of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Vick GW, Fierro MA, Castiglione A, Park L, Brooks C, Muralidharan V
articles.details: Study used conditional mutants to test IMC55 protein function in P. falciparum blood-stage development. Despite transposon mutagenesis predicting IMC55 as essential, conditional expression systems showed IMC55 is not required for asexual replication or replicative fitness, suggesting redundant function or alternative role in mosquito stages.

articles.headline: HLQ-102 quinolone targets P. falciparum cytochrome bc1 complex
articles.title_original: Revisiting 2-Substituted-4(1H)-Quinolones for Targeting the Plasmodium falciparum Cytochrome bc(1) Complex.
articles.authors: Pou S, Liebman KM, Winter RW, Nilsen A, Li Y, Lyche IN, Riscoe TM, Montgomery J, Gramelspacher MJ, Dodean RA, Zakharov LN, Nepal B, Kelly JX, Smilkstein MJ, Kortagere S, Vaidya AB, Alday PH, Doggett JS, Kudyba K, Roncal N, Ashraf K, Leed S, Lee PJ, Madejczyk MS, Roth A, Riscoe MK
articles.details: Researchers synthesized HLQ-102, a 2-position isomer of ELQ-300, and profiled its antimalarial activity and pharmacokinetics. Cross-resistance patterns indicate HLQ-102 targets the Qi site of the cytochrome bc1 complex, suggesting the target protein accommodates large structural features regardless of substitution position.

articles.headline: Community survey shows high acceptability of mass drug administration in Ghana
articles.title_original: Community perspective on feasibility of malaria mass drug administration: a qualitative evidence from the Eastern Region of Ghana.
articles.authors: Ndong IC, Chu CE, Ndang NH, Baah CA, Adu GA, Amponsah E, Peprah NY, Malm K, Ogundahunsi O, Ahorlu CS, Amambua-Ngwa A
articles.details: Cross-sectional qualitative study with 97 community members and 8 key informants in Pokrom subdistrict, Ghana, conducted after pilot MDA interventions. Households reported notable reductions in malaria episodes and decreased healthcare costs. Despite transient adverse events (dizziness, abdominal pain, weakness), community willingness to participate in future MDA rounds remained high.

articles.headline: Mathematical analysis improves ITN distribution modeling across 44 countries
articles.title_original: Dynamical and time-series approach to understanding compartmental stock and flow models: a case study in malaria intervention models.
articles.authors: Tan E, van den Berg M, Vargas C, Gething PW, Symons TL
articles.details: Study applied dynamical systems and time-series analysis to compartmental stock-and-flow models for estimating ITN distribution and attrition. Researchers developed an iterative EM algorithm to address calibration challenges from disparate sampling frequencies and applied it to delivery and household survey data across 44 countries spanning 24 years, providing robust estimates of net distribution volumes.

articles.headline: Kelch13 and pfmdr1 mutations prevalent in P. falciparum from Uganda
articles.title_original: Polymorphism of pfmdr1 Gene Mutation Conferring Resistance to Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapy in Plasmodium falciparum in Patients at Gulu Regional Referral Hospital in Northern Uganda.
articles.authors: Amito FP, Angwech H, Ojok L, Wokorach G, Ochaya S, Ogwang Q, Opiro R, Echodu R
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 353 febrile patients in Uganda (Jan 2022–Jan 2024), with 214 (60.6%) infected with P. falciparum. Genotyping showed pfmdr1 SNP at Codon 1034–1042 in 29.6% (n=94), Codon 86–184 in 28% (n=89), Codon 1246 in 25.8% (n=82), and Kelch13 propeller mutations in 16.7% (n=53). Median parasite loads varied significantly by mutation status (p≤0.0001 for 86–184, p=0.0061 for 1034–1042, p=0.012 for K13).

articles.headline: Social beliefs and gender dynamics shape malaria treatment-seeking in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: The social experience of malaria treatment-seeking: pathways, gender, and inequality in Jimma Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Gerbi T, Teshome D, Sudhakar M
articles.details: Ethnographic study in Jimma City with 32 in-depth interviews and 8 key informant interviews examining malaria treatment pathways. Treatment-seeking followed pluralistic pathways beginning with home remedies and prayer, progressing to biomedical care. Gender dynamics influenced care-seeking, with females recognizing illness but lacking decision-making authority; structural barriers included poverty, drug stockouts, and inadequate housing.

articles.headline: Mixed P. vivax/P. falciparum malaria complicates SLE with hemorrhagic stroke
articles.title_original: Libman sacks endocarditis with multivalvular involvement, hemorrhagic stroke, and concurrent mixed plasmodium malaria in systemic lupus erythematosus: a case report.
articles.authors: Khan A, Tahir O, Batool A, Farman J, Javaid MH, Shehzad E, Soroush AR, Hassan F, Wazir HU
articles.details: Case report of 29-year-old female with 4-year SLE history presenting with fever and encephalopathy (GCS 7/15). Investigations revealed hemolytic anemia (hemoglobin 6.0 g/dL), thrombocytopenia (platelets 39 × 10³/µL), elevated inflammatory markers (CRP 42.2 mg/dL, ESR 120 mm/hr), and mixed P. vivax and P. falciparum infection. Echocardiography confirmed multivalvular Libman-Sacks endocarditis with moderate-severe regurgitation; CT revealed hemorrhagic infarct (7 × 5.2 cm) with 15 mm midline shift. Patient died despite maximal supportive care.

articles.headline: Malaria training improves knowledge but not compliance with guidelines
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria training on community healthcare providers' compliance with malaria treatment guidelines in Nigeria using propensity score matching.
articles.authors: Baruwa S, Akomolafe TO, Okafor EE, Ubuane O, Diallo R, Alagbile M, Ladokun J, Ekele J, Yusuf D
articles.details: Quasi-experimental study using propensity score matching with baseline and follow-up (Feb 2022–Dec 2022) data from Community Pharmacists and Patent and Proprietary Medicine Vendors in Nigeria. Training significantly improved knowledge of diagnosis and treatment (β=0.052, p=0.03) and acceptability of WHO test-treat-refer guidelines (β=0.432, p=0.03). However, training had no effect on actual compliance with treatment guidelines or identification of quality drugs.

articles.headline: Neurological manifestations predict ICU transfer in children with severe malaria
articles.title_original: Clinical profiles and predictors of intensive care unit transfer among children hospitalized with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in a tertiary care hospital in Libreville, Gabon: a retrospective study.
articles.authors: Moutombi-Ditombi BC, Lembet Mikolo AM, Ndong Ngomo JM, M'bondoukwé NP, Mihindou CJ, Moussavou Mabika DA, Mawili-Mboumba DP, Ategbo SJ, Bouyou-Akotet MK
articles.details: Retrospective study of 480 children (median age 6 years) with WHO-defined severe P. falciparum malaria hospitalized Jan 2021–Jul 2022 in Libreville, Gabon. Neurological manifestations (64.2%) predominated (prostration 49.2%, impaired consciousness 10.8%, coma 4.2%). Overall mortality was 0.7%; 8.5% (n=41) required ICU transfer. In multivariable analysis, impaired consciousness (aOR 14.86, 95% CI 5.58–42.40, p<0.01) and coma (aOR 53.3, 95% CI 10.9–178.1, p<0.01) were strongest ICU predictors. Risk increased markedly with ≥3 concurrent severe malaria criteria (aOR 10.59, 95% CI 2.38–42.87, p<0.01).

articles.headline: Automated hematology analyzer achieves 98.4% sensitivity for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Field diagnostic performance of the Sysmex XN31 automated hematology analyser for malaria screening among febrile children in a sentinel site for malaria surveillance site in Libreville, Gabon.
articles.authors: Chesly MDB, Patrick MN, Bedrich PN, Adissa AA, Joanito O, Tatiana N, Patricia MD, Karine BAM
articles.details: Prospective diagnostic study of 1034 febrile children <15 years in Libreville, Gabon comparing Sysmex XN-31 hematology analyzer to microscopy. Malaria prevalence was 23.9% (microscopy) vs 24.3% (XN-31). XN-31 sensitivity was 98.4% (95% CI 96.9–99.9%) and specificity 99.9% (95% CI 98.8–99.9%), with AUC 0.991 (95% CI 0.984–0.997). Sensitivity was slightly lower in children <5 years (96.9%, 95% CI 92.3–98.5%) and at parasitemia <100 parasites/µL (90.7%, 95% CI 82.0–99.3%).

articles.headline: IL-9 drives MDSC expansion and immunopathology in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: IL-9 Orchestrates MDSC Expansion and Inflammatory Programming to Amplify Immunopathology During Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.authors: Ghosh P, Ghosh S, Khamaru P, Gangopadhyay A, Choudhury A, Hossain Daptary A, Halder S, Brahma S, Thakur P, Singha P, Karmakar D, Mukherjee S, Bhattacharyya A
articles.details: Experimental study in rodents infected with P. berghei ANKA investigating IL-9's role in immune response during cerebral malaria. IL-9 promoted myeloid-derived suppressor cell (MDSC) expansion, particularly PMN-MDSCs, and enhanced pro-inflammatory phenotypes in MDSCs, macrophages, and dendritic cells. IL-9 and MDSCs maintained a positive feedback loop where IL-1β from MDSCs controlled Th-9 differentiation. Neutralizing IL-9 in vivo reduced immunopathology and increased host survival.

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum malaria acquired locally in receptive Italy
articles.title_original: Cryptic severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Maremma (Tuscany, Italy), 2024: a case report from a historically receptive area.
articles.authors: Cassol C, Rossetti B, Cosma C, Radi A, Gobbi F, Zacchini F, Stella G, Angeli G, Lanari A, Croci L, Chigiotti S, Carli T, Briganti G, Romani D, Ceriale E, Bonaccorsi G, Lorini C, Del Riccio M, Bonanni P, Nencioni C
articles.details: Case report of Italian patient with no travel history diagnosed with severe P. falciparum malaria in August 2024 in Tuscany's Maremma coast, a historically receptive area. Marked thrombocytopenia prompted blood smear showing 10% parasitemia, confirmed by LAMP. Patient was treated with intravenous artesunate with clinical improvement. No index case, secondary cases, or transmission chain were identified.

articles.headline: Novel PfDHODH inhibitors identified via computational drug design
articles.title_original: Virtual screening and molecular dynamics analysis of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) inhibitors targeting pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway.
articles.authors: Alzahrani AR, Alhuthali HM, Alzahrani S, Hawsawi NM, Alzahrani AYA, Rehman ZU, Almohammed KH, Khan A
articles.details: Computational study employing virtual screening of 1,500 compounds to identify PfDHODH inhibitors from a structurally diverse chemical library. Three compounds shortlisted based on binding affinity; molecular dynamics simulations (500 ns) confirmed stable accommodation in the catalytic pocket. Compounds 17433008 (ΔG = -86.46 kcal/mol) and 17507474 (ΔG = -85.91 kcal/mol) demonstrated most favorable MM/GBSA binding free energies and are proposed as promising lead candidates for experimental validation.

articles.headline: PfEMP1-specific Fc-afucosylated IgG associates with reduced parasite prevalence
articles.title_original: Fc-fucosylation of Plasmodium falciparum-specific IgG varies with antigen and immunization.
articles.authors: Partey FD, Dos Santos MI, Seidu Z, Tiono AB, Thomson-Luque R, Wuhrer M, Larsen MD, Vidarsson G, Kurtis J, Blackman MJ, Lanzer M, Theisen M, Ofori MF, Hviid L, Lopez-Perez M
articles.details: Analysis of plasma IgG Fc-fucosylation against multiple P. falciparum antigens in children naturally exposed to parasites and vaccinated adults. Surface-displayed antigens (PfEMP1, PfGARP) induced markedly Fc-afucosylated IgG; parasite-membrane antigens (merozoite, gametocyte, sporozoite, CSP) consistently induced fully Fc-fucosylated IgG. Children with Fc-afucosylated PfGARP-specific IgG showed reduced risk of carrying parasites independent of total PfGARP-IgG levels, supporting antigen context-dependent Fc glycan modulation.

articles.headline: WHO announces first malaria drug for infants and rapid diagnostics
articles.title_original: WHO Announces First Malaria Drug for Infants and New Rapid Diagnostic Tests.
articles.authors: Anderer S

articles.headline: Hemoglobin digestion genes conserved across lizard-infective Plasmodium species
articles.title_original: Hemoglobin Digestion Genes Are Conserved in Lizard-Infective Plasmodium Species With Different Host Cellular Niches.
articles.authors: Pangburn SJ, Borner J, Misra S, Zavalunova J, Perkins SL
articles.details: Transcriptome analysis of three Plasmodium species infecting Anolis lizards (P. floridense, P. azurophilum, P. leucocytica) from Saban anole blood samples. All three parasites transcribed genes canonically involved in hemoglobin digestion despite inhabiting different cellular niches (RBCs vs WBCs) and variation in hemozoin production; evidence suggests selective pressure shifts on these genes in lizard parasites compared to mammalian species, though genes remain functional.

articles.headline: Hepatic copper overload triggers stellate cell activation and fibrosis
articles.title_original: Copper overload drives hepatic stellate cell activation via cuproptosis to exacerbate acute liver injury in murine malaria.
articles.authors: Mo X, Chen Q, Zhong R, Lin Y, Liu Z, Zhang X, Hou X, Lin H, Zhang M, Liu W, He J, Chen Z, Ye M, Deng Z, Jin X, Liu Z, Song J, Yin H, Huang B
articles.details: P. berghei ANKA-infected mouse study examining copper's role in malarial liver injury. Pb infection triggered hepatic copper accumulation and upregulation of cuproptosis markers (FDX1, DLAT, DLST). Copper elevation via disulfiram exacerbated parasitemia, liver parasite burden, hepatic stellate cell activation (α-SMA/Collagen I), fibrosis, and shifted cytokine balance toward profibrosis (TGF-β/PDGF↑, IFN-γ/HGF↓). Copper chelation with tetrathiomolybdate reversed this pathological cascade.

articles.headline: Organoids and organs-on-chip advance human-relevant malaria research
articles.title_original: Beyond the mouse: organoids, spheroids, and organs-on-chips as the (inevitable) future of malaria research?
articles.authors: Dormoi J, Amalvict R, Pradines B
articles.details: Narrative review examining rodent malaria model contributions and limitations, and emerging complementary role of micro-physiological systems. Murine models do not fully reproduce human malaria, particularly P. falciparum and P. vivax severe, cerebral, placental, and relapsing malaria due to interspecies differences in parasite biology, tissue tropism, and blood-brain barrier interactions. Spheroids offer scalable 3D models; organoids reproduce liver/brain/placenta/vasculature structures; organ-on-chip systems incorporate physiological flow and shear stress.

articles.headline: Real-time PCR assays detect P. inui, P. cynomolgi, P. coatneyi
articles.title_original: Accurate and sensitive TaqMan real-time PCR assays to detect and quantify simian malaria parasites Plasmodium cynomolgi, Plasmodium inui and Plasmodium coatneyi.
articles.authors: Ariffin MM, Syaripuddin K, Kadir KA, Lubim D, Yaman K, Divis PCS
articles.details: Development of TaqMan-based real-time PCR assays targeting species-specific 18S rRNA gene regions of three simian Plasmodium species. Assays demonstrated high specificity for target parasites, detected as few as 10 copies of template DNA (approximately 5 parasites) with >95% amplification efficiency and linear standard curves (10–10⁶ copies). Assays produced equivalent results to nested PCR on macaque and mosquito samples with limit of detection of approximately 5 parasites.

articles.headline: AI-enhanced biosensor enables sensitive ring-stage malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence enabled performance evaluation of an enhanced SPR biosensor for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Utshob MAAI, Juwel MB, Ishtiak KM, Rahman A, Rahman MMA, Imam SA, Huq SMI, Khosru QDM
articles.details: Novel surface plasmon resonance biosensor with multilayer N-FK51a+SiO2+Cu+HfO2+black phosphorus structure analyzed via transfer matrix method, finite element method, and finite difference time domain simulations. Biosensor achieved maximum angular sensitivity of 541.42 deg/RIU, full width half maximum of 1.98 deg, detection accuracy of 0.502 deg⁻¹, and quality factor of 272.21 RIU⁻¹. Artificial neural network, ANFIS, and RBFNN models predicted malaria stages correctly.

articles.headline: Three-tier malaria microscopy framework sustains China's diagnostic capacity
articles.title_original: Development of human resources in malaria microscopy during and after the malaria elimination phase in China.
articles.authors: Li M, Wang Y, Zhou R, Liu H, Xia Z, Yan G, Deng Y, Deng Y, Wang Z, Wang S, Dai Y, Zhou X, Li S
articles.details: Retrospective review of WHO External Competence Assessment (ECAMM) and National Competency Assessment (NCAMM) malaria microscopy courses in China (2017–2024). Among 64 ECAMM participants (mean age 41.0 years), 29 certified Level 1 and 17 Level 2 across 29/31 provinces. Among 72 NCAMM participants, 10 achieved Level A and 33 Level B. Years of microscopy experience showed moderate positive correlation with competence (ρ = 0.4, P < 0.01); species identification scores improved significantly (Z = -7.3, P < 0.01).

articles.headline: Plasmodium bubalis Type I infection documented in Indian water buffaloes
articles.title_original: Clinical, hematobiochemical and phylogenetic studies of bubaline malaria in Indian water buffaloes (Bubalus bubalis).
articles.authors: Chavhan SG, Awandkar SP, Jadhav RK, Kulkarni MB, Kondre BM
articles.details: Survey of 56 water buffaloes (Bubalus bubalis) for P. bubalis; five (8.93%) positive by PCR vs three (5.36%) by blood smear microscopy. Infected buffaloes displayed fever, tachycardia, tachypnea, and icteric/pale mucous membranes; hematobiochemical changes included marked anemia, thrombocytopenia, hyperbilirubinemia, and hypoalbuminemia. Sequence and phylogenetic analysis confirmed P. bubalis Type I, first report in domestic Indian water buffaloes. Human rapid diagnostic tests (pLDH-based) were negative.

articles.headline: Extracellular vesicles enable parasite heat shock adaptation and survival
articles.title_original: Extracellular vesicles participate in proteostasis and heat shock adaptation in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Avalos-Padilla Y, Román-Álamo L, Bouzón-Arnáiz I, Arce EM, Muñoz-Torrero D, Fernàndez-Busquets X
articles.details: PfVps60 knockout study investigating extracellular vesicle (EV) role in P. falciparum heat shock tolerance. ESCRT-dependent trafficking disruption impaired EV cargo composition during thermal stress; 44.8% of EV-associated proteins overlapped aggregation-prone proteins. PfVps60KO parasites showed reduced PfHsp70-x/PfHsp110 export, impaired aggregation dynamics, and early post-heat-shock viability loss. Supplementation with EVs from heat-stressed 3D7 parasites partially rescued heat shock tolerance dose-dependently.

articles.headline: Optimizing spatial repellent impact on malaria in Western Kenya
articles.title_original: Optimizing the impact of a novel spatial repellent on malaria incidence in Western Kenya.
articles.authors: Huwe T, Koepfli C, Ochomo EO, Gimnig JE, Achee NL, Grieco JP, Abong'o B, Moshi V, Perkins A, Moore SM

articles.headline: IgG responses to PfHRP2 distinguish severe from mild malaria
articles.title_original: Differential IgG responses to PfHRP2 across the malaria severity spectrum.
articles.authors: Bhandari S, Krishna S, Verma AK, Ranjha R, Gupta H, Bharti PK
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Study of 240 patients examined Pfhrp2 gene copy numbers and anti-PfHRP2 IgG responses in mild, severe, and cerebral malaria cases. Parasitaemia was higher in mild than severe cases (P=0.0009). PfHRP2 copy numbers were similar across severity groups, but IgG responses were significantly higher in severe, cerebral, and severe non-cerebral malaria versus mild cases (P<0.0001), with positive correlation to age and var P5 peptide (P<0.01). Authors conclude that IgG response to PfHRP2, rather than gene copy number, is critical for determining disease severity.

articles.headline: Wealth and region, not net ownership, predict child malaria in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria among children under five living in net-owning households in Mozambique from the 2022-3 Demographic and Health Survey.
articles.authors: Mooney HA, Muhammad MA, Owusu DN, Adediji PO, Cudjoe NI, Brooks B
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 1,924 households from Mozambique's 2022–3 DHS assessed risk factors for malaria in children under 5. Household net ownership showed no significant association with child malaria after controlling for wealth index. Wealth demonstrated dose-response: middle-income households were 43% less likely to have malaria (aOR=0.57, 95% CI 0.38–0.84, P<0.0048) and highest-income 84% less likely (aOR=0.16, 95% CI 0.09–0.27, P<0.0001) versus lowest-income households. Nampula (aOR=18.97) and Cabo Delgado (aOR=14.70) regions had significantly higher odds (P<0.0005) than Cidade de Maputo.

articles.headline: Light microscopy misses 18–35% of malaria infections in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Diagnostic gaps in light microscopy for malaria case management and control in Dilla Zuria Health Facility, southern Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Bogale A, Tefera S, Mitiku A, Solomon Z, Misganaw T
articles.details: Study of 474 cases at Dilla Zuria Health Facility (Ethiopia) compared light microscopy diagnoses to qPCR confirmation. Microscopy misidentified 24.7% of positive cases overall and 18.8% of negative cases. Species-specific misdiagnosis rates were 21% for P. falciparum, 28.3% for P. vivax, and 34.8% for mixed infections. Notably, 18.8% of microscopy-negative cases were qPCR-positive, indicating submicroscopic infections and preparation/reading errors. Authors recommend structured, competency-based training and robust quality assurance to improve diagnostic accuracy.

articles.headline: Transmission-blocking vaccine study corrigendum published
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi" [Vaccine 84 (2026) 128683].
articles.authors: Pourhashem Z, Siasi S, Nourani L, Yousefi H, Sani JJ, Vand-Rajabpour H, Raz A, Pirahmadi S, Zargar M, Djadid ND, Zakeri S, Mehrizi AA
articles.details: This is a corrigendum (correction notice) to a previously published study on a multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine with Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction findings. No abstract data available.

articles.headline: Mosquito gut proteins critical for Plasmodium transmission control
articles.title_original: Molecular characterization of midgut Peritrophin-44 and Peritrophin-48 and their role in Plasmodium development.
articles.authors: Dubey A, Chauhan VK, Chaitanya RK
articles.details: Study characterized Peritrophin-44 and Peritrophin-48 (Per-44, Per-48) in Anopheles stephensi mosquito midgut. Both proteins contain conserved peritrophin A-domains and are expressed throughout mosquito development, with peak levels in 4th-instar larvae and adults. Following infectious blood meals, both were significantly upregulated during ookinete invasion. RNAi knockdown of Per-44 and Per-48 impaired mosquito fitness and reduced fecundity; Per-48 knockdown most severely reduced oocyst burden. Authors identify these peritrophins as essential transmission components and potential targets for transmission-blocking strategies.

articles.headline: Delayed tafenoquine for vivax relapse lacks sufficient efficacy data
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Delayed Tafenoquine Administration for Radical Cure of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: A Systematic Review.
articles.authors: DeVita TN, Hwang J, Gutman JR, Ridpath AD, Williams S, Chang MA
articles.details: Systematic review searched 6 databases through January 2026 for studies of delayed tafenoquine (≥300 mg given 3+ days after chloroquine/hydroxychloroquine initiation) for P. vivax radical cure. Only 2 randomized trials (1996–1999) met inclusion criteria; both evaluated 500–600 mg tafenoquine on Day 4 or Day 6 after 3-day chloroquine course. Based on limited data using doses higher than currently recommended, authors conclude insufficient evidence exists to definitively recommend delayed tafenoquine, though data suggest it is likely superior to chloroquine alone and could improve adherence compared to 14-day primaquine.

articles.headline: ML-designed biosensor detects glucose and malaria biomarker simultaneously
articles.title_original: Machine Learning Surface Plasmon Resonance Biosensor for Simultaneous Detection of Glucose and Plasmodium falciparum Biomarkers.
articles.authors: Wekalao J, Almawgani AHM, Alhawari ARH, Alammar MM, Abdullah M, Rajakannu A
articles.details: Study presents a theoretically designed multilayer surface plasmon resonance biosensor for simultaneous label-free detection of blood glucose and P. falciparum HRP-II. A polynomial regression machine-learning model trained on 18,000 simulated data points optimized sensor stack composition, achieving 1.82-fold sensitivity improvement over single-layer reference. Optimized sensor achieved angular sensitivities of 221.3 deg/RIU (glucose) and 212.7 deg/RIU (HRP-II) with detection limits of 0.15 mg/dL and 0.8 ng/mL respectively, with estimated per-chip material cost of USD 1.50–4.00.

articles.headline: High-dose primaquine halves P. vivax recurrence in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Analysis of the effect of primaquine dose on efficacy, safety, and tolerability in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: a systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Degaga TS, Thriemer K, Rajasekhar M, Abreha T, Assefa A, Douglas NM, Gadisa E, Hailu A, Hwang J, Koh GCKW, Lacerda MVG, Llanos-Cuentas A, Mekonnen DA, Simpson JA, Tadesse FG, Taylor WRJ, von Seidlein L, Woyessa A, Yeshiwondim AK, Yilma D, Price RN, Bayissa GA, Commons RJ
articles.details: Individual patient data meta-analysis of 5 Ethiopian studies (1,378 total patients) evaluated primaquine dosing for P. vivax radical cure with ≥28 days follow-up. Cumulative incidence of recurrence by day 180 was 61.0% (95% CI 55.3–66.8) without primaquine, 16.6% (95% CI 12.0–22.7) with low-dose (3.5 mg/kg), and 8.9% (95% CI 6.4–12.4) with high-dose (7 mg/kg). High-dose versus low-dose primaquine had adjusted HR 0.40 (95% CI 0.24–0.68, p=0.001). GI disturbance occurred in 16.0–24.1% of patients; clinically significant haemolysis was uncommon in G6PD-normal patients (≥30% activity).

articles.headline: Vector complexity and landscape change complicate Southeast Asian zoonotic malaria
articles.title_original: Zoonotic malaria in Southeast Asia's changing landscapes: vector complexity, challenges, and future strategies.
articles.authors: Jeyaprakasam NK, Zulhaimi AH, Lau KW, Pramasivan S, Phang WK, Ponlawat A, Samung Y, Zhong D, Idris ZM, Sattabongkot J
articles.details: Review synthesizes knowledge on zoonotic malaria (primarily Plasmodium knowlesi) in Southeast Asia, examining vector species complexes with morphological similarity that complicate identification and assessment of competence and biting behavior. Rapid landscape changes from deforestation and agricultural expansion alter mosquito bionomics and promote outdoor/forest-associated transmission. Authors highlight substantial gaps in entomological knowledge, limited vector control strategies suited to zoonotic transmission ecology, and the need for integrated, context-specific, One Health-oriented surveillance and control strategies to address transmission dynamics in rapidly changing Southeast Asian environments.

articles.headline: Serology reveals hidden malaria transmission in Southeast Asia elimination efforts
articles.title_original: Serological surveillance for malaria elimination in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.
articles.authors: Sangaran K, Hassan NW, Goh SJ, Izwan AAMF, Idris ZM
articles.details: Scoping review of 33 studies (26 cross-sectional, 5 longitudinal) across 7 Southeast Asian countries (searched PubMed, Web of Science, Scopus through May 2025) examined malaria serological surveillance. Research focus shifted from high-transmission assessments to low-transmission and elimination contexts, increasingly using multiplex assays and protein microarrays. P. falciparum and P. vivax markers predominated; recent studies incorporated P. knowlesi and vector exposure antigens. Seroconversion rate estimation and machine learning effectively identified historical transmission declines and residual hotspots missed by conventional microscopy and molecular methods. Authors conclude serological surveillance provides high-resolution exposure profiles enabling micro-stratification of transmission foci and verification of transmission interruption.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr ITNs maintain efficacy against resistant mosquitoes for 24 months
articles.title_original: Durability of the insecticidal activity of next-generation insecticide treated nets distributed for malaria control in Mozambique: Findings from the New Nets Project (2020-2022).
articles.authors: Fagbohoun J, Abílio AP, N'Dombidje B, Todjinou D, Baes M, Pigeon O, Padonou GG, Fornadel C, Candrinho B, Koenker H, Robertson M, Wagman J, Ngufor C
articles.details: Study evaluated durability of next-generation ITNs distributed in Mozambique (2020–2022) at 6, 12, and 24 months of household use using WHO cone bioassays and experimental hut trials. All nets maintained 100% efficacy against insecticide-susceptible Anopheles gambiae. Against pyrethroid-resistant populations, Olyset Plus declined from 73% efficacy to 40%, Royal Guard improved from 43% to 87%, and Interceptor G2 (pyrethroid-chlorfenapyr) maintained ≥92% efficacy across all time points. Experimental hut trials confirmed Interceptor G2 superior performance to pyrethroid-only nets, though mortality declined from 55% to 39% by 24 months. Chemical analysis showed major losses of PBO (8% remaining) and chlorfenapyr (32% remaining) by 24 months.

articles.headline: Saquinavir potently inhibits malaria parasite Plasmepsin V enzyme
articles.title_original: Repurposing amide-based drugs unveils inhibition mechanisms of plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin V enzymatic activity for antimalarial therapy.
articles.authors: Prajapati AK, Singh G, Lopus M, Choudhary S
articles.details: Study investigated whether approved drugs favipiravir, 5-fluorouracil, and saquinavir could inhibit Plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin V (PfPlmV) enzymatic activity using isothermal titration calorimetry, enzyme kinetics, and molecular docking. All three compounds bound with affinities ~10⁴ M⁻¹. Saquinavir demonstrated competitive inhibition with IC50 0.35±0.01 μM and Ki 0.14±0.02 μM, interacting with catalytic dyad residues Asp118 and Asp365. 5-fluorouracil showed mixed-type inhibition with higher IC50/Ki values, while favipiravir bound weakly at the cleft periphery without engaging the catalytic dyad. Authors suggest findings may guide drug repurposing strategies for developing selective PfPlmV inhibitors.

articles.headline: Regional disparities in child malaria mortality in Somalia despite declining incidence
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal inequalities in malaria incidence and mortality among children aged 0-4 years in Somalia: a subnational analysis, 2010-2019.
articles.authors: Hassan YSA, Omar MA, Ali AS, Adebayo UO, Okesanya OJ, Sani J, Ahmed MM
articles.details: This retrospective subnational analysis examined P. falciparum and P. vivax incidence and mortality among children aged 0-4 years across 18 Somali regions (2010-2019) using modeled IHME estimates. P. falciparum incidence peaked at 51.6% in 2015 and remained elevated in central/southern regions (Bay 32.0%, Juba Dhexe 31.1%), while P. vivax prevalence stayed low. Despite narrowing incidence gaps (P. falciparum between-group variance declined from 64.3 in 2015 to 20.2 in 2019), mortality inequalities widened markedly: the mortality difference rose from 33.9 (2010) to 118.5 (2014), with children in central/southern Somalia 4.24 times more likely to die than in least-affected regions by 2019.

articles.headline: Prenatal and early childhood malaria associated with impaired growth outcomes
articles.title_original: Prenatal and child malaria and child growth: evidence from a Ghanaian pregnancy cohort.
articles.authors: Sowole O, Kaali S, Li M, Mujtaba M, Wylie BJ, Gyaase S, Chillrud S, Agyei O, Jack D, Lee A, Asante KP
articles.details: In a prospective cohort of 1,144 Ghanaian mother-child pairs, prenatal malaria (21.9%, diagnosed via placental histopathology) was associated with poorer length growth (adjusted OR 1.46, 95% CI 1.05-2.02), poorer length-for-age z-scores (adjusted OR 1.53, 95% CI 1.14-2.05), and smaller head circumference (adjusted OR 1.43, 95% CI 1.04-1.98). Early childhood malaria (45.9%) was associated with poorer length-for-age z-scores (adjusted OR 1.12, 95% CI 1.01-1.24). Prenatal malaria showed a trend toward persistent stunting (adjusted OR 1.63, 95% CI 0.98-2.71).

articles.headline: P. vivax predominates in Papua malaria surveillance; children at higher hospitalization risk
articles.title_original: Epidemiological patterns and healthcare utilization among malaria patients in Sorong City, Papua, Indonesia: A cross-sectional analysis of national surveillance data.
articles.authors: Susilawati TN, Susanto H, Pham OKN, Kondororik F, Dewi VNL, Ahmar H, Putri NR, Lee R, Duong MC
articles.details: Analysis of 3,953 laboratory-confirmed malaria cases in Sorong City (2024) found P. vivax predominant (65.5%) with nearly all uncomplicated infections (99.9%). Microscopy was the primary diagnostic tool (70.1%), yet nearly 21% of patients received non-standard antimalarial regimens. Children under 15 years were more likely to be hospitalized (aOR 1.9, 95% CI 1.18-3.06), while older age and non-Papuan ethnicity predicted hospital attendance. Strengthening community-based services and ensuring adherence to national treatment guidelines are critical to reducing burden.

articles.headline: P. vivax invades Duffy-negative Cameroonians; genetic adaptations in PvDBP1 identified
articles.title_original: Epidemiological insights and genetic diversity of the Duffy binding protein of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative Cameroonians.
articles.authors: Dieng CC, Ning RT, Kipayko C, Schroeder RE, Mdluli-Berndt N, Ayukenchengamba B, Nematchoua Weyou Z, Sona TD, Reward AE, Zhou G, Sumbele Ngole Ule I, Kimbi HK, Lo E
articles.details: Screening of 1,373 Cameroonian samples identified P. vivax in 10.8% of hospital patients and 5.5% of community participants, all in Duffy-negative individuals. PvLDH-based RDTs failed to detect >85% of qPCR-confirmed infections. Genetic analysis of 75 isolates revealed eight nonsynonymous PvDBP1 mutations, with I379L (74.1%) and E225K (61.3%) most common, suggesting adaptive evolution. Phylogenetic analysis showed Cameroonian P. vivax clustered with Botswana isolates, distinct from East African and Asian lineages, indicating regional adaptation.

articles.headline: Socioeconomic and cultural factors drive continued herbal malaria treatment in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Family traditions, accessibility and cost shape the continued reliance on Cajanus cajan and Azadirachta indica for malaria and typhoid despite prescription medicines in urban and rural Nsukka, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Osayi EE, Urama DC, Amujiri AN, Agyo RA, Afyare AAA, Andong FA
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 400 respondents in Nigeria examined indigenous use of pigeon pea and neem for malaria and typhoid. Rural residents relied more on herbal remedies due to accessibility, affordability, and family traditions; urban residents with higher education also used herbal treatments and alternated with prescription drugs. Residence, occupation, and age predicted knowledge and use (P<0.05), but education did not. Perceived efficacy and high prescription costs sustained herbal reliance.

articles.headline: CNBP regulates IL-12/IFN-γ immunity in Plasmodium infection; potential therapeutic target
articles.title_original: Cellular nucleic acid-binding protein (CNBP) dependent cytokine programming shapes host defense against Plasmodium infection.
articles.authors: Rashid R, de Souza Silva L, Banday S, Caffrey DR, Kurt-Jones EA, Fitzgerald KA, Golenbock DT
articles.details: This study characterized CNBP, a transcriptional regulator that translocates to the nucleus upon exposure to Plasmodium-infected erythrocytes and drives IL-12β production in dendritic cells. Genetic ablation of CNBP in mice resulted in markedly reduced IL-12β and IFN-γ responses, elevated peak parasitemia, and impaired parasite clearance with relapse. Paradoxically, CNBP-deficient mice showed delayed mortality in lethal P. berghei ANKA infection. CNBP is positioned as a pivotal modulator of the IL-12β-IFN-γ axis with potential as a therapeutic target.

articles.headline: Gastrointestinal symptoms may delay severe malaria recognition and treatment
