articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Presenting with Gastrointestinal Symptoms in South Sudan: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Tran AD, Nguyen HT, Pham HK
articles.details: Case report of a 29-year-old presenting with nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea initially misdiagnosed as gastrointestinal infection. After 24 hours, he developed fever, hypotension (BP 85-90/55 mmHg), elevated bilirubin (39.6 µmol/L), creatinine (120 µmol/L), and thrombocytopenia (50×10⁹/L). RDT and microscopy confirmed P. falciparum parasitemia (++). Treated with IV artesunate, fluids, and supportive care; became afebrile by day 3. Gastrointestinal presentation can delay recognition of severe malaria.

articles.headline: Optimized P. vivax non-human primate model for vaccine and therapeutic evaluation
articles.title_original: Development of novel Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model for the evaluation of Plasmodium vivax blood stage vaccines and immunoprophylactics.
articles.authors: Ventocilla JA, Tapia LL, Villena FE, Cedamanos D, Buchta J, Pannebaker D, Valdivia HO, Redinger KR, Bosch J, Carias L, Londono-Renteria B, King C, Wilder BK
articles.details: An Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model of P. vivax blood stage infection was optimized using the Vietnam-IV strain at infectious dose 2.5 × 10⁶ iRBCs, producing lowest coefficient of variation (15-23%) in infection kinetics. Hematologic parameters showed increased lymphocytes (5.0-8.5 × 10⁹/L) and decreased neutrophils and platelets. Pilot testing of mAb 099100 against PvDBP-II showed partial protection in 1/3 monkeys versus 0/3 controls. The model provides a platform for evaluating P. vivax vaccines and therapeutics.

articles.headline: P. malariae asymptomatic carriers represent silent reservoir in European screening
articles.title_original: Ten years of Plasmodium malariae infections in an International Health Reference Center in Barcelona: temporal trends and insights.
articles.authors: Pérez-Ugarte A, Sulleiro E, Rovira-Plujà J, Bayram M, Zarzuela F, Maturana CR, Serre-Delcor N, Aznar ML, Pou D, Goterris L, Salvador F, Mediavilla A, Bosch P, Martinez-Campreciós J, Treviño B, Martínez-Vallejo P, Silgado A, Molina I, Oliveira-Souto I
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 6,293 malaria screenings (2015-2025) identified 55 P. malariae infections (13.6% of 404 Plasmodium-positive). Most infections (94.5%) were asymptomatic migrants from sub-Saharan Africa with very low parasite densities (≤80 parasites/µL); 13 patients had been in Spain >12 months prior to diagnosis, indicating long-term persistence. One symptomatic traveler experienced clinical relapse after artemisinin-based combination therapy. The high proportion of asymptomatic chronic carriers represents a silent reservoir impeding malaria elimination.

articles.headline: IV artesunate for artemisinin-resistant severe malaria in Uganda
articles.title_original: Intravenous Artesunate in Artemisinin-Resistant Severe Malaria in Uganda.
articles.authors: Maitland K, Okao M, Conrad MD, Etwop T, Akite M, Oguda E, Alaroker F, Aromut D, Muhindo R, Uyoga S, Olupot-Olupot P, Mogaka C, Connon R, Katairo T, Okitwi M, Briggs J, Gibb DM, Day NPJ, White NJ, Williams TN, Dondorp AM, Walker AS, George EC, Rosenthal PJ
articles.details: No abstract provided; unable to extract specific study design, population, or quantitative results.

articles.headline: Structural basis for high-avidity CSP antibodies in malaria vaccine design
articles.title_original: Structural basis for conserved and distinct antigen recognition by a lineage of malaria-protective antibodies.
articles.authors: Jain M, Cannac F, Agrawal S, Lee WH, Loeffler JR, Fernández-Quintero ML, González-Páez GE, Moskovitz R, Ward AB, Wilson IA
articles.details: Crystal and cryo-EM structures revealed monoclonal antibodies targeting P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) NANP repeats achieve high-avidity binding through distinct mechanisms. Fab 7160 formed germline-encoded inter-Fab homotypic interactions (similar to mAb 399), while Fab 7118 adopted an alternative binding mode without inter-Fab contacts. Both bound NANP6 repeats with high affinity (KD 6-10 nM). VH3-49/VK2D-29-derived antibodies show structural versatility where CDR variations modulate antibody conformation and epitope engagement.

articles.headline: ATAD3A is essential mitochondrial protein and potential antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: ATAD3 megadalton complex in Plasmodium falciparum is essential for mitochondrial and cellular viability.
articles.authors: Okoye IC, Lamb IM, Cheung YW, Morrisey JM, Sharma M, Kumar R, Dass S, Shukla A, Rell RS, Mather MW, Llinás M, Chang YW, Vaidya AB
articles.details: Characterization of PF3D7_0707400, an ATAD3A homolog in P. falciparum mitochondria, revealed it is essential for parasite survival and functions in a megadalton complex critical for mitochondrial RNA stability, membrane potential, ultrastructure, and protein import. This is the first characterization of ATAD3A in a unicellular organism. The findings expand knowledge of apicomplexan mitochondrial biology and identify a potential antimalarial drug target.

articles.headline: Burkina Faso malaria control effectiveness 2020-2024: interventions and forecasts
articles.title_original: Evolution of the Performance of the Malaria Control Program in Burkina Faso: Analysis from 2020 to 2024.
articles.authors: Ouedraogo WD, Kabore A, Kompaore S, Somda S, Sie A, Sawadogo H, Meda ZC, Drabo M, Meda N, Hien H
articles.details: Study evaluated National Malaria Control Program effectiveness in Burkina Faso (2020-2024) using time-series data. Coverage rates in 2024: LLINs 81.02% (pregnant women) and 68.2% (children <5), SMC 97.8%, RDTs 98.5%, ACTs 97.96%. Malaria incidence declined from 537.5 to 446.4 cases per 1,000 people annually, but forecasts predict rise to 473.06 cases per 1,000 by 2030 (CI: 242.81-703.28) due to supply chain disruptions.

articles.headline: French malaria diagnosis recommendations: laboratory standardization and methods
articles.title_original: [Malaria diagnosis: French recommendations ANOFEL/LABAC].
articles.authors: Touafek F, Berry A, Giannoli JM, Ait-Ammar N, Coron N, Haim-Boukobza S, Larréché S, Pernot-Marino E, Robert-Gangneux F, Roques C, Thellier M, Houzé S
articles.details: ANOFEL/LABAC recommendations standardize biological diagnosis of malaria in France following NF EN ISO 15189. Diagnosis involves detecting parasites/DNA via sensitive techniques (molecular biology, thick smear) and thin smear for species identification and parasitemia. RDTs detecting plasmodial proteins are supplementary tools, not standalone. Laboratory competence and reagent control are essential for result reliability; CNR or LBMR-qualified labs may consult on severe cases.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine vs primaquine efficacy for P. vivax relapses and CYP2D6 variants
articles.title_original: Differential impact of Tafenoquine and Primaquine on Plasmodium vivax recurrence: the role of CYP2D6 variants.
articles.authors: Godinho VKC, Mwangi VI, Morais MC, Lopes APB, Alves JRdS, Brito MAM, Lacerda MVGd, Trindade Maranhão Costa F, Almeida ACGd, Melo GCd
articles.details: Nested case-control cohort study in Brazil compared tafenoquine (TQ) and primaquine (PQ) efficacy in preventing P. vivax relapses based on CYP2D6 genotype. CYP2D6 polymorphisms did not affect TQ efficacy (P=0.172), but PQ-treated patients had higher recurrence risk at 6 months (P=0.016), with intermediate/poor metabolizers (gIM+gPM) at increased risk vs normal metabolizers (P=0.036).

articles.headline: Submicroscopic malaria in pregnant women: IPTp-SP dosing and birth outcomes
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic malaria infections and inadequate IPTp-SP coverage among pregnant women in rural Burkina Faso: An observational study.
articles.authors: Tibiri YNG, Soulama I, Zongo D, Ouedraogo O, Sombie S, Millogo ON, Platon L, Nikiema S, Diarra WFS, Yanogo JN, Zoure OADZ, Ily RP, Kabore FCA, Sawadogo S, Saliou YEKI, Wade A, Chala B, Thiebaut L, Caspar E, Strubel PÉ, Muller A, Piola P, Zongo C, Menard D
articles.details: Observational study (June 2023-June 2024) in Burkina Faso assessed malaria in 515 pregnant women at first ANC visit. PCR detected infection in 30.1% vs 8.2% by microscopy and 8.7% by RDT, showing routine diagnostics missed approximately two-thirds of infections. Each additional IPTp-SP dose lowered infection prevalence (aPR 0.89, 95% CI 0.80-0.99); only 18.9% achieved ≥5 doses. PCR-confirmed infection associated with increased low birthweight risk (aPR 1.52, 95% CI 1.01-2.28).

articles.headline: Francesco Torti's cinchona bark treatment: early malaria therapy history
articles.title_original: Francesco Torti (1658-1741) and his description on the therapeutic use of quinine: the first effective method of treating malaria based on an extract of the bark of the Peruvian cinchona tree.
articles.authors: Artico M, Costi R, Gazzaniga V, Iorio S, Pastore FS, Cofone L
articles.details: Historical account of Francesco Torti (1658-1741) who pioneered use of cinchona bark (quinine source) to treat malaria through meticulous patient observation. His 1712 work detailed various intermittent fever forms and their treatment responses, contributing to cinchona's adoption throughout Europe. His approach exemplified the shift toward patient-centered, experience-based medicine.

articles.headline: Placental malaria VEGF dysregulation: mechanisms and angiogenic pathways
articles.title_original: Placental malaria and VEGF modulation by P. falciparum: Bridging the immunopathological gap using evidence from human cerebral and severe, non-cerebral malaria studies.
articles.authors: Jain V, Shanmugam R, Singh PP, Singh VP
articles.details: Review examines P. falciparum-induced VEGF modulation in placental malaria, drawing from cerebral and severe non-cerebral malaria studies. P. falciparum internalize host VEGF and express VEGFR2 on parasitized red blood cells, causing dysregulated angiogenic factors (VEGF, sVEGFR1, Angiopoietins, sEndoglin, PLGF) and altered cytokine signaling. Disrupted VEGF signaling may lead to placental insufficiency, fetal growth restriction, and preeclampsia; quantitative plasma studies and immunohistochemistry in placental malaria from endemic areas are needed.

articles.headline: Dynamic species distribution models for malaria vectors in Brazil
articles.title_original: Predicting short-life-cycle species suitability via dynamic species distribution models: Applications in dengue and malaria epidemiology in Brazil.
articles.authors: Oliveira D, da Silva FF, Teixeira IBDF, Trindade-Vilanova DM, Rodrigues MS, Moura TA, Martinez PA
articles.details: Study developed Dynamic Species Distribution Models (DSDMs) integrating monthly occurrence records (2016-2022) and climatic variables for malaria vector An. darlingi using MaxEnt algorithm. An. darlingi model showed strong performance (AUC=0.9, TSS=0.8) with vapor pressure as key predictor; suitability concentrated in Northern region/Amazon. Estimated IRR demonstrated significant associations between climatic suitability and malaria incidence (p<0.01), identifying seasonal vulnerability windows for targeted surveillance.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria with multiorgan failure: autopsy case report
articles.title_original: Malignant malaria: autopsy findings and morphological features.
articles.authors: Colombo D, Del Nonno F, D'Abramo A, Corpolongo A, Ascoli Bartoli T, De Marco P, Garotto G, Bocci MG, Nicastri E
articles.details: Case report of 54-year-old woman from Central Africa presenting with fever, headache, and rapid progression to multiorgan failure, resulting in death on day 4. Autopsy confirmed P. falciparum cerebral malaria with microvascular sequestration and perivascular hemorrhages. Patient had untreated multiple myeloma, highlighting importance of considering P. falciparum in differential diagnosis of febrile travelers from endemic regions.

articles.headline: ITN and IPTp coverage among pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Use of insecticide-treated nets (ITN) and intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) for malaria prevention and its associated factors among pregnant women in Sub-Sahara Africa.
articles.authors: Alemu EA, Tesfa MA, Nigatu SG, Birhan TY, Yitageasu G, Demoze L, Tadesse AA
articles.details: Analysis of Malaria Indicator Survey/DHS data from 18 sub-Saharan African countries (2018-2024) with 63,561 women. Combined ITN and IPTp (≥3 doses) use was 28.9% (95% CI 28.4-29.3%), while 17.6% used neither. Multilevel regression identified age, education, household wealth, media exposure, and geographic residence as significant predictors of preventive measure utilization. Study emphasizes need for community and individual-level strategies to increase coverage toward 90% malaria reduction goal by 2030.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence and risk factors in Northwest Ethiopia: cross-sectional study
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria and associated factors among febrile patients visiting Gissa Health Center, Awi Zone, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Amare B, Hailegebriel T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study (Feb-April 2024) of 411 febrile patients in Ethiopia found overall malaria prevalence of 34.1%, with P. falciparum 20.9%, P. vivax 9.7%, and mixed infections 2.4%. Risk factors included: age <5 years (AOR=2.77, 95% CI 1.09-7.00), age 6-15 years (AOR=3.85, 95% CI 1.49-9.92), illiteracy (AOR=2.83, 95% CI 1.13-7.08), lack of ITN use (AOR=2.08, 95% CI 1.04-4.17), and absence of IRS (AOR=1.54, 95% CI 1.29-3.24).

articles.headline: Malaria elimination in Cambodia: progress, challenges, and strategic priorities
articles.title_original: Advancing malaria elimination in Cambodia: a scoping review of progress, challenges, and strategic priorities.
articles.authors: Srun M, Langkulsen U
articles.details: Scoping review (2014-2024, 25 articles) of malaria elimination efforts in Cambodia. Review found that varied interventions reduced transmission and case fatalities, but barriers remain including gaps in services/programs, inadequate infrastructure, low coverage, and poor access—particularly in high-risk areas. Climate change and increased temperatures are prioritized drivers. Author recommends enhanced malaria surveillance, case management, and strategies addressing inequities in access to malaria care.

articles.headline: Kenya malaria RDT accuracy: pfhrp2/3 deletions and artemisinin resistance surveillance
articles.title_original: Absence of false negative RDT results due to pfhrp2 deletion among symptomatic malaria infections in Kenya: findings from the 2022 Kenya national longitudinal surveillance of pfhrp2/3 deletions.
articles.authors: Akala HM, Kandie R, Githuka GN, Kiplagat R, Ahmeddin O, Keitany K, Kiarie J, Wamari A, Machini B, Oyugi E, Mahugu S, Otieno JD, Egbo TE, Juma DW, Sang LP, Haynes RC, Osoti V, Vesely BA, Smith D, Travis J, Cunningham J, Cheng Q, Oyier I, Ogutu B
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 5,394 symptomatic malaria patients across 10 Kenyan counties compared PfHRP2-based and PfLDH-based RDTs with sequencing of discordant samples. HRP2- and PfLDH-RDTs showed similar positivity (44% vs 45%) with 1.26% discordance; none of discordant samples carried pfhrp2 deletions, though 12.4% of 598 sequenced samples had single pfhrp3 deletions and 0% had double-deletions. pfk13 mutation prevalence was 0.7%, including A675V (partial artemisinin resistance marker) in two counties.

articles.headline: Pf41 antibodies detect malaria exposure in low-transmission Indonesia
articles.title_original: Pf41 serological profiling reveals potential malaria exposure in low transmission setting in the Menoreh Hills, Central Java, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Ilyas M, Yuwanita MR, Natanegara S, Daud MM, Rahayu AA, Hitipeuw D, Wusono AD, Nuranindita R, Fitriana F, Syahada JH, Jeon BY, Jun H, Martini M, Nuridzin DZ, Lu F, Han JH, Muh F
articles.details: Cross-sectional seroepidemiological study (January–June 2025) in four Central Java villages with 120 endemic individuals and 24 controls. Tested for anti-Pf41 IgG antibodies via protein microarray. Pf41 seropositivity 71% overall (85/120), with village variation: Kembaran 93%, Sedayu 83%, Wadas 60%, Kemejing 47%. Mean standardized MFI significantly higher in all exposed groups vs controls (p < 0.001). Nighttime livestock feeding and repellent use associated with higher antibody levels. Authors conclude Pf41 serology complements surveillance in low-transmission settings by detecting past exposure.

articles.headline: Mechanical ventilation and hypertonic saline fail in severe cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Adjunctive Mechanical Ventilation or Intravenous Hypertonic Saline in Malawian Children with Cerebral Malaria and Highly Increased Brain Volume: A Randomized, Phase 3 Clinical Trial.
articles.authors: O'Brien NF, Postels DG, Chimalizeni Y, Fonseca Y, Wooldridge G, Parker E, Galeano X, Urbina E, Lintner-Rivera M, Wynkoop HJ, Chetcuti K, Goyal MS, Biondo-Savin E, Juvvadi S, Nicholas J, Vidal L, Raees M, Doctor A, Remy KE, Bhutta A, Holloway A, Moxon CA, Seydel KB, Liomba AM, Chilingulo C, Ibrahim K, Gardiner J, Taylor TE
articles.details: Randomized phase 3 trial in Malawian children aged 6 months–12 years with cerebral malaria (Blantyre Coma Score ≤2, P. falciparum parasitemia) and severe brain swelling (brain volume score 6–8), 2018–2023. Randomized to mechanical ventilation (n=23), 3% hypertonic saline (n=17), or usual care (n=17). Neither intervention reduced 7-day mortality compared to usual care (ventilation risk difference –0.075, 95% CI –0.27 to 0.17, p=0.59; HTS 0.002, 95% CI –0.22 to 0.28, p=1.0). HTS increased cerebral blood flow but was harmful in children with baseline cerebral hyperemia (relative risk 3.0 for worse outcomes, 95% CI 1.02–10.9, p=0.04). Authors conclude phenotype-guided rather than non-selective neuroprotective strategies are needed.

articles.headline: Precipitation and illiteracy predict malaria incidence in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Climatic and social health determinants of malaria incidence in Tete, Mozambique: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling of routine data (2016-2022).
articles.authors: Mussafo AJA, da Cruz RF, de Arruda Silveira LV, Govone JS
articles.details: Spatio-temporal Bayesian hierarchical modeling (INLA approach) of malaria cases from Tete Province, Mozambique 2016–2022, incorporating climate and socioeconomic data. Precipitation and illiteracy rate were significant predictors, associated with estimated increases of 6.7% and 1.6% in malaria incidence per unit, respectively. Spatially, Macanga, Chiuta, and Moatize districts showed higher risk; Tete City lower. Incidence rose shortly after rainy season onset and declined during winter. Authors conclude climate and social factors decisively shape transmission dynamics.

articles.headline: Humidity shifts thermal performance and growth in malaria vector
articles.title_original: Beyond Temperature: Relative Humidity Systematically Shifts Juvenile Thermal Performance and Projected Population Growth in a Malaria Vector.
articles.authors: Huxley PJ, Brown JJ, St Laurent B, Johnson B, Cheung OY, Asamoah A, Hollingsworth BD, Bump ER, Wimberly MC, Pascual M, Johnson LR, Murdock CC
articles.details: Laboratory study of relative humidity effects on juvenile life history traits in Anopheles stephensi mosquitoes. Humidity-dependent trait responses integrated into population growth rate (rm) analytic model. Relative humidity alters how temperature affects projected vector population growth. Temperature-humidity interactions alone can alter inference about environmental suitability when temperature-only models are used. Authors highlight importance of incorporating humidity alongside temperature in assessing ectotherm responses to climatic variation.

articles.headline: Settlement patterns shaped malaria mortality in 19th-century Ontario
articles.title_original: Ecology of Malaria Mortality: A Spatiotemporal Mapping Approach.
articles.authors: Cooke A, Brickley MB
articles.details: GIS-based spatiotemporal analysis of probable malaria-attributed deaths in Ontario 1869–1900 (n=2,683) geocoded from death certificates. Spatial clustering assessed via Average Nearest Neighbor analysis and kernel density estimation. Mortality significantly clustered but progressively less concentrated over time. Locations shifted farther from wetlands as drainage intensified. Settlement proximity was the strongest significant predictor of mortality density in regression models; wetland distance not independently associated. Authors conclude malaria risk was embedded in settlement practices and landscape transformation rather than static environmental features.

articles.headline: Photonic crystal biosensor shows high sensitivity for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Highly Sensitive 1D Photonic Crystal Biosensor for Early Malaria Detection via Refractive Index.
articles.authors: Kaspar K, Karuppiah K, Karuppiah P, Ramanujam NR
articles.details: Theoretical study of one-dimensional photonic crystal biosensor using Transfer Matrix Method for rapid malaria diagnosis via refractive index. Optimized design Air/(PbS/SiO2)4/defect/(PbS/SiO2)4/substrate achieved maximum sensitivity of 977.74 nm/RIU at defect thickness 1.2da (quality factor 93.8 × 10³) or 1163.22 nm/RIU at 1.4da (quality factor 82.57 × 10³). Detection limit ~10⁻⁶ RIU with high figure of merit. Operates over refractive index range 1.371–1.402 RIU corresponding to different infection stages. Authors conclude strong potential for cost-effective rapid diagnosis.

articles.headline: Low-density P. vivax infections block malaria elimination in Myanmar
articles.title_original: Sub-RDT Plasmodium vivax infections and G6PD deficiency in Kayin State, Myanmar.
articles.authors: Thu AM, Phyo AP, Pateekham C, Blu SH, Thwin MM, Kajeechiwa L, Watthanaworawit W, Okkararungrot Y, Bancone G, Gornsawun G, Moo PK, Klain K, Aung PL, Nguitragool W, Pan-Ngum W, Nosten F
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey in Kayin State villages, Myanmar 2020–2021 screening 5,509 individuals by mRDT, rRT-PCR, and ELISA. P. vivax rRT-PCR positivity 14.3% (317/2,219), P. falciparum 8.3% (185/2,219). mRDT detection extremely low (0.5%, with 26 P. vivax and 1 P. falciparum detected). Sub-RDT P. vivax prevalence (rRT-PCR+ mRDT–) 14.1% (311/2,211); median village prevalence ranged 4.0–23.6% (Hpapun 6× higher than Myawaddy). G6PD deficiency 21.7% in males, 10.6% in females (p<0.001). No association between G6PD status and sub-RDT infections. Authors conclude sub-RDT reservoirs and high G6PD deficiency are major barriers to elimination.

articles.headline: Poverty drives geographic patterns of child malaria in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Geographic Patterns and Drivers of Paediatric Malaria in Nigeria: Walking in the shadow of Poverty.
articles.authors: Osayomi T, Adedun OA
articles.details: Spatial analysis of paediatric malaria in Nigeria using GIS and Global Moran's I and Getis-Ord Gi* to identify clustering and hotspots. Stepwise linear regression identified poverty as the dominant predictor of paediatric malaria (R²=0.43–0.5). Hotspots varied across years while coldspots consistently found in southwest. Authors conclude free antimalarial treatment for children combined with poverty reduction needed to reduce malaria burden.

articles.headline: Machine learning accurately predicts malaria prevalence in rural Nigeria
articles.title_original: Application of machine learning models in predicting malaria prevalence in Nigeria: an analysis of the 2015-2020 demographic and health surveys.
articles.authors: Ayoka TO, Nnadi CO
articles.details: Predictive modeling using machine learning on 2020 Nigerian Demographic and Health Surveys data from rural communities. Random forest regressor (RFR), multiple linear regression, and ridge models trained on clinical information. Model performance assessed via cross-validation and holdout testing. Coefficient determination (R²) on test set: RFR 0.9937, multiple linear regression 0.9916, ridge 0.9924. RFR learning curve showed consistent performance improvement with increased data volume. Authors conclude shift from reactive diagnostics to proactive risk prediction enables better resource allocation and intervention timing.

articles.headline: Imported malaria drives autochthonous transmission in Iran elimination phase
articles.title_original: Malaria dynamics in Iran: insights from autochthonous and asymptomatic.
articles.authors: Sadeghi F, Nodez SMM, Khosravani M
articles.details: Analysis of CDC surveillance data from Hormozgan province, Iran April 2018–May 2023, including 289 malaria cases (87.5% male, 12.5% female; majority aged 11–29 years). P. vivax 78.9% of cases. Nationals: Afghans (imported) 56.7%, Pakistanis (imported) 30.8%, Iranians (autochthonous) 12.5%. Asymptomatic cases 45.25%, mostly treated. Cases peaked in 2023 in Jask County. Authors conclude imported malaria significantly maintains transmission among native populations; systematic solutions through formal legal processes needed to manage immigration-related malaria.

articles.headline: Pfs230 gene is highly conserved across East Africa as vaccine target
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 gene in four East African countries supports its potential as a malaria transmission‑blocking vaccine candidate.
articles.authors: Kisambale AJ, Moshi R, Pereus D, Mbwambo RB, Mandai SS, Chacha GA, Lyimo B, Madebe RA, Budodo R, Bakari C, Semboja HJ, Petro DA, Challe DP, Aaron S, Mbwambo D, Lusasi A, Kajange S, Lazaro S, Mandara CI, Seth MD, Kulohoma BW, Juma G, Ishengoma DS
articles.details: Genomic analysis using MalariaGEN Pf7 database of 1,312 P. falciparum sequences (from 1,471 screened) from Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda. Assessed genetic diversity and evolutionary dynamics of Pfs230 transmission-blocking vaccine candidate. Nucleotide diversity low across countries (π=5.9 × 10⁻⁴). Haplotype diversity 0.999 with 496 haplotypes among 718 monoclonal sequences; 8.1% haplotypes shared between populations. Non-synonymous to synonymous substitution ratio 0.43. Tajima's D negative (Kenya –2.131 p<0.01, Tanzania –2.056 p<0.05; Ethiopia and Uganda not significant). No population differentiation observed. Authors conclude low genetic diversity supports Pfs230 as promising transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: District health managers review malaria reports but need better analysis
articles.title_original: Attitude and practices of district-level HMIS managers towards malaria routine reporting in Uganda: a 2024 cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Kigozi SP, Okiring J, Kigozi LS, Emuron P, Talimusa EA, Kigozi RN, Alegana V, Sartorius B, Giorgi E, Drakeley C, Yeka A
articles.details: Cross-sectional study across 15 malaria endemicity regions in Uganda January–June 2024 interviewing HMIS managers (n=34 from 30 districts/cities). Participants: biostatisticians 70.6%, HMIS focal persons 23.5%. All reviewed received reports; 75.8% documented errors but only 31.6% could show documented queries. By survey date, 81.6% of expected reports received; 25.4% only after active follow-up. 93.9% reports submitted to DHIS-2 by 15th of month received queries (mean 4.2 per facility). Support supervision gaps: 70% preferred quarterly visits; 39.4% had received 1–2 visits; 51.5% provided supervision. Authors conclude human resources capable but gaps remain in standard operating procedures, supervision frequency, and analytical skills.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genome variants associate with sickle haemoglobin trait
articles.title_original: Associations between the Plasmodium falciparum genome and sickle haemoglobin identified in mild malaria cases from Ghana.
articles.authors: Hamilton WL, Forster AJ, Aniweh Y, Asoala V, Drury E, Rockett KA, Awandare GA, Kwiatkowski DP, Band G, Amenga-Etego LN
articles.details: Genome-wide association study of 1,368 Ghanaians with mild P. falciparum malaria examined human β-globin genotypes. Replicated known associations between Pfsa1/Pfsa3 parasite loci and HbS; identified new candidate Pfsa4 locus in FIKK4.2 that replicated in Mali. These variants vary widely in Africa, absent/low in Asia, highly correlated across populations.

articles.headline: Raman spectroscopy machine learning detects malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Population-Level Raman Biochemical Staging of Malaria in Human Red Blood Cells Using Interpretable Machine Learning.
articles.authors: Wu L, Gupta A, Tripathi AK, Barman I
articles.details: Study using Raman spectroscopy coupled with interpretable machine learning (SHAP analysis) to identify biochemical signatures of P. falciparum in infected red blood cells. Employed confocal and spatially offset Raman spectroscopy on synchronized parasite cultures. Demonstrated subsurface detection beneath tissue-mimetic conditions, providing foundation for noninvasive, stage-aware optical diagnostics targeting ring stages and gametocytes.

articles.headline: Malaria shifts neutrophil subsets, linked to Burkitt lymphoma
articles.title_original: The impact of malaria-induced neutrophil subset shift and a link to Burkitt lymphoma.
articles.authors: Okoth SA, Tonui RK, Maina TK, Agwati E, Oduor CI, Racenet Z, M'Bana V, Njuguna FM, Keitany KK, Chepsiror D, Ayieko C, Moormann AM, Kinyua AW, Forconi CS
articles.details: Prospective study using flow cytometry in Kenyan children with varying P. falciparum exposure and children with Burkitt lymphoma. Malaria-exposed children showed increased aged neutrophil subsets with reduced mature active subsets. Strong positive correlation (rs=0.7, p<0.0001) in immature neutrophils between malaria-exposed healthy and Burkitt lymphoma children, suggesting malaria creates permissive immune environment for tumorigenesis.

articles.headline: ICOS gene variants don't affect P. vivax antibody response
articles.title_original: No Evidence for Association Between ICOS (a T-Cell Co-Stimulatory Molecule) Gene Polymorphisms and Anti-MSP-1(19) IgG Response in Venezuelan Endemic Plasmodium vivax Malaria.
articles.authors: Sevilla RD, Regalado-Gutiérrez OA, Wide A, Puche-Quiñonez R, Patarroyo MA, Noya-González O, Fernández-Mestre M
articles.details: Study of 91 Venezuelans with uncomplicated P. vivax infection examined association between ICOS gene polymorphisms (rs4404254, rs4675379, rs10183087) and anti-PvMSP19 IgG by ELISA. PvMSP19-specific IgG detected in 70.3% of participants. ICOS genotypes/haplotypes showed no significant associations with antibody levels; however, antibody responses strongly correlated with prior malarial episodes (r=0.803, p<0.0001).

articles.headline: Anopheles darlingi shows widespread insecticide resistance evolution
articles.title_original: Reassessing malaria-transmitting mosquito evolution with a neotropical lens.
articles.authors: Carter TE, Gunarathna I
articles.details: Whole-genome analysis of Anopheles darlingi, major Neotropical malaria vector, revealed geographically structured populations with no evidence of sympatric species. Study identified widespread insecticide resistance through convergent evolution, indicating strong selective pressure from insecticide use across the region.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine efficacy 96% in Laos; lumefantrine resistance emerging
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine for the treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Laos: a therapeutic efficacy study coupled with genomic and in vitro phenotypic analyses.
articles.authors: Feufack-Donfack LB, Khim N, Tat B, Banouvong V, Khamlome B, Chindavongsal K, Chanthavisouk C, Reyburn R, Shortus M, Kloeung N, Ke S, Khean C, Seng C, Kul C, Eam R, Orban A, Salvador J, Vezain M, Dorgebray PE, Coppée R, Ariey F, Witkowski B, Ringwald P, Popovici J
articles.details: Therapeutic efficacy study of artemether-lumefantrine in 195 P. falciparum-infected patients (ages 1–60) across southern Laos (Aug 2019–June 2023). PCR-adjusted adequate clinical and parasitological response at day 28: 96% (95% CI 92–98); 2% treatment failure, 2% reinfection. One recrudescent isolate showed markedly reduced lumefantrine susceptibility (IC50 59.9 nM, 2.5× median) with high artemisinin resistance (ring-stage survival 35.8%), carrying pfkelch13 R539T. Prevalence: pfkelch13 R539T 5%, C580Y 23%, both associated with day-3 parasitaemia (p=0.044).

articles.headline: P. vivax antigen diversity doesn't impair serology-based exposure detection
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of parasite antigen genetic diversity on performance of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria: a multicentre observational diagnostic accuracy study.
articles.authors: Bareng APN, Wu KW, Takashima E, Argyropoulos DC, Smith L, Naung MT, Mazhari R, Schoffer K, Kiernan-Walker N, Abraham A, Lamont M, Mehra S, Lim PS, Sattabongkot J, Monteiro W, Lacerda M, Healer J, Chitnis CE, Tham WH, Tsuboi T, Mueller I, Barry AE, Longley RJ
articles.details: Multicentre diagnostic accuracy study of 1,697 participants (Brazil n=923, Thailand n=774) assessing effect of parasite antigen genetic diversity on P. vivax serological exposure markers. Extensive global genetic diversity identified in MSP5 (π=14.8 × 10-3) and DBPII (π=7.7 × 10-3), with sequence identity to reference Sal-1 ranging 0.4%–99%. Despite high diversity, most antigens (5 of 8) showed no effect on accuracy of recent-exposure identification; effects on remaining antigens overcome by multi-antigen classification models.

articles.headline: Delayed diagnosis common in US pediatric malaria; 32% severe
articles.title_original: Malaria in Children at 9 US Hospitals: 2016-2023.
articles.authors: Sundararaman SA, Roper B, Davis AKF, Hanze Villavicencio KL, Mayhew JA, Wang ML, Tang NL, Soma VL, Wang Z, Feeney ME, Trehan I, Weatherhead JE, John CC, Gerber JS, Odom John AR
articles.details: Retrospective study of 171 children treated for malaria at 9 US hospitals (2016–2023). Most cases travel-acquired from West Africa. Fever in 90%, ≥66% with abdominal symptoms. 32% diagnosed with severe malaria; zero deaths. Delayed diagnoses were common (26% of cases), occurred at similar rates across hospitals, and were significant risk factor for severe disease, prolonged hospitalization, increased morbidity.

articles.headline: Nine imported malaria cases in malaria-elimination Iran
articles.title_original: Challenges and strategies in malaria control: A case series from East Azerbaijan Province, 2023-2024.
articles.authors: Golshaniniya P, Porkar Khatibi M, Pourhassan A, Mirzaaghazadeh E, Babapour A, Rezaei H, Koosha A, Khayatzadeh S, Deihim M, Bagheri R, Abbasi M
articles.details: Retrospective case series of 9 malaria cases in malaria-free Iran (East Azerbaijan Province, 2023–2024): 3 P. falciparum, 6 P. vivax. All had travel to endemic areas (southern Iran, Pakistan, Kenya, Sierra Leone), confirmed by rapid diagnostic tests and microscopy. Observations underscore surveillance risk in elimination settings: imported cases during peak vector season could establish local transmission in previously endemic region with suitable breeding habitats.

articles.headline: Healthcare gaps and LLIN underuse hinder India malaria control
articles.title_original: Comprehensive Analysis of Health Service Utilization for Febrile Illness and Malaria in Malaria-Endemic States in India: LLINs as a Pillar of Control Strategies.
articles.authors: Chalageri VH, Marinaik SB, Singhal R, Gupta SK, Nathan SN, Eapen A, Yadav CP, Singh PK, Tripathi PK, Malla WA, Pradhan SC, Sinha DP, Singh SP, Singh K, Bhuyan PJ, Rahi M, Jain T, Sharma R, Anvikar AR
articles.details: Subnational cross-sectional household survey of 37,849 households (177,644 individuals) in 10 Indian malaria-endemic states assessing health service and long-lasting insecticidal net (LLIN) utilization. 57.4% of febrile individuals accessed government healthcare within 24–48 h; 73.6% households used LLINs regularly. Lack of education and tribal status were risk factors for malaria. Identified gap between LLIN knowledge (89.2% adequate) and regular use (73.6%), suggesting targeted interventions needed for India's 2030 elimination goal.

articles.headline: P. vivax MIF structure reveals malaria vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor.
articles.authors: Nair A, Lin M, Srivastava A, Liu L, Cooper A, Battaile K, Harmon E, Myler PJ, Staker BL, Lovell S, Chakafana G, Asojo OA
articles.journal: Acta Crystallogr F Struct Biol Commun
articles.details: Crystal structure of P. vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor (PvMIF) determined at 1.8 Å resolution (PDB 9b0m). PvMIF regulates adaptive and innate immunity with plasma concentrations correlating to P. vivax malaria severity. PvMIF binds host CD74, suppressing host MIF-CD74 signaling; recognized as malaria vaccine candidate. Structure reveals biological unit is trimeric MIF.

articles.headline: Genomic approaches improve insecticide-resistance surveillance in vectors
articles.title_original: Polygenic scores for genomic surveillance of insecticide resistance in malaria control.
articles.authors: Donnelly MJ, Kamau A, McDermott D, Nagi SC, Summers S
articles.details: Opinion article proposing genomic surveillance, including polygenic scores, to accurately characterize insecticide resistance in malaria vectors before control failure occurs. Current phenotyping methods lack sensitivity to detect resistance before long-lasting insecticidal nets and indoor residual spraying efficacy declines. Genomic approaches could provide evidence-based estimates of resistance impact on vector control and guide targeted interventions.

articles.headline: Computational stabilization enables drug discovery against malaria proteins
articles.title_original: Stabilizing Plasmodium falciparum proteins for small molecule drug discovery.
articles.authors: Amann M, Sträßer T, Einsle O, Günther S
articles.details: This study used ProteinMPNN-guided sequence design to create stabilized surrogate constructs of three P. falciparum drug targets (PfBDP1-BRD, PfBDP4-BRD, PfK13-KREP) that retained wild-type-like binding thermodynamics. The stabilized variants showed markedly increased thermal stability, and enabled structural validation and identification of new inhibitors, including a 1.25 Å co-crystal structure for PfBDP4-BRD with a newly identified inhibitor.

articles.headline: P. vivax relapses show lower infectivity to mosquitoes in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Parasite and gametocyte dynamics and infectivity to mosquitoes during recurrent Plasmodium vivax infections: A longitudinal study from Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Tadesse FG, Chali W, Ejigu LA, Abdo M, Kassa FA, Bulto MG, Bezabih MK, Alemayehu L, Hundera NL, Bayu SL, Nibret D, Ashine T, Assefa M, Solomon Z, Demisse M, Gashaw A, Shimelash A, Tebeje SK, Hailemeskel E, Gadisa E, Rosado J, Obadia T, Taylor AR, White M, Massebo F, Ramjith J, Drakeley C, Bousema T
articles.details: A longitudinal study in Ethiopia followed 176 P. vivax patients for one year after chloroquine plus primaquine treatment. 54.6% (96/176) experienced recurrent parasitemia, and 45.7% (42/92) of recurrences were likely relapses. Mosquito infectivity was common (70.2% of feedings produced infection) and correlated with parasite density (ρ=0.29; p<0.0001) and gametocyte density (ρ=0.32; p<0.0001), but relapsing infections showed significantly lower infectivity (OR 0.48, 95% CI 0.26–0.87, p=0.016) compared to new infections.

articles.headline: P. vivax pvmdr1 mutations fixed in North Indian isolates
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of chloroquine resistance in Plasmodium vivax isolates from North India: Analysis of single nucleotide polymorphisms in pvmdr1 gene.
articles.authors: Nityadarshini N, Bir R, Biswas J, Jadon RS, Mirdha BR, Verma N
articles.details: Among 7,530 febrile patients screened, 75 (0.99%) had P. vivax mono-infection; 36 isolates were sequenced for pvmdr1. M908L and F1076L mutations were present in 100% of isolates, while T898A was detected in 5.6%. Phylogenetic analysis showed Indian isolates clustered with Madagascar and Afghanistan haplotypes. Despite high prevalence of M908L and F1076L mutations, their role in clinical chloroquine resistance remains uncertain without Y976F.

articles.headline: BISSAU mosquito form shows genetic distinctiveness in Gambian vector populations
articles.title_original: Genomic insights into the population structure of malaria vectors of the Anopheles gambiae complex in The Gambia reveals distinctiveness and spatio-temporal stability of BISSAU molecular form.
articles.authors: De Marco CM, Caputo B, Pichler V, Hamid-Adiamoh M, Gibba B, Clarkson C, Assogba BS, D'Alessandro U, Amambua-Ngwa A, Della Torre A
articles.details: Population genomic analysis of >200,000 SNPs on chromosome 3 from 2,283 Anopheles specimens across The Gambia identified BISSAU molecular form as a genetically discrete, reproductively isolated lineage within the An. gambiae complex. Results from PCA, ADMIXTURE, and TreeMix analyses showed BISSAU had no admixture with sympatric An. gambiae or An. coluzzii. BISSAU was more prevalent on the north bank of Gambia river, 10–120 km from the seacoast, in breeding sites with brackish/fresh water.

articles.headline: Microsporidia MB infection preserves human-host seeking in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Behaviour of the malaria vector Anopheles arabiensis infected with the endosymbiont Microsporidia MB: host seeking and cuticular hydrocarbons associated with mating.
articles.authors: Chepkemoi L, Cheseto X, Nyanjom SG, Herren JK, Tchouassi DP
articles.details: Behavioral assays of Anopheles arabiensis with and without Microsporidia MB infection showed the symbiont imposed no cost on host-seeking behavior, with infected mosquitoes showing no preferential attraction to human odors over cattle odors and no change in selective feeding on humans vs other vertebrates. Chemical analysis revealed differences in cuticular hydrocarbon (CHC) profiles between infected and non-infected male mosquitoes, suggesting the symbiont may affect mating behavior via CHC changes while preserving human-host seeking.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis: RTS,S and R21 vaccines show age-dependent protection
articles.title_original: Efficacy and immunogenicity of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines: - Systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Asjad H, Sadiq MW, Siddiqui S, Sherali S, Yousafzai MT, Taseen S, Qamar FN
articles.details: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 8 eligible studies compared efficacy and immunogenicity of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M vaccines. RTS,S/AS01 provided 23.0% malaria risk reduction in the 6–12-week age group (RR 0.77) and RR 0.61 in 5–17-month-olds. Both vaccines showed strong antibody responses; efficacy declined over time, highlighting the importance of booster doses. R21 demonstrated similarly high efficacy (event rate 0.22) but with more frequent adverse events than RTS,S/AS01.

articles.headline: Innate immune memory may explain recurrent Plasmodium infection dynamics
articles.title_original: The case for exploring innate memory in Plasmodium infection.
articles.authors: Ho NN, Tongogara T, Tran TM, Kaushansky A, Glennon EK
articles.details: This review examines the role of innate immune memory—epigenetic reprogramming that regulates immune response to sequential infections independently of adaptive immunity—in controlling Plasmodium infection and pathogenesis during recurrent infections in endemic areas. The author suggests innate memory in non-immune cells may have significant consequences for the complex Plasmodium lifecycle, potentially explaining infection dynamics in highly endemic areas where individuals can receive multiple infectious bites daily.

articles.headline: DNA markers track metabolic insecticide resistance in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Metabolic insecticide resistance DNA markers in malaria vectors.
articles.authors: Tchouakui M, Djoko Tagne CS, Tekoh TA, Tatchou-Nebangwa NMT, Kouamo MM, Wondji CS
articles.details: This review synthesizes knowledge on DNA markers in detoxification genes (cytochrome P450s, glutathione S-transferases) associated with metabolic resistance in major malaria vectors, including Anopheles gambiae (CYP6P4, CYP6P3, GSTe2) and Anopheles funestus (CYP6P9a/b, CYP6P4a/b, CYP9K1, GSTe2, ASL). Recent multiomics-based discovery has enabled development of DNA-based diagnostic methods for operational genomic surveillance. These markers support resistance management by assessing spatiotemporal resistance evolution, cross-resistance patterns, fitness costs, and transmission impact.

articles.headline: Recent technologies revolutionize study of Plasmodium mosquito-stage development
articles.title_original: Demystifying Plasmodium development within the Anopheles mosquito.
articles.authors: Shaw WR, Catteruccia F
articles.details: This review summarizes how recent technological advances in microscopy, single-cell RNA sequencing, and genetic screening have revolutionized the study of Plasmodium sporogony (development in Anopheles mosquitoes). The author notes that understanding these essential parasite stages could inform development of novel transmission control tools. The review addresses inherent challenges in in vivo mosquito-stage studies, including substantial bottlenecks in parasite numbers and increases in parasite ploidy.

articles.headline: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control.
articles.authors: Bradley J, Hayes R, Smith PG, Kleinschmidt I
articles.details: This letter expresses concerns about a study on spatial emanators for malaria vector control. No additional details provided in title.

articles.headline: Authors respond to concerns about spatial emanator study
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control - Authors' reply.
articles.authors: Allan RJ, Cross CL, Messenger LA
articles.details: This is an authors' reply to previous concerns about a spatial emanator study for malaria vector control. No additional details provided.

articles.headline: Pan-Plasmodium RT-qPCR assay for blood donor screening
articles.title_original: Development and validation of a pan-Plasmodium molecular assay as a confirmatory method for blood donor screening.
articles.authors: Milani P, Wen L, Montalvo L, Coco SB, Di Germanio C, Bres V, Yadav M, Livezey K, Levi JE, Linnen J, Busch MP
articles.details: This study developed a reverse transcription quantitative PCR (RT-qPCR) assay targeting a conserved 18S rRNA region common to all five human-infecting Plasmodium species for supplemental confirmatory testing in blood donor screening. The assay demonstrated a 95% limit of detection of 5.3 infected RBCs/mL (95% CI: 3.2–8.6), amplification efficiency of 97.2% (R² = 0.99), and clinical sensitivity of 100% across all Plasmodium species with 100% clinical specificity (95% CI: 99–100%) and no cross-reactivity with Babesia microti. Amplicon sequencing enabled accurate species-level identification.

articles.headline: Mitochondrial metabolic shift in vivax-infected monocytes
articles.title_original: The mitochondrial metabolic shift in inflammatory monocytes during Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Antonelli LR, Gazzinelli RT
articles.details: Review of immunometabolic research in P. vivax infection showing metabolic-mitochondrial shifts in highly activated monocytes. These cells display enhanced phagocytic and microbicidal activity but produce high levels of proinflammatory cytokines and reactive oxygen species that may contribute to disease signs during acute P. vivax malaria episodes.

articles.headline: Malaria and Chagas disease epidemiology in non-endemic Brazilian regions
articles.title_original: Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024).
articles.authors: Tudella GCN, Sasazaki FY, Kelnhar I, de Conto T, de Oliveira JVB, Franco FW, Oliveira DH, Cocco OD, Boufleuer IS, Martins FC, Soares TP, da Silva ACM, Wendt JDR
articles.details: Ecological analysis of 9,755 malaria and 391 acute Chagas disease cases (2007-2024) in non-endemic areas of Brazil using SINAN surveillance data. Malaria predominated in males (78%) and adults aged 20-39 years (47.8%), mostly non-autochthonous (89.7%); temporal trend showed significant decrease (β = -0.0174; p = 0.0397). Spatial analysis identified five malaria hotspots in southeastern/southern states. ARIMA models projected stable incidence through 2030.

articles.headline: Micro-level malaria hotspots and elimination targets in Indian district
articles.title_original: Identification of micro-level malaria hotspots and its implications for achieving malaria elimination target in a remote district of India.
articles.authors: Misra BR, Mishra A, Tiwari SK, Srivastava DN, Joshi HS, Mishra N, Dwivedi GR
articles.details: Analysis of 18,217 malaria cases (2017-2024) from Sonbhadra district using Kulldorf scanning to identify high-risk clusters. Test positivity rates decreased 95%; annual parasite incidence fell from 2.7 to 0.1 and annual falciparum incidence rose to 0.09 in 2024 after suppression in 2022-2023. Most significant hotspot at Chopan block; both P. falciparum and P. vivax high-risk clusters identified at sub-district level.

articles.headline: Dengue and malaria coinfection prevalence in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Molecular detection and epidemiological characterization of dengue and malaria coinfection in the Guiriko region, Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Belem LRW, Yao RK, Amara MF, Taita AVW, Kaboré BP, Kouadio KWU, Ibemgbo SA, Sangaré I, Gomgnimbou MK
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 408 blood samples from patients with suspected dengue or malaria (June-September 2023). Serology-based dengue-malaria coinfection prevalence was 17.64% (72/408); acute dengue-malaria coinfection 7.10% (29/408). Among RT-PCR positive dengue cases, 100% (29/29) were PCR-positive for Plasmodium and 68.96% (20/29) positive by microscopy. Elevated temperature and high CRP (mean 83.79 mg/L) associated with acute coinfection.

articles.headline: Avian hemoparasite diversity in Colombian tropical dry forest birds
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of avian hemoparasites (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus) in resident and migratory birds from an urban fragment of tropical dry forest in northern Colombia.
articles.authors: Rodríguez MM, Flores FS, Castro LR
articles.details: Molecular survey of 217 birds (27 resident and 7 migratory species) from urban tropical dry forest fragment using PCR targeting cytochrome b and 18SrRNA genes. Haemoproteus or Plasmodium infections detected in 11 individuals across 6 species with 5.1% overall prevalence. Several documented host-parasite associations represent new records for Colombia. Phylogenetic analyses performed using maximum likelihood and Bayesian inference.

articles.headline: Peripheral blood erythrophagocytosis in falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Peripheral Blood Erythrophagocytosis in Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Almeida T, Ferreira CA
articles.details: No abstract available. Title indicates study of erythrophagocytosis in P. falciparum malaria, a cellular phenomenon potentially contributing to pathophysiology.

articles.headline: Infant malaria immunity: vaccines, antibodies, and prevention strategies
articles.title_original: Malaria immunity in the infant: progress and pitfalls.
articles.authors: Feeney ME
articles.details: Review of recent malaria prevention advances in infants, covering RTS,S/AS01E and R21/Matrix-M vaccines, novel antigen-delivery platforms with whole live-attenuated parasites, and human monoclonal antibodies targeting circumsporozoite protein (CSP). Current vaccines show suboptimal immunity in infants and are recommended only for children ≥5 months. Reviews age-related immune differences in P. falciparum infection and vaccination responses, considering transplacental antibody acquisition and in utero antigen exposure.

articles.headline: Radical cure approaches for Plasmodium vivax infection
articles.title_original: Reimagining radical cure for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Aung PL, Cui L
articles.details: No abstract available. Title suggests discussion of radical cure strategies for P. vivax malaria.

articles.headline: High-dose primaquine and tafenoquine versus low-dose primaquine in vivax malaria
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and safety of 7-day high-dose primaquine and single-dose tafenoquine versus 14-day low-dose primaquine in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria (EFFORT): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, controlled, superiority trial.
articles.authors: Degaga TS, Pasaribu AP, Tripura R, Ghanchi N, Rajasekhar M, Adhikari B, Ley B, Bamboro SA, Jabbar FA, Hasibuan N, Tego TT, Zehra S, Qurashi B, Mnjala H, Lee G, Li P, Kazi AM, Safitri W, Yulita Y, Siagian DSP, Syahputra D, Hadi H, Muhammad T, Ibrahim A, Syed N, Dost K, Puspitrasari AM, Kariodimedjo PP, Rai A, Rumaseb A, Sutanto E, Ean M, Khan A, Sokha M, Commons RJ, Weston S, Noviyanti R, Peto T, Callery JJ, Ali U, Mehmood T, Dondorp A, Devine A, Setiawan E, Mwaura M, Cassidy-Seyoum S, Temesgen R, Abate DT, Erjabo EB, Gessa GG, Kiros FG, Usmani MI, Raza A, Woyessa A, Hailu A, Simpson JA, Karahalios A, Beg MA, von Seidlein L, Dysoley L, Auburn S, Price RN, Thriemer K
articles.details: Multicentre randomized controlled trial in Ethiopia, Pakistan, Indonesia, and Cambodia comparing 7-day high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg total), single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg), or 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg total) in 960 adults with uncomplicated P. vivax (G6PD ≥70%). At 6 months, cumulative P. vivax recurrence was 13.0% (high-dose, HR 0.66, p=0.063), 12.6% (tafenoquine, HR 0.64, p=0.041), and 18.5% (low-dose). Drug-related adverse events: 42.9% (high-dose), 22.2% (tafenoquine), 34.2% (low-dose) by day 42.

articles.headline: White blood cell count channel discrepancy as malaria diagnostic clue
articles.title_original: Discrepancy Between White Blood Cell Count Channels as a Clue to Malaria Infection.
articles.authors: Li C, Wang YH, Meng SI, Chen SC, Lee JA
articles.details: No abstract available. Title indicates CBC parameter discrepancies may serve as diagnostic clue for malaria infection.

articles.headline: Environmental variability impacts genetically modified mosquito development for malaria control
articles.title_original: Beyond the static lab: environmental variability in genetically modified mosquito target gene identification for malaria control.
articles.authors: Dael L, Simões ML
articles.details: Perspective paper arguing that GMM target-gene identification under static laboratory conditions fails to account for environmental and climatic variability affecting Anopheles and Plasmodium biology and mosquito innate immunity. Natural environmental variations influence vector competence and transmission. Emphasizes need to incorporate realistic environmental variability in upstream GMM development, particularly given climate change impacts.

articles.headline: Malaria therapy for neurosyphilis: historical evaluation of treatment efficacy
articles.title_original: Malaria therapy for neurosyphilis at Mont Park Hospital for the insane in Australia, 1927-1928.
articles.authors: Clayton A
articles.details: Historical study examining malaria therapy for general paralysis of the insane at Mont Park Hospital for the Insane (Australia, 1927-1928). Using published accounts and medical records, evaluated psychiatrist Reginald Ellery's claims of effectiveness. Analysis identified diagnostic drift, adjunct interventions, and healthy-patient selection as factors contributing to overestimation of effectiveness. Patient outcomes in medical records were less positive than in published accounts, not supporting malaria as effective treatment for neurosyphilis.

articles.headline: P. vivax circumsporozoite protein diversity in Thailand resurgence
articles.title_original: Genetic signatures of Plasmodium vivax Circumsporozoite Surface Protein during malaria resurgence in Thailand.
articles.authors: Tapaopong P, Holzschuh A, da Silva G, Chintanawiwat P, Bantuchai S, Rungsarityotin W, Cui L, Koepfli C, Sattabongkot J, Nguitragool W
articles.details: Analysis of 69 P. vivax isolates from Thailand's 2022-2023 resurgence identified VK210 (n=66) and VK247 (n=3) CSP variants. VK210 showed 21 haplotypes with 13-20 repeat motifs; haplotype H2 was most common at 50%. Membrane feeding assays with Anopheles dirus showed H2 produced more salivary-gland sporozoites per oocyst. Three motifs identified as prime vaccine targets.

articles.headline: RBC membrane polymorphisms and malaria infection risk in Thailand
articles.title_original: Red blood cell membrane protein polymorphisms related to risk of malaria infection in the population living in malaria-endemic areas of Thailand: An analytic cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Muhamad P, Panrit L, Srikhamdokkhae O, Laohapensaeng K, Tarasuk M, Sa-I S, Na-Bangchang K
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 616 samples (270 malaria-infected, 346 uninfected) from Thai-Myanmar and Thai-Malaysian borders examined RBC polymorphisms. MM antigen more common in infected (61.1% vs 46.5%, OR 3.7, p<0.0001). FYA/FYA or FYA/FYB with wild-type Duffy promoter more common in infected (76.1% vs 46.9%, OR 4.7, p<0.0001). RBC polymorphisms associated with increased malaria infection risk.

articles.headline: Natural compound discovery targeting P. falciparum PfDHFR-TS enzyme
articles.title_original: Discovery of novel Plasmodium falciparum PfDHFR-TS inhibitors from ConMedNP natural compounds: a multi-computational approach.
articles.authors: Haiwang Djefoulna VH, Atiya Atiya M, Fifen JJ, Conradie J
articles.details: Computational screening of 2754 compounds revealed mean binding affinity of -8.8032 kcal/mol (median -8.9), with 75% exceeding artemether's affinity (-8.0 kcal/mol). Random Forest QSAR model achieved test R²=0.8321. Top candidates RDC0118 (-13.5 kcal/mol), RDC0119 (-13.4 kcal/mol), and CA0001 (-13.0 kcal/mol) showed superior binding; CA0001 was safer without hepatotoxicity risk.

articles.headline: Lipid-based nutrient supplements and IPTp in pregnancy review
articles.title_original: Effects of Combined Lipid-Based Nutrient Supplements and Malaria Preventive Treatment during Pregnancy on Malaria, Maternal Nutrition, and Birth Outcomes: A Scoping Review.
articles.authors: Parikh P, Zeilani M, Pieret A, Abbeddou S
articles.details: Scoping review of 17 studies (5 main trials) in Sub-Saharan Africa examining lipid-based nutrient supplements combined with intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy. LNS supplementation did not significantly affect malaria incidence, anemia, or nutritional status. Some studies reported improvements in birth outcomes, particularly among subgroups with baseline undernutrition. Evidence for effects on malaria incidence or maternal anemia remains inconclusive.

articles.headline: Malaria Scoring System validation in non-endemic emergency department
articles.title_original: External validation of the Malaria Scoring System in a non-endemic emergency department.
articles.authors: Paglietta G, Giamello JD, D'Agnano S, Melchio R, Carralero GL, Gianelli A, Giubbini G, Manassero S, Artana N, Barutta L, Basile E, Fenoglio L, Lupia E, Lauria G
articles.details: External validation in 365 emergency patients (114 malaria-positive, 251 negative) in Italy. MSS incorporates travel history and hematological abnormalities (thrombocytopenia 78.1% vs 8.4%, lymphopenia 71.9% vs 32.7%, anemia 53.5% vs 35.1%) plus fever. MSS showed excellent discriminatory ability (AUC=0.902, 95% CI 0.869-0.935) with good calibration (Hosmer-Lemeshow p=0.195). Median MSS higher in malaria cases (4 [3;5] vs 1 [1;2], p<0.001).

articles.headline: Ferroptosis in splenic macrophages exacerbates malaria progression
articles.title_original: HIF-1α/HMOX1-Mediated Ferroptosis in Macrophages Exacerbates Malaria Progression.
articles.authors: Liang G, Li J, Xie X, Yuan H, Xiao W, Chen M, Lu K, Xu L, Pan L, Wei H, Li L, Zhao S, Xie H, Pan X, Huang J
articles.details: P. yoelii infection triggers ferroptosis in splenic macrophages via HIF-1α downregulation and HMOX1 upregulation leading to Fe2+ overload. Ferroptosis inhibitor Fer-1 reduced parasitemia in infected mice. GPX4 conditional knockout mice showed worsened parasitemia. Identical ferroptosis signature detected in monocytes from malaria patient PBMCs. Targeting HIF-1α/HMOX1 axis could alleviate malaria progression via host-directed intervention.

articles.headline: Merozoite surface protein 8 deletion protects mice from lethal malaria
articles.title_original: Deletion of Plasmodium yoelii Merozoite Surface Protein 8 Protects Mice From Lethal Blood-Stage Malaria and Is Associated With Enhanced Splenic CCL19-CCR7 Signaling.
articles.authors: Sun Y, Fan Z, Ge J, Cui W, Zuo S, Tan L, Han S, Yan Y, Wang B, Cao J, Lv Z, Zhu G, Cheng Y
articles.details: P. yoelii msp8 deletion (ΔMSP8) reduced peak parasitemia and splenomegaly, resulting in self-resolving infection. Mouse antisera against P. falciparum MSP8 partially inhibited merozoite invasion in vitro. PyMSP8 immunization conferred partial protection against lethal P. yoelii 17XL challenge. ΔMSP8 upregulated splenic CCL19-CCR7 signaling with increased IFN-γ. CCR7 knockdown partially abrogated ΔMSP8's survival advantage.

articles.headline: COVID-19 pandemic disrupted childhood malaria reporting in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on reported malaria incidence in children under five years of age in Cameroon: an interrupted time series analysis with regional heterogeneity (2017-2022).
articles.authors: Mekontchou LM, Nguefack-Tsague G, Sonegueng AVK, Deffo PY, Sepde E, Samadoulougou S
articles.details: Interrupted time series analysis of DHIS2 data (2017-2022) in children under 5. March 2020 showed immediate 36.9% decline in reported incidence (IRR=0.631, p<0.001), followed by 3.1% monthly increase (IRR=1.031, p<0.001). Counterfactual analysis showed 69,708 fewer notifications in 2020-2021 and 74,831 excess in 2022. LLIN distribution inversely associated with incidence (IRR=0.978, p=0.024). Marked regional heterogeneity with severe declines in Centre (IRR=0.467) and East regions.

articles.headline: Tropical ocean climate patterns drive malaria transmission in Malawi
articles.title_original: Tropical oceans drive variability in soil moisture and malaria in Malawi.
articles.authors: Elling MT, Karnauskas KB, Kowalcyk M, Mategula D, Chirombo J, Livneh B, McCann RS, Buchwald AG
articles.details: Analysis of malaria incidence and climate data identified mechanistic links between ocean patterns and disease. Warm tropical Atlantic leads to wet conditions and increased cases. Warm Indian Ocean drives hot, dry conditions reducing cases. Soil moisture is the crucial link between remote climate drivers and disease dynamics. Future climate change expected to reduce soil moisture by 2100, reshaping transmission patterns.

articles.headline: P. vivax hypnozoite RNA-binding proteins suppress liver stage replication
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.
articles.authors: Vo KC, van Biljon R, Zanghi G, Zavala B, Betz W, Jennison C, Vaughan AM, Painter HJ, Kappe SHI
articles.details: Two hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage schizogony. When individually expressed in P. yoelii, each protein blocked schizogony with parasites remaining small and uninucleate. RNA sequence screening showed enrichment of distinct, highly specific motifs suggesting proteins bind target RNAs to suppress replication. Named IESI-1 and IESI-2 (Initiation of Exo-erythrocytic Schizogony Inhibited), providing mechanistic evidence for hypnozoite quiescence.

articles.headline: P. falciparum schizont spatial proteome maps 3000 proteins across 24 compartments
articles.title_original: The spatial proteome of the Plasmodium falciparum schizont illuminates the composition and evolutionary trajectories of its organelles.
articles.authors: Chisholm SA, Flores V, Kemp A, Breckels LM, Koreny L, Dos Santos Pacheco N, Barylyuk K, Kuroshchenkova A, Lilley KS, Rayner JC, Waller RF
articles.details: High-resolution spatial proteomics using hyperLOPIT mapped 3000 P. falciparum proteins in late schizont stages. Identified 24 distinct cellular niches including organelles and exported sites within host erythrocytes. Supervised machine-learning classified 1646 proteins into specific compartments. Comparative genomic analyses revealed spatial patterns of cell evolution and ongoing adaptive pressures in Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: Optimal age-based sampling improves seroconversion rate estimation precision
articles.title_original: Optimizing age-structured sampling for estimating the seroconversion rate in malaria seroepidemiology: a simulation study.
articles.authors: Ko YK, Li S, Britton T, Kagaya W, Kaneko A
articles.details: Simulation study using reverse catalytic model optimized age-structured sampling for estimating P. falciparum MSP1 seroconversion rate. In stable transmission, sampling older individuals improved precision in low-transmission settings, while younger individuals were more informative in high-transmission when seroreversion rate was known. When seroreversion rate unknown, balanced age distribution yielded higher precision. Framework applied to published serological data from Sri Lanka.

articles.headline: Single-cell transcriptomics reveals drug-response pathways in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Single-cell analysis of Plasmodium falciparum transcripts after drug perturbation identifies feedback regulation as well as increased transmission potential.
articles.authors: Godinez-Macias KP, Calla J, Jepsen K, Winzeler EA
articles.details: Researchers applied single-cell RNA sequencing to P. falciparum-infected red blood cells treated with artemisinin, atovaquone, and ganaplacide. Artemisinin treatment enriched gametocyte-associated genes, while atovaquone showed enrichment of pyrimidine biosynthesis transcripts. Single-cell transcriptomics can profile diverse drug responses and classify mechanisms of action.

articles.headline: Plant compounds inhibit malaria enzyme via computational screening
articles.title_original: In-silico study of the bioactive compounds of Blighia sapida koenig revealed a novel Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor for malaria.
articles.authors: Akinyemi DO, Odunitan TT, Ojeniyi FD, Akinyemi AO, Rahman N, Zafar H, Ehigie LO, Afolabi OK, Ojurongbe O
articles.details: Researchers identified 21 bioactive compounds from Blighia sapida extract using GC-MS and evaluated their binding to P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH) through molecular docking and dynamics simulations. Three compounds demonstrated strong binding affinities (−8.1 to −10.0 kcal/mol), with 2-methyl-6-(4-methylenecyclohex-2-en-1-yl)hept-2-en-4-one showing the most stable interactions (RMSD = 0.12).

articles.headline: Spatial analysis reveals socioeconomic drivers of malaria vulnerability in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Topological Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (ToF-AHP) for geospatial assessment of malaria vulnerability in Nigeria.
articles.authors: Atijosan A, Atijosan A
articles.details: A geospatial study using Topological Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process assessed malaria vulnerability across Nigeria using five indicators: ITN usage, maternal education, socioeconomic status, ANC attendance, and distance to health facilities. Results showed pronounced north-south disparities, with maternal and gender-linked socioeconomic factors accounting for 34.63% of vulnerability. Approximately 62.52% of very-high-incidence zones coincided with high-vulnerability areas.

articles.headline: Gene regulates malaria parasite male fertility and transmission
articles.title_original: The CCCH-type zinc-finger Pf MD3 promotes translation for malaria parasite transmission.
articles.authors: van Biljon R, Bhatnagar S, Kumar R, Gliozzi M, Xu G, Tripathi A, Ashrafi A, Swearingen K, Sinnis P, Llinás M, Painter HJ
articles.details: Researchers characterized the CCCH zinc-finger protein PfMD3, finding that its genetic disruption resulted in male fertility defects that decreased mosquito infectivity. PfMD3 acts as an RNA-binding protein that regulates translation of mRNA transcripts associated with male gamete development through specific RNA sequences and interactions with mRNA-processing proteins.

articles.headline: Niche modeling maps malaria vector habitat suitability in Argentina
articles.title_original: MODELING THE POTENTIAL CLIMATIC SUITABILITY OF ANOPHELES PSEUDOPUNCTIPENNIS, THE HISTORICAL VECTOR OF MALARIA IN ARGENTINA.
articles.authors: Duque PL, León R, Coello Peralta RD, Villarroel Martínez EI, Liria-Salazar J, Dantur Juri MJ
articles.details: Researchers used ecological niche modeling (MaxEnt) with 312 occurrence records and bioclimatic variables to identify climatically suitable areas for the malaria vector Anopheles pseudopunctipennis in Argentina. The best model achieved an omission rate of 0.11 and validation AUC of 0.83, identifying suitable conditions concentrated mainly in northwestern provinces (Jujuy, Salta, Tucumán, Catamarca) with additional areas in other provinces.

articles.headline: Mobility drives malaria transmission in low-endemic Indonesian areas
articles.title_original: Mobility and malaria incidence in Indonesia's low-endemic areas during the COVID-19 period.
articles.authors: Arisanti RR, Maula AW, Ahmad RA, Utarini A
articles.details: A 26-month prospective study across four Indonesian districts analyzed 480,575 mobility events and malaria incidence (March 2020–May 2022). Multivariable Poisson regression showed increased mobility was associated with higher malaria incidence [IRR 1.25, 95% CI 1.21–1.30], with malaria screening and COVID-19 positivity also significantly associated (IRR 2.33 and 1.12, respectively). Outside Java incidence was 64% lower than on Java.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal analysis shows control project impacts on Korean malaria dynamics
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impact of South-North Joint Malaria Control Project on spatiotemporal transmission dynamics in South Korea using dynamic mode decomposition.
articles.authors: Seo H, Kim BN, Abdulali A, Lee S
articles.details: Researchers applied compatible-window dynamic mode decomposition (CwDMD) to malaria incidence data from 2001–2024 across South Korea (17 provinces, 37 subregions) to evaluate control interventions. The South-North Joint Control Project (2008–2011) achieved a 63% incidence reduction, while the 5-Year Re-Elimination Plan (2019–2023) initially decreased incidence by 49% (to 2021) but subsequently increased by approximately 30% by 2023.

articles.headline: SMC extension to older children reduces malaria cases in Niger
articles.title_original: Extension of seasonal malaria chemoprevention to older children: a pragmatic comparative study in two health districts in the Zinder region, Niger.
articles.authors: Djedanem M, Ibrahim A, Nazibou MY, Noura MS, Yacoudima EYM, Zakari A, Mbega HO, Mamane Z, Issaka M, Adehossi E, Testa J, Jambou R
articles.details: A 36-month retrospective comparative study (2021–2023) across two health districts in Niger evaluated the impact of extending seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SP/AQ) from under-5s to children aged 5–10 years. In the intervention district (Damagaram Takaya), children aged ≥5 had significantly higher baseline malaria incidence (33.34 vs 12.45 per 1,000 in <5-year-olds in 2021) and showed significant decreases in uncomplicated and severe malaria by 2023 following SMC expansion.

articles.headline: Long-read sequencing method for P. falciparum genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: Development of an Oxford nanopore sequencing technology-based whole genome sequencing method for Plasmodium falciparum to support malaria molecular surveillance.
articles.authors: Bakari C, Holzschuh A, Seth MD, Madebe RA, Pereus D, Mandara CI, Schneeberger P, Juliano JJ, Bailey JA, Ishengoma DS, Nsanzabana C
articles.details: Researchers developed and optimized an Oxford Nanopore Technologies (ONT)-based whole-genome sequencing protocol for P. falciparum using dried blood spots and whole blood, with parasite DNA enriched via McrBC/MspJI digestion, NEBNext microbiome DNA enrichment, and selective whole-genome amplification. The optimized protocol achieved >30× depth coverage and 99.8% accuracy with median read lengths >2 kb; it showed high concordance with Sanger sequencing and detected additional mutations and copy-number variations (pfmdr1, pfgch1).

articles.headline: Behavioral and structural barriers delay malaria care-seeking in Malawi
articles.title_original: Three delays of malaria care-seeking in Malawi: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Olapeju B, Bride M, Volkmann T, Cash S, Dembo E, Kayange M, Gumbo A, Mafuleka T, Boyle J, Mang'ando A, Mbeye NM, Mukwakwa C
articles.details: A cross-sectional survey of 913 female caregivers in Malawi (May–July 2021) applied the Three Delays Model to identify barriers to prompt (same- or next-day) care-seeking for child fever. Prompt care-seeking was primarily associated with initial decision-making delays (aOR 0.58, 95% CI 0.43–0.78) and psychosocial factors (aOR 0.59, 95% CI 0.44–0.79), including incorrect malaria knowledge (aOR 0.71), negative attitudes (aOR 0.58), and incorrect knowledge of where to seek care (aOR 0.19).

articles.headline: Genetic variants in adhesion genes modify sickle cell severity and malaria risk
articles.title_original: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes influence the severity of sickle cell anemia in a malaria-endemic region : Short title: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes in sickle cell anemia.
articles.authors: Matos I, Santos B, Gonçalves E, Lopes P, Brito M, Arez AP, Faustino P
articles.details: A genetic study of 65 pediatric sickle cell anemia (SCA) patients in Angola genotyped 15 polymorphic sites in CD36 and ICAM-1 genes and evaluated Plasmodium infection status. Several genetic variants significantly influenced SCA severity: CD36 rs3211938_G, ICAM-1 rs5491_T, and rs5496_A affected hematological phenotype, while ICAM-1 rs5494_T carriers showed a 5.63-fold increased risk of malaria infection compared to wild-type carriers.

articles.headline: Parasite surface protein triggers NK cell immunity against malaria
articles.title_original: LFA-1 interaction with GBP-130 on Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells mediates NK cell activation and parasite control.
articles.authors: Mukhtar O, Dutt R, Panda A, Kumari P, Singh SS, Paul G, Prakash N, Abbas M, Islam MM, Arora P, Tammour A, Mohmmed A, Kumar D, Malhotra P
articles.details: Researchers identified glycophorin binding protein-130 (PfGBP-130) as a surface-expressed ligand on P. falciparum-infected red blood cells that binds the integrin LFA-1 on NK cells. Co-culture assays demonstrated that PfGBP-130 promotes NK cell activation and degranulation, facilitating contact-dependent killing of infected cells; neutralizing antibodies against PfGBP-130 significantly impaired these responses.

articles.headline: Placental malaria burden and adverse birth outcomes in Tanzanian primigravidae
articles.title_original: High burden of histology-confirmed placental malaria and associated adverse birth outcomes among primigravidae in Northwestern Tanzania: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Buhaga G, Mujuni F, Matiku S, Kiritta R, Matovelo D, Ndaboine E, Mazigo HD
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 422 primigravidae women at Sengerema hospital (Oct 2024-Apr 2025) screened for malaria via RDT, microscopy, and placental histology. Histology-confirmed placental malaria prevalence was 39.8%; microscopy showed 23.7% placental malaria and 19.7% maternal infection. Placental malaria independently predicted low birth weight (p=0.01), preterm birth (p=0.001), and small-for-gestational-age infants (p=0.048); fewer than four ANC visits and moderate anemia were significant risk factors. Authors conclude early ANC, IPTp, and improved diagnostics critical to reducing complications.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax virus boosts transmission and alters host immune response
articles.title_original: MaRNAV-1, an intracellular virus of Plasmodium vivax , is associated with increased parasite transmission and altered host immune response.
articles.authors: Seng D, Ko K, Orban A, Heng S, Feufack-Donfack LB, Grünebast J, Ya N, Dumetz F, Tebben K, Vignolini T, Dore G, Khim N, Salvador J, Ouaid Z, Lefèvre T, Cohuet A, Sommen C, Flamand C, Ruberto AA, Abebe A, Tsigie M, Tasew G, Tollera G, Malleret B, Shenkutie TT, Lo E, Anstey NM, Petrone ME, Cantaert T, Baumgarten S, Serre D, Popovici J
articles.details: Observational study characterizing MaRNAV-1, an intracellular RNA virus in P. vivax from Cambodia and Ethiopia. The virus was found at multiple parasite life cycle stages (blood, sporozoite, liver) with geographic variation in prevalence. MaRNAV-1 presence was positively associated with increased gametocyte abundance and higher oocyst prevalence/intensity in feeding assays, indicating enhanced transmission potential. Viral infection also drove immune activation including dendritic cell activation, Th1-type response shift, and elevated IFN-γ, CXCL10, IL-1RA, and IL-6 independently of parasitemia.

articles.headline: Fungal metabolites show promise as mosquito vector control agents
articles.title_original: Larvicidal and adulticidal activities of Purpureocillium lilacinum Secondary Metabolites against the Malaria Vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Chen D, Niu G, Lai L, Zerpa CV, Li J
articles.details: In vitro study of secondary metabolites from the entomopathogenic fungus P. lilacinum against A. gambiae larvae and adults. Fourth-instar larvae were significantly more susceptible than second-instar larvae. Extended fermentation of the crude extract reduced LC90 from 129 to 61 µg/mL. Purified compounds showed potent effects: pulixin LC90 was 68 µg/mL, leucinostatin A LC90 was 9 µg/mL against fourth-instar larvae, and leucinostatin A LD90 was approximately 1 µg per adult female mosquito. Authors conclude leucinostatin A may provide a dual-action strategy for blocking transmission.

articles.headline: Glutamate dehydrogenase as alternative diagnostic target for P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum glutamate dehydrogenase with known Plasmodium falciparum diagnostic targets using quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction.
articles.authors: Ahmad A, Nema S, Krishna S, Gupta H, Singh A, Verma AK, Singh MP, Anvikar AR, Bharti PK
articles.details: Retrospective qPCR comparison of pfgdh gene as a novel diagnostic target for P. falciparum against established markers (pfhrp2, pfhrp3, pfaldolase, pfldh) using 150 P. falciparum-positive and 50 malaria-negative samples.
