articles.title_original: Antimalarial Potency of Tinospora crispa Stem Extract and Artesunate Against Plasmodium berghei: A Study on Combination Therapy.
articles.authors: Ounjaijean S, Somsak V
articles.details: An in vitro and mouse (ICR) study investigated Tinospora crispa extract (TCE) combined with artesunate (ART) against Plasmodium berghei ANKA infection. TCE had an ED50 of 1.96 mg/kg and ART of 200.31 mg/kg; the combination demonstrated synergistic effects with a combination index <1 at ED50 and submultiples, resulting in significantly greater parasitemia inhibition than monotherapy, enhanced protection against packed cell volume reduction and body weight loss, and prolonged mean survival time. The authors conclude that TCE+ART combination may offer novel antimalarial benefits, though clinical evaluation is warranted.

articles.headline: Malaria treatment gaps and evidence gaps in infants under six months
articles.title_original: Malaria in children aged <6 months: a narrative review of current evidence, recommendations and practice gaps.
articles.authors: Dalsuco J, Bassat Q, Ahmed H, Pfeffer S, Winnips C, Boulton C, Otsyula N, D'Alessandro U
articles.details: A narrative review synthesizing evidence on malaria in children aged <6 months (or <5 kg), current treatment recommendations, and practice gaps. Clinical malaria occurs far less frequently in this age group than in older children, and diagnosis is challenging due to symptom overlap with other conditions. Evidence on safety and efficacy of available antimalarials is limited; until recently, no agents were licensed for neonates or infants <4.5 kg. The authors conclude that country-specific policy amendments are needed to standardize and improve treatment approaches for this vulnerable population.

articles.headline: Spatial forecasting reveals distinct malaria transmission hotspot patterns in Uganda
articles.title_original: Hotspot Mapping, Spatiotemporal Clustering and Forecasting of Malaria Incidence in Uganda, 2014-2023: Strategies to Inform Targeted Public Health Interventions.
articles.authors: Paasi G, Alunyo JP, Nakiyemba A, Ocen GG, Pande S, Alaroker F, Waako PJ, Olupot-Olupot P
articles.details: An ecological study of Uganda's district-level malaria surveillance data (2014–2023) using Emerging Hot Spot Analysis, Mann-Kendall trend testing, and ARIMA time-series modeling. Spatial analysis identified three distinct hotspot patterns: sporadic hotspots in West Nile, oscillating hotspots in Karamoja, and consecutive hotspots across Lango to southern Buganda, while cold spots concentrated in southwestern Kigezi. Mann-Kendall testing showed upward trends mainly in Karamoja and downward trends in Kigezi. National ARIMA forecasts projected 1.0–1.2 million monthly cases through 2030 with seasonal peaks March–June.

articles.headline: Systematic review evaluates rapid diagnostic test malaria accuracy
articles.title_original: Are rapid diagnostic tests reliable for the detection of malaria in the emergency department?
articles.authors: Moran C, Coyne S
articles.details: A systematic review assessed the diagnostic accuracy of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in febrile adults from malaria-endemic regions. The authors searched MEDLINE, EMBASE, Cochrane, and Google Scholar; six studies met inclusion criteria. Specific accuracy findings are not detailed in this abstract.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria associates with distinct gut microbiota shifts
articles.title_original: Gut microbiome alterations among Ghanaian children with asymptomatic malaria infections.
articles.authors: Larbi AA, Etsey M, Brew O, Koduah B, Mawuenyega RE, Amewu EKA, Essilfie NK, Wireko S, Kwarteng A, Gyan BA
articles.details: A study of Ghanaian children with asymptomatic malaria (predominantly mixed Plasmodium falciparum and P. malariae infections) characterized gut microbiota composition via 16S rRNA V4 sequencing. Compared to healthy controls, infected children showed decreased Firmicutes (52.5% to 44.0%), increased Bacteroidetes (34.7% to 45.6%), and increased Proteobacteria (3.0% to 4.8%). Beta-diversity analysis revealed malaria status significantly affected microbial composition (PERMANOVA p<0.05), explaining 19.1% of variation. The authors conclude that these shifts represent a potential mechanistic link between malaria and microbiome alterations.

articles.headline: T cell–B cell crosstalk drives high-titer anti-P. vivax antibodies
articles.title_original: A functional crosstalk between circulating follicular helper 2 T cells and memory B cells drives anti-Plasmodium vivax antibodies.
articles.authors: Salsabila ZZ, Kochayoo P, Khunsri T, Tianpothong P, Leepiyasakulchai C, Thawornpan P, Hafalla JCR, Adams JH, Chootong P
articles.details: A study of 40 subjects with acute Plasmodium vivax malaria, including 23 seropositive for anti-PvDBPII antibodies (High Responders and Low Responders), examined circulating T follicular helper 2 (cTfh2) cells and memory B cell interactions. High Responders showed elevated ICOS expression on cTfh2 cells, which were specifically activated by PvDBPII antigen. In co-culture, activated cTfh2 cells promoted memory B cell differentiation into antibody-secreting cells producing anti-PvDBPII IgG. The authors conclude that cTfh2 cell activation marked by ICOS upregulation correlates with high-titer anti-PvDBPII antibody responses.

articles.headline: Postpartum artemisinin therapy shows minimal effect on infant malaria risk
articles.title_original: Malaria in Papua New Guinean infants: a prespecified analysis of a randomised postpartum antimalarial treatment trial.
articles.authors: Tesine P, Woon SA, Laman M, Yadi G, Kasian B, Lorry L, Salman S, Pomat W, Manning L, Chih H, Moore BR, Davis TME
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: An open-label randomized controlled trial in Madang Province, Papua New Guinea (n=151 infants completing follow-up) evaluated postpartum artemisinin combination therapy (ACT) impact on infant malaria. Mothers were randomized to no treatment or ACT (artemether-lumefantrine or dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine) with infants assessed monthly over 6 months. The 6-month incidence of slide- or PCR-detected malaria was 3.3% (95% CI 1.2–8.0%), with 4 of 5 cases in untreated mothers' infants; 2 were slide-positive (1 P. falciparum, 1 P. vivax). Postpartum maternal ACT did not significantly affect infant growth or developmental milestones.

articles.headline: Integrating livestock-based mosquito control with insecticide-treated nets
articles.title_original: Integrating zooprophylaxis, insecticide-treated livestock and mosquito nets for malaria control: a One Health proposal.
articles.authors: Otolorin GR, Adegboye OA, Dishon CH, Dunka HI, Adanu AW, Ihieri CO, McBryde ES
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: This short communication proposes combining insecticide-treated livestock (ITL) and zooprophylaxis with insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) for integrated vector management. ITL has demonstrated significant reductions in mosquito survival, while zooprophylaxis diverts mosquitoes from humans using livestock. The proposed strategy targets outdoor-feeding mosquito species where ITNs alone have limited effectiveness due to early-evening biting and insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum malaria shaped human habitat choice prehistorically
articles.title_original: Malaria shaped human spatial organization for the past 74 thousand years.
articles.authors: Colucci M, Leonardi M, Blinkhorn J, Irish SR, Padilla-Iglesias C, Kaboth-Bar S, Gosling WD, Snow RW, Manica A, Scerri EML
articles.details: Combined species distribution models of mosquito vectors with paleoclimatic and epidemiological data to estimate malaria transmission risk in sub-Saharan Africa from 74,000 to 5,000 years ago. Demonstrated that humans avoided or had reduced settlement success in areas with high predicted P. falciparum malaria transmission, indicating disease was a major selective force in human spatial organization alongside climate.

articles.headline: Interpreting Plasmodium RDT standards for Chinese clinical practice
articles.title_original: [Interpretation of a health industry standard Detection of Plasmodium spp. Immune-chromatographic test (WS/T 10029-2025)].
articles.authors: Xu S, Zhu G, Cao J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Interprets China's new health industry standard (WS/T 10029-2025) for immunochromatographic tests detecting Plasmodium antigens. The standard provides technical basis and operational specifications for rapid diagnostic test use across medical and disease control institutions. Context includes China's imported malaria surveillance following achievement of malaria elimination nationally.

articles.headline: Cost-effectiveness of placental malaria vaccine in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Modelling the Cost-Effectiveness of a Placental Malaria Vaccine in Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Chinkhumba J, Manda-Taylor L, D'Alessio F, Madanitsa M
articles.details: Decision-analytic model comparing vaccinating women of childbearing age before pregnancy versus no vaccination. Under baseline assumptions (40% vaccine efficacy, 30% uptake, $5 dose price), the vaccine had incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (ICER) of $321 per perinatal DALY averted for primigravidae versus $4,444 for multigravidae. Cost-effectiveness remained robust across sensitivity analyses when targeting primigravidae, supporting prioritized vaccine deployment strategies.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria diagnosis and management challenges in African settings
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria in resource-limited African regions: Diagnostic and therapeutic challenges.
articles.authors: Akintunde JK, Oluwajembola AM, Oladosu MA, Akintunde DG, Akintunde OG, Ojo AE, Golagha R
articles.details: Review of cerebral malaria (P. falciparum) management in resource-limited sub-Saharan Africa, where mortality remains high at 15-30% with long-term neurological sequelae in 10-30% of survivors. Identifies key diagnostic challenges including misdiagnosis (up to 23% of clinically diagnosed cases), limited fundoscopy and neuroimaging capabilities, absent reliable biomarkers, and delayed presentation. Addresses health systems constraints and provides context-appropriate recommendations for reducing CM-associated morbidity and mortality.

articles.headline: LLIN ownership and utilization gaps in Ethiopian malaria communities
articles.title_original: Assessment of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Net (LLIN) Ownership, Utilization, and Associated Barriers in Malaria-Endemic Communities of Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Waldetensai A, Tasew G, Yewhalaw D, Takie H, Gidey B, Kinde S, Gemechu F, Yirga S, Kinfe E, Hailemariam A, Tadesse H, Solomon H, Assefa G, Dilu D, Bashaye S, Wuletaw Y, Abdulatif B, Kebede T, Tadiwos S, Gebrewold G, Hailu S, Tesfaye F, Tollera G, Hailu M, Guiyun Y, Eukubay A, Gebresillassie A
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 9,222 households (34,427 individuals) across 11 Ethiopian administrative regions (October 2024–January 2025) found household LLIN ownership of 71.5% but sufficient nets for only 58.3%. Overall utilization was 59.9% (72.7% rural vs. lower urban), with higher rates among pregnant women (78.5%) and children under 5 (67.2%). Major barriers included 60.5% lacking confidence in net hanging and 64.1% misconceiving malaria as seasonal-only, with overall utilization below the 80% threshold needed for community-wide protection.

articles.headline: Acute motor axonal neuropathy variant after childhood malaria
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome After Malaria: A Case Report of a 7-Year-Old Child With Asymmetric Onset of Acute Motor Axonal Neuropathy.
articles.authors: Abusaada MIM, Ali MMI, Adam MMA, Ali MHO, Baraka MAM, Bakheit AAM, Mohamed Ahmed KAH, Mohmed IYK
articles.details: Case report of a 7-year-old child who developed acute motor axonal neuropathy (an atypical Guillain-Barré variant) with asymmetric, relapsing weakness following malaria infection. Highlights the need for clinicians in malaria-endemic, resource-limited settings to recognize atypical GBS variants early, as timely recognition and treatment can significantly improve outcomes despite diagnostic and systemic constraints.

articles.headline: Severe childhood malaria associated with long-term cognitive deficits
articles.title_original: Malaria may cause long-term brain deficits, study shows.
articles.authors: Vogel G
articles.details: Study of Ugandan children who survived severe malaria infection, showing worse performance on cognitive tests many years later compared to controls. Results demonstrate persistent neurological sequelae among survivors of severe malaria, suggesting long-term brain dysfunction beyond the acute illness.

articles.headline: Malaria exposure not associated with SARS-CoV-2 infection in Ugandan adolescents
articles.title_original: Life Course Malaria Exposure and SARS-CoV-2 Seroepidemiology in Ugandan Adolescents: A Longitudinal Study Nested in a Birth Cohort.
articles.authors: Zirimenya L, Nayebare D, Baine C, Nassuuna J, Namutebi M, Zziwa C, Nsubuga D, Kiwudhu F, Akello F, Tumusiime J, Oduru G, Kabami G, Akurut H, Nakyesige R, Natukunda A, Wajja A, Lubyayi L, Kimuda S, Nkurunungi G, Serwanga J, Doores KJ, Webb EL, Elliott AM
articles.details: Longitudinal study nested in the Entebbe birth cohort followed 476 Ugandan adolescents (mean age 16.4 years, 43.1% female) over 12 months. Prior malaria exposure measured by clinical malaria history (birth to age 10; 61.3% of cohort), asymptomatic parasitemia (12.0% ever positive), and antibody responses to P. falciparum merozoite surface protein 2 (PfMSP-2) and apical membrane antigen 1 (PfAMA-1). SARS-CoV-2 seropositivity increased from 63% at baseline to 94.9% at 12 months (unvaccinated); multivariable analysis found no consistent association between life-course malaria exposure and SARS-CoV-2 seropositivity or antibody concentration levels.

articles.headline: Abbott malaria RDT shows cross-reactivity with non-target Plasmodium species
articles.title_original: Strong Cross-Reactivity of Anti-Plasmodium falciparum pLDH Monoclonal Antibodies With Plasmodium malariae pLDH in the Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.f/P.v Rapid Diagnostic Test.
articles.authors: Montoia G, Sanchez MCA, Lima-Cooper G, Farinas MLRN, Inoue J, Di Santi SM
articles.details: Evaluated cross-reactivity of the Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.f/P.v rapid diagnostic test (designed to detect P. falciparum and P. vivax via lactate dehydrogenase) with 40 blood samples: 10 P. falciparum, 10 P. vivax, 15 P. malariae, 5 P. ovale (confirmed by nested PCR). The RDT correctly identified all P. falciparum and P. vivax samples but showed strong cross-reactivity with P. malariae (12/14 positive; 85.7%) and P. ovale (4/5), with anti-Pf-pLDH monoclonal antibodies showing only 14.29% specificity against P. malariae (p<0.05). Highlights need for alternative biomarkers and novel point-of-care platforms for accurate species-specific diagnosis.

articles.headline: Selective proteasome inhibitors targeting Plasmodium falciparum 20S proteasome
articles.title_original: An Optimized Route to the Syringolin Natural Products Enables Combinatorial Synthesis of Selective, Bioactive Inhibitors of the Plasmodium falciparum 20S Proteasome.
articles.authors: Gu X, Fajtova P, Yan NL, Saxena A, Fei F, Zhu J, Tse E, Melo A, Yoo E, Buchwald NH, Southworth DR, Bogyo M, Derisi JL, Gestwicki JE, O'Donoghue AJ, Sello JK
articles.details: Developed optimized synthetic route to syringolin-based inhibitors of the P. falciparum 20S proteasome. A library of compounds exhibited high second-order rate constants for Pf proteasome inhibition and nanomolar-level antiparasitic activity, with selectivity for the Pf proteasome over the human proteasome. Cryo-EM structures revealed key contacts favoring species-selective inhibition, providing structural basis for pursuing new antimalarial drugs targeting this emerging parasite target.

articles.headline: Metal-coordinated mefloquine complexes with enhanced antiparasitic activity
articles.title_original: Examination of Coligands in Mefloquine-Metal Complexes Reveals the Structural Determinants of Activity against Plasmodium falciparum and Schistosoma mansoni.
articles.authors: Villarreal W, Quadros HC, Colina-Vegas L, Aboagye SY, Dziwornu GA, Ribeiro GH, Barros AI, Kucharski DJ, Rahbari M, Brandstädter C, D'Alessandro S, Basilico N, Masike K, Mehlala N, Nobrega JA, Deflon VM, Navarro M, Boratyński PJ, Chibale K, Williams DL, Batista AA, Moreira DRM
articles.details: Synthesized mefloquine-metal complexes [M(II)(L)(MQ)]PF₆ with varying metal centers (platinum or palladium) and coligands (phosphine or bipyridine). Tested against Plasmodium falciparum and Schistosoma mansoni, metal centers augmented mefloquine's antiparasitic activity by functioning as thioredoxin/glutathione reductase-targeting moieties. Mefloquine-platinum complexes displayed high in vivo efficacy with selective targeting of essential parasite biochemical pathways while sparing mammalian cells, despite isostructural palladium complexes differing in ligand dissociation behavior.

articles.headline: Malaria surveillance barriers in private-sector pharmacies sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers to malaria surveillance in community pharmacies and over-the-counter medicine sellers in sub-Saharan Africa's health sector: a scoping review protocol.
articles.authors: Bonful HA, Owiredu D, Annor RB, Opoku-Mireku M, Abokyi LN, Vroom BD, Kenu E, Kretchy IA, Koduah A
articles.details: Scoping review protocol to systematically identify facilitators and barriers to malaria surveillance in community pharmacies and over-the-counter medicine sellers in sub-Saharan Africa. Will search PubMed, CINAHL, Scopus, Web of Science, and Hinari from inception through June 15, 2026. Data synthesis will use narrative approach to organize findings by study characteristics and identified barriers and facilitators.

articles.headline: Archaeology and history of malaria in East and Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Historical texts and archaeological evidence for malaria and its interactions with humans from East to South Asia.
articles.authors: Li G, Yuan X, Wang T
articles.details: Review synthesizing archaeological, biological, and ecological evidence on human-malaria interactions in East, Southeast, and South Asia. Examines genetic analysis of Plasmodium species, ancient Chinese historical and medical documents, and skeletal markers. Explores how malaria evolution shaped human demographic shifts and biological resilience, and how human agricultural practices such as rice cultivation inadvertently created transmission-suitable environments.

articles.headline: Oocyst stage parasite biology and transmission-blocking strategies review
articles.title_original: Oocyst: knowns and unknowns about the lengthiest life stage of the malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Bailey AJ, Vlachou D, Christophides GK
articles.details: Review of Plasmodium oocyst development in mosquitoes, the longest parasite life stage occurring on the basal lamina after blood feeding. Identifies major gaps in understanding molecular and cellular processes: transcriptional and epigenetic regulation, nutrient acquisition, metabolic remodeling, cell cycle control, and mosquito immune interactions. Highlights how deeper insight into oocyst stage could inform transmission-blocking strategies and accelerate malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Experimental human P. knowlesi infection model development and parasitemia kinetics
articles.title_original: Development of an experimental human blood-stage model for studying Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Gower JSE, Webster R, Westaway JAF, Tickner JA, Wang CYT, Peters JM, Andrew D, Tan AF, Webb L, Potter AJ, Woo R, Mathison S, Sahai N, Chen N, Cheng Q, Boyle MJ, Anstey NM, Grigg MJ, Woodford J, Jennings HE, Amante FH, Barber BE
articles.details: Study developing a P. knowlesi induced blood-stage malaria (IBSM) model using GMP-grade parasite bank of P. knowlesi YH1 strain. Four adults received intravenous inoculation with dose escalation (~2,800 to ~280,000 parasites). Two of four developed parasitemia; one peaked at 202 parasites/mL on day 24 with parasite multiplication rate (PMR32) of 2.33 (95% CI 1.66-3.25). Adverse events were mostly mild. GMP-grade parasite bank successfully established patent infection in humans.

articles.headline: China-Africa cooperation for malaria control and elimination progress
articles.title_original: [Practice and prospect of China's participation in international cooperation for malaria control from the perspective of the "China-Africa Year of People-to-People Exchanges"].
articles.authors: Liang C, Zhu G, Cao J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Discussion of China's participation in international cooperation for malaria control, particularly in context of the 2026 'China-Africa Year of People-to-People Exchanges.' Introduces strategic significance and practical pathways for China-Africa malaria control cooperation and discusses policy recommendations for optimizing cooperation models between China and African countries to accelerate progress toward global malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Magneto-optical microscopy for quantitative automated malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Magneto-optical microscopy platform for quantitative imaging of hemozoin in blood for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Kinyua DM, Kotar J, Cicuta P
articles.details: Novel magneto-optical (MO) imaging approach exploiting hemozoin crystal magnetic anisotropy and optical dichroism for label-free, quantitative malaria detection within conventional polarizing microscope. External magnetic field induces torque on hemozoin crystals, modulating optical anisotropy to produce measurable field-dependent changes in polarization contrast. Ratiometric intensity analysis and threshold-based segmentation enable quantitative correlation between MO signal strength and hemozoin concentration, with metrics suitable for automated diagnosis without expert intervention.

articles.headline: PfMKT1 protein dispensable for P. falciparum asexual and sexual stages
articles.title_original: An MKT1 domain protein is dispensable for erythrocytic stages of plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Chakraborty B, McGee SM, Kumar S
articles.details: Study investigating role of PfMKT1, an MKT1 domain protein involved in posttranscriptional gene regulation and cellular differentiation. P. falciparum parasites with MKT1 gene deletion (Pfmkt1¯) proliferated asexually and differentiated into gametocytes similarly to wildtype NF54 parasites. Gametocytes underwent normal gametogenesis with no apparent defect in flagellar gamete formation, indicating MKT1-like protein is dispensable during asexual and sexual stage development.

articles.headline: Serological antibody profiles identify hidden malaria transmission in low-prevalence settings
articles.title_original: Malaria antibody responses augment surveillance in low-transmission settings in the Upper River Region, the Gambia.
articles.authors: Aydin RA, Keller T, Dabira ED, Mohammed N, Erhart A, Drakeley C, D'Alessandro U, Stresman G
articles.details: Study of 5,300 seropositive individuals across 32 Gambian villages post-mass drug administration using random forest models to predict surveillance detectability (P(Detect)) based on antibody responses to 19 P. falciparum antigens. Models showed strong discrimination between low/high detectability populations: precision-recall AUC 0.92-0.93 in <5% PCR prevalence stratum and 0.89-0.90 in <10% stratum using 8-antigen panel. PfSEA, PfMSP1-19, gSG6, and Etramp4.Ag2 were key predictive antigens.

articles.headline: Rac1 promoter regulatory elements link hypertension resistance and malaria susceptibility
articles.title_original: The Rac1 Promoter as a Molecular Nexus for Antihypertensive Therapy Resistance and Enhanced Malaria Susceptibility.
articles.authors: Ameke SL, Fosu K, Amenga-Etego L, Sarpong KAN, Kwofie SK
articles.details: In silico analysis of human Rac1 promoter region (-2000 to +500 bp) examining transcription factor binding sites. Rac1 promoter is GC-rich (57.34%), contains 164 CpG sites with observed/expected ratio of 0.75 defining a canonical CpG island, and features binding motifs for mineralocorticoid receptor (MR), hypoxia-inducible factors, inflammatory mediators, and GATA-1. Analysis suggests MR-mediated resistance to ACE inhibitors/ARB therapies while potentially enhancing malaria susceptibility through erythrocyte remodeling pathways.

articles.headline: Cost-efficiency framework for malaria routine data quality audits Zambia
articles.title_original: A framework for identifying cost efficiencies in malaria routine data quality audits: methodology and application in Zambia.
articles.authors: Nguyen A, Malama P, Lungu C, Mwanza M, Phiri D, Silumbe K, Chiwaula J, Hainsworth M, Das S, Roca-Feltrer A
articles.details: Framework for identifying cost efficiencies in routine data quality audits (RDQA) developed with Zambia National Malaria Elimination Centre. Analyzed 9 RDQA interventions (2022-2024) across 10 provinces, 91 districts, and 1,189 health facility catchment areas. Average economic costs were $24,938 per intervention ($205/HFCA), with personnel (64%) and transportation (25%) as primary cost drivers. Optimized scenarios incorporating virtual orientation, streamlined teams, and harmonized allowances reduced costs by 41%; digital RDQA tools projected additional 52% savings.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine baseline survey Kenya high malaria burden strong vaccine acceptance
articles.title_original: The Malaria Vaccine Programme Evaluation in Kenya: results of a baseline household survey prior to the introduction of the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine.
articles.authors: Westercamp N, Nyangau I, Kwambai TK, Seda B, Akach D, Okanda I, Marube E, Siambe P, Radiro E, Wafula F, Ondiegi B, Shah MP, Seffren V, Moore KA, Fogelson A, Milligan P, Kariuki S, Samuels AM
articles.details: Baseline household survey July-October 2019 in 46 western Kenya MVIP sub-counties (4,065 households, 4,948 children age 5-48 months) prior to RTS,S/AS01E vaccine introduction. P. falciparum prevalence was 22% (95% CI 19-26), ranging 1-71% by sub-county. ITN ownership and use were high (93% and 87%). Fever in prior 2 weeks in 38% of children; 70% sought care with 39% receiving antimalarials. Pre-introduction vaccine awareness was 36%, but willingness to vaccinate exceeded 98%.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine provides $24.2 billion socioeconomic benefit sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic Impact of Artemether-Lumefantrine in Malaria Treatment across Sub-Saharan Africa: A Model-Based Historical Analysis with 2022 as Reference Year.
articles.authors: Atitallah A, Peristeris P, Lovera D, Branner J, Shah R, Gandhi P, Gruenberger JB
articles.details: Excel-based model assessing socioeconomic burden and benefit of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) treatment for uncomplicated malaria in sub-Saharan Africa. For 2022, model estimated 30.8 million malaria-related disability-adjusted life years (DALYs) in adults ≥15 years corresponding to approximately $82.8 billion socioeconomic burden. Estimated total socioeconomic benefit of AL versus no-treatment scenario was $24.2 billion through avoided productivity losses including productive years of life lost and caregiving days.

articles.headline: NK cell activation and suppression in acute P. falciparum infection
articles.title_original: NK cell cytotoxicity is transiently enhanced during acute malaria and modulated by the host microenvironment.
articles.authors: Xi P, Sandoz PA, Lautenbach MJ, Bilev E, Önfelt B, Färnert A, Hammer Q, Sundling C
articles.details: Longitudinal study of malaria patients showing transient NK cell cytotoxicity activation during acute malaria with rapid granzyme B-mediated killing and elevated PRF1, GZMB, GZMA expression. Plasma granzymes and proinflammatory cytokines enhanced NK function in vitro. However, plasma also contained inhibitory mediators including soluble cytokine receptors that dampened NK responses, revealing a tightly regulated immune balance.

articles.headline: High-affinity aptamers for PfLDH malaria point-of-care diagnostics
articles.title_original: Engineering High-Performance Aptamers via Optimized SELEX and Structure-Guided Dimer Assembly for Clinical Malaria Diagnostics.
articles.authors: Xiong D, Ren Y, Gu J, Wu B, Dong X, Salena BJ, Zhang Z, Li Y
articles.details: Optimized SELEX and structure-guided dimer engineering of inverted dimeric aptamers (IDA) targeting P. falciparum lactate dehydrogenase achieved picomolar affinity (>800-fold higher than existing aptamers) with enhanced nuclease resistance via 3'-3' linkage. Clinical evaluation on 52 blood samples demonstrated 81.8% sensitivity and 100% specificity, comparing favorably with commercial rapid diagnostic tests.

articles.headline: Palmitoylation inhibition blocks P. falciparum asexual and sexual development
articles.title_original: [Role of protein palmitoylation modification in the proliferation and gametogenesis of Plasmodium falciparum].
articles.authors: Zhang M, Zhang M, Yang T, Zhu G, Tang J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Palmitoyltransferase (PATs) activity measured across P. falciparum developmental stages via click chemistry. 2-Bromopalmitate inhibition (10 μmol/L) significantly reduced parasitemia (AUC 8.4±1.8 vs 25.7±0.7 control, p<0.001), delayed asexual development with reduced merozoite numbers (14.0 vs 18.2, p<0.001), and blocked gametocyte formation and maturation, suggesting a novel antimalarial target.

articles.headline: Malaria epidemiology and severity predictors in Saudi Arabia outbreak
articles.title_original: Clinical Characteristics and Outcomes of Malaria Patients in the Aseer Region, Saudi Arabia: A Retrospective Study (2022-2025).
articles.authors: Alshehri FI, Alkhosafi DA, Al Asmari EA, Saeed AB, Zarbah AM, Algarni SA, Ahmed MG, Mohamed MA, Mady FA, Albakri SMZ, Ghazy RM
articles.details: Retrospective study of 311 malaria patients at Khamis Mushait Hospital (2022-2025; 84% of cases in 2024-2025): predominantly males aged 28.8±11.3 years. P. vivax 51.1%, P. falciparum 40.2%. Severe malaria 43.7% (severe anemia 26%, jaundice 23.2%). Logistic regression identified higher parasitemia (aOR=1.70 per 1% increase, p<0.001) and non-Saudi nationality (aOR=2.40, p=0.027) as independent severity predictors.

articles.headline: ITN ownership and use barriers in pregnant Fulani women, Ghana
articles.title_original: Factors Associated With Insecticide-Treated Bed Net Possession and Utilization in Malaria Prevention Among Fulani Pregnant Women in the Savannah Hinterlands of Ghana: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Salifu Y, Lasong J, Wobi EB, Salifu T, Nantomah B, Dandeebo G
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 159 Fulani pregnant women in West Gonja Municipality (April-June 2022): 54.7% owned ITN, 52.9% of owners used them. ITN ownership predictors: malaria awareness (aOR=3.45), prior counseling (aOR=4.52), lower wealth (aOR=0.21). Among owners, knowledge of symptoms (aOR=0.18) and multiple ITNs (aOR=0.07) associated with use; >3 children reduced use (aOR=0.02).

articles.headline: Herbal capsule formulations for malaria with in vitro antiplasmodial activity
articles.title_original: Formulation and Evaluation of Capsules From Azadirachta indica and Khaya senegalensis for Malaria Treatment.
articles.authors: Adoley WN, Johnson R, Boakye-Gyasi ME, Owusu FWA, Obeng LM, Osei-Asibey A, Asamoa Mensa KA, Zoiku FK
articles.details: Formulated capsules from Azadirachta indica and Khaya senegalensis plant decoctions for malaria treatment. Azadirachta indica with kaolin (35 mg/dose) showed disintegration time 10.01±0.34 min and 78.13±0.37% drug release; antiplasmodial IC50 2.74±0.70 μg/mL. Khaya senegalensis with bentonite (50 mg/dose) showed 4.48±0.23 min disintegration, 95.63±1.00% release, and IC50 24.37±4.19 μg/mL.

articles.headline: Severe clinical features and delayed care predict malaria death in children
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria-related mortality among children under five at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda, 2020-2024: A case-control study.
articles.authors: Kwizera P, Migisha R, Mutesi C, Rukundo G, Kabwama SN, Kwesiga B, Bulage L, Ario AR
articles.details: Case-control study of 100 malaria deaths versus 200 survivors in children ≤59 months at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital (2020-2024), case fatality rate 2.7%. Independent mortality predictors: convulsions (aOR=17, 95% CI 4.2-71), loss of consciousness (aOR=14, 95% CI 1.4-113), severe anemia (aOR=3.4), vomiting (aOR=3.1), and delayed care-seeking >24 hours (aOR=8.8).

articles.headline: Maternal antimalarial treatment effects and infant malaria outcomes
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy: what's good for the goose may not be good for the goslings.
articles.authors: Rogerson SJ, Unger HW
articles.details: Title indicates that antimalarial strategies and treatments used during pregnancy for malaria control may have differential or divergent effects on the infants born to treated mothers; no specific quantitative findings available.

articles.headline: IPTp regimens and infant malaria outcomes in endemic pregnancy
articles.title_original: Impact of intermittent preventive treatment for malaria in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and their combination on infant outcomes: A randomized controlled trial.
articles.authors: Nankabirwa JI, Kakuru A, Nguyen AT, Roh ME, Aguti M, Adrama H, Kizza J, Olwoch P, Camanag K, Benjamin-Chung J, Rosenthal PJ, Kamya MR, Dorsey G, Jagannathan P
articles.details: Double-blind RCT of 871 infants born to mothers receiving IPTp-SP, IPTp-DP, or IPTp-DP+SP, followed to 12 months. Among 667 clinical malaria episodes, incidence similar between groups (IPTp-DP IRR=1.04, 95% CI 0.78-1.38; IPTp-DP+SP IRR=0.93, 95% CI 0.68-1.26). Parasite prevalence 21.8% overall without group differences. Wasting at birth elevated in DP arms (PR=2.30, RR=2.25) but resolved by 3-6 months.

articles.headline: Mechanisms linking chronic malaria to cancer in African populations
articles.title_original: Malaria driven mechanisms shaping cancer risk and aggressiveness in African populations.
articles.authors: Udobi ME, Bella-Omunagbe M, Effiong PE, Afolabi IS, Chinedu SN
articles.details: Narrative review of five mechanistic axes linking malaria to oncogenesis: malaria-induced oxidative stress and inflammation driving genomic instability; microbiome dysbiosis altering systemic immunity and tumor microenvironment; shared molecular targets (EPCR, chondroitin sulfate) between parasites and cancer cells; malaria-oncogenic virus cooperation (EBV); vitamin D deficiency impairing immune surveillance. Antimalarials (artemisinin, chloroquine, quinacrine) noted to possess anticancer properties.

articles.headline: P. vivax and Guillain-Barré syndrome causality assessment
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Vivax Can Only Be Responsible for GBS if All Other Triggers Have Been Ruled Out.
articles.authors: Finsterer J
articles.details: Title discusses diagnostic and epidemiological challenges in attributing Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) to P. vivax infection, emphasizing that other potential triggers must be ruled out before establishing causality; no specific evidence presented.

articles.headline: Historical and cultural documentation of malaria in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data for cultural history of malaria in Hungary].
articles.authors: Kiss L
articles.details: Appears to contain archival data and historical documentation on malaria in Hungary; no contemporary research findings or quantitative data available from title.

articles.headline: Vaccine strategies to prevent malaria in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Immunization strategies to prevent malaria in pregnancy - a multistakeholder workshop.
articles.authors: D'Alessio F, Honkpehedji J, Sirima S, Gamain B, Morten N, Ndam N, Mordmüller B, Dicko A, Adechina R, Dossou A, Chinkhumba J, Kaur M, Houngnihin R, LaMontagne DS, Menendez C, Amani A, Mayani T, Hamel MJ, Carrió E, Sayeh A, Adegnika AA
articles.details: Workshop discussion of vaccine development for malaria in pregnancy (MiP), with 12.4 million pregnant women affected in 2023. PRIMVAC and PAMVAC candidates targeting VAR2CSA antigen are in development. Key recommendations include strengthening community engagement, defining efficacy endpoints, and reinforcing pharmacovigilance systems to support vaccine implementation.

articles.headline: Genetic classification of Plasmodium vivax recurrent infections
articles.title_original: Understanding Plasmodium vivax recurrent infections using an amplicon deep sequencing assay, identity-by-descent and model-based classification.
articles.authors: Rosado J, Han J, Obadia T, Munro J, Traore Z, Schoffer K, Brewster J, Bourke C, Vinetz JM, Taylor AR, White M, Bahlo M, Gamboa D, Mueller I, Ruybal-Pesántez S
articles.details: Study developed PvAmpSeq amplicon sequencing assay targeting 11 SNP-rich genomic regions to classify P. vivax recurrences in Solomon Islands trial and Peruvian cohort. In Solomon Islands, 50% of recurrences showed >50% identity-by-descent relatedness (probable relapses/recrudescences); in Peru, 68% showed <25% relatedness, enabling classification of reinfections and insights into transmission and treatment outcomes.

articles.headline: Long non-coding RNAs regulate P. falciparum gametocyte development
articles.title_original: Dynamic regulation of long non-coding RNAs across asexual and gametocyte development in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Grünebast J, Singhal R, Olson S, Bromley R, Kanatani S, Ko K, Dunning Hotopp J, Sinnis P, Llinás M, Serre D
articles.details: Study used long-read nanopore sequencing, ribosome profiling, and single-cell transcriptomics to characterize stage-specific lncRNAs in P. falciparum asexual and sexual stages. Most lncRNAs showed pronounced stage-specific expression with particular abundance in mature gametocytes. Differential expression between female and male gametocytes suggests roles in gametocytogenesis and transmission.

articles.headline: Vector density and species composition varies across Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Variance partitioning reveals contrasting random effect contributions to the density and species composition of malaria-transmitting mosquitoes in western Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Hui TJ, Epopa PS, Millogo AA, Yao FA, Koulmaga D, Noulin F, Diabate A, Burt A
articles.details: Longitudinal entomological survey (2012-2019) in four Sudano-Sahelian villages of Burkina Faso using pyrethroid spray catches. Between-house variation was the largest variance component for Anopheles gambiae s.l. density, while between-village variation dominated species composition (An. coluzzii vs An. gambiae: 76% of variance). Household size increased density; bed net presence had no effect on density or species composition.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum predominates across Niger's epidemiological zones
articles.title_original: Prevalence and spatial distribution of malaria-causing Plasmodium infections in the Niger Republic.
articles.authors: Sanoussi MK, Issa I, Mahaman Moustapha L, Idrissa Y, Hachimou I, Wilfried H, Seini HI, Abdoulaye D, Abou Y, Mounkaila B, Ibrahim ML
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 5,096 patients in Niger (Sept-Nov 2022) using RDTs, microscopy, and PCR. Among 304 PCR-tested samples, 55% (166/304) were Plasmodium-positive: P. falciparum 90% (149/166), P. ovale 5% (9/166), P. malariae 3% (5/166), P. vivax 2% (3/166). P. vivax was found only in hypo-endemic areas, informing national diagnostic and management strategy.

articles.headline: Protein-protein interactions as resistance-resilient antimalarial drug targets
articles.title_original: Targeting protein-protein interactions in Plasmodium: from asexual replication to sexual development.
articles.authors: Tang J, Tang W, Xie Y, Yang W, Luo X, Yu Y, He B, Liu C, Li Z
articles.details: Review synthesizing structural and functional advances in Plasmodium protein-protein interaction (PPI) networks across asexual and sexual stages using cryo-EM and advanced genetic tools. PPIs governing invasion, metabolism, and transmission are evolutionarily constrained with lower mutational potential than conventional drug targets. Summarizes PPI-targeted neutralizing antibodies, vaccine candidates, and small-molecule inhibitors in development.

articles.headline: Transfluthrin spatial emanator effective against pyrethroid-resistant vectors
articles.title_original: Experimental hut evaluation of mosquito shield, a transfluthrin spatial emanator, for control of wild pyrethroid resistant malaria vectors in Benin.
articles.authors: N'dombidjé B, Syme T, Ahoga J, Pearce B, Yadouleton A, Yamadjako M, Ngufor C
articles.details: Experimental hut trial in Benin evaluating Mosquito Shield™ (transfluthrin emanator) against pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae s.l. over two 32-day cycles. Provided 43% protective efficacy via human landing catches (95% CI: 24-57%; p<0.001), 64% personal protection (95% CI: 21.9-81.8%; p<0.001), with 49% induced mortality in An. gambiae s.l. and 96% mortality in Mansonia africana, supporting WHO prequalification.

articles.headline: Malaria RDT misdiagnosis is frequent in DRC
articles.title_original: Challenges in malaria diagnosis and treatment in Kinshasa Province, Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.authors: François-Zafka R, Westreich D, Banek K, Kashamuka MM, White SJ, Bala JA, Nkalani M, Kihuma G, Atibu J, Mahilu GE, Thwai KL, Kokolomami J, Emch M, Edwards J, Juliano JJ, Tshefu A, Parr JB
articles.details: Cohort study (2015-2022) of 2,269 participants in Kinshasa Province, DRC found high RDT misdiagnosis: one-year cumulative incidence of false-positive 23.1%, false-negative 17.8%; by seven years, 77.7% and 83.4% respectively, using PCR as gold standard. About 58% of RDT-negative participants received antimalarial treatment; fever significantly reduced adherence to test-and-treat guidelines.

articles.headline: Dual-active insecticide-treated nets reduced malaria by 30-40% in Ghana
articles.title_original: Reductions in malaria cases after deployment of dual-active ingredient insecticide-treated nets in Ghana-a Bayesian interrupted time series analysis.
articles.authors: Oppong SK, Owusu-Akrofi O, Atta-Obeng C, Coleman S, Amratia P, Symons TL, Alene KA, Peprah NY, Gething PW, Malm KL
articles.details: Analysis of confirmed malaria cases in Ghana's Western North Region (2018-2023) following 2021 distribution of Interceptor® G2 dual-active ITNs using Bayesian Poisson regression. Immediate 30% case reduction (OR 0.696; CrI 0.623-0.778), sustained at 30 months with 40% reduction (OR 0.594; CrI 0.492-0.718) despite pyrethroid resistance, supporting scale-up of next-generation nets.

articles.headline: Healthcare workers accept Artemisia annua tea despite evidence gaps in DRC
articles.title_original: Public Health Perspectives on Integrating Artemisia annua Tea for Uncomplicated Malaria Treatment: A Cross-Sectional Study of Perceptions and Acceptability Among Healthcare Workers in Kalima District, Maniema, DRC.
articles.authors: Munyangi Wa Nkola J, Akilimali Zalagile P, Lukuke Mbutshu H, Kabala Munyemo S, Ramazani Bin Eradi I, Camara A
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 337 healthcare workers in Kalima District, DRC found 81.0% clinical acceptability for Artemisia annua tea integration, with 82.8% perceiving it as effective. Rural residency was strongest predictor of acceptance (AOR = 6.847; p=0.003). However, 49.0% cited lack of clinical evidence as barrier and 91.4% demanded training on standardized dosage to mitigate resistance risks.

articles.headline: Research on Culex mosquitoes neglected in malaria-endemic regions
articles.title_original: Forgotten in the tropics: research on Culex mosquitoes is overshadowed in malaria and dengue-endemic regions.
articles.authors: Abdi AA, Almeida R, Harris T, Magalhaes T, Juarez JG, Hamer GL
articles.details: Systematic review of 1,389 mosquito studies (2010, 2020) found Culex publications significantly higher in non-endemic countries (64.5%) compared to malaria-endemic (30.2%) and dengue-endemic (34.2%) regions. Between 2010-2020, Aedes reporting increased while Anopheles decreased, reflecting research emphasis on dengue over malaria, with Culex-associated zoonotic pathogens neglected in endemic regions.

articles.headline: Malaria risk perception in Ethiopia clusters by socioeconomic factors
articles.title_original: Household malaria risk perception in Ethiopia: multilevel and spatial analysis using the 2022-2023 National Health Equity Survey.
articles.authors: Leul Y, Ararso D, Girmay AM, Zeru A, Taye G, Bahre W, Berhanu K, Lemlem A, Getachew T, Achamyeleh H, Shibiru Y, Alemu A, Tadele A, Wasie A, Molla G, Getachew F, Tadele W, Beshah SA, Tesfaye H, Zelalem D, Sori SA, Ayele S, Dagne T, Kabeta T, Belay YA, Terefe B, Fekadu L, Tiru G, Tollera G
articles.details: Analysis of 9,157 households in Ethiopian National Health Equity Survey (2022-2023) found 76.4% perceived malaria risk. Rural residence increased odds 54% (AOR = 1.54; 95% CI: 1.22-1.95); higher education increased odds 89% (AOR = 1.89; 95% CI: 1.36-2.63); older age decreased odds (AOR = 0.49; 95% CI: 0.35-0.67). Geographic clustering significant with 38% variance between woredas (Moran's I = 0.42, p<0.001).

articles.headline: Malaria and G6PD variation in conflict-affected Sudan zones
articles.title_original: Characterisation of malaria and glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency in conflict-affected zones of southern and eastern Sudan.
articles.authors: Jebreel WMA, Abdel Hamid MM, Mustafa SA, Awad F, Chamai M, Malpartida-Cardenas K, Boadu EA, Amoah LE, Rodriguez-Manzano J, Cunnington AJ, Mohamed AO
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 717 malaria-suspected patients in Kosti and Kassala, Sudan. Malaria confirmed by PCR in 62.6% (Kassala) and 52% (Kosti); Kassala showed mixed P. vivax (54%), P. falciparum (34%), and mixed infections (12%), while Kosti showed predominantly P. falciparum (97.7%). G6PD genotyping revealed regional variation, with G6PD B variant predominant and the African A− variant newly identified. No significant association between G6PD genotype and parasite density.

articles.headline: Acute kidney injury in malaria patients: prevalence and risk factors Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence of acute kidney injury and its associated factors among malaria patients admitted to public hospitals in the Ilu Aba Bora and Bedelle Zones of Oromia region, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Befkadu Z, Teferi S, Endalu T, Mitiku A, Barkesa B, Darge T
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study of 307 adult malaria patients in southwest Ethiopia (Feb-Apr 2024). AKI (defined as serum creatinine increase ≥0.3 mg/dL within 48 hours) occurred in 28.3% (87/307). Significant risk factors included male sex (AOR 1.98), older age (AOR 2.52–4.46), P. falciparum infection (AOR 2.73), mixed infection (AOR 3.30), and high parasitemia (AOR 2.71).

articles.headline: Neonicotinoid IRS raised malaria incidence in northern Ghana
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence rose following the introduction of neonicotinoid-based IRS in selected districts in northern Ghana: An observational analysis.
articles.authors: Coleman S, Atta-Obeng C, Nkolokosa C, Mohammed AG, Akrofi OO, Isaka I, Mohammed W, Peprah NY, Asiedu S, Dadzie SK, Dery DB, Tangena JA, Epstein A, Malm K
articles.details: Observational analysis of 22 IRS districts in northern Ghana (2015–2022) using interrupted time series model. After transition from organophosphate to neonicotinoid insecticide in 2018–2019, malaria incidence was 26% higher than expected under continued organophosphate use (IRR 1.26, 95% CI 1.07–2.08). Regional variation was substantial, with largest increases in Upper East region (IRR 4.26, 95% CI 2.17–8.50).

articles.headline: Computational analysis of P. falciparum merozoite invasion mechanics
articles.title_original: In silico analysis of the invasion mechanics and invasiveness of the Plasmodium falciparum merozoite.
articles.authors: Msosa C, Abdalrahman T, Franz T
articles.details: In silico study using finite element models and smoothed particle hydrodynamics to simulate merozoite invasion. Found that invasiveness decreases with erythrocyte sphericity and is greater when membrane damage occurs early in invasion. Minimum force required for merozoite invasion predicted at 11 pN. Results suggest nanoindentation assays could screen invasion-blocking antimalarial drugs.

articles.headline: Free healthcare and differentiated diagnosis reduce malaria burden Guinea
articles.title_original: Model-Based Assessment of the Effects of Selective Free Healthcare and Differentiated Diagnosis on Malaria Burden in the Republic of Guinea.
articles.authors: Diallo EM, Loum MA, Diallo OO, Gning LD, Ndiaye K, Ly AY, Bakhoum LN, Diallo A, Gerbaud L, Camara A
articles.details: Epidemiological modeling study using EMOD software to assess nine ecological archetypes in Guinea. Selective free healthcare for children under 5 combined with differentiated diagnosis increased ACT access index to 0.81, with incidence dropping below 50 cases per 1000 in low-transmission areas by 2028. Combined strategy projected to prevent 13,428 complicated pediatric malaria cases between 2025–2030 (95% CI 7768–19,088).

articles.headline: γδ T cell differentiation and metabolism in placental malaria
articles.title_original: Ex vivo cytokine responses and metabolic reprogramming are associated with γδ T cell differentiation in Plasmodium falciparum placental malaria.
articles.authors: Nana Mbianda CM, Kwanou Tchakounte BD, Bitye Zambo BM, Ntankheuh Tchinkou J, Atchombat Nyasse D, Fogang B, Argüello RJ, Ayong L, Megnekou R
articles.details: Case-control study of 50 women at delivery (21 with P. falciparum placental malaria, 29 without) using flow cytometry and immunometabolic profiling. γδ T cell phenotypes varied by blood compartment, with increased terminal differentiation in placental malaria. IL-8 and IL-33 cytokines were associated with enhanced TEMRA phenotype and reduced exhaustion markers (TIM-3, PD-1), while metabolic profiling revealed enhanced mitochondrial metabolism in terminally differentiated γδ T cells.

articles.headline: Chloroquine sensitivity restored in P. falciparum Gabon
articles.title_original: Reversal of Chloroquine Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum in Gabon: A Phenotype-Genotype Relationship over the Last 20 Years.
articles.authors: Inoue J, Rodi M, Bohissou FET, de Carvalho LP, Lohse K, Koehne E, Agnandji ST, Mombo-Ngoma G, Kremsner PG, Kreidenweiss A, Held J
articles.details: Twenty-year surveillance of P. falciparum ex vivo chloroquine susceptibility (IC50) and molecular markers (pfcrt, pfmdr1) in Lambaréné, Gabon. Median IC50 decreased from 113.8 nM (2004) to 2.1 nM (2024), with chloroquine-sensitive pfcrt CVMNK haplotype increasing from 3.6% (2009) to 98.2% (2024) and wild-type pfmdr1 N86 from 23.1% to 100%. Data demonstrate re-establishment of chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum after ceasing chloroquine use.

articles.headline: Malaria control performance partially achieved in Kinshasa 2020-23
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Performance Indicators for Malaria Control in Kinshasa from 2020 to 2023, the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.authors: Lutumbu BB, Mbanzulu KM, Kapour GK, Ndona MM, Zanga J, Mukendi JPK, Kayembe H, Mbangama A, Wumba R
articles.details: Cross-sectional evaluation of malaria control performance in 23 health zones in Kinshasa (2020–2023) using National Malaria Control Program data. Malaria incidence remained 30% with upward trend peaking in 2022. RDT diagnosis achieved 83.1% of suspected cases, and 95% of confirmed cases received antimalarial treatment, but LLIN distribution to pregnant women was inconsistent (45–88%) and no health zone achieved 100% RDT diagnosis.

articles.headline: Novel antimalarial compound targets Plasmodium plasmepsin X
articles.title_original: A novel 2-piperazino-pyrimidine compound exhibits asexual antimalarial activity by targeting Plasmodium falciparum plasmepsin X.
articles.authors: Hong J, Sakura T, Asada N, Fuchino K, Kuwana D, Kaki R, Ishii R, Kato T, Kita K, Matsumura K, Inaoka DK
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Identification of FPSA, a 2-piperazino-pyrimidine compound from Shionogi's library with antimalarial activity against P. falciparum. FPSA blocks merozoite egress by inhibiting plasmepsin X (PMX), with resistant parasites showing PMX I252V or E55G substitutions or gene duplications. Whole-genome sequencing and transcriptomic analysis confirmed PMX as the target, with potential for development as a novel antimalarial drug class.

articles.headline: Plasmodium NDH2 selectivity challenge for antimalarial drug design
articles.title_original: Comparative Structural and Functional Analysis of Plasmodium Type II Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide: Quinone Oxidoreductase and its Human Homologue, Apoptosis-Inducing Factor, Mitochondrion-associated 1 (AIF-M1).
articles.authors: Baye BC, Moses V, Ugbaja SC, Lobb KA
articles.details: Computational study of type II NADH:quinone oxidoreductase (NDH2) across five Plasmodium species using virtual screening against South African Natural Compounds Database. Three lead compounds (SANC00101, SANC00344, SANC00418) showed strong binding to PfNDH2 (−8.6 to −10.0 kcal/mol) but also stabilized human AIF-M1, identifying Phe482 as a critical off-target site. Study demonstrates the selectivity challenge in designing NDH2-targeted antimalarials.

articles.headline: Gut microbiota modulates cerebral malaria severity in mice
articles.title_original: Gut microbiota bidirectionally influences protection and severity in cerebral malaria in mice.
articles.authors: Taniguchi T, Miyauchi E, Kawabata-Iwakawa R, Nishiyama M, Umemiya-Shirafuji R, Toma H, Sasaki N, Hisaeda H, Tomita H, Ohno H, Kishimoto H, Suzuki H
articles.details: Experimental study in C57BL/6N mice showing antibiotic-induced microbiota alteration protected 80% of animals from experimental cerebral malaria (ECM) with reduced BBB disruption. 16S rRNA sequencing identified specific bacterial species that either protected or exacerbated ECM. Mono-colonization experiments confirmed individual bacterial species could independently modulate ECM severity independent of antibiotic effects.

articles.headline: Malaria misdiagnosis high in routine microscopy Northwest Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Unveiling the overlooked burden of malaria misdiagnosis using lamp-based re-evaluation of routine malaria diagnosis in health centers: implications for public health and clinical practice in Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Alula MT, Tegegne B, Setegn A, Yimer M, Tefera MW, Gedfie S, Ashagre A, Abere A, Tamir M, Mezgebu B, Abebe AD, Almaw A, Pillai DR, Lemma W, Eshetu T
articles.details: Health facility cross-sectional study of 384 malaria-suspected individuals comparing routine microscopy to LAMP in northwest Ethiopia (July–Aug 2024). 30% discordant results included 7.6% false-positives, 18.8% false-negatives, and 3.6% species misidentification (p<0.001). Findings highlight diagnostic accuracy challenges with routine microscopy in endemic settings despite its continued operational use.

articles.headline: Mixed Plasmodium infections in severe falciparum malaria increase mortality
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium ovale and Plasmodium malariae Mixed Infections and Associated Mortality in Children with Severe Falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Lima-Cooper G, Datta D, Bond C, Mellencamp KA, Namazzi R, Gupta M, Conroy AL, Opoka RO, John CC
articles.details: Cohort of 440 Ugandan children with severe P. falciparum malaria underwent nPCR testing for mixed Plasmodium infections. Triple infection with P. falciparum, P. ovale, and P. malariae (n=9) showed 33% mortality vs 7% for P. falciparum mono-infection (OR=7.0, 95% CI 1.8-28, p=0.005), and higher respiratory distress (67% vs 35%). Mixed P. ovale infections were detected in 22.8% of cases. Improved detection of non-falciparum species may identify patterns associated with adverse outcomes in severe malaria.

articles.headline: Machine learning classifier for Plasmodium falciparum var gene groups
articles.title_original: upsML: A high-accuracy machine learning classifier for predicting Plasmodium falciparum var gene upstream groups.
articles.authors: Pangilinan EA, Quenu M, Claessens A, Otto TD
articles.details: Developed upsML, a machine-learning classifier trained on 2,530 curated var genes to assign PfEMP1 var genes into upstream groups (upsA, upsB, upsC, upsE). Compared seven methods including SVM, random forests, XGBoost, and HMMER models. Achieved 83% accuracy for DBLα-tag sequences and 92% for full-length PfEMP1 sequences, significantly outperforming existing tools. Applied to global P. falciparum genomes, revealing higher frequency of internal var genes in Asia.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing maps drug resistance in UK cryptic malaria cases
articles.title_original: Rapid whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers provides insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.
articles.authors: Tan MKI, Billows N, Nolder D, Moss S, Phelan JE, Thorpe J, Edgeworth JL, Sutherland CJ, Chiodini PL, Campino S, Clark TG
articles.details: Sequenced 9 Plasmodium isolates from UK traveler blood samples using Illumina and Oxford Nanopore platforms, analyzed with Malaria-Profiler for drug resistance prediction and AI-based geographic origin inference using >15,000 reference genomes. Identified 4 cryptic P. falciparum cases with drug-resistance markers and predicted geographic origins. Also analyzed P. ovale and P. falciparum family clusters using identity-by-descent and multiplicity of infection approaches. Real-time WGS enabled rapid public health response to cryptic malaria cases in non-endemic setting.

articles.headline: Ivermectin for malaria control: cluster-randomized trial reply
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial. Reply.
articles.authors: Chaccour C, Rudd M
articles.details: Reply to proposed use of ivermectin as malaria control intervention in cluster-randomized trial design. No abstract provided; specific quantitative data unavailable.

articles.headline: Ivermectin for malaria control in cluster-randomized trial
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial.
articles.authors: Last A, Hayes R, Bradley J
articles.details: Cluster-randomized trial of ivermectin for malaria control intervention. No abstract provided; specific study design, population, and quantitative results unavailable.

articles.headline: Permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention: reply
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria in Children. Reply.
articles.authors: Boyce RM, Shook-Sa BE, Mulogo EM
articles.details: Reply to study of permethrin-treated baby wraps as malaria prevention intervention in children. Specific methodological details and results not available without abstract.

articles.headline: Permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention in children
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria in Children.
articles.authors: Javelle E, de Santi VP, Briolant S
articles.details: Study of permethrin-treated baby wraps as preventive intervention for childhood malaria. No abstract provided; specific study design, population, and quantitative results unavailable.

articles.headline: CRISPR mutagenesis reveals CLAG3 helix 44 in parasite nutrient uptake
articles.title_original: A CRISPR-Based Mutagenesis Strategy for Examining CLAG3 Helix 44 Contribution to Malaria Parasite Nutrient Uptake Channels.
articles.authors: Gonzalez-Chavez Z, Siddiqui MA, Ganesan S, Desai SA
articles.details: Developed two-step CRISPR transfection strategy to examine CLAG3 helix 44 (α-H44) in plasmodial surface anion channel (PSAC) formation. CLAG3 truncation without α-H44 phenocopied CLAG3 knockout; CLAG3 restoration with recodonized α-H44 fully restored PSAC activity. Two engineered mutants from saturation mutagenesis failed to expand in modified medium approximating in vivo nutrient availability. α-H44 appears critical for channel permeation and drug targeting.

articles.headline: Deep learning achieves 96% accuracy in malaria blood smear detection
articles.title_original: CT-Malaria Detection via Adaptive-Weighted Deep Learning Models.
articles.authors: Gasmi K, Krichen M, Alanazi A, Almenwer S, Almaghrabi S, Yahyaoui S
articles.details: Two-track architecture combined classical machine learning with end-to-end deep learning for malaria blood smear classification. Real-time augmentation, convolution-based feature extraction, calibrated classical classifiers, and multiple convolutional networks were trained, then paired ensembles with hybrid weight selection were constructed. Achieved 96.35% accuracy on test data with reduced false negatives for subtle parasites and false positives from staining artifacts. Optimal fusion of multiple models yields highest classification accuracy.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax clinical forms evoke distinct CD4+ T cell responses
articles.title_original: Different clinical forms of Plasmodium vivax infection result in unique T cell epitope-specific immune responses.
articles.authors: Costa CM, Almeida GG, Lindestam Arlehamn CS, Queiroz-Glauss CP, Gunderman VHG, Pereira MMM, Amaral LRD, Pereira DB, Tada MS, Martins-Filho OA, Gazzinelli RT, Antonelli LRDV
articles.details: Developed library of 310 P. vivax peptides (MPv310) to assess antigen-specific CD4+ T cell responses in symptomatic (before/after treatment) and asymptomatic infected individuals from endemic Brazilian Amazon. MPv310 stimulation increased CD154+ and CD69+CD154+ frequencies in symptomatic patients and IFN-γ+TNF+ frequencies post-treatment. Distinct antigen-specific activation patterns observed between symptomatic and asymptomatic individuals. MPv310 identified as potential tool to assess immune mechanisms of different P. vivax clinical presentations.

articles.headline: Congenital and neonatal malaria in Ethiopia: epidemiology review
articles.title_original: Congenital and Neonatal Malaria in Ethiopia: A Narrative Review.
articles.authors: Leake Y, Hagos HH, Reta BK
articles.details: Narrative review of congenital and neonatal malaria in Ethiopia synthesizing literature on epidemiology, clinical presentation, diagnosis, management, and prevention. Identified 10 studies reporting 46 total cases: P. falciparum (35 cases), P. vivax (8 cases), mixed infections (3 cases); 40 congenital malaria and 6 neonatal malaria. Most reports were case reports or case series. Congenital and neonatal malaria remains under-recognized in Ethiopia despite high national malaria burden.

articles.headline: G6PD deficiency protects against Plasmodium knowlesi in Malaysian Borneo
articles.title_original: G6PD variants but not Filipino beta-thalassemia are associated with reduced risk of Plasmodium knowlesi infection in Sabah, Malaysia.
articles.authors: Nuin NA, Rajahram GS, Barber BE, Anstey NM, Grigg MJ, Chua TH
articles.details: Retrospective case-control study in Sabah, Malaysia comparing 106 PCR-confirmed P. knowlesi cases with 89 controls. Genotyped 8 major G6PD variants and Filipino β-thalassemia deletions. G6PD deficiency present in 3.8% of cases vs 12.4% of controls (OR=0.28, 95% CI 0.09-0.91, p=0.034); Viangchan variant (871G>A) predominant at 71%. Filipino β-thalassemia showed no association. G6PD variants confer substantial protection against P. knowlesi susceptibility.

articles.headline: E3 ligase complex controls Plasmodium falciparum gametocyte development
articles.title_original: GID/CTLH E3 ligase complex control cell fate programs for sexual development of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Marapana DS, Lopaticki S, Balan B, Reaksudsan K, Cobbold SA, Geoghegan ND, Khurana S, Scally SW, Hickey P, Jayakrishnan N, Vaibhav V, Spall S, Yousef JM, Dagley LF, Goodman CD, Cozijnsen A, McFadden GI, Rogers KL, Jex A, Komander D, McCarthy JS, Tonkin CJ, Dixon MWA, Cowman AF
articles.details: Study examined the PfGID E3 ubiquitin ligase complex regulating gametocyte cell fate programs through targeted ubiquitination. Deletion of PfGID complex components arrested gametocyte development and blocked transmission to mosquitoes. PfGID regulated two substrates—GD1 and PfDPL—which control expression of male-specific proteins essential for gametogenesis and translational repression of critical gametocyte transcripts.

articles.headline: Vector survey identifies Anopheles funestus driving malaria transmission in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of malaria in Tanzania: Baseline surveillance of vectors that drive persistent malaria transmission in regions with varying transmission intensities.
articles.authors: Derua YA, Batengana BM, Francis F, Mandara CI, Madebe RA, Seth MD, Challe DP, Ishengoma DS
articles.details: Cross-sectional study in five Tanzanian districts (Aug-Oct 2023) collected 19,898 mosquitoes. An. funestus group represented 44.3% of all mosquitoes and 95.3% of malaria vectors, with An. funestus s.s. predominant (96.3%). Of 1,799 vectors tested, 9 (0.5%) were infected with P. falciparum. Annual entomological inoculation rates were 242.6, 66.5, and 13.0 infectious bites per person per year in Kyerwa, Nyasa, and Muheza respectively.

articles.headline: Low integration of malaria services in antenatal care in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Integrated malaria service delivery and its determinants among pregnant women in Ethiopia: Multi level analysis of 2021/22 National Service Provision Assessment Survey.
articles.authors: Bogale KA, Alemu K, Muchie KF, Asemahagn MA, Engedaw HA, Yenesew MA
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 2021/22 National Service Provision Assessment Survey including 4,273 pregnant women across 662 health facilities. Only 7.9% attended ANC visits where all integrated malaria service components were delivered concurrently. Factors associated with integrated service delivery included: two prior pregnancies (AOR=1.67), ≥3 ANC visits (AOR=1.58), ITN distribution (AOR=2.81), malaria-trained providers (AOR=4.24), and rural facility attendance (AOR=2.73).

articles.headline: Chromatin changes regulate var2csa expression in placental malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa expression and the placental-binding phenotype in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Lenz T, Sirel M, Hoppe H, Shafeeq S, Le Roch KG, Ribacke U
articles.details: Study established phenotypically homogenous P. falciparum populations expressing VAR2CSA that exclusively transcribed var2csa while other var genes remained silenced. Transcriptional activation was strongly associated with depletion of repressive H3K9me3 marks and repositioning of var2csa relative to heterochromatic telomeric clusters. 5-methylcytosine was present in all var genes but independent of transcriptional repression.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax Malaria.
articles.authors: Menkin-Smith L, Koval K, Samandari T
articles.details: No abstract provided; title only.

articles.headline: First documented placental malaria pigmentation in 19th-century Scotland
articles.title_original: Placental malaria in nineteenth-century Scotland.
articles.authors: Brabin BJ
articles.details: Historical review of observations by Scottish obstetrician James Young Simpson (1811-1870) of an archived placental sample at 4 months gestation, likely the first documented case of malaria pigmentation in the placenta. Simpson's 1835 inaugural lecture as President of the Royal Edinburgh Medical Society presented findings on placental pathology and malaria infection in pregnancy.

articles.headline: Drug-resistance genetic backgrounds in Gambian P. falciparum alter antimalarial susceptibility
articles.title_original: Chloroquine resistance transporter drives divergent multi-locus drug resistance genetic backgrounds and susceptibility in Gambian Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Drammeh NF, Bojang F, Nganyewo NN, Jawara AS, Manneh B, Correa S, Amambua-Ngwa BN, Mbye H, Oriero EC, Sutherland CJ, D'Alessandro U, Amambua-Ngwa A
articles.details: Amplicon sequencing of 364 P. falciparum isolates determined resistance genotypes at pfcrt, pfmdr1, pfdhfr, pfdhps, and pfkelch13. In vitro IC50s determined for 68 isolates to six antimalarials. Two main multi-locus drug-resistance backgrounds driven by pfcrt haplotypes: CVIET (62%) showed highest chloroquine IC50s; CVMNK showed reduced sensitivity to mefloquine and dihydroartemisinin. No validated pfkelch13 mutations detected. Artemisinin-based combination therapies remain effective in The Gambia.

articles.headline: Acridone T111 shows activity against all Plasmodium life stages
articles.title_original: Potent acridone antimalarial against all three life stages of Plasmodium.
articles.authors: Kancharla P, Dodean RA, Li Y, Zhang X, Kelly S, Charlton J, Binauhan AJ, Navarrete K, Garcia L, Mu J, Lu J, Caridha D, Madejczyk MS, Jin X, Dennis WE, Kudyba K, McEnearney S, Lee PJ, Blount C, DeLuca J, Vuong C, Pannone K, Dinh HT, Mdaki K, Leed S, Martin ML, Pybus BS, Chin GC, Chim-Ong A, Cui L, Qahash T, Shoue DA, Ferdig MT, Mockenhaupt FP, van Loon W, Ndoli JM, Kudyba H, Vega-Rodriguez J, Okitwi M, Kreutzfeld O, Docan C, Green C, Liu Y, Agulay A, Mirsalis J, Farrell SN, Probst AS, Nilsen A, Alday PH, Stone Doggett J, McFadden GI, Goodman CD, Catteruccia F, Rosenthal PJ, Riscoe MK, Cooper RA, Roth A, Kelly JX
articles.details: Lead candidate T111 demonstrated potent in vitro activity against cultured parasites, ex vivo activity against clinical isolates, and oral single-dose cure in asexual blood-stage rodent models. T111 inhibited sexual blood-stage parasites, showed activity against relapsing parasites in non-human primate liver, and prevented parasite development in mosquitoes. Mechanism involves mitochondrial electron transport chain inhibition with favorable safety profile in rats (no hemolytic, genotoxic, or cardiotoxic concerns).

articles.headline: Point-of-care biosensors advance malaria diagnostics in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Potential biomarkers and their recent applications ofr point-of-care biosensors for the diagnosis of malaria.
articles.authors: Karadurmus L, Cetinkaya A, Kocabay P, Jaballah MB, Baldrich E, Ozkan SA
articles.details: Review of biosensor engineering advances (2022-2025) for malaria point-of-care diagnostics covering electrochemical, optical, and piezoelectric formats. Examines signal transduction mechanisms, analytical trade-offs, and microfluidic integration strategies for real-world deployability. Addresses challenges including atypical/asymptomatic malaria, climate change, healthcare infrastructure limitations, and antimalarial/insecticide resistance. Summarizes biomarkers and prominent POCT biosensor examples to support development of portable, affordable malaria detection technologies.

articles.headline: Multiplex isothermal assay detects P. falciparum in urine for pregnancy
articles.title_original: Multiplexed Isothermal Amplification Assay for the Detection of Plasmodium falciparum and Trichomonas vaginalis in Urine.
articles.authors: Dupaty J, Shiluli C, Schaus B, Lolabattu SR, Gitaka J, Klapperich C
articles.details: Study developed multiplexed isothermal nucleic acid amplification assay (iso-IMRS) for simultaneous detection of P. falciparum and T. vaginalis in urine. Assay detected both pathogens at 100 copies/μL in processed human urine. Addresses placental sequestration of P. falciparum in pregnancy that limits microscopy and rapid diagnostic test detection. Testing in Kenya clinics demonstrated potential for low-cost, non-invasive molecular diagnostic with turnaround time within a prenatal visit.

articles.headline: Serosurveillance reveals age-related malaria exposure patterns in urban Guyana
articles.title_original: Hotspot-based integrated serosurveillance of neglected tropical diseases, malaria, and vaccine-preventable diseases in urban Guyana.
articles.authors: Niles-Robin RA, Sampson A, Valz O, Scott O, Dickson KE, Escalada R, Nicholson D, Woolford J, Ade MP, Carvalho Scholte R, Luciañez A, Bravo P, Rey-Benito G, Ibarra-Ozcariz S, Morice A, Saboyá-Díaz MI
articles.details: Hotspot-based integrated serosurveillance in four urban Guyana regions surveyed 300 individuals per site (total 1,200) using multiplex bead assay to detect antigens including P. falciparum (pfMSP1-19) and P. vivax (pvMSP1-19). Seropositivity to both P. falciparum and P. vivax was higher among adults. Study simultaneously assessed exposure to neglected tropical diseases and seroprotection against vaccine-preventable diseases, providing insights for programmatic action.

articles.headline: Protocol developing core outcome set for severe malaria treatment trials
articles.title_original: Synthesis and Standardization of Outcomes in Severe Malaria Treatment Trials: Protocol for the Development of a Core Outcome Set (the COSSMaT Study).
articles.authors: Asamoah GD, Love SB, Gibb DM, Maitland K, George EC
articles.details: Protocol aims to develop the first core outcome set (COS) for severe malaria treatment trials following COMET Initiative guidance. Updated systematic review identified five eligible randomized controlled trials conducted 2020-2024 in Africa and Asia. Qualitative interviews with 26 parent/caregiver participants identified important outcomes. Two-round Delphi survey (target 60-80 participants) and consensus meeting planned to finalize COS; completion expected September 2026.

articles.headline: IRS and SMC reduce minor Plasmodium species in Ghana, but rebound when IRS stops
articles.title_original: The dynamics of asymptomatic Plasmodium spp. infections following 10 years of malaria control interventions in Northern Sahelian Ghana.
articles.authors: Rios-Teran CA, Tiedje KE, Bangre O, Deed SL, Argyropoulos DC, Koram KA, Oduro AR, Ansah PO, Day KP
articles.details: Interrupted time-series observational study with ~2,000 participants surveyed annually 2012–2022 in Bongo District, Ghana, assessing impact of indoor residual spraying (IRS) and seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) plus long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) on Plasmodium malariae, P. ovale, and P. vivax. Following IRS in 2015, P. malariae prevalence fell from 13.7% to 1.4% and P. ovale from 5.7% to 0.4%. When IRS was discontinued and SMC introduced in 2017, P. malariae rebounded to 2.9% (2-fold increase) and P. ovale to 4.0% (10-fold increase); among children <5 years targeted by SMC, no rebound occurred. By 2022, P. malariae had further increased to 5.8% while P. ovale declined to 1.3%.

articles.headline: P. falciparum protein PfRESA3 hijacks host cytoskeleton as co-chaperone
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum ring-infected erythrocyte surface antigen 3 (PfRESA3) is a cytoskeleton-interacting protein and potential co-chaperone.
articles.authors: Mallick NR, Sahu W, Ojha DK, Mund S, Das A, Pati AK, Elangovan S, Reddy KS
articles.details: Mechanistic study identifying PfRESA3, a dense granule merozoite protein of Plasmodium falciparum, as exported early to the inner erythrocyte membrane where it anchors to the host cytoskeleton and recruits human chaperone HsHSPA8. Recombinant PfRESA3 stimulated ATPase activity of HsHSPA8 and enhanced protein refolding in vitro, though truncated constructs did not significantly enhance refolding activity. The findings suggest PfRESA3 functions as a potential co-chaperone modulating host chaperone activity to support parasite survival.

articles.headline: Review of immune responses to blood-stage malaria
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular interactions underlying the peripheral immune responses to blood-stage malaria infections.
articles.authors: Tebben K, Asawa R, Serre D, Lyke KE
articles.details: A review article examining the cellular and molecular basis of human immune responses to blood-stage Plasmodium parasites and parasite immune evasion mechanisms that allow escape from host defenses.

articles.headline: Military is 18% of Thailand malaria cases; coordination with health ministry improving
articles.title_original: Combatting malaria together: key findings from a rapid formative assessment on Royal Thai Army malaria practices and military-civilian collaboration in Sisaket Province, Thailand.
articles.authors: Dantzer E, Tabprasit S, Suwannarong K, Jongsakul K, Kana K, Dharmarak P, Feldman M, Sudathip P, Chaiwan J, Yokani W, Kramyoo M, Thammasutti K, Cotter C
articles.details: Mixed-methods formative assessment of Royal Thai Army (RTA) malaria risk in Sisaket Province, Thailand, combining 2016–2020 retrospective case analysis (432 military cases) with 2021–2022 qualitative data from 116 respondents across military and civilian health system levels. Military cases constituted 18% of the province's 2,425 total cases; all were male with median age 28 years, with 41% indigenous and 40% imported. Most (96%) were diagnosed and treated at Ministry of Public Health facilities. RTA-MoPH coordination occurred at multiple levels but had gaps in patient follow-up, border control, and vector control efforts.

articles.headline: White rice substrate optimizes Beauveria bassiana for Anopheles control
articles.title_original: Effect of solid substrates on the production and germination of Beauveria bassiana (Balsamo) Vuillemin, and their infectivity against the malaria vector, Anopheles stephensi (Diptera: Culicidae).
articles.authors: Siddaramegowda R, Chalageri VH, Eapen A
articles.details: Experimental study testing 11 solid substrates for production of entomopathogenic fungus Beauveria bassiana PDBC-Bb5a and efficacy against Anopheles stephensi. White rice produced the highest conidial load (6.8 × 10^5 CFU g^-1) with 37% germination at 8 hours and 54.3% at 16 hours. In WHO cone bioassay tests, white rice-derived conidia achieved 90% and 86.25% mortality of An. stephensi on cement and mud panels, respectively, with 5–7 day median survival times. Barley substrate achieved only 51.25% and 47.50% mortality with 10-day median survival times.

articles.headline: Only 50% of pregnant women in Madagascar complete adequate IPTp-SP doses
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria during pregnancy and coverage of intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine in urban Amboasary-Sud, Southern Madagascar.
articles.authors: Zafindraibe NJ, Razafindrakoto AC, Zemey CB, Rakotozandrindrainy R, Rasamindrakotroka A, Razanakolona LRS
