articles.title_original: Lagged effect of temperature and rainfall on malaria incidence in Colombia (2013-2023): An approach with Bayesian spatiotemporal adjustment.
articles.authors: Gutiérrez JD
articles.details: A Bayesian spatiotemporal analysis of 970 Colombian municipalities (2013-2023) using distributed lag non-linear models examined associations between weekly temperature, rainfall, and malaria incidence. Malaria risk increased non-linearly with temperature, peaking around 28°C (exposure of minimum risk [EMR] at 16.43°C) with significant effects at lags of 0-6 weeks, while lower weekly rainfall (EMR 0.85 mm) was associated with higher risk. These climate thresholds provide actionable evidence for early warning systems supporting malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Hexanoic acid and 3-methyl-1-butanol blend attracts An. stephensi
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of synthetic attractants against an important malaria vector, Anopheles stephensi (Diptera: Culicidae) mosquitoes in laboratory conditions.
articles.authors: Hosseini SZ, Basseri HR, Zaim M, Azam K, Khalaj MR, Salari E
articles.details: In laboratory olfactometer experiments with non-blood-fed female Anopheles stephensi, hexanoic acid showed the highest attractancy (P<0.05) when combined with CO2, followed by 3-methyl-1-butanol and L-lactic acid. A blend of 3-methyl-1-butanol and hexanoic acid at optimal dilutions significantly attracted mosquitoes (P<0.05) and represents the most promising odor-baited trap candidate, though further field evaluation is required.

articles.headline: Microscopy and RDT miss 31.5% and 18.9% of malaria
articles.title_original: Malaria misdiagnosis among febrile patients in Senegal: implications for the underestimation of Plasmodium species diversity and malaria burden.
articles.authors: Sambe BS, Diagne A, Diatta AS, Diaw SOM, Sarr I, Diatta HAM, Sané R, Guigma PYB, Senghor B, Diouf B, Sembene PM, Vigan-Womas I, Niang M
articles.details: Among 948 febrile patients in three Senegal regions, PCR detected malaria in 75.29%, with 70.25% involving non-falciparum species (P. ovale, P. vivax, P. malariae). Microscopy missed 31.5% of PCR-positive infections while RDT missed 18.92%. Microscopy and RDT each misdiagnosed half of non-falciparum mono-infections as P. falciparum, and microscopy failed to detect many P. falciparum infections despite parasitemia above its theoretical detection limit.

articles.headline: Nanoparticles enhance quinine efficacy and safety against drug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Fabrication and evaluation of quinine-loaded chitosan-polycaprolactone core-shell polymeric nanoparticles for the treatment of malaria: an in vitro and in silico study.
articles.authors: Amos Y, Montenegro ED, Rubaka C, Swai H
articles.details: Chitosan-functionalized poly(ε-caprolactone) core-shell nanoparticles (312.9±11.7 nm diameter, 81.8±6.9% encapsulation efficiency) loaded with quinine significantly improved the therapeutic profile against chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum FCR3 strain in vitro. Nanoparticles increased cytotoxicity IC50 in Vero cells from 131.93 to 345.93 µg/mL and lowered antiplasmodial IC50 from 130.12 to 32.56 ng/mL, resulting in approximately 10-fold increase in selectivity index and 2-fold enhancement of quinine penetration into infected erythrocytes.

articles.headline: PfEF-1α vaccine candidate elicits robust T-cell and antibody responses
articles.title_original: Immunological response of using emulsifying elongation factor 1 alpha of Plasmodium falciparum-based vaccines in complete Freund's adjuvant.
articles.authors: Hong H, Eom TH, Lee E, Lee E, Lee HY, Won S, Lee DS, Kim MI, Park H, Yeo SJ
articles.details: Immunization with PfEF-1α (elongation factor 1 alpha) plus complete Freund's adjuvant increased survival by 50% in mice, with half of survivors showing no parasitemia compared to all BSA-controls showing patent infections. The vaccine elicited dominant CD4+IFN-γ+ T-cell responses and 16-fold higher antigen-specific IgG endpoint titers than control, with elevated IgG1 and IgG2c titers. The identified PfEF-1α epitope (410-FAIRDMRQTI-419) induced IFN-γ secretion in ex vivo T-cell studies.

articles.headline: SORT IT training strengthens malaria research capacity in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Strengthening operational research to complement Global Fund grants for HIV, TB and malaria in Sierra Leone.
articles.authors: Kenneh S, Kamau EM, Thorson A, Halleux C, Ameh G
articles.details: This article describes a SORT IT partnership-based capacity-strengthening initiative in Sierra Leone involving 19 institutions aimed at making countries 'data rich, information rich and action rich' to improve health care delivery for HIV, TB, and malaria. As a training program overview, no specific quantitative research results are provided.

articles.headline: P. vivax rosetting transcriptomes reveal membrane protein signatures
articles.title_original: Transcriptomics of Plasmodium vivax rosetting.
articles.authors: Bourgard C, Ferraboli JW, Lopes SCP, Lacerda MVG, Sunnerhagen P, Albrecht L, Costa FTM
articles.details: Comparative transcriptomic analysis of 10 P. vivax field isolates from the Brazilian Amazon with high versus low rosetting capacity identified 492 differentially expressed genes, with 172 (34.96%) conserved genes of unknown function. Key findings emphasized differential expression of membrane-associated proteins including 6-cysteine family proteins (TRAG16, 41K blood stage antigen, MSP7-like) and PHIST proteins involved in parasite adhesion.

articles.headline: WWII P. vivax relapses exceeded acute treatment as major challenge
articles.title_original: Post-infection symptoms in U.S. soldiers with malaria during the Second World War: major limitation to return to duty.
articles.authors: Shanks GD
articles.details: During the Second World War, over 100,000 malaria cases were reported among U.S. military in the Pacific in 1943. While acute treatment mortality was low, relapsing P. vivax malaria proved the major obstacle to returning soldiers to duty, with soldiers at Guadalcanal and New Guinea experiencing more than 10 relapses, and approximately 1% repatriated for 'chronic malaria.

articles.headline: Marine algae silver nanoparticles target malaria via metabolic modulation
articles.title_original: Green synthesis of silver nanoparticles using Padina tetrastromatica against malaria and deciphering the mechanism through machine learning-driven metabolomics and network pharmacology.
articles.authors: Yaladanda N, Budige YK, Veeragoni D, Josyula JVN, Rodda R, Singh V, Sripadi P, Mutheneni SR
articles.details: Silver nanoparticles synthesized from brown algae Padina tetrastromatica demonstrated potent antiplasmodial activity superior to chemical nanoparticles in in vitro and in vivo assays. Untargeted metabolomics revealed Ag-PT treatment restored malaria-disrupted fatty acid, arginine, and arachidonic acid metabolism, elevating pro-resolving mediators (DHA, 14-HDHA, 18-HEPE). Network pharmacology identified COX-2 as a key hub with strong binding of the phytochemical eriodictyol.

articles.headline: RALP1 essential for P. falciparum merozoite maturation and invasion
articles.title_original: RALP1 is essential for schizont maturation and erythrocyte invasion in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Wu J, Zhang Z, Pang J, Yang W, Ngim C, Li P, Ye J, Tian B, Cheng X, Wang F, Zhang Q, Shang X
articles.details: A conditional CRISPR-based knockdown of RALP1 (rhoptry neck-associated leucine zipper protein) in P. falciparum demonstrated its essential role in parasite development. RALP1 knockdown impaired schizont maturation, reduced merozoite numbers, and decreased erythrocyte invasion efficiency. RNA-seq revealed widespread downregulation of invasion and host-parasite interaction genes, with structural analyses identifying RALP1 as an epitope-rich candidate for vaccine or therapeutic targeting.

articles.headline: Mali flooding-malaria outbreak: SMC and mobile teams reduce morbidity by 50%
articles.title_original: Extreme rainfall, flooding and malaria in the Sahara: outbreak analysis in Kidal, Mali 2024.
articles.authors: Dolo AM, Cissoko M, Teme A, Keita K, Sanogo M, Traoré CAT, Magassa M, Koné A, Roy FA, Sagara I, Gaudart J
articles.details: A historical cohort study of malaria outbreak in Kidal, Mali (May 2024–February 2025) following extreme rainfall examined 28 weeks of epidemic data with 1,014 cases at peak (week 39), yielding overall incidence of 1.688 cases per 1000 person-weeks, highest in children under five. Interventions included seasonal malaria chemoprevention extended to children under 15 years and mobile health teams, which reduced morbidity with standardized incidence ratios of 0.50 (95% CI 0.33–0.75) and 0.48 (95% CI 0.28–0.82), respectively.

articles.headline: Guava sugar sources most attractive to Anopheles gambiae in Malawi
articles.title_original: Exploring the importance and preference of sugar feeding behaviour of malaria vectors in sugar plantations of southern Malawi.
articles.authors: Zembere K, Coleman S, Chirombo J, Mbewe R, Tangena JA
articles.details: An observational study in Chikwawa, southern Malawi evaluated sugar-feeding preferences of Anopheles gambiae to inform targeted sugar-bait vector control strategies. Over 40% of collected Anopheles (n=74 of 187) fed on natural sugar sources. In controlled olfactory choice experiments with 810 lab-reared An. gambiae s.l., guava was most attractive (1.97-fold higher than sugarcane reference, 95% CI 1.49–2.62, p<0.001), followed by banana (1.68-fold, p<0.001), mango (1.49-fold, p=0.008), and melon (1.45-fold, p=0.014).

articles.headline: SC83288 targets PfDNMT2; resistance via PfATP6 carries fitness cost
articles.title_original: Mechanisms of PfDNMT2 inhibition and PfATP6-mediated resistance to the antimalarial candidate SC83288 in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Sanchez CP, Duffey M, Celada RV, Kucharski M, Hoarau M, Saeyang T, Kamchonwongpaisan S, Sohraby F, de Buhr S, Nunes Alves A, Lucky AB, Miao J, Regnault C, Dziekan JM, Bozdech Z, Barrett MP, Lanzer M
articles.details: SC83288, a novel antimalarial compound, demonstrated high efficacy against P. falciparum blood-stage development in vivo and in vitro, including against artemisinin-resistant strains. The drug inhibits DNA replication and arrests karyokinesis by targeting PfDNMT2 (DNA/tRNA methyltransferase), disrupting epigenetic regulation and S-adenosylmethionine metabolism. Resistance emerges through PfATP6 (SERCA-type Ca²⁺ ATPase) mutations that enable transport away from nuclear targets, though this carries a substantial fitness cost limiting spread potential.

articles.headline: Encapsulated sea cucumber adjunct reduces malaria-related organ damage in mice
articles.title_original: Hepatorenal protective role of encapsulated Holothuria atra in a mice model of Plasmodium berghei ANKA inoculation.
articles.authors: Utami PD, Setianingsih H, Sari DRT
articles.details: A preclinical study in 48 BALB/c mice evaluated hepatorenal protective effects of encapsulated Holothuria atra combined with chloroquine against P. berghei ANKA-induced malaria. Chloroquine monotherapy resulted in the highest renal damage scores, while combination therapy—particularly with a 4:1 gum Arabic/soy protein isolate ratio at 100 mg/kg—significantly reduced both renal and hepatic histopathological damage scores compared to chloroquine alone, approaching negative-control levels.

articles.headline: Unified DFS20 culture medium enables comparative antimalarial drug screening
articles.title_original: A Standardized Culture Medium for Comparative Drug Efficacy Evaluation Across Plasmodium and Babesia Species.
articles.authors: Vydyam P, Ben Mamoun C
articles.details: A standardized culture medium (DFS20: DMEM/F12 with 20% fetal bovine serum) was developed to support comparable asexual growth of multiple P. falciparum strains (3D7, Dd2, HB3, V1/S) and Babesia species, eliminating reliance on species-specific media. This platform enables harmonized cross-species growth inhibition assays; when applied to screening dihydrotriazines and biguanides targeting DHFR-TS, it identified potent antifolate candidates with broad-spectrum activity against both Babesia and Plasmodium.

articles.headline: R21 vaccine highly cost-effective in Zambia; RTS,S borderline at current prices
articles.title_original: Provincial stratification and equity-weighted cost-effectiveness of malaria vaccines in Zambia under climate variability.
articles.authors: Phiri AE
articles.details: A province-stratified SEIR transmission model calibrated to Zambia surveillance data (2024–2028) evaluated cost-effectiveness of RTS,S and R21 malaria vaccines. RTS,S was estimated to avert 9.80 × 10⁵ cases (95% UI 8.88×10⁵–1.08×10⁶) and approximately 1960 deaths over 5 years, while R21 averted 1.09 × 10⁶ cases (95% UI 9.91×10⁵–1.21×10⁶) and approximately 2188 deaths. RTS,S was borderline cost-effective at current prices while R21 was highly cost-effective and potentially dominant, with impact varying across provinces based on baseline transmission and climate-driven risk.

articles.headline: Sudan vaccine awareness low at 38% despite high malaria knowledge
articles.title_original: Assessment of parental knowledge, attitude, and acceptance toward malaria vaccine for children under five years in Sudan, 2025.
articles.authors: Hassan AAE, Ali NAH, Elshareef MAA, Sidahmed TSM, Elobied HES, Ali AEO, Salih DAD, Al Rahma AMM, Abdallha AWA, Hussain AMS, Hussain AMS, Mohammed EYM, Ahmed KAH, Awadallah SYM
articles.details: A cross-sectional survey of 706 parents in high malaria-burden Sudan regions (April–June 2025) assessed knowledge and acceptance of malaria vaccination for children under five. While 98.2% were aware of malaria, only 38.0% had heard of the malaria vaccine. Vaccine awareness was significantly higher among fathers versus mothers (19.0% vs 10.7%, p=0.002) and associated with higher education, residence in Kassala (30.6%), public sector employment (13.1%), and higher monthly income over 500K SDG (26.9%, p=0.010).

articles.headline: Splenic rupture: rare severe malaria complication
articles.title_original: Malaria-associated splenic haemorrhage requiring emergency splenectomy: a rare complication managed in a resource-constrained setting.
articles.authors: Kumar A, Agatrao BA, Prakash A, Pogatyanatti B, Uppaluri D, Krishna VS, Farook AA, Sharma A, Patel A
articles.details: Two case reports of mixed Plasmodium falciparum and P. vivax malaria in South Sudan presented with spontaneous splenic rupture causing hemodynamic instability, requiring emergency splenectomy. One patient survived with surgical intervention and supportive care; the other died despite emergency management.

articles.headline: Plasmodium tubulin adaptations shape drug susceptibility
articles.title_original: Adaptations in Plasmodium tubulin determine distinct microtubule architectures, mechanics and drug susceptibility.
articles.authors: Bangera M, Wu J, Beckett D, Fachet D, Ferreira JL, Voth GA, Reber S, Moores CA
articles.details: Cryo-electron microscopy study of P. falciparum tubulin revealed parasite-specific amino acid sequences modify tubulin dimer structure, altering microtubule mechanics and drug susceptibility. Parasite microtubules exhibited smaller but stronger lateral contacts and stiffer lattice properties compared to mammalian brain microtubules.

articles.headline: QSAR models identify novel HDAC inhibitors for malaria
articles.title_original: Improved quantitative structure-activity relationship (QSAR) models to predict the activity of hydroxamic acids as HDAC inhibitors against malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Dawood WA, Macdonald JR, Skinner-Adams TS, Hesping E, Gorse AJP, Andrews KT
articles.details: Quantitative structure-activity relationship models developed from 572 hydroxamic acid compounds predicted P. falciparum HDAC inhibitor activity with high accuracy (AUC 0.953–0.984). In vitro validation of 60 compounds from virtual screening identified three potent antiplasmodial hits with IC50 values of 8, 14, and 80 nM, two confirming P. falciparum histone H4 hyperacetylation.

articles.headline: Deep learning predicts CRISPR gene drive malaria impact
articles.title_original: A simulation-based deep learning framework for spatially explicit malaria modeling of CRISPR suppression gene drive mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Allegretti YH, Sun W, Champer J
articles.details: Deep learning framework modeling spatially explicit CRISPR suppression gene drives in Anopheles mosquitoes predicted that reducing embryo and functional resistance while increasing drive conversion efficiency would suppress mosquito populations and malaria transmission. The parameter space for malaria elimination was substantially larger than for mosquito elimination, suggesting incomplete drives may still reduce transmission.

articles.headline: P. vivax genetic diversity declines in eastern Cambodia
articles.title_original: Temporal trends in Plasmodium vivax diversity in eastern Cambodia evidence declining transmission.
articles.authors: Eam R, Hoon KS, Sutanto E, Rai A, Trimarsanto H, Rumaseb A, Thin S, Hor S, Chhorvann C, Bunkea T, Price RN, Popovici J, Auburn S
articles.details: Population genetic analysis of 182 P. vivax isolates from eastern Cambodia using microhaplotype genotyping (vivaxGEN panel) found polyclonal infection prevalence decreased from 22–48% (2014–2019) to 5% in 2023 (p<0.05). In 2023, 81.4% of isolates showed identity-by-descent >0.95 compared to 8.9–10.8% in earlier years, indicating transmission reduction and population inbreeding.

articles.headline: Yellow fever and malaria transmission in Atlantic Forest
articles.title_original: When yellow fever silenced the forest: Ecological collapse and reduced malaria transmission in the Rio de Janeiro Atlantic Forest, Brazil.
articles.authors: de Pina-Costa A, Lourenço-de-Oliveira R, Brasil P, Daniel-Ribeiro CT
articles.details: No abstract available; article addresses ecological impacts of yellow fever on malaria transmission dynamics in the Rio de Janeiro Atlantic Forest, Brazil.

articles.headline: Correction to Yanomami malaria and gold mining study
articles.title_original: Correction: 'Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria' (2026), by de Angeli Dutra et al.
articles.authors: de Angeli Dutra D, Fontes CJ, Braga ÉM, Mordecai EA
articles.details: Erratum to article investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak and its association with gold mining.

articles.headline: Malaria resurgence driven by climate and spatial ecology
articles.title_original: Climate, environmental, and programmatic correlates of malaria resurgence in Amhara, Ethiopia (2018-2024): a Bayesian spatiotemporal analysis.
articles.authors: Lake MW, Asemahagn MA, Gelaye KA, Muchie KF, Nigussie TZ, Engedaw HA, Yenesew MA
articles.details: Bayesian spatiotemporal analysis of 5.7 million confirmed malaria cases across 166 Ethiopian districts (2018–2024) found mean monthly incidence increased 5.5-fold from 1.19 to 6.53 per 1,000 population. Higher temperature and rainfall were associated with higher incidence; IRS protection, ITN ownership, and larval source management were protective. Climate-incidence associations were predominantly spatial rather than temporal.

articles.headline: Maternal parasitemia predicts neonatal malaria infection
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic determinants and maternal malaria: impact on neonatal parasitemia in Osun State, Southwest, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Odediji SA, Busari LO, Olawuyi AO, Amoo KO, Amoo-Adeboye BO, Surakat OA, Adeleke MA
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 85 mother-infant pairs in Nigeria found neonatal parasitemia prevalence of 8.2% (7/85), with maternal parasitemia as the primary predictor (100% of infected neonates born to parasitemic mothers, 0% from aparasitemic mothers, p<0.001). Socioeconomic factors including maternal education, occupation, insecticide-treated net use, and intermittent preventive therapy showed no significant associations.

articles.headline: Limited malaria vaccine knowledge among Cameroon caregivers
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and factors associated with the awareness of caregivers of under-five children regarding the malaria vaccine in the Tiko Health District, Cameroon: A community-based cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Idang MA, Nsobinenyui D, Ukah CE, Yunika LK, Ngu CN, Fuanghene Wefuan R, Dang SZ, Ndip NE, Feguem MP, Shei CM, Nsagha DS
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 410 caregivers in Cameroon found 60.7% had heard of malaria vaccines but only 26.6% had adequate knowledge and 25.1% held positive attitudes. Trust in health workers (AOR 3.02, 95% CI 1.83–4.99) and routine immunization attendance (AOR 3.57, 95% CI 2.27–5.60) were significantly associated with vaccine awareness, while prior child malaria episode was protective (AOR 0.55, 95% CI 0.28–0.97).

articles.headline: Correction to prolyl-tRNA synthetase targeting article
articles.title_original: Correction to: Chemical Targeting of Prolyl-tRNA Synthetase Stalls Ovarian Development and Kills Malaria Vectors.
articles.details: Erratum to article on chemical targeting of prolyl-tRNA synthetase to disrupt ovarian development and kill malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Regional drug resistance patterns in Indian P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Targeted Genomic Surveillance Unveils Genetic Variations Linked to Regional Malaria Drug Resistance Dynamics in India.
articles.authors: Khilari A, Sharma S, Bajpai M, Viswan K A, Chaturvedi R, Mirdha BR, Rahi M, Sharma A, Shanmugam D
articles.details: Nanopore sequencing of 238 P. falciparum samples from 6 Indian states identified regional differences in antimalarial resistance markers. Northeast states showed high chloroquine resistance (Pfcrt K76T, CVIET haplotype) and complete sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance (quintuple/sextuple Pfdhfr-Pfdhps haplotypes), while Central India showed emerging lumefantrine tolerance (Pfmdr1 Y184F, Pfaat1 S258L). No WHO-validated Pfk13 artemisinin resistance mutations were detected.

articles.headline: Sickle cell screening hub prioritization in Uganda using malaria co-risk mapping
articles.title_original: A maximal-coverage approach to prioritizing newborn sickle cell screening sites in Uganda using a sickle cell-malaria co-risk surface.
articles.authors: Paasi G, Ndeezi G, Kiyaga C, Namazzi R, Mupere E, Munabi IG, Kiguli S, Idro R, Olupot-Olupot P
articles.details: Spatially heterogeneous sickle cell disease burden in Uganda (13.1% trait prevalence, 0.7% disease) overlaps with intense P. falciparum transmission (PfPR2-10 26.0% area-weighted mean). Using Empirical Bayesian Kriging for HbS allele frequency (median 6.5%) and P. falciparum parasite prevalence, researchers applied maximal-coverage optimization to place 50 candidate screening hubs, capturing 65.6% of baseline co-risk, with 28 (56%) colocatable to existing health facilities within 60-minute travel time.

articles.headline: Vector surveillance in Benin reveals widespread pyrethroid resistance and heterogeneous transmission
articles.title_original: Entomological surveillance of malaria vector: species, behaviors, and insecticide resistance in southern and central Benin.
articles.authors: Hougbe SZ, Ossé RA, Akpodji S, Ahouandjinou MJ, Koumodji KD, Affolabi ZK, DePina AJ, Oussou O, Bocco EO, Souler MI, Idrissou S, Alohoutade SV, Adjottin B, Mokuba A, Chitou S, Totin F, Ossah M, Traore I, Cisse MBM, Baba-Moussa L, Akogbeto M
articles.details: Entomological survey (December 2023–October 2024) across six districts in southern and central Benin identified Anopheles gambiae s.l. as predominant vector (>98%) with site- and season-specific heterogeneity. Pyrethroid resistance kdr allele frequencies exceeded 65% in all sites; pirimiphos-methyl showed generally higher susceptibility but reduced mortality in some districts. EIRs were highest in Ifangni and Toffo. Findings support stratified vector control including next-generation nets and insecticide rotation.

articles.headline: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of AMA-1 in Plasmodium malariae from Vietnam
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of apical membrane antigen-1 in Plasmodium malariae isolated from Vietnam.
articles.authors: Nguyễn ĐTD, Lê HG, Võ TC, Nguyễn TH, Kang JM, Nguyễn KO, Trinh NTM, Van Khanh C, Hanh NTL, Van TTH, Trung NK, Cho M, Son TT, Quang HH, Na BK
articles.details: Sequencing of pmama-1 from 95 Vietnam P. malariae isolates yielded 117 sequences grouped into 19 distinct haplotypes. Majority of polymorphisms clustered in domains I and II; most amino acid changes predicted surface positioning. Genetic heterogeneity of pmama-1 mirrors other Southeast Asian countries but with position and frequency variation by country; evidence of natural selection and evolutionary trends observed.

articles.headline: Compound effector gene-drive strains block Plasmodium transmission in Anopheles gambiae and coluzzii
articles.title_original: Compound effector genes suppress malaria parasite infections in gene-drive population modification strains of the African malaria mosquitoes, Anopheles gambiae and Anopheles coluzzii.
articles.authors: Carballar-Lejarazú R, Dong Y, Pham TB, Tushar T, Tavadia M, Dimopoulos G, James AA
articles.details: Four population modification gene-drive strains (AgTP24, AgTP43, AcTP24, AcTP43) carrying compound effector genes were developed in A. gambiae and A. coluzzii with 95–100% inheritance of gene-drive constructs. Compound effector modules significantly reduced P. falciparum parasite prevalence and infection intensities in both species following challenge assays, with mixed fitness impacts dependent on species and copy number.

articles.headline: LLIN distribution reduced malaria incidence 35% in four Zambian provinces, 2010–2021
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence before and after long-lasting insecticidal net distribution in four Zambian provinces, 2010-2021.
articles.authors: Mwenya RM, Ngulube ID, Richardson S
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Interrupted time series analysis of 1,357 health facility catchment areas across 5.96 million people in four Zambian provinces (Eastern, Luapula, Northern, Northwestern) from January 2010–December 2021 showed statistically significant decline in monthly malaria incidence following LLIN distribution (rate ratio 0.65, 95% CI 0.50–0.85, p=0.002), representing up to 35% reduction in seasonally adjusted incidence.

articles.headline: Murine cerebral malaria model poorly predicts human intervention efficacy
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria: of mice and men.
articles.authors: Weerasekera CJ, White NJ
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Systematic review comparing human and murine cerebral malaria since 2010 found 149 interventions (118 adjuvants) evaluated in C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei ANKA, with 95% reported as successful. However, only 26% tested after cerebral syndrome development and 65% without concomitant antimalarial treatment. Clinical research on human cerebral malaria declined with no adjuvant interventions proven effective, indicating very poor predictive value of the murine model.

articles.headline: SPAQ chemoprevention shows low efficacy against P. falciparum in Mozambique pilot
articles.title_original: Parasitological efficacy of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, northern Mozambique.
articles.authors: Bonnington C, Sitoe M, Pulido Tarquino IA, Enosse SM, Sararat C, Suwannasin K, Proux S, Koesukwiwat U, Tarning J, Imwong M, Theiss-Nyland K, Nosten FH, White NJ
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: In northern Mozambique, 460 healthy children received monthly sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine-amodiaquine (SPAQ); parasitaemia prevalence decreased from 68% to 41% overall and 71% to 44% at day 28. Preventive efficacy was 97% for P. ovale but only 42% for P. falciparum. Amodiaquine showed low efficacy with breakthrough infections at high desethylamodiaquine concentrations, while sulphadoxine and pyrimethamine contributed minimally; all 250 P. falciparum isolates were Pfcrt 76K (susceptible marker).

articles.headline: CYP450 polymorphisms and P. vivax treatment failure in Brazil: systematic review inconclusive
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms in Cytochrome P450 genes: implications for Plasmodium vivax malaria treatment in Brazil.
articles.authors: Cerilo-Filho M, Almeida MNG, Sampaio MP, Mello AGNC, Fernando DJV, Braz RP, Miceli LA, Reis NF, Guimarães RJP, Silva JRS, Storti-Melo LM, Baptista ARS, Machado RLD
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Systematic review of eight studies examining Cytochrome P450 gene polymorphisms (particularly CYP2D6) and P. vivax malaria treatment failure in Brazil, where P. vivax comprises ~85% of annual cases. Review revealed lack of consensus on relationship between CYP450 single nucleotide polymorphisms and antimalarial drug response, with most studies using genotype-based approaches to predict metaboliser phenotypes.

articles.headline: Subpatent malaria detected via RACD in mobile/migratory populations in India
articles.title_original: Detection of subpatent malaria among febrile mobile and migratory population individuals: a strategy for malaria elimination.
articles.authors: Kaura T, Mewara A, Devi S, Singh N, Takkar J, Kumar N, Sehgal R, Grover GS
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Case investigation with reactive active case detection (RACD) around 28 index cases in Punjab, India detected 11 malaria infections (6.3%) among 176 febrile mobile and migratory population individuals: 93.3% P. vivax (111/119) and 6.7% P. falciparum (8/119); three (27.3%) subpatent cases detected only by nested PCR. None tested positive for G6PDd.

articles.headline: Uganda sustained malaria services during COVID-19 pandemic with adapted protocols
articles.title_original: Ensuring continuity of malaria services in times of crisis: lessons learned in Uganda during the COVID-19 pandemic.
articles.authors: Manning EK, Magumba G, Tibenderana JK, Nuwa A
articles.details: During COVID-19, Uganda's National Malaria Control Division maintained essential malaria interventions through updated operational protocols. Partners supplied >33,000 health workers and village health teams with infection prevention and control equipment; Malaria Consortium provided critical support. Case study emphasizes importance of proactive risk planning, timely data reporting, and sustainable funding/commodity sources.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling optimizes mass drug administration timing and coverage in Senegal
articles.title_original: Mathematical modeling for analyzing mass drug administration operational factors for efficient malaria incidence reduction in southern Senegal.
articles.authors: Ndiaye K, Diop EHS, Slater HC, Loum MA, Ndiaye JA, Diallo I, NDiop M, Kaly SN
articles.details: Impulsive differential equation model analyzing mass drug administration (MDA) operational factors in Tambacounda, Senegal predicted successful campaigns require high coverage, multiple rounds yearly, 5–6 week intervals, and start in early transmission season (mid-to-late July). Model incorporated asymptomatic carriers and found starting date and interval highly correlated, suggesting outcomes improve with context-specific optimization.

articles.headline: Androgens enhance Plasmodium liver-stage survival by suppressing hepatic innate immunity
articles.title_original: Host biological sex directs immune control of the Plasmodium parasite liver stage in mice.
articles.authors: Duncombe CJ, Poehlman AS, Conrad EN, Sen N, Ayenew A, Blyn RC, McElfresh GW, Glennon EKK, Boey K, Kalata AC, Kaushansky A, Minkah N, Shears MJ, Murphy SC
articles.details: BALB/cJ mice (male, female, orchiectomized, androgen-treated) infected with P. yoelii sporozoites showed androgens increased parasite survival by reducing elimination of infected hepatocytes. Females and orchiectomized males had 44% fewer infected hepatocytes by 44 hours post-infection. Intact males had lower baseline densities of Kupffer cells, monocytes, and dendritic cells persisting during infection, with diminished antigen presentation and blunted Type I interferon responses compared to females and orchiectomized males.

articles.headline: R21 and RH5 vaccine B and T cell responses in children
articles.title_original: B and T cell responses to pre-erythrocytic R21/Matrix-M and blood-stage RH5.1/Matrix-M malaria vaccines in endemic settings.
articles.authors: Bundi CK, Muñoz O, Kibwana E, Bellamy D, Stockdale L, Barrett JR, McHugh K, Mtaka I, Kalinga W, Kimani D, Nyamako L, Keter K, Ogwang R, Hill AVS, Bejon P, Hamaluba M, Silk SE, Minassian AM, Draper SJ, Olotu AI, Kapulu MC, Ewer KJ, Nielsen CM
articles.details: Phase 1b clinical trials analyzed PBMC for antigen-specific memory B cell and cTfh responses to R21/Matrix-M and RH5.1/Matrix-M vaccines. For R21: higher NANP-specific activated memory IgG+ B cells in infants versus adults, increased with 50μg versus 25μg dose. For RH5.1: higher previous malaria exposure associated with increased RH5-specific cTfh frequencies; RH5-specific IgG+ memory B cells greatest with delayed booster dosing (10-10-10μg at 0-1-6 months), correlating with peak and late serum antibody.

articles.headline: RNAi yeast pesticides kill Anopheles stephensi larvae and adults
articles.title_original: Targeting the Highly Invasive Malaria Vector Anopheles stephensi using Yeast RNAi Pesticides.
articles.authors: Njoroge TM, Hamid-Adiamoh M, Mysore K, Stewart ATM, Sun L, Akaiso DD, Duman-Scheel M
articles.details: Sh.463 yeast RNAi pesticide targeting A. stephensi Shaker gene was evaluated in laboratory conditions. Treatment with Sh.463-56.10R yeast silenced the Shaker gene, resulting in high mortality. Female-specific RNAi yeast larvicides resulted in significant female mortality and higher male:female ratios in offspring. RNAi pesticide targeting mosquito Shaker genes represents a novel biorational intervention for integrated A. stephensi control.

articles.headline: Malaria vulnerability hotspots linked to ITN barriers in western Zamfara
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of gender-sensitive malaria vulnerability and ITN usage barriers in Zamfara State, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Atijosan A, Atijosan A, Salau AO
articles.details: Composite Gender-Sensitive Malaria Vulnerability Index (CGMVI) developed for Zamfara State, Nigeria, integrating malaria incidence/mortality data, ITN access/usage, maternal education, and household wealth. Hotspots identified in Gummi (CGMVI 3.86) and Bukkuyum (3.62) with low ITN usage (82-85%), high maternal education deficits (89-91%), and high poverty (85-90%). Northern/eastern LGAs showed low vulnerability with higher ITN coverage (≈92%). Getis-Ord Gi* analysis confirmed statistically significant western clustering (z-scores 2.11-4.89 at 99% confidence).

articles.headline: P. falciparum DPAP-1 inhibitor discovered and experimentally validated
articles.title_original: Structure-based discovery of Plasmodium falciparum DPAP-1 inhibitor and its experimental validation as an antimalarial agent.
articles.authors: Verma D, Tripathi M, Rathore S, Ahmad I, Saeed M, Dwivedi VD, Tripathi RC
articles.details: Structure-based discovery of Plasmodium falciparum aspartic protease 1 (DPAP-1) inhibitor with experimental validation as antimalarial agent. Specific quantitative results not available from title alone.

articles.headline: Severe imported malaria more common without prior malaria exposure
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in Portugal: A Retrospective Analysis from a Tertiary Public Hospital.
articles.authors: Valença Ferreira T, Alves Silva G, Duque V
articles.details: Retrospective study of 88 adult malaria cases at Portuguese tertiary hospital (2020-2024), mostly male expatriates exposed in Angola. Severe malaria diagnosed in 25.0%, significantly more frequent without prior malaria history (p=0.027). Severe cases had significantly higher creatinine (p=0.009) and lactate dehydrogenase (p=0.038). Urea and parasitemia independently associated with longer hospital stay (p<0.001 and p=0.016). Findings support semi-immunity hypothesis in previously exposed individuals.

articles.headline: HRP2/3 deletions undermine malaria RDT performance in high-prevalence areas
articles.title_original: Are HRP2/3 deletions silently crippling malaria rapid diagnostic tests?
articles.authors: S R S, Holla R, Rao M, Unnikrishnan B, B M S, Chalageri VH
articles.details: Systematic review of 28 studies examining pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 gene deletions affecting HRP2-based rapid diagnostic tests. Gene deletion rates 2.4–57.8% globally (Chhattisgarh 3.8%, Brazil ~18.3% dual deletions, Southern Ethiopia 57.8%). RDT diagnostic performance declines substantially in regions exceeding WHO-recommended 5% deletion threshold. PCR, whole-genome sequencing, and digital droplet PCR needed for effective monitoring in high-deletion regions.

articles.headline: Drought severity and temperature modulate malaria incidence in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Hydroclimatic extremes indexed by SPI and malaria case dynamics in Oyo State, Nigeria: evidence for lagged, site-specific effects.
articles.authors: Odjegba EE, Akanji OE, Bankole AO, Makinde AA, Aromolaran AK, Olagoke VO
articles.details: Study examined drought-temperature effects on malaria incidence in Saki and Sepeteri, Oyo State, Nigeria (2013–2023) using standardized precipitation indices and regression models. In Saki, one-unit increase in 12-month SPI associated with 267% rise in malaria cases (β=1.299, p=0.011), significantly moderated by temperature (β=−0.049, p=0.011); lagged models improved predictive performance (ΔAIC≈47–59). In Sepeteri, 3-month SPI lagged models improved fit (ΔAIC≈45–50). Findings highlight non-linear, location-specific drought-malaria dynamics.

articles.headline: P. chabaudi infection causes reversible thymic atrophy through inflammation
articles.title_original: Thymic Atrophy Development in a Non-Lethal Plasmodium Infection.
articles.authors: Corral-Ruiz GM, Pérez-Vega MJ, Mancilla-Herrera I, Barrios-Payán J, Fabila-Castillo L, Hernández-Pando R, Sánchez-Torres LE
articles.details: Study examined thymic atrophy mechanisms during Plasmodium chabaudi AS infection. Thymic atrophy marked by decreased weight and cellularity at 20–30% parasitemia, loss of DP and SP thymocytes, cortical shrinkage, and DNA fragmentation indicating increased apoptosis. Elevated serum cytokines (IFN-γ, TNF-α, IL-6, CXCL9) correlated with atrophy degree. Early thymocyte release into peripheral tissues, especially spleen, worsened thymic cellularity decline. Thymus recovered after parasitemia resolved.

articles.headline: CCND3 enhancer variant reduces malaria risk
articles.title_original: CCND3 enhancer variant reduces malaria risk.
articles.authors: Faial T
articles.details: Genetic study identifying CCND3 enhancer variant associated with reduced malaria risk. Specific study design, population, methods, and quantitative results not available from title alone.

articles.headline: Malarial retinopathy: 75% sensitive marker for severe malaria
articles.title_original: A Study on Prevalence of Malarial Retinopathy among Malaria Cases and to Determine Its Presence as a Marker of Severe Malaria: Observations from Eastern India.
articles.authors: Chatterjee R, Bhadra TR, Chatterjee N, Mukherjee S, Halder P, Sarkar K, Chatterjee I, Saha B, Karmakar PS, Pramanik N, Samajdar SS
articles.details: Retrospective clinical study of 71 adult malaria cases in Kolkata, India. Among 12 severe cases, 8 P. vivax, 3 P. falciparum, 1 mixed. Malarial retinopathy features observed in 9/12 (75%) severe cases versus 2/59 (3.4%) uncomplicated cases. Retinopathy had 75% sensitivity, 96.6% specificity, and 81.8% positive predictive value for severe malaria (p<0.05). Malarial retinopathy may serve as important clinical biomarker for predicting severe malaria.

articles.headline: Nested-PCR optimized for P. malariae detection with 0.5 parasites/μL limit
articles.title_original: Optimization of Nested-PCR for the detection of Plasmodium malariae in laboratory settings in Southern Vietnam.
articles.authors: Nguyen KT, Nguyen NH, Dinh QL, Nguyen HLV, Nguyen MAT, Huynh HQ, Van Thi Tran H
articles.details: Study optimized nested-PCR protocol for specific Plasmodium malariae detection in Southern Vietnam. Assay targeted P. malariae 18S rRNA gene using MAL1/MAL2 primers. Optimal conditions: 35 cycles first round, 25 cycles second round, 54°C annealing. MAL1/MAL2 primers showed high specificity for P. malariae with no amplification for P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. ovale, P. knowlesi or contaminants. Limit of detection: 0.5 parasites/μL.

articles.headline: Climate shocks threaten malaria control efforts in Africa
articles.title_original: Climate shocks, not just warming, threaten malaria control efforts in Africa.
articles.authors: Messina J, Carrel M
articles.details: Analysis of how climate variability and extreme weather events (shocks), beyond gradual warming, impact malaria control effectiveness in African regions. Specific methods, data sources, and quantitative findings not available from title alone.

articles.headline: Insecticide-treated nets select for distinct resistance mechanisms
articles.title_original: Genetic Surveillance Reveals Differential Evolutionary Dynamic of Anopheles gambiae Under Contrasting Insecticidal Tools Used in Malaria Control.
articles.authors: Njoroge HN, Namuli L, Nagi SC, Hernandez-Koutoucheva A, McDermott DP, Knight E, Gonahasa S, Lynd A, Oruni A, Maiteki-Sebuguzi C, Opigo J, Yeka A, Katureebe A, Kyohere M, Kamya MR, Dorsey G, Hemingway J, Staedke SG, Clarkson C, Miles A, Lucas ER, Donnelly MJ
articles.details: Genomic study in Uganda comparing standard pyrethroid ITNs versus pyrethroid-PBO nets in Anopheles gambiae. Despite reduced indoor mosquito densities, genetic diversity showed no significant decline in effective population size. Cyp9k1 gene duplication increased in pyrethroid-only net populations but decreased with PBO nets; alternative resistance mechanisms selected in different contexts.

articles.headline: Malaria and dengue reduce fertility and increase pregnancy complications
articles.title_original: Consequence of malaria and dengue on reproductive vigor. A scoping review.
articles.authors: Chatterjee PK, Chatterjee P, Dutta B, Anupama N, R M, Dhar S, Ks R, Bijoor SN, Tomar A, Tripathy A
articles.details: Scoping review (2013–2024) examining malaria and dengue effects on reproduction. Malaria causes decreased semen quality, accounting for 40–90% of male infertility cases. Pregnant women experience higher rates of miscarriage, stillbirth, preterm delivery, low birth weight, neonatal death, and maternal mortality. Antimalarial drugs (chloroquine, quinine, quinacrine, pyrimethamine) cause male infertility; dengue causes temporary sperm alterations.

articles.headline: Structure-guided PfCyRPA vaccine improves blood-stage malaria immunity
articles.title_original: Structure-guided design of a PfCyRPA-based vaccine against blood-stage malaria.
articles.authors: Alam N, Wolfle C, Butkeviciute E, Quinkert D, King LDW, Higgins MK
articles.details: Structure-guided design of PfCyRPA epitope mimic (PfCyRPA-EM) containing blades 1 and 2 with known growth-inhibitory epitopes. In pre-clinical models, PfCyRPA-EM elicited antibodies inhibiting parasite growth at lower concentrations than full PfCyRPA, with superior thermostability and expression as an I53-50 nanoparticle fusion.

articles.headline: Zoonotic malaria parasites prevalent in Malaysian wildlife-human zones
articles.title_original: Molecular and phylogenetic analysis of zoonotic Plasmodium in long-tailed macaques: insights from wildlife-human conflict zones in Selangor, Malaysia.
articles.authors: Zamri SZ, Aziz NAA, Mazlan M, Saidu YS, Zulhisam A, Che-Amat A, Sipin Q
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 100 long-tailed macaques (September 2022–April 2023) in Selangor, Malaysia found P. cynomolgi 24%, P. inui 23%, and P. knowlesi 4% prevalence; 51% single infections, 18% dual, 2% triple infections. Phylogenetic analysis identified S-type and A-type lineages for all species, underscoring zoonotic transmission potential.

articles.headline: Host CD44 protein enhances Plasmodium falciparum invasion
articles.title_original: With a little help from my friend: engaging CD44 facilitates Plasmodium falciparum invasion of erythrocytes.
articles.authors: Rénia L
articles.details: Identifies host transmembrane protein CD44 as critical coreceptor; when crosslinked, CD44 significantly enhances P. falciparum invasion of human erythrocytes, linking early parasite attachment to final invasion steps.

articles.headline: Metabolic shift triggers mosquito-stage ribosomal RNA expression
articles.title_original: Shifting metabolism alters rRNA expression in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Hadjimichael E, Deitsch KW
articles.details: Study of Plasmodium rRNA expression across transmission cycle. Parasite transition to mosquito stages involves metabolic shift altering NAD+/nicotinamide ratios, initiating derepression of mosquito-specific rDNA loci and distinct ribosomal RNA expression patterns.

articles.headline: Taurine controls asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum infections
articles.title_original: How taurine keeps asymptomatic malaria infections in check.
articles.authors: Taylor TE
articles.details: Research highlight on the role of taurine in asymptomatic malaria infections. Title only; no abstract data provided.

articles.headline: Ivermectin mass drug administration cost-effective in Kenya
articles.title_original: Cost and cost-effectiveness of ivermectin mass drug administration for malaria control in Kwale county, Kenya: a modelling analysis of a cluster-randomised trial.
articles.authors: Xie K, Otuko R, Marathe A, Gutierrez AS, Kariuki M, Musyoka L, Yaa E, Mbarak J, Omondi I, Kasiwa L, Rabinovich R, Maia M, Chaccour C, Deng X, Rist C
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis of BOHEMIA cluster-randomized trial in Kwale county, Kenya. Monthly ivermectin dosing (3 months, ~64% coverage) reduced malaria incidence 26%; intervention cost $11.83/person; base-case ICER $905.23/DALY averted, below Kenya's GDP-based threshold of $974.65/DALY.

articles.headline: New malaria control tools aid complex decision-making
articles.title_original: A new tool at a complex time for malaria decision making.
articles.authors: Devine A, Kamya C
articles.details: Opinion piece on emerging tools for malaria control strategy. Title only; no abstract data provided.

articles.headline: Plasma taurine inhibits P. falciparum cytoadhesion and severity
articles.title_original: Longitudinal plasma metabolomics reveals a role for taurine in asymptomatic malaria and seasonal persistence.
articles.authors: Diffendall G, Millot P, Chen P, Fogang B, Dorin-Semblat D, Aprahamian F, Durand S, Gamain B, Claessens A, Scherf A
articles.details: Longitudinal plasma metabolomics study (n=199) in Gambia during high and low transmission periods. Plasma taurine increased during low transmission; taurine inhibited and prevented iRBC adhesion via PfEMP1 to CD36 and EPCR (cerebral malaria receptors), suggesting a mechanism by which taurine reduces symptomatic malaria through reduced cytoadhesion.

articles.headline: Cyclin D3 reduction in erythroid cells protects against malaria
articles.title_original: Reduced cyclin D3 expression in erythroid cells protects against malaria.
articles.authors: Marini MG, Mingoia M, Steri M, Tsamesidis I, Idda ML, Manca A, D'Avino C, Virdis F, Lodde V, Mulas A, Asunis I, Li X, Steiner MC, Loi A, Caria CA, Marongiu MF, Floris M, Marongiu M, Manunza L, Pitzalis M, Orrù V, Fiorillo E, Zoledziewska M, Moi P, Turrini FM, Pala M, Sidore C, Schlessinger D, Novembre J, Pantaleo A, Cucca F
articles.details: Mechanistic study of rs112233623-T variant in CCND3 erythroid enhancer associated with reduced cyclin D3 expression, lower erythrocyte count, and increased cell size. P. falciparum growth impaired in infected erythrocytes from carriers, correlating with elevated ROS levels; mechanism parallels G6PD deficiency-conferred protection.

articles.headline: Cell-cycle protein repression inhibits malaria parasite growth
articles.title_original: Malaria is hindered by repression of a cell-cycle protein.
articles.authors: Band G
articles.details: Research highlight on parasite growth inhibition mechanisms. Title only; no abstract data provided.

articles.headline: Cambodian P. vivax parasites clear artesunate slowly in some infections
articles.title_original: Parasite clearance in patients with Plasmodium vivax monoinfection treated with artesunate in Cambodia: an observational secondary analysis of trial data.
articles.authors: Tebben K, Eng V, Seng D, Tat B, Feufack-Donfack LB, Orban A, Yeat R, Salvador J, Sin S, Ko K, Khim N, Flamand C, Sommen C, Lek D, Serre D, Popovici J
articles.details: Study of 161 P. vivax infections from 87 Cambodian patients (median age 20 years) treated with 2 mg/kg/day artesunate for 7 days. All infections cleared by day 3; however, 49 (30%) showed slow clearance, including 9 (6%) with parasite clearance half-life >5 hours. Slow-clearing parasites had delayed gene expression response with downregulation of haemoglobin metabolism genes. Authors conclude slow clearance could allow parasites to survive treatment and facilitate artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: mRNA vaccine restores RAS protection in malaria-exposed mice
articles.title_original: mRNA vaccination overcomes haemozoin-mediated impairment of whole-parasite malaria vaccines in mice.
articles.authors: Hassert M, Drewry LL, Pewe LL, Hancox LS, He R, Arumugam S, Mix MR, Salem AK, Harty JT
articles.details: In mice, previous P. yoelii blood-stage infection compromised P. berghei RAS vaccine-induced CD8+ T cell responses and protection, mediated by persistent haemozoin impairing dendritic cell antigen uptake. A lipid nanoparticle-encapsulated mRNA vaccine encoding Plasmodium CD8+ T cell epitopes restored T cell responses and protection; combined RAS-plus-mRNA vaccine enhanced liver-resident memory T cells and protection in malaria-exposed hosts. This demonstrates haemozoin as an obstacle to vaccine efficacy in endemic areas.

articles.headline: G6PD Viangchan status does not affect P. falciparum development in vitro
articles.title_original: Invasion and development of Plasmodium falciparum in erythroblasts of humans carrying G6PD viangchan.
articles.authors: Boonpeng K, Palasuwan A, Ketprasit N, Kulkeaw K, Palasuwan D
articles.details: Study examined P. falciparum invasion and development in human erythroblasts from subjects with normal, heterozygous, or hemizygous G6PD Viangchan (871G>A) variant, the most common in Southeast Asia. Parasites successfully invaded erythroblasts and developed to mature trophozoites; no significant differences in parasitization between G6PD genotypes (n=6, limited by small sample). Authors conclude that protection against malaria is not observed in this in vitro erythroblast model.

articles.headline: ddPCR assays accurately quantify P. vivax parasitemia and G6PD status
articles.title_original: Development and implementation of droplet digital PCR assays for accurate quantification of Plasmodium vivax parasitemia and G6PD viangchan genotyping.
articles.authors: Srisutham S, Ausit K, Boonpeng K, Suksuwanont M, Kijprasong K
articles.details: Developed and validated duplex ddPCR assays for precise P. vivax parasitemia quantification (targeting tubulin and human RHCE genes) and G6PD Viangchan (871G>A) genotyping with >90% accuracy and 100% sensitivity/specificity. Applied to clinical samples in Kanchanaburi Province, Thailand; parasite densities ranged 315–82,429 parasites/µL. Chloroquine treatment monitoring showed 99.2–99.9% parasitemia reduction from day 0 to day 2, with complete clearance by day 3. G6PD Viangchan prevalence: 98.9% wild-type, 1.1% heterozygous.

articles.headline: Multidrug antimalarial regimens show similar efficacy to standard therapy
articles.title_original: Artesunate-pyronaridine-atovaquone-proguanil and artesunate-fosmidomycin-clindamycin compared with standard artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated malaria (MultiMal): a randomised, controlled, clinical, phase 2 trial in Gabon and Ghana.
articles.authors: Agobé JCD, Maïga-Ascofaré O, Adegnika AA, Pfaffendorf C, Boaheng JM, Edoa JR, Agyiri ID, Bayode Adegbite R, Bart-Plange EY, Ahenkan E, Ekoka Mbassi D, Sarpong FN, Placca E, Kanin DK, Bakari P, Loag W, Kettenbeil J, Krishna S, Lell B, Agnandji ST, Amuasi JH, Zoleko-Manego R, Mombo-Ngoma G, Kremsner PG, Wicha SG, Ramharter M, Mischlinger J
articles.details: Phase 2 RCT in Gabon and Ghana of 100 patients with uncomplicated falciparum malaria (parasitemia 1000–100,000/µL) across three age groups, randomized to three-day courses of artesunate-pyronaridine (AP), artesunate-pyronaridine-atovaquone-proguanil (APAP), or artesunate-fosmidomycin-clindamycin (AFC). Day 28 PCR-corrected ACPR: AP 100%, APAP 100%, AFC 97%; day 42: AP 87.5%, APAP 85.3%, AFC 94.4%. All regimens were safe with no differential adverse event profiles; no evidence for differential efficacy across treatment arms.

articles.headline: Climate change projected to cause 123 million additional African malaria cases
articles.title_original: Projected impacts of climate change on malaria in Africa.
articles.authors: Symons TL, Moran A, Balzarolo A, Vargas C, Robertson M, Lubinda J, Saddler A, McPhail M, Harris J, Rozier J, Browne A, Amratia P, Bertozzi-Villa A, Bhatt S, Cameron E, Golding N, Smith DL, Noor AM, Rumisha SF, Palmer MD, Weiss DJ, Desai N, Potere D, Sukitsch N, Woods W, Gething PW
articles.details: Geotemporal model integrating 25 years of African climate, malaria burden, control, and socioeconomic data with climate projections (SSP 2-4.5) estimated that climate change could cause 123 million (range 49.5–203 million) additional malaria cases and 532,000 (195,000–912,000) additional deaths in Africa between 2024 and 2050 under current control levels. Extreme weather emerged as the primary driver, accounting for 79% (50–94%) of additional cases and 93% (70–100%) of additional deaths. Most increases stem from intensification in existing endemic areas rather than geographic range expansion.

articles.headline: Cytokine-induced killer cells inhibit P. falciparum through cytolytic secretions
articles.title_original: Cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells inhibit Plasmodium falciparum parasitemia through cytolytic effector activity in vitro.
articles.authors: Vaseq R, Ferkamchwi B, Weiher H, Lukacs-Kornek V, Mahleqa N, Hübner MP, Sharma A, Schmidt-Wolf IGH
articles.details: In vitro study demonstrated that cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells inhibit P. falciparum growth and reduce parasitemia within 24 hours. Cytotoxicity is primarily mediated by secreted lytic agents (granzyme B, granulysin, and perforin) acting synergistically, rather than cell-cell contact. Priming CIK cells with dendritic cells pulsed with parasite lysate antigen diminished CIK cytotoxicity. CIK cells showed comparable efficacy to chloroquine; combination with artemisinin showed slight enhancement versus artemisinin alone.

articles.headline: Central India shows 27% febrile sub-microscopic Plasmodium infections mixed
articles.title_original: High Burden of Febrile Sub-microscopic Plasmodium Mixed Infections in Central India: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Deora N, Pande V, Pal DK, Sharma H, Dube S, Sinha A
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1,030 febrile patients in Central India (June–November 2020) identified 27% Plasmodium infection by PCR: 18% mono-P. falciparum, 0.5% mono-P. vivax, and 8% mixed infections. All detected infections were sub-microscopic (undetected by microscopy or rapid diagnostic tests), revealing significant diagnostic gaps. Among 9 mixed-infection patients followed for 12 months, none reported recurrences or clinical complications. Authors note that this high burden of sub-microscopic infections represents a significant challenge for malaria elimination in a category-1 elimination zone.

articles.headline: EMAP3 protein on infected RBCs enables parasite cytoadherence research
articles.title_original: Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 3 (EMAP3) Is Exposed on the Surface of the Plasmodium berghei Infected Red Blood Cell.
articles.authors: Hernandez SRC, Rashpa R, Jonsdottir TK, Paoletta MS, Ter Beek J, Díaz MR, Krol JMM, Chevalley-Maurel S, Ishizaki T, Berntsson RP, Janse CJ, Franke-Fayard B, Brochet M, Bushell ESC
articles.details: Study identified EMAP3, a novel protein exported to the surface of P. berghei-infected RBCs. EMAP3 is outer-surface exposed and likely interacts with EMAP1, but parasites lacking EMAP3 showed no significant growth or sequestration defects, indicating EMAP3 is not a major CD36-binding protein. The outer-surface location of EMAP3 provides a scaffold for displaying P. falciparum proteins on P. berghei iRBCs, offering a platform for in vivo screening of putative inhibitors of P. falciparum cytoadherence.

articles.headline: Trust and values drive community engagement in malaria control
articles.title_original: The importance of values in a community-based response to malaria in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic: results from a longitudinal, mixed-methods study.
articles.authors: Keys H, Ureña K, Bardosh K, Taveras D, Desir L, Noland G
articles.details: Mixed-methods study in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic combining cross-sectional survey (489 households, 2020) and 2-year qualitative phase with 66 interviews from 39 key informants. Trust module showed acceptable reliability (ordinal α=0.70); households rated institutions as "very trustworthy" and neighbors/community associations as "somewhat trustworthy." Thematic analysis identified two value domains: trust linking people to institutions, and solidarity/reciprocity connecting people to each other. Authors conclude both value domains contributed to malaria intervention outcomes.

articles.headline: Only 59% of P. vivax patients complete 14-day primaquine treatment
articles.title_original: Suboptimal primaquine adherence in Plasmodium vivax malaria: Evidence from high-burden tribal districts in Odisha.
articles.authors: K AV, Thiruvengadam K, Sharma R, Perumalsamy N, Arumugam RD, Srirama S, Rahul A, Rahi M
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 269 P. vivax patients in two tribal districts of Odisha, India (January–December 2024) assessed adherence to treatment regimens. Adherence to chloroquine (3 days) and artesunate-SP exceeded 93%, but only 58.7% (95% CI 52.6–64.9%) completed the 14-day primaquine course. Treatment discontinuation was primarily due to symptom resolution (61.9%) and forgetfulness (21.4%); only one patient stopped due to adverse event. Older age and household malaria history associated with better adherence; authors recommend behavioral interventions and shorter or single-dose alternatives.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi Pk32 antigen shows conservation and promising vaccine epitopes
articles.title_original: In silico profiling of the plasmodium knowlesi 32 kDa antigen: Diversity, epitope prediction, and structural modeling.
articles.authors: Saif A, Baruah P, Alghamdi JM, Acharyya R, Lau YL, Han JH, Ahmed MA
articles.details: In silico analysis of 39 P. knowlesi Pk32 (32 kDa) sequences from Malaysian Borneo revealed low nucleotide diversity (π=0.0061) and limited polymorphism, with strong evidence of purifying selection. Three common B-cell epitopes were identified, including two highly conserved (145PKERES150 and 167DIGKKQNS174) mapping to stable 3D structural regions. Authors conclude that the combination of limited genetic variation and stable, conserved B-cell epitopes makes Pk32 a compelling candidate for inclusion in a blood-stage vaccine against P. knowlesi.

articles.headline: Room-temperature stable LAMP test for P. knowlesi detection
articles.title_original: A cold-chain free loop-mediated isothermal amplification test for detection of human malaria in point-of-care settings.
articles.authors: Rosli NN, Lau YL, Lai MY
articles.details: Developed sucrose-preserved LAMP assay for P. knowlesi detection. Achieved 10 copies/μL analytical sensitivity, 100% specificity, and 12-day room-temperature stability without cold-chain infrastructure. Results support deployment in remote clinics and field surveillance settings.

articles.headline: Clathrins and AP-1 regulate P. falciparum hemoglobin uptake
articles.title_original: Clathrins Are Involved in the Endocytosis of Host Cytosol in the Malaria Parasite.
articles.authors: Miao J, Lucky AB, Ning G, Xia X, Liang X, Siddiqui F, Min H, Wang C, Li X, Zhou ZH, Cui L
articles.details: Characterized clathrin heavy chain (PfCHC), light chain (PfCLC), and AP-1 γ subunit in P. falciparum. Knockdown of PfCHC produced abnormally long cytostome tubes and impaired hemoglobin uptake. Clathrins localize to parasite periphery, cis-Golgi, and cytostome collars, indicating involvement in nutrient acquisition.

articles.headline: Three novel Kelch-13 SNPs in Nigerian P. falciparum isolates
articles.title_original: Three new single nucleotide polymorphisms identified in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Kogi State, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Akoma ON, Ike AC, Ayogu EE, Agbo MC, Ezeh CC, Okoh FN, Arome SO, Ezeonu IM
articles.details: Screened Pfmdr1 and Kelch-13 genes in 40 P. falciparum samples from Kogi State, Nigeria. Amplicons obtained from 45% (18/40) samples. Bioinformatics identified three non-synonymous K-13 mutations (K430E, W706C, Q707K) with 99.88% sequence homology to wild-type. None previously associated with artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: East Africa ACT quality 11.2% failure; clinical efficacy ≥90%
articles.title_original: Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapies in East Africa: A Systematic Review of Quality, Clinical Efficacy, and Regulatory Progress Towards Sustainable Malaria Control.
articles.authors: Mekasha YT, Tegegne AA, Mashe T, Girma M, Hasen G, Belew S
articles.details: Systematic review of 40 articles testing 3,363 ACT samples across Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda. Overall 11.2% (375/3,363) failed quality parameters: Uganda 33.3%, Kenya 14.1%, Tanzania 11.6%, Ethiopia 4.2%. Despite poor quality, clinical trials reported ≥90% polymerase chain reaction-corrected cure rates. Artemether-lumefantrine showed strong outcomes; dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine provided extended prophylaxis.

articles.headline: Amodiaquine inhibits P. falciparum plasmepsin V with IC50 0.42 μM
articles.title_original: Inhibiting catalytic activity of Plasmodium falciparum aspartate protease plasmepsin V: A biochemical approach to malaria intervention.
articles.authors: Prajapati AK, Kumar Y, Choudhary S
articles.details: Repurposed amodiaquine and clioquinol as plasmepsin V inhibitors. Amodiaquine showed moderate binding affinity and competitive inhibition with IC50 0.42 ± 0.04 μM, binding to catalytic dyad residues Asp118 and Asp365. Clioquinol did not bind at catalytic site. Molecular dynamics confirmed amodiaquine forms stable, hydrophobic-driven complex.

articles.headline: I. umbellulata achieves 85.65% parasite suppression in malaria mice
articles.title_original: Sub-acute toxicity studies and dynamics-based molecular insights on the in vivo antimalarial activity of Ilex umbellulata (Wall.) Loes bark in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.
articles.authors: Zothantluanga JH, Tayeng D, Zonunmawii, Rahman O, Abdalla M, Chetia D
articles.details: Evaluated 80% methanol bark extract of I. umbellulata in P. berghei-infected mice using Peter's 4-day suppressive test. LD50 >2000 mg/kg. At 200 mg/kg dose: parasitaemia 5.00 ± 0.97%, parasite suppression 85.65 ± 2.77%, survival 15.33 ± 2.82 days. Cyanidin-3-O-rutinoside inhibits enoyl-acyl-carrier-protein reductase.

articles.headline: Z. chalybeum most potent; IC50 0.81–1.28 μg/ml among five plants
articles.title_original: In vitro antiplasmodial activity, acute toxicity and phytochemical quantification of selected medicinal plants used for the management of uncomplicated malaria in eastern Uganda.
articles.authors: Obakiro SB, Nabitandikwa C, Kiyimba K, Ocan M, Gavamukulya Y, Andima M, Lukwago TW, Maseruka R, Chebijira M, Opio M, Hokello JF, Kibuule D, Nabatanzi A, Orena S, Waako P, Owor RO
articles.details: Tested five medicinal plants against chloroquine-sensitive (3D7), resistant (Dd2), and clinical P. falciparum isolates. Z. chalybeum most potent (IC50 0.81–1.28 μg/ml), followed by A. coriaria and E. abyssinica (IC50 10–50 μg/ml). A. coriaria and M. senegalensis showed moderate acute toxicity (LD50 < 2000 mg/kg); others non-toxic.

articles.headline: IPTp-SP reduces maternal parasitaemia and anaemia; network meta-analysis
articles.title_original: Comparative effect of chemoprevention in malaria infection during pregnancy: A network meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Naing C, Ni H, Htet NH, Aung HH, Anamalai Y, Ahmed SI, Whittaker MA
articles.details: Network meta-analysis of 22 RCTs (N=14,705) evaluating intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp). Moderate certainty evidence: monthly IPTp-sulphadoxine/pyrimethamine reduced maternal parasitaemia and anaemia vs placebo. Low-certainty evidence of fewer low-birth-weight cases vs combined dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and azithromycin. Authors recommend head-to-head comparisons across multiple antimalarials.

articles.headline: Acute cholecystitis and post-artesunate hemolytic anemia complications
articles.title_original: Acute Acalculous Cholecystitis and Post-artesunate Hemolytic Anemia: Two Rare Complications in Severe Malaria.
articles.authors: Nunes CF, Dias JV, Nunes BS, Gouveia C, Varandas L
articles.details: Describes two rare complications of severe malaria: acute acalculous cholecystitis and post-artesunate hemolytic anemia. No quantitative data provided in title alone.

articles.headline: PfExomit1 essential for P. falciparum blood-stage establishment
articles.title_original: Plasmodium mitochondrial exodeoxyribonucleases, Exo(mit1) and Exo(mit2), are conserved within alveolates with Exo(mit1) essential for the establishment of blood-stage infection.
articles.authors: Mishra S, Chatterjee T, Mehra P, Gahlawat A, Pradhan S, Gupta R, Bhattacharyya MK, Mishra S, Habib S
articles.details: Identified mitochondrial exonucleases PfExomit1 (ssDNA-specific) and PfExomit2 (dsDNA/ssDNA active) in P. falciparum. Conditional knockout of PbExomit1 in rodent P. berghei allowed sporozoite invasion and liver development but prevented blood-stage establishment. Indicates mitochondrial DNA integrity and ssDNA processing critical for parasite development.

articles.headline: Microarray patches deliver antimalarials; 99.8% parasitaemia reduction
articles.title_original: Formulation and evaluation of hydrogel-forming microarray patches for transdermal primaquine and chloroquine delivery in malaria therapy.
articles.authors: Anjani QK, Volpe-Zanutto F, Miatmoko A, Adhami M, Novais JT, Gaitán XA, Hariawan BS, Cahyani DM, Octavia RT, Mubarok AS, Setyaningsih WAW, Bargieri DY, Donnelly RF
articles.details: Developed hydrogel-forming microarray patches (MAPs) for transdermal primaquine and chloroquine. In vitro: ~40 mg (70% efficiency) of each drug delivered over 24 hours. Pharmacokinetics in rats: Cmax increased 2.5-fold (CQ: 0.68→1.68 ng/mL) and 3.3-fold (PQ: 14.32→47.20 ng/mL); AUC0-72h 4.4-fold higher (CQ) and 1.3-fold (PQ) vs oral. Murine efficacy: 99.8% parasitaemia reduction.

articles.headline: Pregnancy increases lumefantrine clearance 23%; 100% cure maintained
articles.title_original: Population pharmacokinetics of lumefantrine in pregnant and non-pregnant women with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Western Kenya.
articles.authors: Juma E, Ding J, Ongas M, Koskei N, Onyango K, Oloo F, Aman R, Kokwaro G, Tarning J, Ogutu B
articles.details: Pharmacokinetic study of 75 women with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria (25 non-pregnant, 30 second-trimester, 20 third-trimester) receiving artemether-lumefantrine 80/480 mg twice daily×3 days. Pregnancy increased elimination clearance 23% (95% CI: 10.8–34.8%) and volume of distribution 28% (95% CI: 7.3–51.8%). Lower drug exposure: AUC GMR 0.76, Cmax GMR 0.79, Day 7 GMR 0.69. Despite lower exposure, 28-day PCR-corrected cure: 100% both groups.

articles.headline: Plasmodium-Anopheles molecular interactions inform novel vector control strategies
articles.title_original: Deciphering the molecular targets of Plasmodium and Anopheline interactions for malaria control.
articles.authors: Janjoter S, Kataria D, Dahiya N, Yadav M, Singh H, Garg S, Sehrawat N
articles.details: Review examining molecular interactions between Plasmodium parasites and Anopheles mosquitoes, including ookinete invasion of mosquito midgut and sporozoite invasion of salivary glands. Explores roles of Plasmodium and mosquito genes in parasite transmission and mosquito immune defenses. Authors conclude understanding this parasite-vector biology may help design more efficient vector control strategies.

articles.headline: Malaria-resistance alleles prevalent in Colombian Pacific coast population
articles.title_original: Prevalence of the Main Human Genetic Variants Related to Resistance to Malaria in a Population of the Colombian Pacific Coast.
articles.authors: Ortega DC, Arango MP, Cañón S, Cárdenas H, González R, Barreto G
articles.details: Cross-sectional molecular characterization of 819 residents in Buenaventura, Colombia. PCR-RFLP and allele-specific PCR determined frequencies of malaria-resistance alleles: HbS 3.1%, HbC 2.2%, Duffy 72.2%, β-thalassemia 2.1-2.8%, G6PD 11%. Adolescents/young adults (13-26 years) and insular communities showed highest proportions of resistance genotypes. Authors recommend tailored public health interventions targeting identified age groups and geographic areas.

articles.headline: Bacterial larvicides reduce vector density but limited malaria incidence impact
articles.title_original: Systematic literature review of bacterial larvicides Bacillus thuringiensis var israelensis and Bacillus (Lysinibacillus) sphaericus for malaria control in Africa.
articles.authors: Mataba GR
articles.details: Systematic review of 54 studies on Bacillus thuringiensis var israelensis (Bti) and B. sphaericus (Bs) larvicides in Africa. Most were small-scale field trials. Bti/Bs reduced larval density by 50-100%, but only 14 studies measured malaria incidence reduction (31-62%). Only one study evaluated large-scale countrywide implementation. Authors conclude serious lack of large-scale evidence impedes evidence-based malaria elimination policies.

articles.headline: Weakening MBER-TPR correlation indicates progress toward malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Weakening TPR-MBER correlation: Marker of success in India's malaria elimination progress.
articles.authors: Prajapat K, Libny YS, Syed Ashish EA, Prasad P, Yalavarthi A, Das A, Chaudhary K, Jain I
articles.details: Analysis of 2020-2024 Monthly Malaria Information System data from Madhya Pradesh, India. Assessed relationship between Monthly Blood Examination Rate (MBER) and Test Positivity Rate (TPR). Progressive weakening of MBER-TPR correlation observed, indicating improved surveillance saturation and reduced underlying transmission. Authors conclude MBER-TPR correlation is useful dynamic indicator for monitoring malaria elimination progress.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 40.5%; risk factors include age, ITN use, sleeping habits
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated risk factors of malaria among febrile patients at Kidist Hana Health Center Fogera District, Amhara Regional State, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Alebele S, Abate A, Sisay M, Asnake T, Alehegn M, Adisu D, Munshea A
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study of febrile patients at Kidist Hana Health Center, Fogera District, Ethiopia. Blood samples examined by microscopy and RDTs. Overall malaria prevalence 40.5% by microscopy; P. falciparum predominated (78.4% of cases). Multivariate analysis identified significant associations: widowed marital status, age <5 years, low household income, lack of insecticide-treated net (ITN), stagnant water near residence, outdoor sleeping. RDT sensitivity 95.91%, specificity 91.24%.

articles.headline: Biocredit LDH-based RDT: 91% sensitive for falciparum, 96% for vivax
articles.title_original: Diagnostic accuracy of novel Biocredit species-specific LDH-based immunochromatographic assay for Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax detection: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Gebremeskele BT, Getu E, Demeke AD, Bogale A, Churiso G
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 19 datasets from 8 studies (3923 participants) evaluating Biocredit Pf/Pv RDT detecting species-specific LDH isomers. Pooled sensitivity 91% (95% CI 77-97%) for P. falciparum, 96% (95% CI 88-99%) for P. vivax; pooled specificity 99% (95% CI 95-100%) for P. falciparum, 100% (95% CI 96-100%) for P. vivax. Against microscopy: 95% and 97% sensitivity; against PCR: 81% and 90%. P. falciparum sensitivity 94% in high-transmission but 73% in moderate-transmission areas.

articles.headline: Myanmar border: Msp1 MAD20 allele predominates; migrant workers show higher multiplicity
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum genetic diversity in border areas of Myanmar assessed by genotyping merozoite surface protein genes.
articles.authors: Ma J, Wu Y, Zeng W, Chen X, Xiang Z, Li C, Zhang Y, Zhao H, Duan M, Liang T, Mou Y, Tang X, Liu C, Kyaw MT, Thu AN, Kyaw MP, Cui L, Yang Z
articles.details: Genotyping study of 176 P. falciparum samples from Paletwa Township, Bhamo, Myitsone (Myanmar) and migrant workers from Yunnan, China (2013-2022). Nested PCR genotyped Msp1 and Msp2 genes (176 and 162 samples successful). Msp1 showed higher genetic diversity than Msp2. MAD20 allele most frequent in all regions (67-89%); K1 predominated in Myitsone (65%) and Bhamo (44%). Multiplicity of infection (Msp1): migrant workers 1.87, Bhamo residents 1.33, Paletwa residents 1.56. Multi-infections more frequent in migrants (72%) versus residents (43-45%).

articles.headline: Heme oxygenase-1 shows dual protective and pathological roles in malaria
articles.title_original: Heme oxygenase-1 and malaria pathogenesis.
articles.authors: Wakai T, Chinedu S, Afolabi I
articles.details: Review of heme oxygenase-1 (HO-1) enzyme in Plasmodium infection and malaria pathogenesis. Experimental and clinical studies show HO-1 strongly induced during malaria. In murine models, HO-1 induction protects against cerebral malaria and organ dysfunction, partly by modulating pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing vascular permeability. However, elevated HO-1 in specific immune populations associated with heightened inflammation and disease severity in humans. Authors discuss potential as diagnostic/prognostic biomarker and therapeutic target.

articles.headline: Deep-sea metabolites show promise as PfDHODH inhibitor drug candidates
articles.title_original: Structure-Based Screening of Deep-Sea Microbial Metabolites Against Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase.
articles.authors: Singh A, Rengasamy KRR, Benjakul S
articles.details: In silico drug discovery integrated high-throughput structure-based virtual screening of 1549 deep-sea marine microbial metabolites against P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH). Molecular docking yielded five top-ranked compounds with Glide scores -12.02 to -10.61 kcal/mol (versus Primaquine -6.920 kcal/mol). MM-GBSA binding free energies -63.28 to -31.37 kcal/mol. Lead compounds: (±)-puniceusine P, aspergilol F, tersaphilone C, 4-carbglyceryl-3,3'-dihydroxy-5,5'-dimethyldiphenyl ether, 15-O-methyl ML-236A. Molecular dynamics simulations showed stable protein-ligand complexes warranting experimental validation.

articles.headline: Malaria attributable fraction 54-64% across Uganda; density thresholds underestimate incidence
articles.title_original: Estimating malaria attributable fraction using quantitative PCR in a longitudinal cohort in Eastern Uganda.
articles.authors: Martin AC, Wang Q, Babirye S, Arinaitwe E, Zedi M, Ssewanyana I, Namirimu FN, Nayebare P, Olwoch P, Tukwasibwe S, Jagannathan P, Nankabirwa JI, Kamya M, Dorsey G, Greenhouse B, Briggs J, Rodriguez-Barraquer I
articles.details: Longitudinal study of 659 individuals followed up to 3 years across three zones in eastern Uganda using qPCR parasite density distributions. P. falciparum prevalence 47-63%; over 95% parasitemic at least once. Overall malaria attributable fraction (MAF) 54-64% across zones. MAF highest in under-5 year-olds (72%), next highest in 5-15 year-olds (56%), lowest in adults >16 (45%). Nearly 50% of fevers with 10-100 parasites/μL attributed to malaria. MAF-corrected incidence 18% higher in under-fives versus definition using fever + ≥5000/μL threshold.

articles.headline: TGF-β-Tim-3 axis maintains macrophage phagocytic function in malaria infection
articles.title_original: TGF-β regulated Tim-3 sustains macrophage phagocytic function and confers protection in Plasmodium yoelii NSM-infected mice.
articles.authors: Xie X, Liang G, Chen M, Luo L, Yuan H, Chen S, Lu K, Peng W, Xu L, Xie H, Li L, Zhao S, Wei H, Pan X, Huang J
articles.details: Mechanistic study using Plasmodium yoelii NSM murine model with scRNA-seq, flow cytometry, and functional assays. Infection-induced Tim-3 downregulation observed on splenic macrophages. Tim-3+ macrophages exhibited enhanced antigen presentation and elevated reactive oxygen species and pro-inflammatory cytokine production. In vivo Tim-3 blockade exacerbated disease severity, increased parasitemia, and impaired macrophage phagocytic capacity without affecting T-cell responses. TGF-β upstream regulates Tim-3; TGF-β-induced Tim-3 upregulation potentiated macrophage phagocytosis of infected RBCs and conferred protection.

articles.headline: Abaya Woreda: 38% highly vulnerable to malaria in low-elevation areas
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria vulnerability hotspots using multi-criteria decision analysis, GIS, and remote sensing: a case study in Abaya Woreda, West Guji Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Diriba D, Demise D, Kenate B, Gobena D, Lemi M, Teferi N, Karuppannan S, Churiso G
articles.details: Geospatial vulnerability analysis of Abaya Woreda, West Guji Zone, Ethiopia integrating GIS, remote sensing (Landsat 8, SRTM DEM, CHIRPS precipitation), and Analytic Hierarchy Process weighting. Ten climatic, topographic, environmental, and demographic factors integrated via weighted overlay analysis in ArcGIS. Results: 38.3% of area highly vulnerable (associated with lower elevations, wetlands, water bodies, greater distance from health facilities, high population density); 49.1% moderately vulnerable; 12.6% low vulnerability. Authors conclude findings support evidence-based targeted malaria control planning by stakeholders.

articles.headline: Iron deficiency reduces malaria parasitemia in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Host iron deficiency protects against Plasmodium infection and drives parasite molecular reprofiling.
