articles.headline: 2025 funding cuts threaten Africa's malaria control progress
articles.title_original: African leaders and regional institutions need to take the bull by the horns: a perspective on the impact of the 2025 funding cuts to malaria programmes.
articles.authors: Osoro CB, Hill J
articles.details: Perspective article on 2025 malaria program funding reductions from US and other donors, affecting insecticide-treated bed net distribution, seasonal chemoprevention campaigns, and surveillance. Africa bears 94% of 263 million global malaria cases (2023). Calls for increased domestic funding and public-private partnerships across African governments and regional institutions.

articles.headline: Spatial mapping identifies high-risk urban malaria areas in Ghana
articles.title_original: Fine-scale spatial mapping of urban malaria prevalence for microstratification in an urban area of Ghana.
articles.authors: Oppong SK, Dosoo DK, Peprah NY, Adu GA, Mohammed W, Rozier J, Kayan K, McPhail M, Amratia P, Alene KA, Asante KP, Gething PW, Malm KL
articles.details: Geospatial study of 12,371 children (6 months–10 years) in Greater Accra, Ghana, integrating environmental, climatic, and urban covariates to predict malaria risk. Predicted prevalence ranged 0–49%. Satellite data on brightness, vegetation index, and urban covariates were predictive. Maps showed probability of prevalence exceeding 10%, identifying districts with high elimination-priority areas.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance reveals asymptomatic P. vivax reservoir in Ecuador
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic parasitaemia in a malaria pre-elimination setting in the Putumayo area of the Ecuadorian Amazon basin.
articles.authors: van der Ende J, Dávila MV, Menting S, Sáenz FE, Hanscheid T, Schallig HDFH, Grobusch MP
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 293 asymptomatic individuals in Putumayo, Ecuador (2023) using tiered diagnostics: RDT, microscopy, and qPCR (18S rRNA). RDT detected 0/293; microscopy identified 3 P. vivax infections (1.0%, 95% CI 0.2–2.4%); qPCR detected 5 infections (1.7%, 95% CI 0.6–3.9%), including 2 submicroscopic cases with very low parasite density (mean Cq 32.6). Demonstrates hidden reservoir missed by routine diagnostics.

articles.headline: P. falciparum–infected RBCs suppress megakaryocytic differentiation
articles.title_original: Interactions between Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells and their extracellular vesicles with megakaryocytes: implications for platelet-like particle formation.
articles.authors: Nunthanasup N, Palasuwan A, Palasuwan D, Combes V
articles.details: In vitro study using MEG-01 megakaryoblasts co-cultured with P. falciparum–infected RBCs (pRBCs) or their extracellular vesicles. pRBCs suppressed megakaryocytic markers, downregulated NOTCH3, and increased platelet-like particle production but with impaired clotting activity. pRBCs and EVs increased IL-8, RANTES, and MCP-1. Findings illuminate defective platelet production as a mechanism of malaria-associated thrombocytopenia.

articles.headline: Burkitt's lymphoma incidence declined with reduced malaria burden
articles.title_original: A "Familiar Foe Revisited": examining the relationship between endemic Burkitt's lymphoma and changing malaria admissions in the coastal region of Kenya.
articles.authors: Mwaniki MK, Mohammed S, Kariuki N, Wamalwa DC, Mutisya F, Newton CR
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 95 pediatric Burkitt's lymphoma cases over 30 years (1990–2020) in coastal Kenya. First decade: 29 cases (incidence 93.1/100,000); second: 62 cases (130.3/100,000); third: 4 cases (10.2/100,000; p<0.0001). Median P. falciparum parasite density decreased from 13,966 to 7,224 across epochs (p=0.0021). Correlation between BL incidence and malaria infection: r=0.53 (p=0.0024), suggesting malaria reduction explains BL decline.

articles.headline: Organoid models advance liver-stage malaria drug testing
articles.title_original: Experimental models of liver-stage malaria: Progress, gaps, and challenges.
articles.authors: Nitaramorn N, Kulkeaw K, Imwong M
articles.details: This review examines experimental in vitro models for Plasmodium liver-stage development, emphasizing organoid technology—three-dimensional, self-organizing structures that recreate native tissue microarchitecture. The authors identify strengths and limitations of current platforms and discuss how organoid advances can address key gaps in liver-stage modeling. No quantitative results are provided. These models are essential for developing preventive strategies and radical cures, particularly for P. vivax hypnozoites that cause relapse.

articles.headline: Dioscorea dumetorum fraction shows antimalarial activity in mice
articles.title_original: n-Butanol Fraction of Dioscorea dumetorum Tuber Aqueous Extract Exhibited Potent Antimalarial Activity in Plasmodium berghei-infected Mice.
articles.authors: Okeme U, Yakubu MT
articles.details: This study evaluated n-butanol and other fractions from D. dumetorum tuber extracts for antimalarial activity in P. berghei-infected mice. The n-butanol fraction (nBF) and its column-chromatography subfraction (CCF1) demonstrated the most potent inhibition, suppressing parasite growth by 49.13% and 40.80%, respectively (p < 0.05). Chemical profiling of CCF1 identified 11 compounds, with allantoin and protodioscin being relatively most abundant. Authors conclude nBF and CCF1 represent promising sources for novel antimalarial therapy development.

articles.headline: Barriers to formal malaria treatment persist in rural Malawi
articles.title_original: Predictors and characteristics of malaria treatment-seeking in Malawi: a longitudinal cohort study.
articles.authors: Wan C, Katsabola H, Sixpence A, Mangani C, Matengeni A, Taylor TE, Wilson ML, Valim C, Mathanga DP, Laufer MK, Cohee LM, Buchwald AG
articles.details: A longitudinal cohort study of 962 rural Malawian participants (7,293 total visits) examined treatment-seeking behavior from 2019–2020. Despite 82% of visits resulting in formal-sector treatment-seeking, longer walking distance (OR = 0.28 for >90 min vs <30 min) and higher household education (OR = 0.41 for secondary vs. no education) were associated with reduced formal-sector treatment uptake. P. falciparum infection and parasite density were not associated with health center attendance, while fever was positively associated (OR = 1.60, 95% CI 1.16–2.21).

articles.headline: Submicroscopic malaria infections high in indigenous Peruvian communities
articles.title_original: Hidden burden: submicroscopic Plasmodium spp. infections in indigenous populations of the Peruvian Amazon.
articles.authors: Saavedra-Samillán M, López-Ugarte LC, Sandoval-Bances J, Rojas LM, Udhayakumar V, Valdivia HO, Sanchez JF, Chenet SM
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 926 indigenous Peruvians in Río Santiago District found 17.0% (157/926) Plasmodium spp. infection, with 8.5% (79/926) submicroscopic infections; P. vivax was most frequent. Among submicroscopic infections, 74.7% (59/79) were asymptomatic; asymptomatic individuals had 2.32-fold higher prevalence (aPR = 2.32, 95% CI 1.63–3.29). Household possession of animals was protective (aPR = 0.64, 95% CI 0.48–0.85), and Caterpiza community residents showed 2.34-fold higher prevalence (aPR = 2.34, 95% CI 1.68–3.24).

articles.headline: Anxiety common among guardians of severely malarial children
articles.title_original: Prevalence of anxiety and associated factors among guardians of children admitted with severe malaria at Thyolo District Hospital in Malawi.
articles.authors: Chidzere N, Munthali C, Chapweteka G, Munthali T, Kumwenda P, Chikasema B, Mkwinda E, Chironda G
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 187 guardians of children with severe malaria in Malawi found 79.7% experienced mild anxiety, 10.7% moderate, and 9.6% minimal anxiety. Sociodemographic factors associated with higher anxiety included age, guardian type, education level, residence, and income (all p < 0.001). Clinically, longer hospital stays and repeated admissions were significantly associated with increased anxiety severity (p < 0.001). Authors conclude that integrating psychological support into pediatric care is essential for managing caregiver mental health in low-resource settings.

articles.headline: Microtubule acetylation not essential for Plasmodium or Toxoplasma
articles.title_original: Luminal acetylation of microtubules is not essential for Plasmodium berghei and Toxoplasma gondii survival.
articles.authors: Kumar T, Röver K, Stortz JF, Binder AM, Spreng B, Konert M, Meissner M, Frischknecht F, Jimenez-Ruiz E
articles.details: Genetic studies in P. berghei and T. gondii examined the role of luminal microtubule acetylation. Introducing a Q40K mutation in P. berghei α1-tubulin did not affect parasite development or infectivity; deletion of α-acetyltransferase (αTAT) in T. gondii had no impact on in vitro growth. Results indicate that luminal K40 acetylation is not essential for microtubule function in either species. Authors conclude this highlights functional redundancy and the plasticity of cytoskeletal regulation in apicomplexan parasites.

articles.headline: Clinical breast examination uptake very low in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Factors associated with the uptake of clinical breast examination for early detection of breast cancer: interaction analysis of the 2022 Tanzania Demographic and Health Survey and Malaria Indicator Survey.
articles.authors: Nyamhanga T, Luoga P, Paulo H, Wangwe P, Adam J, Eustace R
articles.details: Secondary analysis of Tanzania Demographic and Health Survey 2022 and Malaria Indicator Survey examined clinical breast examination (CBE) uptake among women aged 15–49. Women with low incomes, unemployment, and lack of health insurance had lower odds of CBE uptake. Interactions between age and residence, and between age and education, influenced the relationship with CBE. Overall CBE uptake was only 5%, emphasizing the need for targeted breast health awareness programs.

articles.headline: Severe malaria case with multiple WHO-listed complications
articles.title_original: We Still Have to Fear Malaria: A Case Report of Severe Malaria With Almost all the Listed WHO Complications in a Patient Living in a Sub-Saharan Endemic Area.
articles.authors: Simeni Njonnou SR, Tsiazok Dongmo M, Djilo Kouonang LO, Tsafack Tapondjou B, Ndembe Djeni E, Pierre Choukem S
articles.details: Case report of a 67-year-old woman with severe malaria presenting with nearly all WHO-listed complications: confusion, hemolytic anemia, severe hypoglycemia, status epilepticus, diffuse ecchymoses, digital ischemia, respiratory failure, hepatocellular failure, acute kidney injury, and hyperkalemia (6.24 mmol/L). Despite therapeutic improvements in consciousness, seizure resolution, and correction of metabolic abnormalities, the patient ultimately died of sepsis before digital amputation could be performed. The case underscores the severe consequences of malaria.

articles.headline: P. falciparum nucleoside transporters show low genetic diversity
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of Plasmodium falciparum equilibrative nucleoside transporters PfENT1 and PfENT4: Implications for purine-based antimalarial drug development.
articles.authors: Tashie W, de Koning HP, Duah-Quashie NO, Quashie NB
articles.details: Analysis of >7,000 P. falciparum isolates from worldwide regions examined genetic diversity in PfENT1 and PfENT4, nucleoside transporters in the purine salvage pathway and potential antimalarial drug targets. Only single nucleotide polymorphisms were identified; missense variant frequencies were 4.5% in PfENT1 and 6.2% in PfENT4. Common variants included F36S, V129I, and L133F in PfENT1 (minor allele frequencies 4.0%, 4.1%, 3.0%) and I188F, N209I, A260T, and S383A in PfENT4 (MAF 2.2%, 1.6%, 1.5%, 38.9%). High conservation and low diversity support their suitability as drug targets.

articles.headline: P. falciparum strains shifted from superinfection to co-transmission
articles.title_original: Assessing the impact of temporal changes in transmission on Plasmodium falciparum strains in Asembo, western Kenya (1996-2017) using within-host metrics via 24-SNP barcodes.
articles.authors: Vestal G, Zhou Z, Sergent S, Sheth M, Lee J, Otieno K, Kariuki S, Hill A, Ter Kuile FO, Lindblade KA, Slutsker L, Hamel MJ, Desai M, Gimnig JE, Samuels AM, Vigfusson Y, Shi YP
articles.details: Genomic surveillance using 24-SNP barcode genotyping of P. falciparum from Asembo, Kenya (1996–2017) assessed temporal transmission dynamics. Mean multiplicity of infection (MOI) decreased from 4.32 (1996) to 3.34 (2012) then increased to 3.49 (2017); ITN distribution campaigns tracked these changes. Within-host strain relatedness by identity-by-descent (IBD) was inversely correlated with MOI, and the within-host IBD–MOI slope metric indicated transmission dynamics shifted toward co-transmission from 2001–2012, then returned to superinfection patterns by 2017.

articles.headline: Dual-AI nets reduce malaria vector density in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Three-year trends in malaria transmission parameters under deployment of Interceptor®G1, Interceptor®G2 and PermaNet®3.0 insecticide-treated bed nets in South-Western Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Tapsoba M, Guelbeogo WM, Sanou A, Zongo S, Gogue C, Debe S, Arnett K, Davis K, Shannon J, Digre P, Mwesigwa J, Tynuv K, Fornadel C, N'Falé S, Robertson M, Challenger JD, Tougri G, Gansané A, Ranson H, Olivier G, Wagman J
articles.details: Longitudinal entomological study (2019–2022) in three Burkina Faso districts compared three ITN types in areas with high pyrethroid resistance: Interceptor®G1 (pyrethroid-only), Interceptor®G2 (pyrethroid-chlorfenapyr), and PermaNet®3.0 (pyrethroid-PBO). In Banfora (Interceptor G2), Anopheles density declined 88.7% over 3 years; in Gaoua (pyrethroid-only), density initially declined but rebounded in year 3; in Orodara (pyrethroid-PBO), density remained relatively stable (73,101 female mosquitoes collected). Sporozoite infection rate and entomological inoculation rate trends mirrored vector density changes.

articles.headline: ARIMA model forecasts stable malaria incidence in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Unraveling the spatiotemporal clustering of malaria incidence and modeling a decade of epidemiological data in Jimma Zone, Oromia, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Biruksew A, Demeke A, Birhanu Z, Golassa L, Sisay AL, Kemal J, Yewhalaw D
articles.details: Spatiotemporal analysis of malaria surveillance data (2012–2021) from 15 districts in Jimma Zone, Ethiopia, involving 1,718,471 screened individuals and 359,655 confirmed cases (20.93% positivity). P. falciparum accounted for 52.71%, P. vivax 43.62%, and mixed infections 3.67%. Annual parasite incidence decreased 96.13% (from 87.97 per 1,000 population in 2012 to 3.40 in 2021). ARIMA(0,1,3)×(1,1,0) modeling for 2022–2026 forecasts malaria incidence stability with seasonal fluctuations (peaks Aug–Nov and Mar–Jun).

articles.headline: Malaria elimination status in Cabo Verde reassessed
articles.title_original: "Cabo Verde: is it really malaria-free?".
articles.authors: Diogo R, Carvalho BT, Mendonça L, da Cunha MR, Pedro DM, Paramés F, Marques T, Paulo SE, Cristino JM, Pereira ÁA
articles.details: Article examines whether Cabo Verde is truly malaria-free. No abstract provided; specific epidemiological findings cannot be extracted.

articles.headline: Delayed diagnosis of imported malaria in Vietnam
articles.title_original: Delayed diagnosis and severe outcomes in imported malaria in Vietnam highlighting systematic gaps in detection.
articles.authors: Nguyen TTT, Do CD, Nguyen BHT, Pham HTT, Nong VM, Van Vu G, Doan TT
articles.details: Article examines delayed diagnosis and severe outcomes in imported malaria cases in Vietnam, highlighting systematic gaps in detection. No abstract provided; specific findings cannot be extracted.

articles.headline: Room-temperature stable LAMP for rapid knowlesi malaria detection
articles.title_original: Rapid Detection of Knowlesi Malaria Using Room-Temperature Stable and Ready-to-Use Colorimetric Loop-Mediated Isothermal Amplification Reagents.
articles.authors: Lai MY, Lau YL
articles.details: A dried-format, room temperature-stable loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) assay was developed for detecting P. knowlesi malaria in resource-limited settings. Testing on 75 Malaysian clinical samples showed 100% sensitivity (95% CI: 92.1–100.0%) and 100% specificity (95% CI: 88.4–100.0%) compared to nested PCR. Reagents remained stable at room temperature for up to 15 days, enabling deployment without cold-chain requirements.

articles.headline: Feasible tablet-based neurorehabilitation for cerebral malaria survivors
articles.title_original: A Pilot Feasibility Study of a Home Tablet-Based Neurorehabilitation Program and Serial Brain Vital Sign Monitoring for Survivors of Pediatric Cerebral Malaria.
articles.authors: Macoun SJ, Silverman JM, Brunet PR, Lewis JM, Kafoteka E, Ziba T, Kirby ED, Ighalo J, D'Arcy RCN, O'Brien N
articles.details: A pilot study evaluated home-based tablet neurorehabilitation (Dino Island) and objective brain health assessment via event-related potentials (ERPs) in 3–12-year-old survivors of pediatric cerebral malaria. Programs achieved 5% attrition and 100% positive family ratings, with 60% of parents reporting behavioral improvements. Both interventions were feasible, well-accepted, and affordable in sub-Saharan African settings.

articles.headline: Deep learning CNN models for automated malaria parasite detection
articles.title_original: Malaria Parasite Cell Classification Using Transfer Learning with State-of-the-Art CNN Architectures.
articles.authors: Laghari AA, Muhammad W, Memon ML, Hussain A, Kumar A
articles.details: Transfer learning with pretrained CNN architectures (VGG16, VGG19, Inception-v3, ResNet-18/34/50/101, Xception) classified malaria parasites in microscopic blood-smear images. ResNet-50 and ResNet-101 achieved approximately 89% accuracy, Xception 88%, compared to VGG-16's 80%. Models were fine-tuned on extensive labeled datasets of parasitized and uninfected cells.

articles.headline: Next-generation ITN effectiveness: entomology to real-world outcomes
articles.title_original: Cascades of effectiveness of next-generation insecticide-treated nets against malaria, from entomological trials to real-life conditions.
articles.authors: Champagne C, Lemant J, Assenga A, Kibondo UA, Lekundayo RG, Mbuba E, Moore J, Muganga JB, Ntabaliba WS, Odufuwa OG, Swai JK, Alexa M, Goers R, Golumbeanu M, Chitnis N, Ross A, N'Guessan R, Moore S, Pothin E
articles.details: Mathematical modeling quantified the cascade from entomological efficacy to population-level effectiveness of two next-generation insecticide-treated nets (Interceptor G2 with chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid and Olyset Plus with piperonyl butoxide-pyrethroid) in Tanzania. Beyond entomological factors, operational factors—functional survival, ITN use, and in-bed exposure—critically impacted overall effectiveness. Results were extensible via dashboard tool for setting-specific product selection.

articles.headline: No artemisinin resistance mutations detected in Iranian P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Absence of Artemisinin Resistance-Associated Mutations in the Plasmodium Falciparum Kelch 13 (K13) Propeller Domain Gene Following Widespread Use of Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapy in Iran.
articles.authors: Mohammadi S, Forouzesh F, Mehrizi AA, Shafi ZSM, Pakdel JD, Raeisi A, Pirahmadi S
articles.details: A study of 50 sequenced P. falciparum samples from symptomatic patients in Iran (2022–2023) found 98% (49/50) carried wild-type pfk13 alleles, with one sample harboring a novel nonsynonymous mutation (R513C, C1537T) not validated as a resistance marker. No validated artemisinin resistance-associated pfk13 mutations were detected 18 years after nationwide ACT implementation.

articles.headline: VAR2CSA-reactive IgG differences between P. falciparum and P. vivax
articles.title_original: VAR2CSA-reactive IgG in Brazilian women exposed to Plasmodium falciparum or P. vivax infection during pregnancy.
articles.authors: Dos Santos MI, Dombrowski JG, Hoffmann-Veltung H, Quintana MdP, Marinho CRF, Hviid L, Lopez-Perez M
articles.details: A serological study of pregnant Brazilian women measured VAR2CSA-reactive antibodies using two recombinant VAR2CSA preparations (FV2BIC and FV2CHO) and native antigen (IT4VAR04), alongside GLURP and PvDBP-specific IgG. FV2CHO-specific IgG was restricted to P. falciparum-exposed women and markedly Fc-afucosylated, while FV2BIC was not. PvDBP-specific IgG correlated with P. vivax exposure but did not correlate with VAR2CSA IgG, contradicting cross-reactivity hypotheses.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi Hsp90 structure and druggable pockets identified
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi Heat Shock Protein 90s: In Silico Analysis Reveals Unique Druggable Structural Features.
articles.authors: Daniyan MO, Singh H, Blatch GL
articles.details: Computational structural analysis of P. knowlesi heat shock protein 90 (PkHsp90) identified four isoforms with key differences from human Hsp90, particularly a plasmodial-specific hydrophobic extension in the cytosolic isoform's glycine-rich helix loop. Virtual screening identified three ZINC database compounds (ZINC22007970, ZINC724661072, ZINC724661078) predicted to bind strongly to the PkHsp90 active site with minimal human Hsp90 binding.

articles.headline: Non-falciparum malaria species circulation across West Africa
articles.title_original: Circulation of non-falciparum malaria species in West Africa: A literature review from 2000 to 2024.
articles.authors: Garba MN, Moustapha LM, Diallo MA, Diongue K, Seck MC, Doutchi M, Ndiaye M, Ibrahim ML, Ndiaye D, Badiane AS
articles.details: A systematic literature review of 31 studies (2000–2024) from West Africa documented Plasmodium species prevalence across 9 countries: P. falciparum in 93.5%, P. malariae in 80.6%, P. ovale in 77.4%, and P. vivax in 35.5% of reviewed papers. Most studies (54.8%) used RDTs, predominantly PfHRP2-based (70.6%). The review emphasizes need for more sensitive diagnostics and control strategies targeting asymptomatic infections.

articles.headline: Malaria diagnostic delays and epidemiology in Brazilian non-endemic state
articles.title_original: Clinical and epidemiological profiles of confirmed malaria cases in the state of Pernambuco, Brazil, from 2001 to 2022.
articles.authors: Guedes DL, Guimarães HMR, Padilha MCG, Pinto MCP, Silva MRND, Andrade EVO
articles.details: Analysis of 350 malaria cases in Pernambuco, Brazil (2001–2022) found median diagnostic delays of 4–10 days (Indigenous 10 days, Black 5 days, Mixed-race/White 4 days). Multivariate analysis found ethnicity, education, and displacement were not statistically significant delay predictors. P. vivax and P. falciparum were equally prevalent (49.7% and 47.5%), with cases showing seasonal clustering (p=0.0382) and non-significant declining annual trend (p=0.138).

articles.headline: Free healthcare policy improves malaria control in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Evaluating the effects of the free healthcare policy on clinical visits and malaria among children in Burkina Faso: a modeling study of past trends and future forecasts.
articles.authors: Ouédraogo AL, Offosse MJ, Zhang J, Toé S, Yaméogo P
articles.details: A modeling study in Burkina Faso (2015–2018) evaluated the free healthcare policy's effects using GLMM and Bayesian forecasting on 199,664 children across 192 villages. Clinical visits increased 82% (from 1.1 to 2 per child, p<0.001); malaria prevalence decreased from 98% to 80% (p<0.001); severe malaria declined from 6% to 2.2% (p<0.001). Bayesian forecasts predicted further reductions to 64% prevalence and 1.02% severe malaria by 2020, though geographic inequities in healthcare access persisted.

articles.headline: RBC donor variability affects P. falciparum culture reproducibility
articles.title_original: Improving reproducibility of Plasmodium falciparum culture: a large-language-model-driven literature review and a parasite growth variability assessment of donor red blood cells.
articles.authors: Mahomed SM, Hanscheid T, Almeida Y, Grobusch MP, Rebelo M
articles.details: LLM-assisted systematic review of 963 studies (2000-2025) plus experimental analysis with 69 RBC donors. Found inconsistent RBC sourcing reporting: 61.1% unspecified donor origin, 72.6% omitted blood group. Parasite growth varied 6.4-fold across donors (range 3.0-19.3). Low-growth donors had lower hemoglobin (12.3 vs 14.8 g/dL, p=0.03), history of anemia (p=0.01), greater malaria-endemic ancestry (p=0.03). Proposed STRuBeC reporting standard.

articles.headline: P. vivax mitochondrial NATs regulate severe malaria functions
articles.title_original: Integrative transcriptomic and machine learning approaches to decipher mitochondrial gene regulation in severe Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Roy P, Aggarwal Y, Kochar SK, Kochar DK, Das A
articles.details: Transcriptomic and machine learning analysis of mitochondrial gene regulation in severe P. vivax malaria. Found distinct expression signatures with natural antisense transcripts (NATs) as potent regulators linked to mitochondrial and translational pathways. Study based on limited clinical cohort; validation in diverse populations needed. Positions mitochondrial NATs as promising targets for antimalarial drug development disrupting parasite bioenergetics.

articles.headline: Abbott-Bioline RDT shows poor sensitivity in P. vivax outbreak
articles.title_original: The underperforming Abbott-Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.v rapid diagnostic test: a whiter shade of pale - where the truth is not plain to see.
articles.authors: Thein KS, Soe K, Tun SKS, Kuipers R, Janssen S, Tarning J, Htun Y, Day NP, Kyaw TT, Dondorp AM, White NJ, Smithuis FM
articles.details: Field comparison of Abbott-Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.v RDT vs microscopy in 187 febrile patients during P. vivax outbreak, Myanmar. RDT sensitivity 0.24 (95% CI 0.12-0.36) overall; 0.39 (95% CI 0.21-0.57) at WHO detection threshold (≥200/µL parasites); specificity 1.0. Low test-line intensity confirmed by ImageJ. Dangerous treatment delays resulted; WHO response was ineffective.

articles.headline: IgE-PfCSP interactions activate immune effector cells potently
articles.title_original: Biophysical and functional characterization of IgE interactions with a malaria antigen.
articles.authors: Adam AI, Sutton BJ, McDonnell JM
articles.details: Studied IgE variant CIS43ε binding to P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) via surface plasmon resonance. Found higher affinity for core Peptide 21 vs NANP repeat motifs. Multimeric Peptide 21 induced significant FcεRI cell degranulation, maximal at trimeric state. Full-length PfCSP triggered greater degranulation than tetrameric Peptide 21, supporting therapeutic potential of IgE antibodies.

articles.headline: Aurora kinase inhibitors active across P. falciparum life stages
articles.title_original: Targeting Aurora Kinases as Essential Cell-Cycle Regulators to Deliver Multi-Stage Antimalarials Against Plasmodium Falciparum.
articles.authors: Langeveld H, Maepa K, Maree M, Thibaud JL, Salomane N, Bridgwater R, Famodimu MT, Godoy LC, Pasaje CFA, Boonyalai N, de Souza ML, Fong J, Rabie T, van der Watt M, Theart RP, Ghidelli-Disse S, Niles JC, Lee MCS, Winzeler EA, Delves MJ, Chibale K, Wicht KJ, Coulson LB, Birkholtz LM
articles.details: Evaluated human Aurora kinase inhibitors against P. falciparum across developmental stages. Multiple compounds showed multistage potency (<250 nM) against asexual blood stages, liver schizonts, and male gametes with >1000x selectivity. Hesperadin, TAE684, AT83 most potent; hesperadin targets PfArk1, causing defective mitotic division with unsegregated, multi-lobed nuclei and aberrant microtubule organization.

articles.headline: PfARMA N-terminal region drives antibody response without protection
articles.title_original: The N-terminal region of malaria vaccine candidate Plasmodium falciparum asparagine-rich merozoite antigen is immunodominant and targeted by polyreactive antibodies.
articles.authors: Garza R, Marchioni JM, Honeycutt JD, Hurlburt NK, Torres C, Garcia A, Loranc E, Yemington E, Towers D, Ssewanyana I, Pancera M, Lavinder JJ, Jagannathan P, Greenhouse B, Bol S, Bunnik EM
articles.details: Studied naturally acquired B cell and antibody responses to PfARMA vaccine candidate in P. falciparum-exposed individuals. IgM+ and IgG responses predominantly targeted N-terminal intrinsically disordered region (IDR1), increasing with exposure. However, anti-PfARMA antibody levels did not correlate with protection in age/exposure-matched children. ~30% of unexposed individuals had polyreactive IgG binding IDR1.

articles.headline: Malaria RDT self-testing achieves 81.9% practicability score
articles.title_original: Practicability of a malaria rapid diagnostic self-test among artisanal gold miners in French Guiana: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Le-Querriou R, Lambert Y, Petit-Sinturel M, Plessis L, Bardon T, Carboni C, Suarez-Mutis M, Vreden S, Muller J, Bordalo-Miller J, Sanna A, Douine M
articles.details: Cross-sectional assessment of Bioline Malaria Ag RDT self-testing among 40 ASGM workers (median age 37, 73% male) in French Guiana after training. Overall practicability score 81.9% (95% CI 78.3-85.6): knowledge 83.9%, practice 84.6% unassisted (100% with guidance), interpretation 77.2%. Correct interpretation: 96.0% positive, 70.0% negative, 65.8% invalid strips. All RDTs produced valid results.

articles.headline: Seasonal Anopheles dynamics in southern Odisha endemic districts
articles.title_original: Seasonal dynamics and species diversity of Anopheles mosquitoes in malaria endemic districts of Southern Odisha India.
articles.authors: Baig MM, Koppula DT, Panigrahi DK, Vijayakumar B, Choudhary D, Acharya P, Patnaik M, Gunasekaran K, Kumar A, Rahi M, Shriram AN
articles.details: Survey of 10,807 Anopheles mosquitoes across 11 southern Odisha districts (March 2021-Feb 2023) identified 18 species. An. subpictus dominated summer (42.99%) and rainy season (46.60%); An. culicifacies (primary vector) 31.58% (rainy 2021) and 38.35% (rainy 2022); An. varuna peaked in winter (35.56% winter 2021-2022). Diversity highest in Ganjam (Shannon index 1.26-2.2). Seasonality significantly influenced diversity (p<0.001).

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria 3.4% in Ethiopian schoolchildren; age-associated
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic Plasmodium infection and predictors among schoolchildren in Bahir Dar Zuria District, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Muhye A, Abate MA, Ayehu A, Dejen P
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 416 schoolchildren in Bahir Dar Zuria, Ethiopia (March-May 2024). Asymptomatic Plasmodium prevalence 3.4% (95% CI 1.7-6.3), with 64.3% P. falciparum. Predictors: age (AOR=3.53, p=0.001), gender (AOR=2.87, p=0.021), stagnant water near home (AOR=3.76, p=0.031), never used LLINs (AOR=2.90, p=0.007), prior-year malaria (AOR=2.23, p=0.047).

articles.headline: Colombian malaria vectors distributed across ecoregions and elevations
articles.title_original: Ecoregional and altitudinal distribution of the principal malaria vectors in Colombia.
articles.authors: Carrasquilla MC, González R, Pineda SR, Ocampo CB, Montoya-Lerma J, Pareja-Loaiza P, Ahumada ML
articles.details: Analysis of 2,371 historical records (1929-2023) of five principal Colombian malaria vectors: An. albimanus, An. darlingi, An. nuneztovari s.s., An. neivai s.l., An. calderoni. Mostly below 500 m elevation, with An. albimanus and An. calderoni also above 1,000 m. An. darlingi strongly associated with forests; species show differential ecoregional distribution and elevation increase over time.

articles.headline: Malaria mortality drivers in South Sudanese children identified
articles.title_original: A Cox Proportional Hazards Model Approach to Identifying Malaria Risk Factors in Children in South Sudan.
articles.authors: Jumi LGL, Mohmmed AOA
articles.details: Hospital retrospective study of 6,410 children with malaria (January-December 2021, Al Sabah Children's Hospital, Juba); 56.08% male, mean age 23.66 months, 4.73% mortality. Cox hazards model: increased age (HR=1.0223, p<0.01), comorbidity (HR=0.5530 for absence, p<0.01) significant; quinine vs artesunate (HR=0.7107, p<0.01); higher body weight protective (HR=0.8513, p<0.01).

articles.headline: Anopheles daytime activity requires revised surveillance timing
articles.title_original: Malaria Mosquito Host-Seeking Activity Times in Manhiça District, Rural Mozambique, and the Need to Better Match Entomological Surveillance Strategies to Daylight Cycles.
articles.authors: Jobe NB, Máquina M, Opiyo MA, Martí-Soler H, Malheia A, Marrenjo D, Cuamba N, Pino D, Saúte F, Paaijmans KP
articles.details: Host-seeking activity assessment of Anopheles mosquitoes in Manhiça district, Mozambique. An. tenebrosus most abundant; daytime (06:00-18:00) host-seeking observed in only one An. ziemanni specimen. Sunrise/sunset times across African countries vary >2 hours annually by latitude/longitude. Conclusion: entomological surveillance strategies need adjustment to match actual daylight cycles for actionable data.

articles.headline: Hospital malaria burden and seasonal patterns in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Malaria Trends, Burden, Seasonal Variation, and Interventions in Western Tigray, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Kassahun GB, Berhe AM, Brhanu MA, Aregawi BB
articles.details: Retrospective review of 36,438 microscopically confirmed malaria cases from 2011-2019 in Western Tigray, Ethiopia found 50 deaths (0.14% mortality) and 2,016 hospital admissions (5.5%). P. falciparum predominated (22,621 cases, 62.1%). Highest deaths occurred in patients ≥15 years (38 deaths, 0.10%) and during October-December (21 deaths). Malaria case trends showed significant annual fluctuation (p=0.001), though mortality trends were not statistically significant (p=0.62), indicating malaria remains a major health burden despite Ethiopia's control progress.

articles.headline: Malaria risk varies by location and outdoor activity timing
articles.title_original: Malaria risk, treatment-seeking, and prevention in Northeastern India: clinic-based surveillance of persistent transmission.
articles.authors: Lyngdoh P, Sarkar R, Wilson ML, Lyngdoh I, Kessler A, Carlton JM, Albert S
articles.details: Facility-based surveillance of 1,031 symptomatic patients across three districts in Meghalaya, India (2018-2021) identified 45 Plasmodium infections (4.4%), predominantly P. falciparum (60%). Prevalence ranged from 1.8% to 12.3% across districts. Prior malaria diagnosis within 12 months was significantly associated with infection (OR=9.2, p<0.001), as were outdoor activities before bedtime (OR=2.0, p=0.023) and early morning (OR=3.1, p=0.010), highlighting heterogeneous local transmission risk factors.

articles.headline: Dried blood spot PfHRP2 detection stable up to 120 days
articles.title_original: The reliability of malaria rapid diagnostic test kit for detecting P. falciparum PfHRP2 in dried blood spot samples preserved under different conditions and durations.
articles.authors: Aninagyei E, Apekey J, Boatey CA, Banono E, Adu JO, Boakye AA
articles.details: Study of 900 dried blood spot samples from 60 P. falciparum patients tested PfHRP2 detectability across storage conditions (room temperature, 6°C, -60°C) and durations (30-150 days). PfHRP2 remained reproducibly detectable through 120 days regardless of storage condition. At 150 days, positivity rates declined to 90% (room temperature), 88.3% (6°C), and 80% (-60°C), with no statistically significant differences (p=0.236). Room temperature provided the most cost-effective, equipment-free approach for up to 120 days of storage.

articles.headline: P. simium reference genome reveals zoonotic host adaptation
articles.title_original: Novel zoonotic cases of Plasmodium simium from São Paulo with a reference genome of the Brazilian strain.
articles.authors: Manko E, Billows N, Higgins M, Ward D, Ibrahim A, Campino S, Di Santi SM, Clark TG
articles.details: High-quality P. simium reference genome (Ps_SãoPaulo, 21.8 Mb) was generated from Brazilian zoonotic cases using Nanopore and Illumina sequencing. Comparative genomic analysis with P. vivax identified mitochondrial mutations, deletions in reticulocyte-invasion genes (RBP2a, DBP), and variants in blood-stage antigens (msp3.1, msp3.2). Genome-wide analysis of 38 Brazilian P. simium samples revealed expected divergence from P. vivax and potential signatures of selection and host adaptation, advancing understanding of zoonotic malaria control.

articles.headline: Intradermal vaccine delivery reduces protective immune responses
articles.title_original: Intradermal immunization with Plasmodium berghei late-arresting genetically attenuated sporozoites induces PD-L1 expression on regulatory macrophages and dendritic cells.
articles.authors: van Schuijlenburg R, Naar CM, de Bes-Roeleveld HM, Chevalley-Maurel S, Zigó LA, Houlder EL, Lambooij JM, Baalbergen E, Roestenberg M, Franke-Fayard B
articles.details: Comparison of intradermal (ID) versus intravenous (IV) delivery of P. berghei late-arresting attenuated sporozoites in mice showed ID vaccination produced lower frequencies of splenic CD8+ tissue-resident memory T cells (1.3%, p=0.3) and lung Trm (8.1%, p=0.005). ID-vaccinated mice had significantly reduced hepatic granzyme B (p<0.0001) and perforin (p<0.0001), indicating diminished cytotoxic potential. ID immunization induced a regulatory myeloid phenotype with low CD86 and high PD-L1 expression in skin-draining lymph nodes, suggesting systemic immunosuppression compared to IV delivery.

articles.headline: Monthly prophylaxis outperforms test-and-treat for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Evaluating malaria elimination strategies among military forces in Cambodia: a multi-arm clinical trial comparing monthly prophylaxis and focused screening and treatment.
articles.authors: Wojnarski M, Chaudhury S, Boonchan T, Bun R, Chann S, Gosi P, Soveasna K, Song S, Buathong N, Ittiverakul M, Sriwichai S, Arsanok M, Kuntawunginn W, Saingam P, Chaisatit C, Ponlawat A, Fansiri T, Vanachayangkul P, Jaichapor B, Sinoun M, Chuor CM, Kheangheng T, So M, Wanja E, Davidson S, Spring M, Rekol H, Dysoley L, Saly K, Livezey JR, Lin JT, Smith PL, Satharath P, Manning JE, Sok S, Saunders DL
articles.details: Randomized trial in 1,050 Cambodian military personnel compared monthly malaria prophylaxis (MMP: three doses of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine plus 22.5 mg weekly primaquine) versus focused screening-and-treatment (FSAT) over 6 months. MMP reduced P. falciparum positivity 90% (10% to 1%; ARR 9%), though 32% of cases recrudesced at 1 month. FSAT achieved 66% reduction (7.6% to 2.7%; ARR 4.9%). MMP eliminated P. vivax by month 3 but relapsed to 6.7% by month 6; FSAT showed no significant vivax reduction. Low-dose weekly primaquine was safe in 15% of volunteers with G6PD-deficiency.

articles.headline: Plasmepsin V confirmed as essential parasite drug target
articles.title_original: Deconvolution of the On-Target Activity of Plasmepsin V Peptidomimetics in Plasmodium falciparum Parasites.
articles.authors: Su W, Nguyen W, Siddiqui G, Dziekan JM, Marapana D, Penington JS, Mehra S, Razook Z, McCann K, Ngo A, Jarman KE, Barry AE, Papenfuss AT, Gilson PR, Creek DJ, Cowman AF, Sleebs BE, Dans MG
articles.details: Chemoproteomic studies validated peptidomimetic inhibitors of plasmepsin V (PMV), an aspartyl protease essential for P. falciparum protein export into host red blood cells. Whole-genome sequencing of parasite lines with reduced peptidomimetic sensitivity identified a single nucleotide variant in the pmv gene, confirmed by reverse genetics to mediate resistance. These findings provide strong evidence that peptidomimetics directly target PMV and validate it as a promising target for antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: Humanized mouse model aligns drug metabolism with clinical outcomes
articles.title_original: Humanization of Drug Metabolism in the Plasmodium berghei Mouse Model for Antimalarial Drug Discovery.
articles.authors: MacLeod AK, Merino C, Viera-Morilla S, Frame L, Tavendale A, Duncan C, Ferguson L, Pinto EG, Simeons FRC, Gomez-Jimenez V, García AB, Riley J, Shishikura Y, Martínez-Martínez MS, Wolf CR, Read KD
articles.details: The 8HUM mouse line, genetically humanized for cytochrome P450 enzymes and transcriptional regulators, was infected with P. berghei to improve translational accuracy of antimalarial drug discovery. Approved antimalarials showed greater metabolic stability in 8HUM compared to wild-type mice. Major species differences between humans and mice in metabolic elimination routes for quinine derivatives were eliminated in 8HUM, enabling early proof-of-concept demonstration of drug efficacy with improved alignment to clinical drug metabolism.

articles.headline: Plant essential oils show promise as mosquito spatial repellents
articles.title_original: Spatial Exposure Responses of Malaria Vectors to Eucalyptus grandis (W. Hill ex Maiden) and Cymbopogon citratus (DC.) Stapf Essential Oils.
articles.authors: Kaddumukasa MA, Mutekanga NM, Kula F, Batume C, Kato AB
articles.details: Laboratory Y-tube olfactometry testing of Anopheles gambiae Kisumu strain evaluated spatial behavior responses to essential oils from Eucalyptus grandis and Cymbopogon citratus. E. grandis contained L-α terpineol and eucalyptol as major constituents, while C. citratus contained lavandulol methyl ether and citral. Both oils demonstrated dose-dependent mosquito repellency at multiple concentrations. The study identifies these natural plant products as promising lead compounds for developing botanical spatial repellents as alternatives in the era of insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: Parasite modulates vector sterol metabolism, facilitating transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax infection-driven modulation of sterol carrier protein reveals a metabolic link to reproductive physiology in Anopheles stephensi.
articles.authors: Kumari S, Yadav P, Sankhala N, Rani J, Sharma G, Chauhan C, Tevatiya S, Dixit R
articles.details: During P. vivax infection in Anopheles stephensi, sterol carrier protein (SCP) expression is dynamically modulated—upregulated in midgut and salivary glands but suppressed in hemocytes—despite normal strong induction after blood feeding. RNAi-mediated SCP knockdown significantly reduced mosquito fecundity, demonstrating critical role in reproductive physiology. The findings reveal SCP as a key metabolic link between nutrient uptake and reproduction, with parasite-driven modulation potentially facilitating parasite growth and transmission.

articles.headline: CD103+ T cells remain pre-activated during Plasmodium infection
articles.title_original: Characteristics of CD103(+)CD8(+) T cells in the spleen of Plasmodium yoelii NSM-infected mice.
articles.authors: Pan X, Shi F, Tang S, Liu M, Pan L, Liang G, Li L, Xie H, Zhao S, Huang J
articles.details: Single-cell RNA sequencing of spleens from P. yoelii NSM-infected C57BL/6 mice showed CD103+CD8+ T cells declined substantially post-infection and exhibited lower activation markers than CD103-CD8+ T cells, with higher CD62L but lower CD44, CD69, and TIGIT expression. CD103+CD8+ cells rarely secreted IFN-γ or granzyme B upon stimulation. Transcriptomic analysis enriched for ribosome biosynthesis pathways, suggesting a pre-activation preparatory state. Bioinformatics predicted transcription factor LEF1 regulates Itgae transcription.

articles.headline: Malaria burden remains high despite Uganda's control progress
articles.title_original: Spatial and Temporal Distribution of Febrile Illnesses in Uganda: Case Studies of Brucellosis, Malaria and Typhoid Fever (2016-2023).
articles.authors: Aceng FL, Arinaitwe ES, Muruta A, Deza-Cruz I, Ekiri AB
articles.details: Retrospective surveillance analysis of Uganda (2016-2023) recorded 110.1 million malaria cases with overall 4% prevalence decrease (43,316 to 29,271 cases/100,000 population), alongside 2% decrease in typhoid (538 to 445 cases/100,000) and 8% decrease in brucellosis (151 to 89 cases/100,000). Northern regions showed highest malaria and brucellosis burden; central regions highest typhoid. Despite progress, malaria prevalence substantially exceeded other febrile illnesses, indicating continued need for strengthened national control programs in most-affected regions.

articles.headline: Community acceptability of perennial malaria chemoprevention in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Investigating factors enabling the acceptability of perennial malaria chemoprevention implementation in Osun State, Nigeria: evidence from a qualitative process evaluation.
articles.authors: Ekholuenetale M, Aransiola JO, Ujuju C, Ogbulafor N, Oroge O, Garba RA, Rahman SA, Maxwell K, Awosanya RA, Adekola MA, Ilesanmi BB, Ajayi A, Ogundun OA, Oresanya O, Tibenderana JK
articles.details: Qualitative study of caregivers, health workers, and community leaders in Osun State on acceptability of PMC for children under 24 months delivered through EPI platform. Acceptability influenced by perceived effectiveness, child-friendliness, free services, and delivery platform; negatively by transportation costs and side-effect fears. Health worker attitudes and religious acceptance shaped uptake.

articles.headline: High malaria vaccine acceptance despite low prior awareness in Sudan
articles.title_original: Awareness and acceptance of the malaria vaccine among caregivers attending primary health care centres in Sudan: a mixed-methods study.
articles.authors: Hamid A, Elsir R, Abbas E, Ibrahim N, Ahmed F, Mohamed S, Adam M, Abdalla R, Abuzaid M, Alameen F, Ahmed M, Bakhit A, Abdalla E
articles.details: Cross-sectional mixed-methods study of 159 caregivers at primary healthcare centres (Nov 2024–Jan 2025). Despite 11.3% prior awareness, 86.2% accepted malaria vaccine. Acceptance associated with perceived malaria severity (p<0.001), caregiver vaccination history (p=0.034), and complete child immunization (p=0.047). Safety concerns and healthcare system skepticism were main hesitancy drivers.

articles.headline: Modeling ITN use and behavior to achieve malaria elimination targets
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated net use and elimination of malaria in Sub-Saharan African countries: assessing the Global Technical Strategy using an evolutionary game approach.
articles.authors: Laxmi, Oraby T, Tyshenko MG, Danquah I, Bhattacharyya S
articles.details: Behavior-incidence model and statistical analysis of historical ITN usage and malaria case data across sub-Saharan Africa (2014–2021). Three-phase analysis (pre-estimation, estimation, projection) classified countries by transmission rates, mosquito prevalence, and socioeconomic conditions. Some countries can achieve GTS 2025/2030 targets through current ITN efforts; others require enhanced social awareness campaigns, economic assistance, and improved ITN efficacy.

articles.headline: Geostatistical modeling identifies persistent high-risk malaria villages in Myanmar
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal trends in P. falciparum malaria and identification of high-risk villages in Eastern Myanmar: an 8-year observational study.
articles.authors: Rae JD, Parker DM, Maude RJ, Thu AM, Devine A, Nosten FH, Simpson JA
articles.details: Geostatistical analysis of routine surveillance data (2014–2021) from 507 village-based posts in Hpapun Township, Myanmar. P. falciparum incidence declined 93.9% from 2014–2019. Targeted-intervention villages showed higher pre-intervention incidence (IRR 4.72, 95% CI 4.56–4.90) and lower post-intervention incidence (IRR 0.26, 95% CI 0.24–0.27). In 2021, 12 high-risk villages identified; 8 remained persistently high-risk.

articles.headline: High RDT variability in Africa driven by HRP2 genetic polymorphism
articles.title_original: Performance and challenges of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in endemic regions of Africa.
articles.authors: Ojeniyi FD, Ayoola AO, Ibitoye O, Opaleye OO, Olowe OA, Ehigie LO, Thomas BN, Ojurongbe O
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 48 studies evaluating malaria RDT diagnostic performance across sub-Saharan Africa. Found considerable sensitivity/specificity variability influenced by parasite genetic diversity, antigen persistence, cross-reactivity, and HRP2 gene polymorphisms leading to false positives and negatives. Recommends region-specific diagnostic strategies and multi-platform approaches to enhance accuracy and detect low-level parasitemia.

articles.headline: K13 R622I artemisinin resistance expands in Ethiopia with HRP2-RDT failure
articles.title_original: High prevalence and emerging positive association of kelch13 R622I and HRP2-based RDT negativity in Plasmodium falciparum in northern Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Zeleke AJ, Fola AA, Tollefson GA, Niaré K, Leonetti A, Taropawala O, Marglous J, Crudale R, Brhane BG, Assefa A, Kiyuka P, Parr JB, Hailu A, Tegegne MA, Bailey JA
articles.details: Genomic surveillance of 903 P. falciparum isolates (year-long study) in northwestern Ethiopia using MIP panels. K13 R622I mutation found in 44.3% overall (52% Gondar Zuria vs 35% Tach Armachiho; p<0.001); persisted year-round. HRP2-RDT negativity in 39.3% with district variation (48.7% vs 27.6%; p<0.001). R622I and HRP2-negativity co-occurred in 22% (28.9% vs 12.9%; p<0.001); HRP2-negativity more common in R622I than wild-type (48.3% vs 30.7%; p<0.05). K13 C580Y detected at 0.4%, first Horn of Africa report with distinct African haplotype.

articles.headline: Anopheles funestus drives malaria resurgence after IRS change in Uganda
articles.title_original: Disentangling the roles of different vector species during a malaria resurgence in Eastern Uganda.
articles.authors: McClure M, Oruni A, Arinaitwe E, Musiime A, Kyagamba P, Otto G, Adiga J, Rwatooro JA, Kilama M, Krezanoski P, Briggs J, Rosenthal PJ, Nankabirwa JI, Kamya MR, Dorsey G, Greenhouse B, Rodriguez-Barraquer I
articles.details: Cohort study of 422 children in 94 households (2021–2023) in Tororo and Busia districts using qPCR and entomologic sampling. Overall, doubling of annualized household EIR increased P. falciparum hazard by 29% (adjusted HR 1.29, 95% CI 1.25–1.33). Species-specific: An. funestus doubling increased hazard by 29% (HR 1.29, 1.25–1.34) while An. gambiae showed smaller effect (HR 1.04, 1.01–1.08). Associations stronger in Tororo.

articles.headline: Widespread pyrethroid and DDT resistance in Anopheles gambiae across Ghana
articles.title_original: Trends and patterns of insecticide susceptibility of Anopheles gambiae between 2015 and 2020: implications for malaria vector control interventions in Ghana.
articles.authors: Asafu-Adjaye A, Chabi J, Malm K, Akrofi OO, Coleman S, Dery DB, Yihdego Y, Frempong KK, Pi-Bansa S, Asigbaase MA, Pwalia R, Joannides J, Sakyi KY, Akuoko OK, Osei JHN, Athinya DK, Baffoe-Wilmot A, Bart-Plange C, Boakye DA, Dadzie SK
articles.details: Five-year monitoring (2015–2020) of insecticide susceptibility in 30 sentinel sites using WHO tube assays on adult female An. gambiae. High resistance to pyrethroids (deltamethrin, permethrin) and DDT detected in all sites with declining susceptibility over time. Reduced susceptibility to carbamates and organophosphates across all sites. L1014F (kdr-west) frequency higher than G119S ace-1 mutations with site variations.

articles.headline: Diagnostic delay increases severe malaria risk during COVID-19 co-circulation
articles.title_original: Diagnostic delay inducing severe imported falciparum malaria: a time-sensitivity comparison of two imported cases during COVID-19 co-circulation.
articles.authors: Gong QH, Wan SB
articles.details: Comparative analysis of two imported P. falciparum cases in Shanghai (2021). Case 1: 96-hour diagnostic delay progressed to severe malaria with delirium, thrombocytopenia (33×10⁹/L), acute kidney injury (creatinine 278 μmol/L), intensive care required. Case 2: 24-hour diagnosis via RDT and microscopy with immediate artesunate avoided severe complications. Highlights critical importance of rapid parallel SARS-CoV-2/malaria testing.

articles.headline: High SP resistance markers but no validated artemisinin resistance in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum drug resistance and vaccine targets in high-transmission settings in Africa.
articles.authors: Aliyu IA, Bala JA, Musa BM, Amole TG, Ahmed AB, Kovalenko G, Umar A, Garba Y, Gwarzo SA, Mansur A, Hidaya MG, Aliyu MH, Galadanci HS
articles.details: Oxford Nanopore amplicon sequencing of P. falciparum isolates from northern Nigeria profiling drug-resistance genes (crt, dhfr, dhps, mdr1, k13) and vaccine-target gene (csp). Found dhfr-IRNI at 90%, dhps-SGKAA at 45% (SP resistance markers), but no WHO-validated artemisinin-resistance k13 mutations; novel nonsynonymous k13 variants detected. Pfcrt-76T at ~25% frequency; csp polymorphisms predicted to affect R21/Matrix-M vaccine protein stability. Geographic clustering in Kano/Yobe.

articles.headline: Blood group antigens shape malaria susceptibility via parasite-host mechanisms
articles.title_original: Blood group antigens and malaria susceptibility.
articles.authors: Liu FF, Liang J, Gao M, Li K
articles.details: Review synthesizing evidence on how erythrocyte surface molecules (ABO, Duffy, MNS, Gerbich, Knops, Diego blood groups) modulate malaria susceptibility. Covers molecular mechanisms affecting parasite invasion, cytoadherence, immune evasion, and disease severity. Discusses population heterogeneity, parasite-host adaptations and counter-adaptations, and clinical implications for risk stratification, transfusion safety, and vaccine design.

articles.headline: Healthcare-associated falciparum transmission identified in New York
articles.title_original: Case Report and Epidemiological Investigation of Healthcare-associated Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Transmission in Westchester County, New York-2023.
articles.authors: White JL, Southwick K, Madison-Antenucci S, Piedmonte NP, Gayen S, Clemons B, Quinn M, Barrett K, Backenson PB, Antwi M, Slavinski S, Lee E, Huang A, Smith K, Guzzardi E, Hunter JC, McKinney KL, Mace KE, Ridpath AD, Williams S, Barratt JLN, Jacobson D, Pierre-Louis E, Bajic M, Kelley J, McElroy PD, Raphael BH, Bulbin A
articles.details: Epidemiological investigation of P. falciparum case in 65-year-old Westchester County, New York resident (diagnosed October 2, 2023) with no recent endemic-area travel. Genetic analysis and epidemiologic investigation implicated blood-borne transmission in healthcare setting from concurrently hospitalized traveler with malaria (mechanism unclear, likely infection-control lapse). No mosquito-borne transmission detected; no secondary cases identified.

articles.headline: Suriname's malaria elimination strategy applicable to Amazon regions
articles.title_original: Is malaria elimination in the Amazon rainforest feasible? The case of Suriname.
articles.authors: Vreden S, Eersel M, Bretas G, Cairo H, van Eer E, Adhin M
articles.details: This opinion paper outlines Suriname's malaria elimination strategy, which combines robust surveillance, early diagnosis and treatment access, retreatment approaches, widespread insecticide-treated net distribution, and sustained community engagement. The authors assert that similar strategies could be effectively adapted and applied in comparable Amazon rainforest settings.

articles.headline: Burundi malaria control: perceived barriers across intrapersonal, organizational, policy levels
articles.title_original: Understanding perceptions of malaria control measures in Burundi: a qualitative descriptive study using a socio-ecological approach.
articles.authors: Niyonsaba T, Ndayisaba R, Dumont C, Muhoza C, Lampaert E, Alfred AD, Pellecchia U, Uwimana F, Bucumi A, Irabaheta N, Marisasi D, Bigirimana I, Ndayiragije S, Rwimo A, Nishemezwe D, Nimubona D, Niyomwungere L, Ndihokubwayo F, Iradukunda O, Irambona E, Nicobaharaye C, Sindayikengera J, Van Bortel W, Colombe S, Meudec M
articles.details: A qualitative descriptive study conducted in Burundi from March to May 2023 involved 60 individual interviews and 28 focus group discussions (primarily farmers, 53–57.5% female) examining perceptions and practices regarding four control interventions: LLINs, IRS, IPTp-SP, and environmental management. The study identified adoption barriers across intrapersonal (adverse effects, perceived needs), organizational (information dissemination, facility hygiene), and policy/environmental (contextual adaptation, socio-economic constraints) levels. The authors recommend a multifaceted approach integrating communication, contextual adaptation, adverse effect management, and socio-economic consideration.

articles.headline: Genetically engineered mosquitoes block malaria parasite development
articles.title_original: Genetically engineered mosquitoes block development of circulating malaria strains.
articles.details: Title indicates genetically engineered mosquitoes can block development of circulating malaria strains. No abstract available.

articles.headline: Plasmodium inositol phosphate pathway and IPMK identified as novel antimalarial drug targets
articles.title_original: Unveiling the Plasmodium inositol (pyro)phosphate pathway: Highlighting inositol polyphosphate multikinase as a novel therapeutic target for malaria.
articles.authors: Obuobi A, Quashie NB, Duah-Quashie NO, Sayers JR
articles.details: An exploratory study characterized Plasmodium falciparum inositol phosphate pathway (IPP) kinases using in silico and molecular approaches, validating findings across laboratory strains and a clinical isolate. The study identified a novel p.Pro375Gln substitution in IPMK1 in field isolates and provides molecular evidence that the IPP represents an attractive antimalarial drug target. Inositol polyphosphate multikinase (IPMK) was identified as a particularly promising target due to its unique structural characteristics.

articles.headline: Angola: limitations of RDTs necessitate microscopy and molecular diagnostics
articles.title_original: The risks of exclusive reliance on rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in Angola.
articles.authors: Chitumba HO
articles.details: This opinion piece highlights the risks of relying exclusively on rapid diagnostic tests in Angola's malaria programs. The author notes that RDTs limit species identification and parasite quantification, and recommends strengthening microscopy capacity and integrating molecular diagnostics to improve clinical management, surveillance capability, and detection of drug-resistant parasites.

articles.headline: Florida P. vivax outbreak: enhanced blood donor screening yields all nonreactive results
articles.title_original: Heightened Nontesting Risk Mitigation Blood Donor Screening with Retrospective Transcription-Mediated Amplification Testing during a 2023 Autochthonous Florida Malaria Cluster.
articles.authors: Sullivan DJ, Lanteri MC, Bres V, Hanhan M, Pagan M, Rey A, Thomas K, Reik RA
articles.details: During a 2023 autochthonous Plasmodium vivax outbreak in Sarasota County, Florida (7 cases without travel history), a blood center implemented escalated donor screening using transcription-mediated amplification (TMA, limit of detection 2–7 infected erythrocytes/mL) on 258 samples from Sarasota and 178 from Miami (July–October). All 436 donor samples tested nucleic acid nonreactive, and no local transfusion-transmitted malaria cases were reported despite an estimated baseline transfusion-malaria risk of less than 1 per 10 million donations.

articles.headline: Five adjuvants significantly enhance anti-CSP antibody magnitude and durability in VLP vaccine
articles.title_original: A Comparative Evaluation of Adjuvants for Enhancing Antibody Responses to a Nanoparticle-Based Malaria Vaccine.
articles.authors: Nepal Y, Francian A, Roberts BT, Khan R, Wondra G, Demierre A, Apel F, Chackerian B
articles.details: A comparative study evaluated different adjuvant classes to enhance the immunogenicity of a circumsporozoite protein (CSP)-epitope-targeted virus-like particle (VLP)-based vaccine against Plasmodium falciparum. Five formulations significantly increased anti-CSP IgG titers, and several enhanced antibody durability. These findings identify promising adjuvants that enhance both the magnitude and longevity of VLP-induced immunity.

articles.headline: Microhaplotype diversity metrics estimate malaria prevalence but have limited incidence prediction
articles.title_original: Accuracy of Plasmodium falciparum genetic data for estimating parasite prevalence and malaria incidence in Uganda.
articles.authors: Kiyaga S, Mbabazi M, Katairo T, Kabbale KD, Asua V, Nsengimaana B, Wiringilimaana I, Semakuba FD, Mwubaha C, Nakasaanya J, Watyekele E, Ayitewala A, Tukwasibwe S, Mulondo J, Nsobya SL, Agaba B, Maiteki-Sebuguzi C, Kamya MR, Kateete DP, Nabende JN, Jjingo D, Mboowa G, Batte C, Ssewanyana I, Aranda-Díaz A, Dorsey G, Rosenthal PJ, Conrad M, Greenhouse B, Briggs J
articles.details: A genetic epidemiology study analyzed P. falciparum microhaplotype data (amplicon deep sequencing of 165 polyallelic microhaplotypes) from 3563 symptomatic cases across 26 Uganda sentinel facilities (2023). Prevalence ranged 5.0%–49.23% and incidence ranged 33–1667 cases per 1000 person-years across sites. Within-host complexity of infection (COI) and effective COI (eCOI) were strongly positively associated with prevalence and yielded good predictive models (cross-validated r=0.79 for prevalence), while between-host relatedness metrics best predicted incidence (r=0.37). Authors conclude that parasite genomic metrics reliably capture spatial variation in prevalence but have limited ability to predict incidence.

articles.headline: NEK4 kinase is essential regulator of Plasmodium meiosis and zygote morphogenesis
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.
articles.authors: Yanase R, Hair M, Zeeshan M, Ferguson DJP, Brady D, Pasquarello C, Bottrill A, Bhanvadia S, Neal A, Tromer EC, Le Roch KG, Hainard A, Holder AA, Vaughan S, Guttery DS, Tewari R
articles.details: A mechanistic study in P. berghei identified NEK4 (NIMA-related kinase) as a key regulator coupling meiotic initiation with zygote morphogenesis. NEK4 localizes to the microtubule-organizing center and apical polar complex after fertilization. Deletion of nek4 causes complete developmental arrest with failure of MTOC duplication, microtubule formation, chromatin condensation, and nuclear migration. Transcriptomic and phosphoproteomic analyses revealed that NEK4 absence collapses transcriptional and phosphoregulatory networks critical for meiosis and cytoskeletal organization, with reduced expression of HOP1, REC8, and AP2-O.

articles.headline: Fractional-order mathematical model captures memory effects and multi-pathway malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Analytical and data-driven fractional-order malaria transmission model with vector and non-vector pathways.
articles.authors: Ahman QO, Okpara PA, Agbata BC, Senewo EO, Didigwu NE
articles.details: This study develops a Caputo fractional-order differential equation model of malaria transmission that incorporates both vector and non-vector pathways and embeds memory effects in human-mosquito dynamics. Using parameter estimation against weekly malaria incidence data from Nigeria (NCDC), the model demonstrates that decreasing fractional order q delays epidemic peaks and prolongs oscillatory persistence. The addition of non-vector transmission pathways increases infection persistence and improves model correspondence with field data, validating the framework for malaria forecasting and control applications.

articles.headline: Nigerian RDTs show 69.7% sensitivity, 81.5% specificity; suboptimal versus WHO benchmarks
articles.title_original: Performance of rapid diagnostic tests for malaria diagnosis in Nigeria: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Ismail M, Mukhtar A, Odetokun IA, Ibrahim MA, Salman AA, Bawa-Sani HG, Nass SS, Goni BW, Nuhu A, Zhang X, Usman A, Isah MB
articles.details: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 studies evaluated malaria RDT performance versus microscopy in Nigeria. Pooled analysis found RDT sensitivity of 69.7% (95% CI 67.7–71.6%), specificity of 81.5% (95% CI 79.9–83.1%), and diagnostic odds ratio of 21.87 (95% CI 7.45–64.21). SROC curve was 0.902 with high heterogeneity (I²=96.8%), and urban settings showed better RDT performance than rural areas. Authors concluded that RDTs show suboptimal sensitivity and specificity compared with WHO benchmarks and recommended integration with microscopy or molecular methods.

articles.headline: ML-designed vaccine scaffold presents three PfCSP minor repeats targeting L9 antibody
articles.title_original: Machine learning enables de novo multiepitope design of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein to target trimeric L9 antibody.
articles.authors: Nelson JAD, Garfinkle SE, Lin ZJ, Park J, Kim AJ, Bayruns K, McCanna ME, Konrath KM, Agostino CJ, Kulp DW, Odom John AR, Pallesen J
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Researchers developed MESODID, a machine learning-driven pipeline for multiepitope vaccine design, and used it to design scaffolds presenting three copies of P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) minor repeat. The top design, M-TIM, was validated by 3.6 Å cryo-EM structure demonstrating successful binding and orientation of the L9 protective monoclonal antibody, effectively recapitulating critical homotypic antibody interactions. This rational design approach offers a powerful method for developing vaccines against challenging infections with repeated epitopes.

articles.headline: Acyclic nucleotide analogues show antimalarial activity
articles.title_original: Acyclic Nucleoside Phosphonates As Potential Inhibitors of Plasmodium Falciparum Egress.
articles.authors: Cheviet T, Wein S, Lionne C, Badji Y, Venturini N, Cerdan R, Peyrottes S
articles.details: Two series of acyclic nucleoside phosphonates were synthesized targeting P. falciparum egress. Several compounds showed in vitro antimalarial activity against 3D7 strains with IC50 values of 11.3–38.8 µM. Molecular docking revealed crucial interactions with the putative target, establishing this family as promising antimalarial scaffolds warranting further optimization.

articles.headline: Environmental factors predict malaria risk in Ghana
articles.title_original: Mapping Malaria Susceptibility in the Oti Region of Ghana Using Remote Sensing and GIS: A Retrospective Observational Study.
articles.authors: Okyere G, Abakah A, Kwarteng A, Kwarteng EVS
articles.details: Retrospective observational study mapped malaria risk in Oti Region using Getis-Ord Gi* statistic and predictive models (BRT, GAM, SVM). Over 3 years, Krachi East reported 91,376 cases and Nkwanta South 73,592 cases. Elevation (27.60% importance), land surface temperature (25.48%), NDVI (23.12%), and distance to rivers (20.00%) were key environmental factors guiding targeted control.

articles.headline: An. gambiae shows pyrethroid resistance in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Pyrethroid Resistance in Anopheles gambiae s.l.: A Focus on Permethrin and Deltamethrin for Malaria Vector Control.
articles.authors: Adesoye OA, Awoniyi AM, Adeogun AO
articles.details: CDC bottle bioassays tested Anopheles gambiae (collected June 2024, Abuja, Nigeria) against permethrin and deltamethrin at standard and sub-lethal doses. At standard dose, permethrin caused 84.4% mortality at 30 min (P=0.031) and 100% at 35 min; deltamethrin caused 76% at 30 min and 100% at 40 min (P=0.04). No mortality at sub-lethal doses (≤0.8 µg/bottle) after 24 hours.

articles.headline: Smartphone location data identifies transmission hotspots
articles.title_original: Smartphone-based retrospective analysis for malaria hotspot detection.
articles.authors: da Silva BM, Giddaluru J, Cardozo LE, Martins FM, Antolini APR, Bargieri DY, de Lacerda MVG, Monteiro WM, Sampaio VS, Nakaya HI
articles.details: Sickness Positioning System applied density-based clustering to anonymized smartphone location data from over 200 malaria-infected individuals to identify transmission hotspots. Field validation confirmed two previously unknown mosquito breeding sites. Compared to Brazil's annual federal surveillance system, this approach provided more precise and timely spatial assessment of malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Low-dose primaquine is well-tolerated for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Haematologic responses to primaquine used for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax in public health facilities in Ethiopia: a prospective observational study.
articles.authors: Shifraw T, Abdelmenan S, Zewde A, Worku A, Abera A, Mohammed H, Etana K, Keating J, Chibsa S, Girma S, Teka H, Mumba P, Murphy M, Karmarkar E, Price RN, Hwang J, Berhane Y
articles.details: Prospective observational study (May 2019–June 2021) enrolled 879 patients with P. vivax and mixed malaria receiving low-dose primaquine (0.25 mg/kg daily for 14 days) without prior G6PD testing at four Ethiopian health centers. Median hemoglobin decline was 0.9% on day 3 and 0.8% on day 7; nine patients had >25% decline but none were symptomatic. By day 13, 91.9% had hematological recovery with no serious adverse events.

articles.headline: Human-vector behavior sustains residual malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Human and vector behavioural determinants of malaria transmission in the Nandi highlands, western Kenya.
articles.authors: Machani MG, Sunkara A, Awandu SS, Ombok M, Ouko A, Abel L, Turnbull LB, Rodriguez I, Kim J, Clemens JC, Ayodo G, Ochomo EO, John CC, Lobo NF
articles.details: Prospective study (May and October 2018) monitored Anopheles biting rates and human behavior across three ecological sites in Kenya's Nandi highlands with high species diversity. LLINs prevented 24.5–44.9% of bites across sites and seasons. Highest exposure occurred indoors during early night (1900–0100 h) for unprotected sleepers and outdoors in early evening (1800–2100 h), indicating persistent residual transmission gaps.

articles.headline: Solar geoengineering reduces malaria across South Asia
articles.title_original: Malaria transmission dynamics under climate change and solar geoengineering in South Asia: a GLENS-based assessment.
articles.authors: Hussain A, Shoaib M, Latif M
articles.details: Modeling study used VECTRI to simulate malaria transmission from 2020–2097 across seven South Asian countries under GLENS-SAI (stratospheric aerosol injection) and control (RCP 8.5 unmitigated warming) scenarios. GLENS-SAI reduced malaria transmission intensity with significant EIR declines in India, Nepal, Bangladesh, and Pakistan, though localized increases appeared in southeastern Pakistan and eastern Iran. Seasonal transmission patterns shortened in amplitude and duration.

articles.headline: Novel pfGSK3β inhibitors identified computationally
articles.title_original: In silico investigation of novel Plasmodium Falciparum glycogen synthase kinase (pfGSk3β) inhibitors for the treatment of malaria infection.
articles.authors: Adebambo KF, Otify S
articles.details: In silico study screened CHEMBL database for GSK-3β inhibitors, yielding 53 candidates. Molecular docking, MD simulations (100 ns), and MMPBSA analysis selected three lead compounds (S20, S39, S56). Compound S56 demonstrated superior binding affinity (−5.64 kcal/mol vs −7.12 for control) and lower RMSD (1.0 nm vs 4.5 nm), with comparable MMPBSA energy (−14.45 kJ/mol). Protox III indicated potential toxicity concerns.

articles.headline: COVID-19 disrupted malaria control in Brazil
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on malaria transmission in the state of Pará, Brazil.
articles.authors: de Sá CAF, Sardinha DM, de Paula Souza E Guimarães RJ, Vallinoto ACR, Dos Santos EJM, Viana GMR, do Socorro Souza Chaves T
articles.details: Observational study analyzed malaria surveillance data in Pará, Brazil (2018–2023): pre-pandemic (2018–2019) 79,125 cases, critical period (2020–2021) 44,830 cases, less critical (2022–2023) 47,502 cases. Miners' proportion increased from 17.2% to 58%; P. falciparum cases increased from 2.6% to 11.3%. Seasonal peaks persisted August–November. Case reduction during pandemic attributed to control program actions; increases post-vaccination to resumed social interaction.

articles.headline: CD44 cross-linking enhances P. falciparum invasion
articles.title_original: CD44 cross-linking promotes Plasmodium falciparum invasion.
articles.authors: Kongsomboonvech AK, Scally SW, Le Guen Y, Valissery P, Salinas ND, Cowman AF, Tolia NH, Egan ES
articles.details: Forward genetic screen identified CD44 as a host factor for P. falciparum invasion. Anti-CD44 antibody BRIC 222 promoted invasion through cross-linking, altering RBC cytoskeletal phosphorylation and increasing Basigin accessibility. Parasite ligand EBA-175 similarly enhanced invasion and increased PfRH5/PCRCR binding to Basigin. CD44 cross-linking coordinates enhanced ligand-receptor interactions and host cytoskeleton signaling during invasion.

articles.headline: Genomic toolkit enables Southeast Asian vector surveillance
articles.title_original: A genomic toolkit for surveillance and elimination of the principal malaria vectors in Southeast Asia.
articles.authors: Dennis TPW, Vantaux A, Kobylinski KC, Alam MS, Witkowski B, Al-Amin HM, Lobo NF, Wilson AJ, Drury E, Goncalves S, Harding N, Clarkson CS, Hernandez-Koutoucheva A, Kwiatkowski D, Miles A, Donnelly MJ, St Laurent B
articles.details: Study released Adir1.0, a curated catalog of 540 An. dirus whole-genome sequences with cloud-based analysis API, and updated Amin1.0 for An. minimus. Population genomic analysis of An. dirus from Bangladesh, Thailand, and Cambodia revealed population structure, candidate physiological resistance regions, and genomic structural variation. Resources provide foundation for genomic surveillance to inform vector control and malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Ivermectin's role and complexity in malaria control
articles.title_original: Ivermectin's role in malaria control: the simplicity and the complexity.
articles.authors: Schwartz E, Orshan L, Schlagenhauf P
articles.details: Based on title only—no abstract provided. This article appears to discuss ivermectin's potential and complications in malaria control. Specific study design, population, interventions, and quantitative results cannot be provided without the full abstract.

articles.headline: Artemisinin combination formula strengthens immune response in mice
articles.title_original: Proteomics-driven screening of artemisinin-based combination ratios and mechanistic insights into Plasmodium berghei infection in mice.
articles.authors: Hao L, Sun J, Li J, Li Z, Yu Z, Huang H, Liu G, Shen Z, Huo H, Yuan Q, Li H, Huang L
articles.details: A murine malaria study screened artemisinin-derived compound combinations (arteannuin B, artemisinic acid, scopoletin) using proteomics. The optimized formula enhanced antimalarial efficacy by modulating phagocytic activity in splenic macrophages, dendritic cells, and NK cells via Fcγ receptor-mediated pathways. Results suggest this combination strategy could overcome artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: Host defense peptides: key players in malaria immunity
articles.title_original: Host defense peptides in malaria infection: their contributions, significance and constraints.
articles.authors: Alfaki DA, Elbasheir MM
articles.details: A review of host defense peptides (defensins, cathelicidins, NK-2 peptide, platelet factor 4, macrophage inflammatory protein-3α, hepcidin) and their roles in malaria protection. These peptides target multiple Plasmodium developmental stages through direct antimicrobial activity and immunomodulation. The review discusses incomplete understanding of HDP-specific roles and interactions with Plasmodium at various infection stages.

articles.headline: Malaria control strategies in Northern Ghana: progress and gaps
articles.title_original: Malaria control in Northern Ghana: a scoping review.
articles.authors: Zankawah MM, Issakah MA, Yakubu E, Tiwaa-Boateng NA, Baatiema L, Moomin A, Banchani JS, Chimdi IE, Edusei AK, Ordinioha B
articles.details: A scoping review of 38 studies from Northern Ghana (2013-2023) found most research (44.7%) in Upper East region, focused on pregnant women (19%), children (14%), and patients (14.3%). Primary interventions studied: case management (28.9%), intermittent preventive treatment for pregnancy (23.7%), and long-lasting insecticidal nets (15.8%). Few studies examined vaccines (2.6%) or combined intervention effects. Notable gaps in regional balance and larval source management research.

articles.headline: Malaria treatment in neonates remains inconsistent and under-studied
articles.title_original: Management of malaria in newborns: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Sultanli A, Beck JS, Kremp M, Sondermann H, Bonitz M, Honkpehedji YJ, Remppis J, Adegnika AA, Esen M, Bélard S
articles.details: Systematic review of 26 publications (22 case reports, 3 case series, 1 cross-sectional) reporting antimalarial treatment in 40 neonates with confirmed Plasmodium infection. Median age at diagnosis: 19 days; 45% P. falciparum, 43% P. vivax. Treatment was highly inconsistent: only 28% of P. falciparum cases received artemisinin-based therapy; most (56%) received oral amodiaquine or chloroquine monotherapy. All neonates survived with parasitemia clearance in 2–7 days. 70% were initially misdiagnosed as sepsis.

articles.headline: Multiple tools needed for outdoor malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Outdoor biting by malaria vectors: what are the options for intervention?
articles.authors: Braack L, Macdonald MB
articles.details: Literature review of interventions for outdoor biting malaria mosquitoes in Asia-Pacific. Larval source management is effective in some contexts with WHO endorsement as supplemental vector control. Spatial repellents recently received WHO recommendation for indoor use; topical repellents are effective short-term but less so for long-term community protection. Endectocides show potential at focal locations with benefits for helminth control. Gene drive remains a longer-term vision. Integration of complementary techniques with community engagement is recommended.

articles.headline: Cambodia's malaria surveillance system strong; gaps identified for elimination
articles.title_original: Key findings and recommendations from assessment of Cambodia's national malaria surveillance system.
articles.authors: Sovannaroth S, Kugler AM, Ngor P, Brindle H, Chhun B, Filip E, Hem V, Khy V, Matoy RJJ, Ou V, Ran S, Ringwald P, Sok K, Yoem R, Zhang Z, Rekol H
articles.details: Cambodia reported 1,384 malaria cases in 2023, targeting elimination by 2025.
