articles.authors: Gómez-Pérez GP, Vidal M, Campo JJ, Moncunill G, Jimenez A, Vázquez-Santiago M, Ruiz-Olalla G, Sanz H, Ayestaran A, Angov E, Dutta S, Chitnis C, Chauhan V, James ER, Billingsley PF, Sim BKL, Kremsner PG, Hoffman SL, Lell B, Mordmüller B, Dobaño C
articles.details: Controlled human malaria infection (CHMI) study comparing IgM, total IgG, and IgG subclass (IgG1-4) responses to 21 P. falciparum antigens in: malaria-naive (n=22), PfSPZ-CVac-vaccinated naive (n=27), and lifelong-exposed Africans (n=20, including 9 with sickle cell trait). Vaccinated individuals had higher IgM/IgG to CSP and SSP-2/TRAP; semi-immune showed broader blood-stage antibody responses, longer prepatent periods (PPPs), and fewer symptoms. Cytophilic IgG1/IgG3 to blood-stage antigens associated with longer PPPs; elevated noncytophilic IgG2/IgG4 in semi-immune may reflect regulatory responses limiting vaccine efficacy.

articles.headline: Clinical features of induced P. vivax malaria in 1950s-60s Vienna psychiatric patients
articles.title_original: Clinical and Parasitological Characteristics of Plasmodium vivax Malaria in Malaria-Naïve Patients: A Review of Malaria Fever Therapy in Patients with Schizophrenia and Neurosyphilis during the 1950s and 1960s in Vienna, Austria.
articles.authors: Wolff S, Kainz RM, Reimold D, Lagler H, Mischlinger J, Ramharter M
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 322 patients (schizophrenia n=147; neurosyphilis n=175) treated with malaria fever therapy at Vienna General Hospital (1951-1969). Intravenous inoculation doses 4-8 mL blood. First fever peaks ≈7 days post-inoculation; temperature increased over successive paroxysms while afebrile intervals shortened progressively (41 to 31 hours). After mean 5-6 fever peaks, all received quinine monotherapy or combination therapy. Extra-hepatic P. vivax incubation period ≈7 days; study reveals naturally short periodicity of fever cycles in untreated infection.

articles.headline: Transfluthrin spatial emanator protects against resistant malaria vectors
articles.title_original: The BiteBarrier transfluthrin emanator demonstrates significant protection against susceptible and resistant malaria and arbovirus vectors in semi-field trials in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Maasayi MS, Swai JK, Muganga JB, Moore J, Stevenson JC, Coleman M, Lobo NF, Moore SJ, Tambwe MM
articles.details: Semi-field trial in Tanzania (8 weeks) evaluated BiteBarrier transfluthrin emanator against pyrethroid-susceptible (An. gambiae s.s., Ae. aegypti) and resistant (An. gambiae KDR, An. funestus, Cx. quinquefasciatus) species. BiteBarrier provided >93% protection indoors (95% CI 92-93%) and 80% outdoors (95% CI 78-81%) against mosquito bites, with mortality 47% indoors (95% CI 43-53%) and 26% outdoors (95% CI 22-30%), regardless of species or resistance status. Protection sustained over ≥8 weeks.

articles.headline: Epigenetic inhibitors target P. vivax hypnozoites for radical cure
articles.title_original: A drug repurposing approach reveals targetable epigenetic pathways in Plasmodium vivax hypnozoites.
articles.authors: Maher SP, Bakowski MA, Vantaux A, Flannery EL, Andolina C, Gupta M, Antonova-Koch Y, Argomaniz M, Cabrera-Mora M, Campo B, Chao AT, Chatterjee AK, Cheng WT, Chuenchob V, Cooper CA, Cottier K, Galinski MR, Harupa-Chung A, Ji H, Joseph SB, Lenz T, Lonardi S, Matheson J, Mikolajczak SA, Moeller T, Orban A, Padín-Irizarry V, Pan K, Péneau J, Prudhomme J, Roesch C, Ruberto A, Sabnis SS, Saney CL, Sattabongkot J, Sereshki S, Suriyakan S, Ubalee R, Wang Y, Wasisakun P, Yin J, Popovici J, McNamara CW, Joyner C, Nosten FH, Witkowski B, Le Roch KG, Kyle DE
articles.details: Screened ReFRAME and epigenetic inhibitor libraries against P. vivax liver stages; identified DNA methyltransferase inhibitors and histone-modification inhibitors as selective against P. vivax and P. cynomolgi hypnozoites. Bisulfite sequencing showed DNA methylation in most coding genes and proximal promoters/first exons correlating with gene expression. MMV019721 (acetyl-CoA synthetase inhibitor) validated in vitro. Epigenetic mechanisms modulate hypnozoite formation, offering new radical cure antimalarial discovery pathways.

articles.headline: Microsporidia MB sporogeny discovery in Anopheles mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Discovery of sporogeny in the malaria transmission-blocking symbiont of Anopheles mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Herren JK
articles.details: Detailed visualization of Microsporidia MB sporogeny reveals localization to mosquito germline tissues with octosporogony in developing oocytes, suggesting both vertical and potential horizontal transmission routes. Identification of spores with microsporidian structures supports environmental dissemination via egg deposition and strengthens feasibility of this symbiont as a sustainable, transmission-blocking malaria control tool.

articles.headline: RIPR genetic diversity in P. falciparum isolates, Senegal
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of the Malaria Vaccine Candidate PfRIPR in a High Transmission Region of Senegal.
articles.authors: Nair M, Geering G, Agarwal A, Li R, Qiao Y, Xiao Q, Pouye MN, Thiam LG, Ba A, Hagadorn KA, Cisse A, Guerra N, Tawaldemedhen Y, Mangou K, Moore AJ, Diallo F, Sene SD, Sadio BD, Zhang E, Shapiro L, Patel SD, Mbengue A, Vigan-Womas I, Sheng Z, Bei AK
articles.details: Deep amplicon sequencing of RH5-Interacting Protein (RIPR), a vaccine candidate, in 89 P. falciparum clinical isolates from high-transmission Kédougou, Senegal identified nonsynonymous SNPs in 64/89 (71.9%) samples. Researchers identified 26 non-synonymous SNPs total (15 novel); structural modeling predicted 7/16 would destabilize RIPR while 2/16 increased stability; 3 SNPs mapped to EGF5-8 domains where neutralizing antibodies bind.

articles.headline: Domestic funding strategies for malaria/HIV/TB, Tanzania
articles.title_original: Stakeholders' perspectives on funding malaria, HIV/AIDS and tuberculosis services in Tanzania through domestic resources mobilisation: a qualitative study.
articles.authors: Ngadaya FD, Isangula K, Kimambo H, Soka G, Kitinya C, Philbert D, Mwenda L, Kagaruki GB, Kimaro G, Ngadaya E, Kahwa A, Mfinanga S
articles.details: Qualitative study of 76 Tanzanian health stakeholders explored alternative domestic funding approaches for malaria, HIV/AIDS, and TB services. Current funding sources included multilateral donors, government, and internal sources. Proposed strategies included universal health insurance, reducing service exemptions, disease-specific funds, and taxation, but faced implementation barriers of poor awareness, conflicting political promises, and accountability challenges.

articles.headline: R21 seasonal mass vaccination trial for malaria
articles.title_original: Seasonal mass vaccination with R21/Matrix-M for malaria elimination (SERVAL): protocol of the cluster randomised trial.
articles.authors: Dabira ED, Natama HM, Jaiteh F, Grietens KP, Bocoum FY, Ndiath MO, Mohammed N, Gibba B, Hill AVS, Ghani A, Erhart A, Tinto H, D'Alessandro U
articles.details: Cluster-randomized controlled trial across Gambia (30 villages, low-moderate transmission) and Burkina Faso (24 villages, intense transmission) testing seasonal mass vaccination with R21/Matrix-M vaccine. Intervention villages received R21 in three-monthly rounds (May–July 2024) with booster (June 2025); 30 and 24 villages randomized 1:1 to intervention/control. Primary outcome: malaria prevalence at peak transmission (November 2024); secondary outcomes include safety, clinical incidence, coverage, and cost-effectiveness.

articles.headline: Correction to P. knowlesi transmission study
articles.title_original: Correction: Human-to-Anopheles dirus mosquito transmission of the anthropozoonotic malaria parasite, Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Kumpitak C, Duangmanee A, Thongyod W, Rachaphaew N, Suansomjit C, Manopwisedjaroen K, Aung PL, Imad HA, Cui L, Sattabongkot J, Nguitragool W, Bantuchai S
articles.details: Erratum to article on human-to-Anopheles dirus transmission of the anthropozoonotic malaria parasite, Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Malaria and helminth co-infection in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Malaria and soil transmitted helminth infection and associated factors among pregnant women attending public health facilities in Dilla town, South Ethiopia: cross sectional study design.
articles.authors: Tefera S, Bogale A, Kassie A, Abreham A, Bayih A, Ali MM
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 331 pregnant women in Dilla town (April–June 2023) identified malaria prevalence of 8.8% (P. vivax 4.8%, P. falciparum 4.0%), soil-transmitted helminth prevalence of 18.1%, and malaria-STH co-infection of 5.7%. Risk factors for malaria included rural residence (OR 2.66), proximity to stagnant water (OR 3.09), and no ITN use (OR 3.67); STH and co-infection associated with poor hygiene and handwashing practices.

articles.headline: Azithromycin and SMC on child mortality (CHAT)
articles.title_original: The role of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in the effect of azithromycin on child mortality: A secondary analysis of the CHAT cluster randomized clinical trial.
articles.authors: Gebreegziabher EA, Ouattara M, Bountogo M, Coulibaly B, Boudo V, Ouedraogo T, Lebas E, Hu H, O'Brien KS, Hsiang MS, Glidden DV, Arnold BF, Lietman TM, Sié A, Oldenburg CE
articles.details: Secondary analysis of CHAT cluster-randomized trial (341 communities, Burkina Faso) examined whether azithromycin effect on all-cause mortality in children <5 varied with seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) season or coverage. During SMC season, azithromycin showed mortality benefit (RR 0.77, 95% CI 0.60–0.98); non-SMC season showed no effect (RR 0.89, 95% CI 0.68–1.15). In clusters with <80% SMC coverage, azithromycin effect was 0.73 (95% CI 0.56–0.96); interaction not statistically significant.

articles.headline: Deep learning for Plasmodium vivax detection
articles.title_original: Leveraging MobileNetV2 and deep learning innovation for high accuracy Plasmodium Vivax detection in blood smears.
articles.authors: Prathap VM, Yadav S, Qidwai T
articles.details: Developed YOLOv3 model with modified MobileNetv2 backbone and Transformed Convolution Layer for automated P. vivax detection in thin blood smear images. Achieved high performance metrics: accuracy 1.00, precision 0.98, recall 0.98, F1 score 0.97, and mean average precision (mAP) 0.90, providing improved diagnostic accuracy compared to traditional manual microscopy.

articles.headline: Alstonia boonei ameliorates malaria-diabetes comorbidity
articles.title_original: Alstonia boonei stem bark aqueous extract ameliorates elevated parasitemia levels, normalises blood glucose, modulates inflammatory biomarkers and enhances antioxidant status in Plasmodium berghei-infected/diabetic mice.
articles.authors: Agunloye OM, Akintoye OF, Oboh G
articles.details: Study in Plasmodium berghei-infected/diabetic mice tested A. boonei stem bark aqueous extract (40–80 mg/kg) against untreated controls and chloroquine-metformin. Extract produced dose-dependent reductions in parasitemia and fasting blood glucose, enhanced antioxidant enzymes (SOD, catalase, GSH), restored hematological indices (RBC, hemoglobin, packed cell volume), and modulated inflammatory markers (reduced C-reactive protein, elevated interleukin-10); effects at 80 mg/kg comparable to standard drug.

articles.headline: Water, sanitation, hygiene predict diarrhea outcomes
articles.title_original: Association of Diarrhea Outcomes with Drinking Water Factors, Sanitation, Hygiene, and Malaria Practices in the Population of Béré, Chad.
articles.authors: Boutrin MC, Andersen M, Gately Z, McLarty C, Santos E
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health
articles.details: Survey of 484 residents in Béré, Chad examined associations between diarrhea outcomes and water, sanitation, hygiene, and malaria practices. Drinking water treatment/transport/storage, malaria-related factors, and sanitation/hygiene practices significantly predicted diarrhea in both adults and children (all p<0.001). Risk factors included poor water management, absence of insecticide-treated nets, and inadequate handwashing.

articles.headline: Tetanus and cerebral malaria co-infection in child
articles.title_original: Generalized Tetanus Associated With Malaria and Probable Meningitis in a 4-Year-Old Patient: A Case Report in Limbe Regional Hospital, Cameroon.
articles.authors: Tchuisseu Ngapjang CD, Fotso C, Gobina R, Lingo PA, Shumbang Tchumukong QJ, Kouam BB, Effila M, Berinyuy Nyuydzefon E, Vincent Siysi V
articles.details: Case report of unvaccinated 4-year-old boy presenting with fever, seizures, and neck stiffness who developed generalized paroxysmal muscle spasms and opisthotonos. Diagnosed with tetanus and cerebral malaria with probable bacterial meningitis. After empiric treatment in ICU with antitetanus toxoid, antibiotics, and antimalarials, patient showed considerable clinical improvement within 48 hours.

articles.headline: B cell responses to malaria infection by immunity
articles.title_original: Effect of controlled human Plasmodium falciparum infection on B cell subsets in individuals with different levels of malaria immunity.
articles.authors: Requena P, Gómez-Pérez GP, McCall MBB, Barrios D, Aguilar R, Fernández-Morata J, Vidal M, Campo JJ, Sanchez C, Yazdabankhsh M, Sim BKL, Hoffman SL, Kremsner P, Lell B, Mordmüller B, Dobaño C, Moncunill G
articles.details: Controlled human malaria infection (CHMI) study compared B cell phenotypes across malaria-naïve, PfSPZ-CVac vaccinated, and semi-immune volunteers. Semi-immune individuals showed increased baseline frequencies of immature (CD19+CD10+), naive, and atypical memory B cells versus naïve. Day 11 post-infection showed expansion of classical memory B cells (IgD+CD27+CD21+) in all groups. Vaccination positively associated with classical B cells (β=0.190, 95% CI 0.011–0.368) and negatively with CD1c+ atypical MBCs (β=−0.328, 95% CI −0.621 to −0.032); sickle cell trait modulated effects.

articles.headline: Primary health centres see sicker children with severe malaria in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Treatment-Seeking for Children with Suspected Severe Malaria Attending Community Health Workers and Primary Health Centres in Adamawa State, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Lee TT, Omoluabi E, Ayodeji K, Yusuf O, Okon C, Brunner NC, Delvento G, Signorell A, Lambiris MJ, Kwiatkowski M, Burri C, Lengeler C, Emedo E, Cheshi F, Fashanu C, Wiwa O, Hetzel MW
articles.details: Prospective study of 589 children <5 years with fever and danger signs attending CORPs (n=314) or PHCs (n=275) in Adamawa State, Nigeria. Case fatality was 19% at PHCs versus 6% at CORPs. Children at PHCs were more likely to have ≥4 clinical danger signs (50% vs 39%, p=0.02), CNS involvement (90% vs 74%, p<0.01), and prior home treatment (42% vs 33%, p=0.04). Authors conclude PHCs may see sicker children due to perceived professional capacity, necessitating health system strengthening in rural areas.

articles.headline: Climate change projected to expand malaria transmission into Papua New Guinea highlands
articles.title_original: Climate Change Is Expected to Expand Malaria Transmission Range and Population at Risk in Papua New Guinea.
articles.authors: Karl S, Skinner EB, McEwen S, Keven J, Kisomb J, Robinson LJ, Laman M
articles.details: Analysis using satellite temperature data (1960–2019) and climate models projecting through 2040 to estimate malaria transmission suitability via temperature-dependent R0. Suitable transmission areas increased from 58% of PNG population (1960) to 61% (2019), projected to reach 74% by 2040, representing an additional 2.8 million people. Total population requiring malaria control expected to double to 13.4 million by 2040. Dense highlands with susceptible populations face increased epidemic and disease risk under climate change.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine awareness highest among educated, internet-connected Ghanaian women
articles.title_original: Determinants of Malaria Vaccine Awareness Among Women in Ghana: Findings From the 2022 Ghana Malaria Indicator Survey.
articles.authors: Sarfo M, Larbi-Ampofo AK, Lawal O, Bonnah SK, Eshun G
articles.details: Analysis of 38,459 women aged 15–49 from 2022 Ghana Malaria Indicator Survey examining sociodemographic determinants of vaccine awareness. Tertiary education strongly associated with awareness (AOR 2.89, 95% CI 2.50–3.34) as were past 12-month internet use (AOR 1.85, 1.74–1.97) and residence in Greater Accra region (AOR 1.75, 1.54–2.00). Awareness increased with age, peaking in women 45–49 years (AOR 2.03, 1.60–2.57). Authors recommend equitable vaccine information campaigns targeting underserved populations to support Ghana's 2028 elimination goals.

articles.headline: Umbrella review shows 10% malaria prevalence in vulnerable Ethiopian populations
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria infection among most vulnerable populations in Ethiopia: an umbrella review of systematic reviews and meta-analyses.
articles.authors: Demeke A, Kassa G, Gebrie H, Tefera S, Getu E, Dube SJ, Bayable A, Kassie A, Muleta B, Eba WW, Gebremeskele BT, Demeke AD
articles.details: Umbrella review synthesizing 6 systematic reviews and meta-analyses of malaria in children, pregnant women, and migrants in Ethiopia. Overall pooled prevalence 10.10% (95% CI 0.36–19.85). Non-use of insecticide-treated nets strongly associated with malaria risk (pooled OR 5.22, 95% CI 3.93–6.94) as was proximity to stagnant water (OR 4.21, 2.75–6.46). Consolidated evidence highlights substantial malaria burden among vulnerable groups and identifies ITN use and environmental management as key prevention strategies.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid-resistance cytochrome P450 genes confer cross-resistance to new insecticide
articles.title_original: The duplicated cytochrome P450 CYP6P9a/b confers cross-resistance to a mitochondrial complex I inhibitor in the African malaria vector Anopheles funestus.
articles.authors: Tekoh TA, Mugenzi LMJ, Menze B, Tchapga W, Wondji M, Tchouakui M, Small G, Wondji CS
articles.details: Bioassay and molecular studies testing pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles funestus from Cameroon against novel mitochondrial complex I inhibitor (Sherlock). The CYP6P9a_R allele was strongly associated with Sherlock resistance across multiple assays: CDC bottle bioassays at discriminating concentration (OR 5.3–18.6, p<0.0001), WHO cone bioassays (OR 5.1, p<0.0001), and WHO tunnel tests (OR 6.6, p<0.0001). qRT-PCR revealed elevated CYP6P9a expression (fold-change 24.7–45.6). The L119F_GSTe2 marker did not confer cross-resistance, highlighting metabolic-resistance complexity.

articles.headline: Nearly half of children in Horn of Africa experience delayed malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated factors of delay in seeking malaria treatment among under five children in the Horn of Africa: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Agimas MC, Aweke MN, Mengistu B, Baffa LD, Fentie EA, Shewarega ES, Belew AK, Muhammad EA
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of treatment-seeking delay in children <5 across Horn of Africa. Pooled prevalence of delayed treatment-seeking 48% (95% CI 34–63%). Major risk factors included high transport costs (OR 4.39, 95% CI 2.49–7.76), travel distance >3000 meters (OR 2.59, 2.03–3.30), concerns about drug side effects (OR 2.94, 1.86–4.67), and history of prior child death (OR 2.5, 1.73–3.59). Protective factors: formal education (OR 0.69, 0.49–0.96) and middle-income status (OR 0.42, 0.28–0.63). Financial and geographic barriers to healthcare access are critical drivers of treatment delay.

articles.headline: Vegetation near homes increases malaria vector abundance and human malaria incidence
articles.title_original: Housing and household characteristics associated with malaria vectors abundance and clinical malaria incidence in a semi-urban and a rural area of Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Nikiema M, Dambach P, Kabore J, Some SYM, Ouedraogo A, Barry Y, Boudo V, Chabi BAM, Sie A, Badolo A, Gneme A
articles.details: Cross-sectional entomological survey using CDC light traps (July–October 2021) in 355 houses across semi-urban and rural Burkina Faso. Vegetation around houses significantly increased mosquito abundance (RR 2.5, 95% CI 1.43–4.15, p<0.001), vector infection rate (RR 2.5, 1.74–3.33, p<0.001), and presence of human malaria cases (RR 1.4, 1.03–2.0, p=0.048). Presence of children <5 years (RR 1.52, 1.25–1.84) and female householder (RR 1.23, 1.02–2.78) were also associated with malaria cases. Building materials (wall/roof type), breeding sites, and LLIN use were not significantly associated.

articles.headline: Plasmodium infection alters skin flora and odor to attract mosquitoes more
articles.title_original: [Skin flora, odor and malaria transmission].
articles.authors: Song X, Wang J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Review of recent findings showing that Plasmodium infection induces remarkable changes in host skin microbiota and volatile organic compounds. Mouse studies identified ethylbenzene as a parasite-induced volatile that increases host attractiveness to female Anopheles mosquitoes. These changes in host odor-mediated attractiveness during infection may enhance transmission. The odor-based mechanisms underlying altered vector-host interactions provide novel insights for developing control strategies targeting mosquito attraction behaviors.

articles.headline: P. vivax invasion requires transferrin receptor, basigin, and novel prohibitin-2 co-receptor
articles.title_original: The reticulocyte restriction: invasion ligand RBP1a of Plasmodium vivax targets human TfR1, prohibitin-2, and basigin.
articles.authors: Molina-Franky J, Röth D, Ararat-Sarria M, Patarroyo MA, Kalkum M
articles.details: Study using JK-1 and BEL-A erythroid cell lines as P. vivax reticulocyte surrogates, combined with LC-MS membrane proteomics and proximity labeling. Confirmed TfR1 (CD71), CD98hc, and basigin (BSG) as P. vivax invasion receptors. Identified prohibitin-2 (PHB2) as a novel co-receptor interacting with PvRBP1a invasion ligand—the first report implicating PHB2 in P. vivax host-cell entry. These molecular insights into reticulocyte-restricted invasion inform development of targeted antimalarial therapeutic strategies.

articles.headline: Plasmodium ARK1 kinase controls mitotic spindle and forms non-canonical chromosomal passenger complex
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.
articles.authors: Tewari R, Nagar A, Yanase R, Zeeshan M, Ferguson DJP, Abel S, Pashley S, Mishra A, Eze A, Rea E, Brady D, Bottrill A, Vaughan S, Roch KL, Guttery D, Holder A, Tromer E, Sharma P
articles.details: Conditional depletion and interactome studies identifying Aurora-related kinase 1 (ARK1) as essential for spindle formation, kinetochore dynamics, and mitotic progression during Plasmodium schizogony and gametogony. ARK1 depletion disrupts kinetochore segregation, karyokinesis, cytokinesis, and parasite transmission. ARK1 nucleates a divergent chromosomal passenger complex (CPC) containing two INCENPs but lacking canonical Survivin and Borealin—representing a mitotic architecture distinct from model eukaryotes and potentially targetable for therapeutic intervention.

articles.headline: Parasite density correlates with TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma, and TGF-beta1 in malaria patients
articles.title_original: Assessment of Cytokines TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma, TGF-beta-1, and IL-10 in Malaria Patients of the River Nile State , Sudan: A critical study of immune response.
articles.authors: Hamad MNM, Albadrani GM, Ghazwani AA, Abdelmola A, Suliman RS, Mahjaf GM, Fahmy SA, Badi S, Yassin HM, Othman GA, Altaher TA, Noor SK
articles.details: Study of 90 participants with microscopy-confirmed malaria in River Nile State, Sudan, measuring serum cytokines via ELISA. TNF-α, IFN-γ, and TGF-β1 levels were significantly associated with parasite density (r = 0.42, 0.38, 0.51; p < 0.01). TGF-β1 was significantly higher in P. vivax infections, while IL-10 was elevated in P. falciparum cases (560.0 vs 415.6 pg/mL, p=0.019). COVID-19 co-infection was associated with lower uric acid (p<0.05). Cytokine profiles vary by parasite species and reflect immune response intensity.

articles.headline: Clinical immunity to P. vivax develops after single controlled human infection
articles.title_original: Development of clinical immunity to Plasmodium vivax following repeat controlled human malaria infection.
articles.authors: Hou MM, Harding AC, Barber NM, Kundu P, Bach FA, Salkeld J, Themistocleous Y, Greenwood NM, Cho JS, Barrett JR, Nugent FL, Rawlinson TA, Hodgson SH, Khozoee B, Mac Lochlainn DJ, Cowan RE, Poulton ID, Baker M, Kingham L, Mitton CH, Platt A, Lopez Ramon R, Ramos Lopez F, Thomas M, Skinner K, Quinkert D, Pipini D, Lias AM, Bardelli M, Edwards NJ, Donnellan FR, Biswas S, Rayner JC, Nielsen CM, Silk SE, Draper SJ, Nahrendorf W, Spence PJ, Minassian AM
articles.details: Multi-cohort controlled human malaria infection (CHMI) study: 19 participants received primary P. vivax infection (PvW1 clone), 12 underwent secondary homologous CHMI, 2 tertiary, and 6 received heterologous P. falciparum rechallenge. Clinical immunity to P. vivax developed rapidly after single CHMI, protecting against fever and laboratory abnormalities through attenuated inflammatory cytokine and chemokine responses and reduced coagulation activation. Notably, clinical immunity operated independently of anti-parasite immunity and was species-specific, conferring no cross-protection to P. falciparum.

articles.headline: Uganda malaria cases surge despite control efforts in Elgon region
articles.title_original: From Endemic to Epidemic: A Qualitative District-Level Assessment of an Increase in Malaria Cases in the Elgon Region of Uganda.
articles.authors: Fuller B, Ssekitoleko R, Kyozira C, Makumbi I, Bakainaga A, Moore CC, Kiirya HI
articles.details: Structured assessment of 10 districts in Uganda's high-burden Elgon region identified critical control gaps. Vector control lapses, logistical delays (mean 117±9 days for antimalarial resupply), and diagnostic shortages characterized the response. Specifically, 70% of districts lacked adequate insecticide-treated nets, 30% had no indoor residual spraying, and 80% had limited or no rapid diagnostic tests available.

articles.headline: Standardized data reporting for malaria molecular genomic surveillance proposed
articles.title_original: Toward Setting Minimum and Optimal Data to Report for Malaria Molecular Surveillance with Targeted Sequencing: The "What" and "Why".
articles.authors: Juliano JJ, Meier-Scherling CPG, Wernsman Young N, Tollefson GA, Connelly SV, Parr JB, Conrad MD, Sadler JM, Hennelly CM, Assefa A, Okell L, Fola AA, Niaré K, Marglous J, Carey-Ewend K, Futila Kyong-Shin R, Gerdes Gyuricza I, Cuomo-Dannenburg G, Ruybal-Pesántez S, Watson OJ, Verity R, Bailey JA
articles.details: This article proposes minimum and optimal data standards for reporting malaria molecular surveillance using targeted sequencing, addressing variability in current practices. It emphasizes reporting infection-level polymorphism and microhaplotype data to maximize impact on tracking challenges including artemisinin partial resistance and P. falciparum histidine-rich protein 2/3 gene deletions.

articles.headline: Temperature shows nonlinear relationship with malaria transmission in Colombia
articles.title_original: Stochastic treatment regimes in climate-health research: Reassessing malaria risk under warming scenarios in Colombia.
articles.authors: Gutiérrez JD
articles.details: Analysis of 100 Colombian municipalities (2007–2023) using causal machine learning found malaria transmission peaks at 15–23.5°C and declines at higher temperatures. Temperature increases of 0.5°C and 2.0°C showed average treatment effects of −0.007 and −0.063 on excess malaria cases, respectively, suggesting warming may constrain transmission in already-hot regions.

articles.headline: Natural product ellagic acid shows cross-activity against drug-resistant falciparum
articles.title_original: A bio-guided investigation of Mitragyna inermis (Willd) O. Kuntze (Rubiaceae) unveils natural product isolates with potent cross-activity against sensitive and multidrug resistant Plasmodium falciparum strains in vitro.
articles.authors: Hassan S, Osei Bobbie S, Tchatat Tali MB, Dize D, Kamche Youbi A, Lawane AI, Kemgne Madiesse E, Mahamat Alio H, Baker B, Fekam Boyom F
articles.details: Compounds from Mitragyna inermis were tested against P. falciparum 3D7 and multidrug-resistant strains. Ellagic acid exhibited potent activity with IC50 0.84–1.62 μg/mL against both susceptible and drug-resistant strains (Dd2, INDO, W2, K1). The compound met Lipinski's criteria, predicting favorable oral bioavailability and ADME properties for therapeutic development.

articles.headline: Herbal extracts reverse malaria-induced testicular oxidative damage in mice
articles.title_original: Bitter leaf (Vernonia amygdalina) and siam weed (Chromolaena odorata) aqueous extracts alleviate testicular damage induced by Plasmodium berghei in male mice via modulation of oxidative stress pathways.
articles.authors: Oyeleye IS, Bolarinde AA, Ademiluyi AO, Oboh G, Ojo OR
articles.details: Bitter leaf and Siam weed aqueous extracts were tested in 64 male mice infected with P. berghei strain NK65. Treatments at 100–400 mg/kg for 5 days dose-dependently reduced reactive oxygen species and thiobarbituric acid reactive substances while increasing antioxidant levels, reversing parasite-induced testicular damage compared to untreated infected controls.

articles.headline: Olfactory gene variants drive host-seeking preference in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Genetic variation in olfactory pathways associated with host-seeking behavior in natural populations of Anopheles minimus, a primary malaria vector in western Thailand.
articles.authors: Pusawang K, Zhong D, Sriwichai P, Samung Y, Saeung A, Aupalee K, Somboon P, Junkum A, Wongpalee SP, Saingamsook J, Sattabongkot J, Cui L, Yan G
articles.details: Whole-genome sequencing of wild Anopheles minimus females (July 2019–Nov 2020, Thailand) stratified by host-seeking behavior identified 12,659,785 variants and 68,975 non-synonymous SNPs. Between-group comparisons revealed 2,629–4,369 significant variants (FDR p<0.001), enriched for olfaction-related genes. Eight candidate olfactory pathway genes were identified and validated through Sanger sequencing.

articles.headline: ARIMA model outperforms FB-Prophet for Uganda malaria forecasting
articles.title_original: Comparison of ARIMA and FB-Prophet time series models for the prediction of national and regional malaria incidence in Uganda.
articles.authors: Fuller B, Ssekitoleko R, Kyozira C, Colston JM, Makumbi I, Bakainaga A, Moore CC, Kiirya HI
articles.details: Time series analysis of Uganda's District Health Information System data (2020–2023) compared ARIMA and FB-Prophet models for predicting national and regional malaria incidence. Malaria cases increased from 200.5 to 265.4 per 1,000 persons (2021–2022), with West Nile, Acholi, and Busoga regions most affected. ARIMA achieved superior performance (MAPE 31.2%) versus FB-Prophet (47.8%).

articles.headline: Human genetic adaptations conferring malaria resistance reviewed
articles.title_original: Human genetic variations conferring resistance to malaria.
articles.authors: Zhang X, Wu J, Peng Y, Luo L, Zhang L, Huang X, Chen G, Li Y, Yi H
articles.details: This review examines genetic variations protecting against malaria, including hemoglobinopathies (sickle cell trait, thalassemia), glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency, blood group polymorphisms, and inflammation/immune-related variants. Prevalence of these adaptations varies geographically, reflecting historical malaria burden. The review notes that precise protective mechanisms remain incompletely understood and emphasizes complex gene-environment interactions.

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance widespread in Chinese Anopheles sinensis vectors
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and the role of target-site insensitivity mutations among malaria vectors in China: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: He Z, Wang D, Kou Y, Liu Y, Zhao D, Yang C, Zhou R, Zhang H, Deng Y
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 30 studies (30,065 An. sinensis specimens) assessed insecticide resistance and target-site mutations. Pooled mortality rate was 61% (95% CI 53–68); kdr mutation frequency 36% (13% homozygote, 13% heterozygote, 74% susceptible), ace-1 mutation frequency 78% (42% homozygote, 25% heterozygote, 33% susceptible). Resistance detected to pyrethroids, organochlorines, carbamates, and organophosphates.

articles.headline: Education paradoxically decreases ITN use despite increased malaria awareness
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic and educational influences on malaria prevention and treatment behaviours in rural Nigeria.
articles.authors: Alao JO, Olowoshile OP, James TA, Okezie CC, Adebayo ZP, Ogbonna SC, Oyelayo EA
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 2021 Malaria Indicator Survey data (5,021 households, 9,546 women aged 15–49) in rural Nigeria examined socioeconomic influences on prevention/treatment. Insecticide-treated net ownership was near-universal (97.7%), but use was 70.3%. Low-income households had 3.6× higher ITN use odds (95% CI 3.07–4.29), while higher education increased prevention awareness (p<0.001) but paradoxically decreased actual ITN use.

articles.headline: Adjuvants overcome subdominance of P. vivax Duffy binding protein epitopes
articles.title_original: Adjuvants and MHCII modulate the immunogenicity of subdominant epitopes in Plasmodium vivax Duffy binding protein.
articles.authors: Vinals DF, Hoque MR, Oludada OE, Jansen EB, Mitran CJ, Enterina JR, Macauley MS, Hawkes MT, Yanow SK
articles.details: Study of Plasmodium vivax Duffy binding protein immunogenicity found that antigens with mutations in immunodominant epitopes, combined with GLA-SE or alum adjuvants, enhanced subdominant epitope responses. However, MHCII restriction largely negated these effects. Boosting altered epitope hierarchies and enabled germinal center B cell recruitment to subdominant epitopes.

articles.headline: Suriname achieves malaria elimination in Amazon region
articles.title_original: Suriname: first malaria-free country in the Amazon region.
articles.authors: Das M
articles.details: No abstract provided. Title indicates Suriname has been declared malaria-free and is the first country in the Amazon region to achieve malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Blood donor screening enables real-time malaria surveillance
articles.title_original: Blood donors as a sentinel population for real-time malaria surveillance using the Sysmex XN-31: a one-year review from the National Blood Transfusion Centre in Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Sawadogo S, Hwandih T, Saker J, Coetzer TL, Paszkowska A, Kompaoré SCB, Münster M
articles.details: One-year prospective study screened 53,575 consecutive blood donations at two Burkina Faso centers using Sysmex XN-31 automated analyzer from August 2023 to August 2024. Malaria prevalence: 5.91% (3,164/53,575). Key predictors: age ≤30 years (OR 2.85, p<0.001), male sex (OR 1.47, p<0.001), rural residency (OR 2.40, p<0.001). Strong seasonal variability mirrored rainfall with 3-month lag.

articles.headline: REL2 immune pathway regulates mosquito gut bacteria
articles.title_original: The major role of the REL2/NF-κB pathway in the regulation of midgut bacterial homeostasis in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Zaković S, Rivera GE, Gomaid R, Martinez CG, Kappler C, Marois E, Levashina EA
articles.details: Examined tissue-specific immune responses in Anopheles gambiae females post-blood meal and P. falciparum infection. REL2 regulates antimicrobial peptide genes and shapes midgut bacterial composition. Loss of REL2 led to midgut dysbiosis with Serratia spp. overgrowth and mosquito lethality; dysbiosis reduced P. falciparum prevalence in surviving mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Anopheles stephensi genome assembly completed
articles.title_original: Whole genome Shotgun Sequence of Anopheles stephensi, The Host of Malaria parasite, Plasmodium sp.
articles.authors: Farah J, Jang J, Madhi K, Phan J, Pratt E, Sankrit A, Sharma NL, Suchak A, Thomas DW, Ghazal SM
articles.details: Genome assembly of female Anopheles stephensi, a major malaria vector in Asia. Genome size: 201 Mbp, 32,280 contigs, N50 21.1 kb, GC content 45%.

articles.headline: Genetic and evolutionary mechanisms drive antimalarial drug resistance
articles.title_original: Editorial: Mechanisms driving drug resistance in tuberculosis and malaria: genetic, environmental, and evolutionary insights.
articles.authors: Kushwaha AK, Bansal A, Arora G, Kushwaha D
articles.details: Editorial examining genetic, environmental, and evolutionary mechanisms underlying drug resistance in tuberculosis and malaria; no abstract provided.

articles.headline: Rare dual P. vivax and P. falciparum infection
articles.title_original: Exflagellation of Plasmodium vivax and Dual Infection Diagnosed on Peripheral Blood Film: A Rare Case Report.
articles.authors: Patil BU, Ghongade P, Bhatkule M, Chavhan P
articles.details: Case report of 18-year-old male with simultaneous P. vivax (trophozoites, schizonts, exflagellated microgametes) and P. falciparum (trophozoites) on peripheral blood smear. Exflagellation during developmental phase is very unusual in humans. Case highlights diagnostic challenges and importance of correct identification to prevent transmission.

articles.headline: P. vivax genetic diversity structured across Mexican foci
articles.title_original: Population Dynamics of Plasmodium vivax in Mexico Determined by CSP, Pvs25, and SSU 18S rRNA S-Type Polymorphism Analyses.
articles.authors: González-Cerón L, Gómez-Pérez DJ, Santillán-Valenzuela F, Ovilla-Muñoz M, Guzmán-Bracho C, Pech-May A, Amores GR, Montoya-Pérez A, Villarreal-Treviño C
articles.details: Analyzed P. vivax from five Mexican malaria foci using CSP, pvs25, and 18S SSU rRNA markers. VK210 and VK247 pvcsp types both detected; VK210 had higher haplotype diversity (0.860) vs. VK247 (0.198). Two pvs25 mutations (Q87K, I130T) identified. AMOVA and FST values showed structured population among foci (p<0.001); Lacandon region harbored broadest haplotype reservoir.

articles.headline: Guillain-Barré Syndrome complication after P. falciparum infection
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome Following Falciparum Malaria in an 18-Year Male Adolescent: A Rare Case Report and Literature Review.
articles.authors: Getahun GA, Arega G, Jemal MK, Woldie BG, Gudina EK
articles.details: Case report of 18-year-old male who developed GBS (AMSAN variant) after P. falciparum infection with progressive bilateral weakness and bulbar dysfunction. CSF protein 172 mg/dL with no cells; nerve conduction studies confirmed AMSAN. Treated with intravenous artesunate and supportive care; weakness stabilized by day 14, significant improvement at 1 month.

articles.headline: Large gap between estimated and reported malaria cases
articles.title_original: Time to address the drawbacks in estimation and reporting of malaria cases in India and beyond.
articles.authors: Deora N, Singhal R, Sinha A
articles.details: Analysis of estimated-to-reported (ERR) malaria cases 2010-2022 shows consistently high ERR (India >10, WHO-SE Asia >6). Gap attributed to private sector under-reporting and WHO methodology assumptions. Contemporary research shows microscopy-positivity 3-55 times higher than NMCP reports, indicating substantial burden underestimation despite years of improvement efforts.

articles.headline: Parasitized erythroblasts revealed in cerebral malaria model
articles.title_original: Visual Detection of Malaria Parasite-Parasitized Erythroblasts in Peripheral Blood via Immunization-Based Model.
articles.authors: Ito K, Tateishi YS, Imai T, Miyazaki S, Miyazaki Y, Kagaya W, Nakashima M, Sase M, Yoshioka-Takeda M, Shimokawa C, Hayashi K, Itokawa K, Komagata O, Ngo-Thanh H, Shimo A, Araki T, Annoura T, Murakami T, Hisaeda H
articles.details: Immunized mice with live P. yoelii 17XNL then challenged with P. berghei ANKA. Immunized mice protected from cerebral malaria and survived longer, with parasitized erythroblasts (pEBs) appearing in peripheral circulation and detectable on Giemsa-stained smears. Model enabled in vivo/ex vivo pEB analysis without bone marrow/spleen isolation, lowering technical barriers.

articles.headline: Vector ecology reveals malaria transmission risk in Iran
articles.title_original: Culicidae vector ecology in southern Iran: Integrating Faunistics and molecular analysis of blood-feeding preferences to strengthen malaria surveillance in transition zones.
articles.authors: Kalantari M, Azizi K
articles.details: Entomological survey April 2023-January 2024 in Shiraz collected 1,249 adult mosquitoes. Dominant species: Culex pipiens (39.0%), Cx. quinquefasciatus (23.6%), Aedes caspius (19.6%), Anopheles dthali (80.6%), An. sacharovi (19.4%). PCR-RFLP of 50 blood-fed females: Anopheles fed on rodents/dogs (41%) and humans (28%); Cx. pipiens anthropophilic (54% human). An. sacharovi clustered in irrigation-rich areas (χ²=12.7, p<0.01).

articles.headline: Age, wealth, maternal education predict childhood malaria
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic Factors Associated with Malaria Infection among Under-Five Children in Nigeria.
articles.authors: Agboola HO, Ajibola O, Lasisi AJ, Adebayo F, Aremu O, Khan S
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 2018 Nigeria Demographic Health Survey, n=10,352 children, using malaria RDT. Child age (χ²=166.200, p<0.001), mother's education (χ²=762.530, p<0.001), and wealth index (χ²=1020.442, p<0.001) were statistically significant. Rural residence: OR=1.633 (p<0.001). Conclusion: age, wealth, maternal education, and residence are key predictors of malaria in under-five children.

articles.headline: SMC knowledge gaps limit full coverage in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Knowledge and Uptake of Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention among Caregivers of Under-five Children in a Tertiary Hospital in Kaduna State, North-western Nigeria.
articles.authors: Tom HM, Suleiman AG, Usman S, Darma MA, Yahaya U, Wakawa MM, Hamza AS, Babandi ZS
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 234 caregivers in Kaduna, Nigeria. 182 (76.8%) heard of SMC, 156 (65.8%) knew it as preventive, 128 (54.0%) correctly identified as rainy-season activity. 137 (58.4%) received ≥1 dose, only 65 (27.2%) completed all 4 doses. Barriers: not knowing SMC (54.0%), child absence (20.0%). Factors associated with uptake: education, occupation, ethnicity, religion, knowledge (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Mixed P. falciparum-vivax infection in G6PD-deficient patient
articles.title_original: Navigating Challenges in Mixed Malaria Infections: A Case of Mixed Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Infection in a Patient With Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase (G6PD) Deficiency.
articles.authors: Lau CKS, Kek XY, Gan WF
articles.details: Case report of imported mixed malaria infection in a patient with glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency. P. falciparum and P. vivax were identified through malaria PCR. Patient's condition improved after completing IV artesunate, oral artemether/lumefantrine, and primaquine.

articles.headline: A. australe extract inhibits P. falciparum heat shock protein 70
articles.title_original: Acanthospermum australe Extract Inhibits the Chaperone Activity of Plasmodium falciparum Heat Shock Protein 70-1.
articles.authors: Koza NA, Myeza NN, Hoppe H, Mosa RA, Kappo AP, Simelane MBC, Opoku AR
articles.details: Dichloromethane extract of Acanthospermum australe tested against chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum strain 3D7 showed IC50 of 1.3 µg/mL. Extract inhibited PfHsp70-1 chaperone activity via decreased protein aggregation and ATPase inhibition. Results suggest compounds targeting parasite Hsp70 functions could serve as antimalarials.

articles.headline: Mitochondrial function as emerging antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Targeting Mitochondrial Function in Plasmodium falciparum: Insight into Antimalarial Drugs and the Emerging Role of Saccharomyces cerevisiae as a Model System.
articles.authors: Greco S, Assalve G, Lunetti P, Kayentao K, Dara A, Scaramuzzi D, Zara V, Ferramosca A
articles.details: Review of advances in compounds targeting P. falciparum mitochondrial function and utility of Saccharomyces cerevisiae as model organism for antimalarial drug discovery. S. cerevisiae offers cost-effective, experimentally accessible platform for elucidating drug mechanisms and accelerating therapeutic development through heterologous expression of parasite mitochondrial proteins.

articles.headline: Supporting global fight against AIDS, TB, and malaria
articles.title_original: Supporting the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis, and Malaria.
articles.authors: Piot P, Abubakar I
articles.details: [No abstract provided]

articles.headline: Novel malaria prevention strategies needed in pregnancy
articles.title_original: A need for new tools for prevention of malaria in pregnancy.
articles.authors: Saito M, McGready R
articles.details: Commentary highlighting need for improved understanding of how intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine reduces low birthweight in pregnant women and development of novel prevention measures earlier in gestation.

articles.headline: Controlled human malaria infection studies advance vaccine development
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection studies: insights into recent advances and key immunological and ethical implementation lessons.
articles.authors: Ogwang R, Adan M, Bejon P, Kapulu MC
articles.details: Review of controlled human malaria infection (CHMI) models and their applications in malaria interventions. CHMI studies supported clinical development of RTSS/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M vaccines and enabled study of drugs, monoclonal antibodies, and immunity mechanisms to identify correlates of protection.

articles.headline: P. vivax hotspot districts challenge malaria elimination in India
articles.title_original: Hotspot districts of persistent Plasmodium vivax malaria pose a formidable challenge to malaria elimination in India.
articles.authors: Ramasamy A, Thiruvengadam K, Viswan K A, Sharma R, Nagarajan SA, Yadav CP, Sharma A, Rahi M
articles.details: [No abstract provided]

articles.headline: P. vivax invades Duffy-negative individuals via PvDBP gene duplication
articles.title_original: High prevalence of mixed Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax co-infections in Duffy negative individuals in Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Tat B, Feufack-Donfack LB, Neneyoh Yengo B, Eng V, Tefera Shenkutie T, Abebe A, Tsigie M, Tacoli C, Khim N, Feleke SM, Lo E, Popovici J
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 1,154 febrile patients in three Ethiopian regions (2020–2021) found P. falciparum in 49%, mixed P. falciparum/P. vivax in 26%, and P. vivax mono-infections in 23%. Among 41% Duffy-negative individuals, P. vivax mono-infections were rare (2.4%), yet 25% of mixed infections occurred in Duffy-negatives. PvDBP gene duplication detected in 78% of tested samples, especially in Gambela (94%), suggesting parasite adaptation to overcome host receptor barriers.

articles.headline: Regulatory T1 cells balance malaria immunity and parasite persistence
articles.title_original: CXCR6(+) CD127(-) Tr1 Cells Balance Immunity and Persistence in Plasmodium falciparum Infection.
articles.authors: Nideffer J, Bach F, Strubbe S, Lopez L, Zedi M, Nankya F, Briggs J, van der Ploeg K, Musinguzi K, Kim S, Romero AG, Keya A, Camanag K, Lewis S, Abdelbasset M, Wang B, Boss A, Nansubuga E, Nankabirwa JI, Arinaitwe E, Takahashi S, Dorsey G, Greenhouse B, Rodriguez-Barraquer I, Kamya MR, Bacchetta R, Ssewanyana I, Haque A, Roncarolo MG, Jagannathan P
articles.details: Study in children from malaria-endemic Uganda identified CXCR6+ CD127- Tr1 cells as dominant P. falciparum-specific responders via scRNAseq. Higher Tr1 frequencies correlated with lower symptomatic malaria risk but also delayed parasite clearance in untreated, asymptomatic children. Tr1 abundance associated with elevated plasma granzyme A, IFNγ, IL-10, and LAG3.

articles.headline: Anopheles funestus genomic diversity and vector control resistance evolution
articles.title_original: Genomic diversity of the African malaria vector Anopheles funestus.
articles.authors: Boddé M, Nwezeobi J, Korlević P, Makunin A, Akone-Ella O, Barasa S, Gadji M, Hart L, Kaindoa EW, Love K, Lucas ER, Lujumba I, Máquina M, Nagi SC, Odero JO, Polo B, Sangbakembi C, Dadzie S, Koekemoer LL, Kwiatkowski D, McAlister E, Ochomo E, Okumu F, Paaijmans K, Tchouassi DP, Wondji CS, Ayala D, Durbin R, Miles A, Lawniczak MKN
articles.details: Analysis of 656 modern (2014–2018) and 45 historic (1927–1967) A. funestus specimens from 16 African countries revealed stable continental population structure despite high genetic diversity. Vector control exerted strong selection pressure with resistance alleles shared through gene flow or arising independently. Gene drive target conserved in A. gambiae is highly conserved in A. funestus.

articles.headline: DHA-piperaquine plus SP no additional benefit over SP in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine plus sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine versus either drug alone for intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy: A double-blind, randomized, controlled phase 3 trial from Uganda.
articles.authors: Kakuru A, Kizza J, Aguti M, Adrama H, Ategeka J, Olwoch P, Nakalembe M, Nankabirwa JI, Opira B, Ozarslan N, Ranjit A, Dela Cruz E, Clark TD, Roh ME, Gaw SL, Jagannathan P, Rosenthal PJ, Kamya MR, Dorsey G
articles.details: Double-blind randomized trial of 2,757 pregnant women in Uganda compared monthly IPTp: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-P), or DHA-P plus SP. Composite adverse birth outcome was 30.0% with DHA-P+SP vs 26.4% with SP (RR 1.14 [95% CI 0.98–1.33], p=0.10). DHA-P showed 94% reduction in symptomatic malaria and 97% reduction in microscopic parasitemia versus SP (p<0.001).

articles.headline: Rwanda's malaria resurgence despite progress: resistance and surveillance gaps
articles.title_original: Investigating the resurgence of malaria in Rwanda: factors contributing to rising cases amidst near-eradication success and strategies for sustained control.
articles.authors: Julius N, Diane BI
articles.details: Review of Rwanda's 45.8% case increase from 2023/24 to 2024 (87,000 cases in March 2024) identified contributing factors: meteorological changes widening A. gambiae habitat, pyrethroid and artemisinin resistance including K13 mutations, suboptimal IRS coverage, poverty-driven nonadherence, and human mobility. Proposed solutions include community health workers, drone-mediated larviciding (96.4% community acceptance), non-pyrethroid IRS, and molecular resistance screening.

articles.headline: P. falciparum evolution and host-parasite-vector arms race
articles.title_original: Evolutionary Trajectory of Plasmodium falciparum: From Autonomous Phototroph to Dedicated Parasite.
articles.authors: Pikor D, Hurla M, Drelichowska A, Paul M
articles.details: Review of co-evolutionary dynamics between humans, Anopheles vectors, and P. falciparum. Discusses host genetic adaptations (hemoglobinopathies, polymorphisms modulating immune responses), parasite mechanisms (antigenic variation, drug resistance mutations, epigenetic regulation), and vector adaptations to insecticides. Highlights genomic streamlining optimizing parasitic lifestyle and rapid mutation accumulation facilitating immune escape and drug resistance emergence.

articles.headline: Maternal risk factors for parasitemia and low birth weight in Uganda
articles.title_original: Maternal and household risk factors for malaria in pregnancy and low birthweight: a prospective cohort study from Uganda.
articles.authors: Aguti M, Kizza J, Kakuru A, Nakalembe M, Nankabirwa JI, Gaw SL, Opira B, Ssemukuye T, Ozarslan N, Ranjit A, Cruz E, Clark TD, Roh ME, Jagannathan P, Rosenthal PJ, Adrama H, Olwoch P, Mayende J, Odongo B, John A, Kamya MR, Dorsey G
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 2,728 pregnant women in Busia District, Uganda (2020-2024). At enrollment, 38.1% had parasitemia; after IPTp initiation, 6.5% had parasitemia; 6.4% had high-grade placental malaria; 6.8% had low birth weight. Younger age (16-21 years, RR=1.62), primigravida (RR=1.86 at enrollment, RR=2.40 after IPTp), and traditional housing (RR=1.17-1.30) associated with parasitemia. Primigravida strongly associated with high-grade placental malaria (RR=4.20) and low birth weight (RR=2.14).

articles.headline: Climate change and malaria transmission dynamics in Zimbabwe
articles.title_original: Impact of Climate Change on Malaria Transmission and Management in Zimbabwe: A Scoping Review of the Literature.
articles.authors: Chivasa T, Nunu WN, Dhlamini M, Maviza A, Ndagurwa GN
articles.details: Scoping review (22 literature sources) examining climate change impacts on malaria in Zimbabwe. Most studies showed climate change shifts malaria's geographic distribution, timing, and intensity. However, non-climatic factors (human, ecological, health system) also critically shape transmission. Identified key gaps: lack of longitudinal data, limited localized predictive modeling, and limited focus on climate-parasite dynamics.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol on progression to severe malaria
articles.title_original: Progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria among children in settings receiving different malaria control interventions in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review protocol.
articles.authors: Okek EJ, Lutwama J, Kinengyere AA, Asio J, Awor S, Le Doare K, Musinguzi B, Sande JO, Ocan M, Kayondo J
articles.details: Systematic review protocol (PROSPERO CRD42024619945) examining progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria in sub-Saharan Africa children receiving concurrent malaria control interventions. Will search PubMed, Web of Science, Embase, Scopus, and Google Scholar. Expected completion by end of December 2025. No results presented.

articles.headline: Mosquito-based transmission-blocking vaccine development and strategies
articles.title_original: Mosquito-based transmission-blocking vaccine candidates for malaria: progress, challenges, and innovations.
articles.authors: Dahiya N, Yadav M, Kataria D, Janjoter S, Sehrawat N
articles.details: Review of transmission-blocking vaccine (TBV) development. Current vaccines (RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M) show lower efficacy. Discusses how mosquito-based TBVs can effectively target diverse parasite types and reduce vector survival. Reviews TBV antigen candidates, challenges in development, and promising approaches to improve transmission-blocking efficacy.

articles.headline: Strain-specific antibody responses in controlled human malaria infection
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection with NF54 and 7G8 strains elicit differential antibody responses to Plasmodium falciparum peptides.
articles.authors: Friedman-Klabanoff DJ, Jensen TL, Lyke KE, Laurens MB, Silva JC, Stucke EM, Ouattara A, Ifeonu OO, Hodges T, Moser KA, Gelber CE, Goll JB, Hoffman SL, Patel JJ, Pinapati RS, Tan JC, Deye GA, Takala-Harrison S, Travassos MA, Berry AA
articles.details: CHMI study with 38 malaria-naïve adults challenged with NF54 (n=21) or 7G8 (n=17) strains. Designed peptide microarray with 638,817 unique peptides from 227 proteins. Sera tested pre-infection and 28 days post-infection showed similar intensity of responses between strains but 7G8 elicited higher breadth of responses to 17 of 20 proteins. Of 543 predicted epitopes, 66 overlapped with identified epitopes; 6 were highly cross-reactive (>95%).

articles.headline: El Niño and shifting malaria transmission in Amazon
articles.title_original: El Niño-Southern Oscillation and shifting malaria risks in the Amazon.
articles.authors: Laporta GZ
articles.details: Article examining how El Niño-Southern Oscillation climate patterns affect malaria transmission risks in the Amazon region.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria pathophysiology and emerging biomarkers
articles.title_original: Neurovascular Pathophysiology and Emerging Biomarkers in Cerebral Malaria: An Integrative Perspective.
articles.authors: Pikor D, Hurła M, Banaszek-Hurła N, Drelichowska A, Paul M
articles.details: Review of cerebral malaria (P. falciparum, pediatric) pathophysiology in endemic sub-Saharan Africa. Discusses cytoadherence in cerebral microvasculature, inflammatory response, endothelial activation, blood-brain barrier disruption, and cerebral edema. MRI studies identify characteristic findings (basal ganglia infarcts, brainstem lesions, corpus callosum abnormalities) strongly predicting poor outcomes. Extracellular vesicles from parasitized RBCs and activated endothelial cells are emerging diagnostic and prognostic biomarkers. Survivors often have neurocognitive impairment, behavioral problems, and epilepsy.

articles.headline: P. vivax malaria severity and complications in India
articles.title_original: Clinical spectrum and complications of Plasmodium vivax malaria: A retrospective study from Delhi, India.
articles.authors: Singh D, Uddin A, Bajaj K, Chaturvedi A, Garg R, Chandra K, Das A, Kohli S, Jain V
articles.details: Retrospective study of 361 P. vivax malaria patients at tertiary hospital in South Delhi (June 2020-May 2024). 167/361 (46.3%) developed complications. Mean age 31 years, 64.6% male. Common findings: anemia (73.4%), thrombocytopenia (57.9%), leucocytosis. Thrombocytopenia (OR=3.20, p<0.001) and leucocytosis (OR=2.37, p<0.05) linked to severity. Elevated creatinine (OR=6.07, p=0.001) and hyperbilirubinemia (OR=3.71, p<0.001) strongly correlated with complications. Females had higher complication rates (42% vs 32%, p=0.039).

articles.headline: African representation on Global Fund governance board
articles.title_original: It is time to increase Africa's governmental representation on the governing board of the global fund to fight AIDS, tuberculosis and malaria.
articles.authors: Nalugala RM, Kakoma J, Bridge G, von Agris JM, Hayes Badenhorst R, Fransen L, Brown GW
articles.details: Opinion piece advocating for increased African government representation on the governing board of the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria.

articles.headline: Malarial retinopathy accuracy for cerebral malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Re-evaluating malarial retinopathy to improve its diagnostic accuracy in paediatric cerebral malaria: A retrospective study.
articles.authors: Wilson KJ, Muiruri Liomba A, Seydel KB, Banda OK, Moxon CA, MacCormick IJC, Harding SP, Beare NAV, Taylor TE
articles.details: Retrospective analysis from Blantyre, Malawi (1996-2010) of 84 children with fatal cerebral malaria, 65 meeting WHO criteria. 18/65 (28%) lacked cerebral parasite sequestration at autopsy. Malarial retinopathy showed sensitivity 89.4% (95% CI 77.6-95.6%) and specificity 73.0% (95% CI 57.2-84.8%) for predicting cerebral parasite sequestration. Improved to 94.3% (95% CI 81.7-98.7%) sensitivity and 88.0% (95% CI 70.4-96.2%) specificity by reclassifying single-eye hemorrhages (1-5) as retinopathy-negative.

articles.headline: HLA-G polymorphism and severe malaria in iron-fortified children
articles.title_original: HLA-G 14 bp polymorphism: Implications and its associated risk to malaria severity among iron-fortified Ghanaian infants and young children.
articles.authors: Tetteh Danquah A, Courtin D, Asamoah Sakyi S, Nakotey GK, Tchum SK, Afriyie Duah N, Lagrave A, Sewor C, Amoani B
articles.details: Secondary analysis of double-blinded cluster RCT with 432 archival samples from Ghana (Tain District, Wenchi Municipal). Children aged 6-36 months. Among iron-fortified children, HLA-G 14bp+/- and 14bp-/- variants associated with increased risk of severe malaria. HLA-G 14bp+/- variant also linked to higher anemia risk in those receiving iron supplements.

articles.headline: Novel Plasmodium lineages identified in Vietnamese passerine birds
articles.title_original: Hidden Plasmodium diversity revealed in southeastern Asian passerines using next-generation amplicon sequencing.
articles.authors: Damnjanović D, Nazarizadeh M, Nováková M, Linh NV, Tolstenkov O, Kerimov A, Bushuev A, Štefka J
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Next-generation sequencing of the cytochrome b gene identified two known and ten novel Plasmodium lineages in three Vietnamese passerine bird species (little spiderhunter, white-rumped shama, blue-winged pitta) at Cat-Tien National Park, Vietnam. Species delimitation identified five distinct operational taxonomic units. Each bird species carried a specific subset of parasite diversity, suggesting that host identity and habitat use filter parasite distribution.

articles.headline: Repeated Plasmodium vivax relapses despite primaquine treatment
articles.title_original: Recurrent Malaria with Plasmodium vivax: A Case Report and Brief Review of the Literature.
articles.authors: Andrejkovits ÁV, Pop AV, Fejér M, Gîrbovan EC, Coșeriu RL, Vintilă C, Văsieșiu AM
articles.details: Case report of a 41-year-old man from Romania who developed P. vivax malaria after travel to Southeast Asia. After initial treatment with doxycycline and atovaquone-proguanil, the patient experienced two relapses, both confirmed as P. vivax by RT-PCR. Despite receiving primaquine as radical cure after the first relapse, a second relapse occurred. Each episode resolved with blood-stage therapy, highlighting the challenges of P. vivax radical cure.

articles.headline: Household practices and P. falciparum infection rates in Ugandan children
articles.title_original: Correction: Household practices and infrastructure associated with high Plasmodium falciparum infection rates among children under five years old in Northern Uganda.
articles.authors: Echodu R, Nyeko C, Okot J, Iwiru T, Nakiwu V, Aryemo F, Ajolorwot S
articles.details: Correction article on household practices and infrastructure associated with high P. falciparum infection rates among children under five years old in Northern Uganda. The original study appears to examine household-level risk factors for malaria infection in this age group in a low-resource setting.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention reduces malaria burden in Sahelian children
articles.title_original: Status and prospects of seasonal malaria chemoprevention among children in Sahelian countries: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Djedanem M, Salé NM, Aminou EYYM, Testa J, Jambou R
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC), which delivers sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine monthly to children under five during the transmission season in Sahelian countries over ten years. Meta-analysis of randomized and observational studies from 2013-2023 found SMC effective in reducing incidence of uncomplicated malaria, severe malaria, and mortality in children under five compared with control groups. However, the epidemiological context is changing with persistence of dry-season transmission and increasing malaria in older children.

articles.headline: Gracilaria seaweed extract shows antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of Gracilaria sp. ethanol extract against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Setyaningrum E, Septiani L, Wibowo H, Arifiyanto A, Suryaningkunti JA
articles.details: In vitro screening of ethanol extract from the seaweed Gracilaria sp. for antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum strain D37. Phytochemical analysis identified saponins, tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids in the extract. Alkaloids, flavonoids, and derivative compounds inhibited parasite growth with an IC50 of 43.38 μg/mL, suggesting potential for antimalarial drug development from seaweed resources.

articles.headline: Biophysical mechanisms of P. falciparum-infected erythrocyte adhesion in flow
articles.title_original: Contact Dynamics of Cytoadhering Plasmodium falciparum-Infected Erythrocytes in Flow.
articles.authors: Scholz K, Papagrigorakes M, Lettermann L, Pennarola F, Patra P, Dasanna AK, Sanchez CP, Kehrer J, Cavalcanti-Adam EA, Schwarz US, Tanaka M, Lanzer M
articles.details: Study investigating dynamic adhesion behavior of P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes to endothelial cell adhesion molecules (ICAM-1, CD36) using molecular force sensors and high-speed microscopy. Trophozoite-stage parasites exhibited complex motion including flipping and sliding with patchy adhesion, while schizont-stage parasites showed more uniform rolling. Adhesion increased on CD36-containing surfaces for both developmental stages, with some footprints extending across endothelial cell-length distances. Findings provide insights into cytoadhesion mechanisms driving microvasculature pathology.

articles.headline: High seroprevalence of transmission-blocking vaccine target antigens in Tanzanian populations
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum transmission-blocking target proteins Pfs230D1M and Pfs48/45 in Tanzanian populations of diverse malaria transmission intensity.
articles.authors: Mulamba C, Kalinga WF, Mtaka I, Lazaro LO, Kamage J, Nkumama I, Odufuwa OG, Kreppel K, Mekhaiel D, Miura K, Long CA, Olotu AI, Williams C
articles.details: Serosurvey of 290 Tanzanian participants (114 children ages 5-12, 44 adolescents 13-17, 132 adults 18-45) for IgG antibodies to transmission-blocking vaccine candidates Pfs230D1M and Pfs48/45. Seroprevalence was 56% for Pfs230D1M (157/281) and 49% for Pfs48/45 (141/290), with ~30% seropositive for both. Antibody prevalence increased significantly with age (Pfs230D1M adjusted OR: 3.16, 95% CI: 1.81-5.53, p<0.0001; Pfs48/45 OR: 3.06, 95% CI: 1.79-5.25, p<0.0001). Naturally acquired antibody responses were stable and associated with transmission-reducing activity.

articles.headline: Malaria drug libraries yield SARS-CoV-2 protease inhibitors
articles.title_original: Screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture Open Boxes Identifies Potent SARS-CoV‑2 Papain-like Protease (PL(pro)) Inhibitors.
articles.authors: Gawriljuk VO, Noske GD, Fernandes RS, Nakamura AM, Freire MCLC, Godoy MO, Bonatto V, Chelucci R, Andricopulo A, Bakowski MA, Wolff KC, Riva L, Burrows JN, Wells TNC, Laleu B, Martins R, Souza JP, Arruda E, Samby K, Laskar S, Guido RVC, Oliva G, Godoy AS
articles.details: Target-based screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture open-access compound libraries (including malaria box, pathogen box, COVID box) against SARS-CoV-2 proteases, particularly papain-like protease (PLpro). MMV1634397 emerged as a promising inhibitor with IC50=0.7 μM; optimization produced more potent analogs with IC50 values of 0.16, 0.34, and 0.06 μM. The most potent compound and its isomer showed antiviral activity in cell culture (EC50=2.9 and 3.7 μM) with favorable pharmacokinetic properties.

articles.headline: Malaria and chemoprophylaxis among US military during Vietnam War
articles.title_original: US Malaria Casualties and Chemoprophylaxis during the Vietnam War.
articles.authors: Shanks GD
articles.details: Historical perspective on malaria as a major military casualty in Vietnam, with hospitalization rates matching combat wounds in 1965 and infection rates exceeding 10 per 1,000 man-days in front-line units. Chemoprophylaxis using chloroquine-primaquine (from Korean War) proved inadequate; addition of daily dapsone improved efficacy but caused rare agranulocytosis and at least 8 deaths. After Vietnam, thousands of US military infections occurred, with severe malaria causing 78 deaths (Army) and 46 (Marines). Average lost productivity was over 1 month per case and 200,000 lost man-days annually.

articles.headline: Helminth co-infection does not increase severe malaria risk in Ugandan children
articles.title_original: Low prevalence of helminth infection in Ugandan children hospitalized with severe malaria.
articles.authors: Capone D, Jahan N, Namazzi R, Opoka RO, John CC
articles.details: Case-control analysis of 177 stool samples from Ugandan children (6-48 months) hospitalized with severe malaria and 25 community control children for intestinal helminth co-infection using quantitative PCR. Helminth prevalence was low in both groups: 5.1% (9/177) in severe malaria cases and 4.0% (1/25) in community controls. Helminth infection did not modify severe malaria risk (adjusted relative risk = 1.0, 95% CI: 0.82-1.3, p=0.78). The Uganda national deworming program has been highly successful in reducing helminth prevalence.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic non-falciparum malaria prevalence in Rwanda survey of adults
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic non-falciparum and falciparum malaria in the 2014-15 Rwanda Demographic Health Survey.
articles.authors: Gaither C, Morgan C, Kirby R, Karema C, Gashema P, Zuromski J, White SJ, Topazian HM, Giesbrecht D, Thwai K, Young NW, Goel V, Boyter K, Munyaneza T, Muvunyi CM, Butera JD, Bailey JA, Mazarati JB, Juliano JJ
articles.details: Quantitative PCR screening for four Plasmodium species in 5,050 primarily adult Rwandans from the 2014-2015 Demographic Health Survey. Overall infection prevalence was 22.3% (95% CI: 20.3-24.3%), with P. falciparum at 16.3% (95% CI: 14.5-18.1%) and non-falciparum species at 8.0% (95% CI: 6.6-9.3%). Non-falciparum infections were predominantly P. ovale and P. malariae, with P. vivax rare. Mixed-species infections were common where transmission was highest. P. falciparum associated with lower wealth, rural residence, and low elevation.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure reveals P. falciparum fertilization complex as transmission-blocking target
articles.title_original: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 and Pfs48/45 fertilization complex.
articles.authors: Dietrich MH, Chmielewski J, Chan LJ, Tan LL, Adair A, Lyons FMT, Gabriela M, Lopaticki S, Dite TA, Dagley LF, Pazzagli L, Gupta P, Kamil M, Vaughan AM, Rojrung R, Abraham A, Mazhari R, Longley RJ, Zeglinski K, Gouil Q, Mueller I, Fabb SA, Shandre-Mugan R, Pouton CW, Glukhova A, Shakeel S, Tham WH
articles.details: Cryo-electron microscopy structure of the endogenous P. falciparum Pfs230-Pfs48/45 complex, which mediates parasite fertilization in the mosquito midgut. Pfs48/45 interacts with Pfs230 domains 13 and 14; transgenic parasite lines with these domains deleted showed defective gamete localization and reduced oocyst formation. Nanobodies against domains 13-14 inhibited complex formation and reduced transmission in feeding assays. These domains were targets of naturally acquired immunity and immune sera from mRNA-LNP immunizations effectively blocked transmission.

articles.headline: Imported malaria patterns in New York State, 2018-2024
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in New York: Geographic Patterns and Implications for Emergency Physicians.
articles.authors: Webb Z
articles.details: Surveillance study of imported malaria cases in New York State from 2018-2024. New York City accounted for 79.1% of reported cases; 11 of 57 counties outside NYC contributed 88.3% of state cases. Cases declined in 2020 during COVID-19 travel restrictions and rebounded in 2021. Study emphasizes importance of travel history awareness and county-level epidemiological knowledge for emergency department evaluation of febrile patients.

articles.headline: Walnut leaf extract protects against oxidative stress in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Effect of walnut leaves on oxidative stress caused by murine cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Abdel-Gaber R, Alharbi A, Almohawis N, Al Quraishy S, Al-Shaebi E
articles.details: Preclinical study in female C57BL/6 mice infected with Plasmodium berghei treated with Juglans regia leaf extract (JRLE). Daily JRLE treatment significantly reduced oxidative stress markers (nitric oxide, malondialdehyde, hydrogen peroxide) and enhanced antioxidant enzyme activity (catalase, superoxide dismutase, glutathione, total antioxidant capacity) compared to infected controls. JRLE also mitigated increased inducible nitric oxide synthase expression. Findings support J. regia as a source for novel antioxidant and antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: Plasmodium telomere maintenance as target for novel antimalarials
articles.title_original: Plasmodium telomere maintenance: uncovering the Achilles' heel for novel antimalarials.
articles.authors: Wakai TN, Anzaku DO, Afolabi IS
articles.details: Review examining Plasmodium telomere biology as a potential target for antimalarial drug development. Discusses how P. falciparum maintains continuous telomerase activity distinct from humans and harbors G-quadruplex DNA structures that stabilize telomeres. The parasite's dependence on PfTERT (telomerase catalytic subunit) for survival makes it a promising target; inhibiting PfTERT blocks telomere elongation and impairs replication. Authors propose combining telomere-directed therapies with existing antimalarials to overcome resistance, though current evidence remains limited.

articles.headline: SARS-CoV-2 and malaria prevalence and co-infection in Equatorial Guinea
articles.title_original: Circulation of SARS-CoV-2 and Co-Infection with Plasmodium falciparum in Equatorial Guinea.
articles.authors: López-Farfán D, Ncogo P, Oki C, Riloha M, Ondo V, Cano-Jiménez P, Martínez-Martínez FJ, Molina-de la Fuente I, Comas I, Irigoyen N, Berzosa P, Benito Llanes A, Gómez-Díaz E
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 1,556 participants at six health centers in Equatorial Guinea (June-October 2021). SARS-CoV-2 prevalence was 3.03%, malaria prevalence 22.25%, and co-infection 0.37% (6 cases). Malaria was most frequent in children and teenagers, while SARS-CoV-2 occurred at similar frequencies across age groups. Sequencing of 43 SARS-CoV-2 isolates identified Delta lineage, predominantly from European introductions. Study provides first estimates of SARS-CoV-2 prevalence in a malaria-endemic setting.

articles.headline: Plant metabolites with potent antimalarial activity against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Plant-derived secondary metabolites for malaria treatment: extraction, mechanisms, and therapeutic potential.
articles.authors: Wassie AT, Ahmed IN, Bachheti RK, Tadesse MG, Wotango AS, Bachheti A, Gonfa YH, Assen AH
articles.details: Systematic review identifying 34 medicinal plants with antimalarial potential. Key compounds include alkaloids, flavonoids, terpenoids, and derivatives (quinine, artemisinin, quercetin, chalcones) with activity against Plasmodium falciparum. Highly potent compounds demonstrated: joziknipholone A (IC50 0.17 μM), joziknipholone B (0.26 μM), knipholone clooxanthrone (9.42-14.58 μM depending on parasite strain), and ceramicine R (2.80 μM). Review covers extraction techniques, mechanisms of action, and potential for combating drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: Correction to article on malaria vector habitat and LLINs in Côte d'Ivoire
articles.title_original: Correction: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission over 1 year of long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.authors: Talbot B, Alou LPA, Koffi AA, Sih C, Dangbenon E, Zoh MG, Camara S, Assi SB, N'Guessan R, Messenger LA, Protopopoff N, Cook J, Kulkarni MA
articles.details: Correction notice to a previously published article examining ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat during a year-long long-lasting insecticidal net (LLIN) intervention study in Côte d'Ivoire. No additional substantive findings provided in this correction.

articles.headline: High Plasmodium species complexity in asymptomatic carriers in Ghana
articles.title_original: Metagenomic complexity of high, seasonal transmission of Plasmodium spp. in asymptomatic carriers in Northern Sahelian Ghana.
articles.authors: Tan MH, Bangre O, Rios-Teran CA, Tiedje KE, Deed SL, Zhan Q, Rasyidi F, Pascual M, Ansah PO, Day KP
articles.details: Metagenomic study of 188 afebrile residents in Northern Ghana with high, seasonal malaria transmission. Asymptomatic submicroscopic infection rate was 2.67% detected by qPCR and genotyping of antigens and microsatellites. High within-host metagenomic complexity observed: single-, double-, and triple-species infections including P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale curtisi, and P. ovale wallikeri, with P. vivax predominant. Detected high frequencies of antimalarial drug-resistance mutations: pfk13 C580Y, pfcrt M74I/N75E/K76T, and pvmdr-1 F1076L all at 100%.

articles.headline: PfPP1 phosphatase regulates P. falciparum transmission to mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PP1 phosphatase is a key regulator of malaria parasite transmission.
articles.authors: Royer L, Colard-Itté E, Tavella T, Lorthiois A, Goussin S, N'Dri M-E, Sabra R, Deiss A, Thiberge S, Lamarque MH, Lavazec C
articles.details: Functional study using conditional depletion to examine PfPP1 phosphatase role in Plasmodium falciparum gametocyte development and transmission. PfPP1 regulates mechanical properties of mature gametocyte-infected erythrocytes via PKA substrate dephosphorylation, controlling gametocyte persistence in blood. PfPP1 also controls gametocyte activation into gametes through cGMP/PKG pathway regulation. Results demonstrate PfPP1 is essential for both asexual proliferation and sexual transmission stages, identifying it as a potential transmission-blocking drug target.

articles.headline: Melochia umbellata extract suppresses parasitemia and protects organs in malaria
articles.title_original: Curative antimalarial efficacy and hepato-renal protective effects of Melochia umbellata leaf extract in rats infected with Plasmodium berghei.
articles.authors: Ngouana V, Tsouh Fokou PV, Tsakem Nangap MJ, Kemzeu R, Kamche Youbi A, Boyom FF
articles.details: In vivo study in Plasmodium berghei-infected rats treated for 5 days with M. umbellata leaf aqueous extract (MULAE) at 100, 200, or 400 mg/kg or chloroquine (10 mg/kg). MULAE dose-dependently suppressed parasitemia, with 400 mg/kg achieving 86.20% chemosuppression and restoring hemoglobin to 14.9 g/dL. Extract improved liver and kidney markers (creatinine, ALAT, ASAT, bilirubin) and reversed histopathological changes. Acute toxicity testing showed LD50 >5000 mg/kg. Findings validate traditional use and support M. umbellata as source for antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: High antimalarial drug-resistance marker prevalence in asymptomatic malaria carriers, Vietnam
articles.title_original: Molecular Profiles of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium Species from Asymptomatic Malaria Carriers in Gia Lai Province, Vietnam.
articles.authors: Lê HG, Võ TC, Kang JM, Van Khanh C, Trinh NTM, Hanh NTL, Cho M, Quang HH, Na BK
