articles.details: Surveillance study of 2,171 individuals in Gia Lai Province, Vietnam identified 2.67% asymptomatic submicroscopic infection rate by qPCR and species sequencing. Mixed and mono-infections of P. falciparum and P. vivax detected, with P. vivax predominant. High frequencies of drug-resistance-associated mutations detected: pfk13 C580Y (100%), pfcrt M74I/N75E/K76T (100%), and pvmdr-1 F1076L (100%) in asymptomatic individuals. Asymptomatic infections were more common in females and adult age groups. Findings emphasize urgent need for improved surveillance to prevent silent spread of drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: Public-private partnerships for climate-resilient malaria control in Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Challenges and opportunities for greater public-private partnership for the implementation of the WHO operational framework for building climate resilient health systems to improve malaria control and elimination in Sub-Saharan Africa: a rapid review.
articles.authors: Lumumba S, Kamau S, Ntwiga I, Muchangi JM, Kiarie J, Kosgei S, Mwamburi M, Kimathi G
articles.details: Systematic review (PRISMA-compliant) of 11 studies examining public-private partnerships (PPPs) for climate-resilient health systems supporting malaria control in Sub-Saharan Africa. Identified challenges: ineffective legal and policy frameworks, bureaucratic barriers, limited private sector adherence to guidelines, insufficient integration of climate risk assessments in health planning. Opportunities exist through health workforce training via online courses, coordinated emergency preparedness, and co-production mechanisms for climate and malaria research. Concludes PPPs remain viable for adapting WHO operational framework despite challenges.

articles.headline: Delays in malaria care-seeking in Myanmar and Thailand
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking among febrile patients in Myanmar and Thailand: an accelerated failure time analysis.
articles.authors: Htwe KZZ, Aung PL, Sripoorote P, Soe MT, Petchvijit P, Aung PP, Khamsiriwatchara A, Lawpoolsri S, Cui L, Parker DM, Kyaw MP, Sattabongkot J, Kaewkungwal J
articles.details: Longitudinal study of care-seeking behavior in Myanmar (n=2,960, 2017-2021) and Thailand (n=15,576, 2018-2024) using accelerated failure time (AFT) analysis. Mean fever duration at diagnosis: Myanmar 1.8 days (SD 1.3), Thailand 2.9 days (SD 1.8). In Myanmar, delayed care-seeking associated with Shan ethnicity (TR 1.48, 95% CI 1.41-1.55), P. vivax infection (TR 1.23), uncertain malaria history (TR 1.26). In Thailand, farmers (TR 1.07), P. falciparum (TR 1.27) and P. vivax (TR 1.20) infections, lower education were associated with delays. Daily bed net use and prior malaria experience associated with prompter care-seeking in both settings.

articles.headline: Voltage-switching mass spectrometry improves malaria detection
articles.title_original: Voltage Switching on a Dual Nano-Electrospray/Atmospheric Pressure Chemical Ionization Platform Provides Higher Discrimination Power for Malaria Detection via Direct Infusion Mass Spectrometry.
articles.authors: Ferguson R, Kulyk DS, Badu-Tawiah AK
articles.details: A dual nanoelectrospray/APCI mass spectrometry platform with voltage switching was evaluated on 18 malaria-infected and uninfected blood samples. The voltage-switching approach achieved 100% correct classification of all samples, while constant-voltage nESI and APCI modes misclassified 2 and 4 samples respectively. The composite spectra enabled higher discrimination power for malaria detection.

articles.headline: Selective P. falciparum proteasome inhibitors discovered via cryo-EM
articles.title_original: A recombinant expression system for the Plasmodium falciparum proteasome enables structural analysis of its assembly and the design of selective inhibitors.
articles.authors: Fajtova P, Zhang H, Urich L, da Silva EB, da Silva CH, Almaliti J, Al-Hindy M, Boura E, Kirkman LA, Lin G, Bogyo M, Fidock DA, Gerwick WH, Zhao J, O'Donoghue AJ
articles.details: A recombinant Pf20S proteasome platform was developed for drug discovery. Clinical proteasome inhibitors (bortezomib, carfilzomib, marizomib) lacked selectivity. Novel compounds showed improved selectivity: J-80 inhibited Pf20S β5 with IC50 of 22.4 nM and 90-fold selectivity over human β5; TDI-8304 and analogs confirmed potency and selectivity. Cryo-EM analysis elucidated the structural basis for selective binding.

articles.headline: BinaxNOW malaria RDT achieves 100% sensitivity and specificity
articles.title_original: BinaxNOW vs malaria: A diagnostic showdown: Design and implementation of a verification strategy across 4 academic hospitals in an area with low malaria prevalence.
articles.authors: Turbett SE, Bielawski D, Dighe AS, Kaseta N, Lewandrowski K, Mercer L, Patten C, Sta Cruz ML, Shenoy ES, Branda JA
articles.details: The BinaxNOW Malaria rapid diagnostic test was evaluated on 294 blood specimens and verified using archived well-characterized samples at four sites. For P. falciparum-positive specimens, sensitivity and specificity were 100%, with 96% achieving species-level identification. Site-based verification confirmed 100% positive and negative percent agreement, concordance, and reproducibility across all locations.

articles.headline: Anopheles maculatus wing geometry varies seasonally in Thailand
articles.title_original: Seasonal variation in wing geometry of the malaria vector Anopheles maculatus (Diptera: Culicidae) in Western Thailand.
articles.authors: Sumruayphol S, Chaiphongpachara T, Laojun S
articles.details: Geometric morphometrics analyzed wing structure and contour of A. maculatus from malaria hotspots in western Thailand across three seasons (hot, wet, dry). Significant differences in wing structure size were detected between hot and dry seasons (p<0.05), and in shape between dry/hot and dry/wet populations (p<0.05). Wing contour shape differed significantly only between dry and wet seasons (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Pfcoronin mutations reduce hemoglobin uptake and artemisinin activation
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of Pf Coronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Ullah I, Farringer MA, Khushu M, Burkhard AY, Hathaway E, Willett BC, Shin SH, Sharma AI, Martin MC, Harrigan O, Shao KL, Dvorin JD, Hartl DL, Volkman SK, Bopp S, Absalon S, Wirth DF
articles.details: Pfcoronin mutations identified in artemisinin-resistant African P. falciparum strains were shown to disrupt drug efficacy during the ring stage. Resistance-associated mutations impair Pfcoronin's interaction with Pf Actin and its ring-stage localization, reducing endocytosis and hemoglobin acquisition. Pfcoronin and Pfkelch13 mutations function in distinct cellular regions but converge to limit heme availability and artemisinin activation.

articles.headline: Fever amplifies P. falciparum cytoadhesion via glycocalyx disruption
articles.title_original: Febrile temperature enhances Plasmodium falciparum cytoadhesion by disrupting the endothelial glycocalyx.
articles.authors: Introini V, Long R, Oyerinde OR, Sender SS, Stein F, Hwang GM, Gutierrez BL, Seydel KB, Birbeck G, Bernabeu M
articles.details: A 3D fever-on-a-chip model demonstrated that febrile temperatures (40°C) rapidly enhance infected red blood cell and immune cell binding in human brain and lung microvessels. Fever-induced endothelial glycocalyx shedding exposes receptors EPCR and ICAM-1, increasing cytoadhesion. Broad-spectrum MMP inhibitors prevented the temperature-induced rise in cytoadhesion, identifying fever as a host-specific amplifier of cerebral malaria vascular pathology.

articles.headline: CIS43 junctional vaccine shows promise alone and with R21
articles.title_original: Protective immunity against malaria by a nanoparticle CIS43-based junctional vaccine alone or in combination with R21.
articles.authors: Tripathi P, Koo JH, Chen X, Da Silva Pereira L, Dillon M, Zhang B, Lofgren M, Nguyen KT, Teng IT, Bonilla B, Kerscher S, Kong WP, Ransier A, Stephens T, Tsybovsky Y, Weldon SR, Douek DC, Pierson TC, Batista FD, Idris AH, Seder RA, Kwong PD, Zhou T
articles.details: A nanoparticle-based vaccine displaying PfCSP junctional epitope was compared to the benchmark R21 vaccine and their combination. At 1 μg dose, both junctional and R21 vaccines achieved comparable liver burden reduction. At 0.25 μg, junctional vaccine showed modest reduction versus R21. Combining junctional and R21 induced modestly enhanced protection compared to either vaccine alone.

articles.headline: Strong COVID-19 vaccination intent predicts malaria vaccine acceptance
articles.title_original: Attitudes to malaria vaccination in children: what can be learned from their consistency with the temporal dynamics of COVID-19 vaccination intention? A prospective cohort study in rural Senegal (July 2020-November 2023).
articles.authors: Seror V, Cortaredona S, Ba EH, Maradan G, Sokhna C
articles.details: A six-wave telephone survey of 558 Senegalese households (2020-2023) found that 65.28% expressed very strong positive attitudes toward malaria vaccination for children. Very strong attitudes were associated with higher COVID-19 vaccine uptake (p<0.001). Households where both members maintained continuously strong COVID-19 vaccination intention were more likely to support malaria vaccination (p=0.001).

articles.headline: Malaria resurgence in Africa characterized as avoidable crisis
articles.title_original: The resurgence of malaria in Africa is an avoidable crisis-here's what we must do.
articles.authors: Kaseya J, Dereje N
articles.details: This commentary discusses the resurgence of malaria in Africa, arguing that the crisis is avoidable with appropriate interventions. Specific findings and quantitative data are not available from the title alone.

articles.headline: High genetic diversity of P. falciparum in Côte d'Ivoire
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum in Korhogo Health District, Northern Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.authors: Benié EMA, Beuret C, Schnoz A, Cantoreggi SL, Ding XC, Silué KD, Nsanzabana C
articles.details: P. falciparum genetic diversity was assessed using msp1, msp2, and glurp markers in 179 samples from dried blood spots collected in Korhogo district, Northern Côte d'Ivoire (2019-2020). msp1 alleles: K1 (20 genotypes), MAD20 (23), RO33 (1); msp2: 3D7 (59 genotypes), FC27 (33); glurp (45 genotypes). Expected heterozygosity ≥0.9 for all markers indicates high population genetic diversity.

articles.headline: Malaria-driven dyserythropoiesis: mechanistic synthesis of four pathways
articles.title_original: Malaria and dyserythropoiesis: a mini review.
articles.authors: Liu FF, Li K
articles.details: This mini-review synthesizes four converging pathways driving malaria-associated anemia: (1) cytokine storm suppressing erythropoietin and diverting progenitors via GATA-1 destabilization; (2) hemozoin-catalyzed oxidative stress and erythroblast apoptosis; (3) parasite infection of orthochromatic erythroblasts prolonging gametocyte maturation; (4) hemozoin-laden macrophages remodeling erythroblastic islands and causing iron restriction. The review highlights emerging single-cell and spatial-omics technologies for studying bone-marrow pathology.

articles.headline: P. falciparum prevalence low among HBV-coinfected persons in Ghana
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Helicobacter pylori, Salmonella typhi, Plasmodium falciparum, and Toxoplasma gondii infections and levels of liver function markers among Hepatitis B Virus infected Ghanaians: A cross-sectional study in the Greater Accra Region.
articles.authors: Nyarko EN, Amakye EK, Ofori EK, Appiah M, Anim M, Lartey NL, Ametepe S, Adu EA, Kumi J, Dodoo DND, Adom M, Owiredu E, Kwafo E, Obirikorang C
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 120 HBsAg-positive persons in Greater Accra Region, Ghana screened for coinfections with H. pylori, S. typhi, P. falciparum, and T. gondii. P. falciparum prevalence was 2.5% (95% CI: 0.8-7.1), while H. pylori (40.8%) and T. gondii (19.2%) were more common. HBV-H. pylori coinfected persons had significantly elevated ALT (33.5 vs 23.5 IU, p<0.001) compared to HBV-only.

articles.headline: Web tool automates virtual screening for P. falciparum enzyme targets
articles.title_original: PlasmoDocking: A User-Friendly Open-Source Web Tool for Virtual Screening Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Enzymes.
articles.authors: Guariero FL, Macedo EP, Laia EB, Evaristo JAM, Evaristo GPC, Zanchi FB
articles.details: PlasmoDocking is a web-based platform automating molecular docking simulations against 38 predefined Plasmodium falciparum targets. Users submit up to 10 molecular structures for screening against pre-configured parasite targets with binding energy comparison to co-crystallized ligands. Developed in Python and Next.js, the platform facilitates multi-target drug discovery simulations in a single framework with open-source code availability.

articles.headline: Self-medication with antimalarials affects 55% in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Issues associated with malaria self-medication in sub-Saharan Africa: A systematic literature review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Amaka JI, MacLeod E, Picozzi K, Fyfe J, Ezenyi IC, Ijaiya IS, Attah DD, Godwin BA, Obisike VU, Galamaji MM
articles.details: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 27 studies published through April 2023 found pooled prevalence of 55.3% for malaria self-medication in sub-Saharan Africa. Drivers included low-income level, cheap non-prescription drug availability, large family size, lack of health insurance, and difficulty accessing formal healthcare. Authors concluded that health authorities should incorporate informal drug market stakeholders into frameworks promoting proper antimalarial use.

articles.headline: Spatial repellents reduce malaria infection risk by 52%
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Spatial Repellents in Malaria Prevention: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.authors: Prager M, Bergmann F, Pracher L, Zeitlinger M, Jorda A
articles.details: Meta-analysis of four randomized controlled trials (6,745 participants from China, Indonesia, and Kenya) found spatial repellents reduced malaria infection risk by 52% (pooled risk ratio 0.48; 95% CI: 0.33–0.70; P<0.001). Malaria infection occurred in 20.0% of spatial repellent users vs 31.0% of controls. No significant effects on mosquito density or sporozoite rates were observed; high heterogeneity limited certainty of pooled estimates.

articles.headline: Burkina Faso halts transgenic mosquito malaria control program
articles.title_original: Burkina Faso halts plan to fight malaria with transgenic mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Kupferschmidt K
articles.details: Brief report noting that misinformation may have eroded public trust and contributed to suspension of a transgenic mosquito research initiative, resulting in a raid on a research facility. No quantitative data or study outcomes are provided in the abstract.

articles.headline: Correction: aspartic protease inhibitors for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Correction: Computational identification of aspartic protease inhibitors for antimalarial drug development against Plasmodium Vivax.
articles.authors: Alruwaili M, Alhassan HH, Almutary H, Tahir Ul Qamar M
articles.details: This is a correction notice with no additional substantive information provided beyond the title.

articles.headline: In-silico multi-epitope FREP1 vaccine targeting Anopheles stephensi
articles.title_original: Designing a multi-epitope vaccine against the midgut-specific fibrinogen-related protein 1(FREP1) of Anopheles stephensi to enhance protection against the malaria parasite: a step beyond traditional vaccine development approaches.
articles.authors: Yadav M, Dahiya N, Singh H, Kataria D, Janjoter S, Sehrawat N
articles.details: Immunoinformatic design of a multi-epitope vaccine targeting fibrinogen-related protein 1 (FREP1) of Anopheles stephensi mosquito midgut to block parasite transmission. In-silico analyses assessed allergenicity, antigenicity, toxicity, immunogenicity, and population coverage. Molecular docking showed efficient stable complex formation with TLR4 receptor; molecular dynamics simulation and in-silico immunization profiling indicated strong free binding energy and high immune response potential. Codon-optimized constructs demonstrated efficient protein expression in E. coli.

articles.headline: Pyronaridine/artesunate combinations show additive antimalarial killing
articles.title_original: Fractional curative killing of pyronaridine or artesunate combinations with tafenoquine, 4-aminoquinolines, or azithromycin in a murine malaria-luciferase model.
articles.authors: Lee G, Aneke JS, Sullivan DJ Jr
articles.details: In a Plasmodium berghei ANKA-luciferase mouse blood-stage model with high parasitemia, daily artesunate or single-dose pyronaridine were tested against three single-dose quinolines (chloroquine, amodiaquine, tafenoquine) and azithromycin. Tafenoquine/artesunate and pyronaridine/amodiaquine had fractional combination curative doses of 0.83 and 0.93 respectively; other combinations ranged 0.9–1.1, in the additive interaction range. All tested combinations demonstrated additive rather than synergistic antimalarial activity.

articles.headline: Chloroquinoline-azithromycin analogs enhance antimalarial quick-kill potency
articles.title_original: Characterizing the quick-killing mechanism of action of azithromycin analogs against malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Mao EY, Nguyen W, Jana GP, Maity BC, Pazicky S, Giannangelo C, Reader J, Famodimu MT, Birkholtz L-M, Delves MJ, Creek DJ, Bozdech Z, Laleu B, Burrows JN, Sleebs BE, Gancheva MR, Wilson DW
articles.details: Five azithromycin analogs were tested for quick-killing mechanism against malaria parasites. Non-chloroquinoline analogs showed 10–42 fold improvement over azithromycin; chloroquinoline-containing analogs showed additional 2–17 fold improvement. Chemical studies linked both classes to chloroquine-like mechanisms via heme-polymerization inhibition and hemoglobin digestion. Thermal shift proteomics revealed stabilization of 34–155 different proteins; the most potent chloroquinoline analog demonstrated improved gametocytocidal activity and moderate inhibition of chloroquine-insensitive gametocytes.

articles.headline: P. ovale dominates asymptomatic Plasmodium infections in South Africa
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium species in asymptomatic individuals in North-Eastern South Africa: 2018 - 2019.
articles.authors: Munzhedzi M, Guler JL, Krivacsy S, Shifflett P, Operario DJ, Dillingham R, McQuade ETR, Bessong PO
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 985 asymptomatic individuals in Ha-Lambani, Vhembe, South Africa (Nov 2018–May 2019) found 7.1% Plasmodium prevalence (95% CI: 5.4–8.7%). Species distribution: P. ovale 57.1% (n=40), P. falciparum 37.1% (n=26), P. malariae 1.4%, P. vivax 1.4%, and 2.9% mixed P. falciparum/P. ovale infections. Pfk13 sequencing of 30 P. falciparum samples revealed 57 SNPs (≥20% minority threshold); 70.1% were non-synonymous, and five novel propeller-domain SNPs were detected, none previously associated with artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: Armed conflict in DRC increases malaria outbreak risk in children
articles.title_original: The impact of armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on the risk of malaria outbreaks in children under five years old.
articles.authors: Yokolo H, Tague C, Ekouo J, Makeda D, Akilimali A
articles.details: No abstract provided. Based on title, the study examines the relationship between armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo and the risk of malaria outbreaks in children under five years of age.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine with G6PD screening costs US$2,894 per DALY averted
articles.title_original: The cost-effectiveness of tafenoquine following screening with STANDARD™ G6PD screening for the treatment of vivax malaria in the Brazilian Public Health System.
articles.authors: Peixoto HM, Bastos Gottin LL, Brito-Sousa JD, Sampaio V, Daumerie PG, Jambert E, Monteiro W, Lacerda MVG, Devine A
articles.details: Decision tree cost-effectiveness model in Brazil compared three vivax malaria radical cure strategies over 12 months: tafenoquine (single-dose after G6PD screening ≥70% activity), primaquine without G6PD screening (7-day, 0.5 mg/kg/day), and primaquine with G6PD screening (≥30% activity). Tafenoquine cost US$2,894 per DALY averted vs current practice (below Brazil's US$7,752 willingness-to-pay threshold) and dominated the primaquine-screening strategy, with >98% likelihood of cost-effectiveness.

articles.headline: Preterm birth increases malaria susceptibility in multigravid offspring
articles.title_original: Preterm Birth and Malaria Susceptibility in Offspring of Uninfected Multigravid Women.
articles.authors: Barry A, Dang L, Sidibe Y, Issiaka D, Gaoussou S, Hu Z, Dicko Y, Mahamar A, Attaher O, Diarra BS, Keita S, Dicko A, Duffy PE, Fried M
articles.details: Longitudinal cohort study of 1,679 mother-child pairs in Mali (mean follow-up 25.8 months, up to 5 years) examined pregnancy malaria and preterm delivery effects on child malaria susceptibility. Preterm vs full-term birth was associated with increased hazard of first malaria infection in offspring of multigravid women (HR 1.76; 95% CI 1.05–2.95; P=.03). Parasitemia incidence in preterm vs full-term offspring of uninfected multigravida women showed IRR of 2.74 (95% CI 1.80–4.18). Pregnancy malaria was associated with increased infection hazard in children of all gravidity strata.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic Plasmodium infections in migrants show abnormal labs
articles.title_original: Clinical Findings in Migrants With Asymptomatic Plasmodium Infections.
articles.authors: Eliasson I, Wyss K, Tafesse Bogale R, Forsblom S, Lindquist E, Rönnberg C, Hansson M, Beshara S, Nordling I, Hertting O, Wångdahl A, Färnert A
articles.details: Retrospective study comparing 65 PCR-positive Plasmodium-infected to 54 PCR-negative migrants in Stockholm. Plasmodium-positive individuals had higher anemia (21.1% vs 6.1%, P=0.048), elevated ESR (58.1% vs 25.0%, P=0.008), elevated IgM (30.5% vs 10.5%, P=0.030), and splenomegaly (25.4% vs 2.5%, P=0.002). After antimalarial treatment, parameters improved.

articles.headline: Gold mining creates breeding sites for malaria vectors in Colombia
articles.title_original: Anopheles species associated with malaria transmission in gold-mining areas of northwest Colombia.
articles.authors: Rojas MA, Ahumada ML, Velasco-Pareja MC, Chaparro P, Yasnot-Acosta MF, Orjuela LI
articles.details: Cross-sectional entomological study in five El Bagre localities identified 11 Anopheles species with six PCR-positive specimens: An. darlingi (Plasmodium falciparum), and An. nuneztovari s.s. and An. punctimacula (P. vivax). Entomological inoculation rate ranged 1.5–6.6 infective bites/person/month. Gold mining excavations provided breeding habitats for these vectors. Larval habitat parameters showed no significant association with species abundance.

articles.headline: MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry identifies secondary malaria vectors accurately
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for identification of Afro-tropical secondary malaria vectors.
articles.authors: Tuwei M, Karisa J, Wanjiku C, Kiuru C, Ondieki Z, Odongo T, Mure F, Otieno B, Meli P, Okoko M, Bartilol B, Gona R, Constantino L, Cole G, Anastácio T, Armazia R, Alves C, Rui P, Ramaita E, Rono M, Candrinho B, Mwangangi J, Mbogo C, Charlwood D, Saute F, Rabinovich R, Chaccour C, Maia MF
articles.details: Validation study of MALDI-TOF MS for secondary malaria vector identification in Kenya and Mozambique, confirmed by Sanger sequencing of ITS2 and COI genes. Method achieved 91% overall accuracy with kappa=0.87, and 100% sensitivity/specificity for most species except An. cf. coustani 2 NFL-2015 and An. ziemanni. Six Anopheles species identified across study sites.

articles.headline: Low immunization coverage limits perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery
articles.title_original: Assessing immunization coverage in Southern togo: implications for perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery.
articles.authors: Arikawa S, Tchankoni MK, Gbeasor-Komlanvi F, Atekpe SP, Atcha-Oubou T, Figueroa-Romero A, Orne-Gliemann J, Menendez C, Gonzalez R, Briand V, Ekouevi D
articles.details: Analysis of 583 children aged 12–23 months in Togo in 2022 found 60.7% fully immunized and 15.8% zero-dose. Zero-dose status associated with low caregiver education (aOR 5.02, 95% CI 1.85–13.60), negative vaccine safety attitudes (aOR 8.60, 95% CI 3.32–22.30), and lack of modern healthcare reliance (aOR 3.06, 95% CI 1.26–7.48). Health facility access showed no significant association.

articles.headline: Designed multistage vaccine combines CSP and Pfs48/45 antigens
articles.title_original: A combined designed CSP and Pfs48/45 infection and transmission blocking vaccine for malaria.
articles.authors: Gupta R, Dickey TH, Salinas ND, Patel PN, Ma R, Shi D, Singleton M, Ouahes T, Pham TP, Miura K, Long CA, Lambert LE, Tolia NH
articles.details: Preclinical development of self-assembled ferritin nanoparticles presenting CSPj5c and 17-4 immunogens from circumsporozoite (CSP, pre-erythrocytic) and Pfs48/45 (transmission-blocking) antigens. Mouse vaccination conferred protection against Plasmodium falciparum transgenic sporozoite challenge. Purified IgG from immunized rabbits showed potent transmission-reducing activity. Addition of engineered 17-4 improved immune responses to CSPj5c.

articles.headline: Herbal extract combined with artesunate treats experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Antimalarial and neuroprotective effects of ethanolic extracts of the five-flower remedy in an experimental cerebral malaria model.
articles.authors: Plirat W, Chaniad P, Phuwajaroanpong A, Konyanee A, Lalert L, Septama AW, Punsawad C
articles.details: Experimental cerebral malaria study in C57BL/6 mice infected with Plasmodium berghei ANKA. Ethanolic five-flower remedy extract (600 mg/kg) combined with artesunate for 7 days significantly reduced parasitemia (80%), increased survival, reduced neurological deficits, and decreased brain inflammation. Histology showed reduced hemorrhage, leukocyte infiltration, and neuronal apoptosis.

articles.headline: Cyclic nucleotide signaling in ookinetes enables mosquito infection
articles.title_original: A divergent cyclic nucleotide binding protein promotes Plasmodium ookinete infection of the mosquito.
articles.authors: Kwecka D, Wang Z, Eigminas E, Liu L, Regan JC, Kim C, Philip N
articles.details: Mechanistic study identifying cryptic cyclic nucleotide-binding protein (CBP-O) localized to the ookinete apex. PbCBP-O disruption severely compromised ookinete invasion of mosquitoes. Domain analysis revealed distinct N- and C-terminal functions. Protein exhibits dual binding specificity to cGMP and cAMP, suggesting the apical tip is a signaling platform essential for parasite transmission.

articles.headline: Flavin-deficient red blood cells inhibit Plasmodium falciparum growth
articles.title_original: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes offer protection against malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Hemasa A, van Loon W, Fu J, Spry C, Jäger J, van Schalkwyk DA, Reverberi R, Ndoli J, Mockenhaupt FP, Contini C, Saliba KJ
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Screening identified flavin-deficient erythrocytes (FDE) in 23% of 150 individuals in Ferrara, Italy and 13% of 169 in Huye, Rwanda. In vitro studies showed FDE and riboflavin-depleted erythrocytes inhibited P. falciparum intraerythrocytic proliferation. Oxidative stress in FDE may explain parasitic inhibition. Genetic analysis identified FAD synthase gene mutations in three Rwandan individuals with FDE.

articles.headline: Engineered antibodies with enhanced Fc regions protect against malaria
articles.title_original: Analysis of Fc-dependent effector functions of anti-malaria circumsporozoite protein antibodies.
articles.authors: Stefanutti E, Ramani R, Whitener B, Dang H, Bélanger S, Somasundaram L, Cortina K, De Marco A, Tam T, Chai Q, Cameroni E, Gupta R, Schmid MA, Miller JL, Zumsteg AB, Purcell LA, Drewry LL
articles.details: Study analyzing Fc-dependent immune effector functions of circumsporozoite protein (CSP) monoclonal antibodies 317, L9, and CIS43. MAbs L9 and CIS43 drove stronger antibody-dependent phagocytosis, NK activation, and complement deposition, while 317 enhanced CSP-responsive T-cell activation. Fc engineering modulated activity of multiple CSP mAbs. Results suggest Fc-engineering potential for enhanced anti-CSP antibody potency.

articles.headline: Plant-derived compound shows repellent activity against malaria vectors
articles.title_original: COMPARATIVE BEHAVIORAL RESPONSES OF Β-CARYOPHYLLENE AGAINST ANOPHELES MOSQUITO SPECIES, POTENTIAL VECTORS OF MALARIA IN THAILAND.
articles.authors: Lhaosudto S, Sathantriphop S, Kongmee M, Karpkird T, Leepasert T, Ngoen-Klan R, Chareonviriyaphap T
articles.details: Excito-repellency assay tested β-caryophyllene against four Anopheles species. An. minimus exhibited highest escape rates (100% at 2.5% contact, 5% noncontact). An. harrisoni showed strong responses (94.34% contact, 72.73% noncontact at 7.5%). An. epiroticus showed minimal response (12–25.42%). Kaplan-Meier analysis revealed dose-dependent repellency effects and faster escape in contact conditions. No knockdown or mortality observed.

articles.headline: Mosquito breeding sites and ITN use linked to child malaria
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Associated Factors among Under Five Febrile Children Attending Ener Amanuel Health Center, Southern Ethiopia: An Institution-based Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Tseha ST, Ebabu M, Aberar T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 218 febrile children under five in Ethiopia (December 2022–April 2023) found 21.1% malaria prevalence with Plasmodium falciparum predominant (60.9%). Factors significantly associated with infection: proximity to mosquito breeding sites (P=0.025), not sleeping under ITNs (P=0.021), and unsuitable beds for ITN hanging (P=0.002). Health facility access showed no significant association.

articles.headline: Stakeholder analysis identifies barriers to malaria elimination scale-up
articles.title_original: Assessing scalability factors of a malaria intervention using Malakit and target drug administration among hard-to-reach populations: An original approach focusing on key stakeholders - implementers and decision-makers - in the Guiana shield.
articles.authors: Carboni C, Sanna A, Lambert Y, Plessis L, Bardon T, Adenis A, Nacher M, Vreden S, Suárez-Mutis M, Douine M
articles.details: Pre-planning assessment (June 2023–March 2024) in Guiana Shield analyzing scalability of Malakit (self-testing/self-treatment) and targeted P. vivax drug administration across Brazil, French Guiana, and Suriname. Qualitative analysis using ExpandNet/WHO Framework identified key stakeholders and conducted SWOT analysis. Strengths included Malakit effectiveness; weaknesses included data gaps and regulatory challenges. Funding and regulatory barriers posed threats to scale-up.

articles.headline: Malaria control in hard-to-reach populations: challenges and solutions
articles.title_original: Malaria in hard-to-reach populations: Challenges and solutions.
articles.authors: Fouque F, Ravasi D, Masoudi GA, Rodriguez-Morales AJ
articles.details: Article discusses challenges and potential solutions for controlling malaria in hard-to-reach populations, addressing barriers to access and intervention effectiveness.

articles.headline: Malaria transmission diversity and human mobility complicate elimination
articles.title_original: Biodiversity of malaria ecosystems, challenges for control: When human diversity is forgotten.
articles.authors: Fouque F, Boutsika K
articles.details: Review of how malaria elimination success in temperate regions 150 years ago did not translate globally. Recent decades show declining incidence and mortality, but diverse transmission contexts and increased human population mobility limit further progress. Access to healthcare is now the primary driver of malaria control.

articles.headline: Targeted interventions achieved P. falciparum elimination in Suriname
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Suriname: how targeted interventions in marginalized populations led to elimination.
articles.authors: Hiwat-Van Laar H, Cairo H, Hardjopawiro L, Eersel M, Vreden S, Adhin M, Panchoe D, van Eer E, Jintie H, van Dijk M, Mesones Lapouble O
articles.details: Analysis of national malaria surveillance data 2000-2024 tracked P. falciparum elimination in Suriname. Pf was the primary species until 2007, subsequently shifted from endemic disease in indigenous/tribal interior communities to occupational disease in migrant gold miners, with last indigenous case in 2018. Strategy focused on low-barrier healthcare access for all at-risk populations.

articles.headline: Village health workers in malaria control: systematic review of roles
articles.title_original: Role of village health worker in control activities for malaria elimination efforts: A systematic review.
articles.authors: Wangdi K, Wangchuk D, Alamnia T, Cao P, Kotepui M, Suwannatrai AT, Ugyel L, Vardoulakis S, Tsheten T
articles.details: Systematic review of 44 studies (from 14,884 screened) on village health workers in malaria control across hard-to-reach areas, primarily in Africa (65.9%). VHWs were most commonly involved in diagnosis and treatment (21/44), with fewer engaged in prevention interventions (4/44) or chemoprevention (2/44). Authors recommend integrating VHWs into national malaria elimination programs.

articles.headline: Vernonia amygdalina exhibits anti-plasmodial activity against P. knowlesi
articles.title_original: Vernonia amygdalina (Delile) exhibits in vitro anti-plasmodial activities against Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Zulkefli NZ, Cheong FW, Amir A, Lau YL
articles.details: In vitro schizont maturation inhibition assay tested three crude extracts of Vernonia amygdalina against P. knowlesi. Methanol extract showed strongest activity (IC50 1.131 ± 0.16 μg/mL) with high selectivity index >10 and low mammalian cell cytotoxicity (CC50 63.73 ± 0.78 µg/mL). All extracts demonstrated potent inhibition (IC50 values 1.1-2.0 μg/mL), suggesting potential for novel antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: pgSIT facility scaling optimizes cost-effectiveness for Anopheles control
articles.title_original: Estimated cost and operational structure of pgSIT malaria vector control programs in selected West African countries.
articles.authors: Gendron WAC, Raban R, Mondal A, Sánchez C HM, Zilberman D, Ilboudo PGC, D'Alessandro U, Marshall JM, Akbari OS
articles.details: Study modeled optimal facility size for precision guided sterile insect technique (pgSIT) against Anopheles gambiae in West Africa. Previous estimates used 15-31% capacity for sex sorting. Authors calculated cost per million mosquitoes suppressed and determined optimized facility size of 9.2 million mosquitoes, increasing intervention cost-effectiveness for vector control with potential to interrupt transmission.

articles.headline: Severe falciparum malaria causes age-dependent electrolyte abnormalities
articles.title_original: Exploring the Effect of Malaria on Biochemical Parameters Among Sudanese Patients with Complicated Falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Ali MO, Tamomh AG, Abdulrahman NA, Ibrahim NS, Eltayeb MA, Osman FI
articles.details: Case-control study of 200 severe falciparum malaria patients versus 200 controls in Sudan (August 2022–January 2023). Infected children showed significantly decreased plasma potassium and sodium (p<0.05) but unchanged urea/creatinine. Infected adults showed significantly elevated urea (p<0.05) but unchanged electrolytes. Positive correlation between urea and sodium in infected children (r=0.322, p<0.001). Early detection of electrolyte abnormalities may help prevent severe malaria complications.

articles.headline: Fatal P. falciparum malaria complicated by acute subdural hematoma
articles.title_original: A fatal case report of Plasmodium falciparum malaria complicated by acute subdural hematoma.
articles.authors: Xie N, Ye H, Jiang X
articles.details: Case report of a 90 kg male traveler to Burkina Faso who developed P. falciparum malaria with acute subdural hematoma. Fever, headache, and myalgia began 3 days post-return; diagnosis was delayed 9 days. Despite artesunate treatment, patient died from multiple organ failure. Case illustrates severe hemorrhagic complications of falciparum malaria and importance of rapid diagnosis.

articles.headline: Stachytarpheta jamaicensis extract clears parasites and modulates immunity
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial and immunomodulatory activities of dichloromethane extract of Stachytarpheta jamaicensis in Plasmodium berghei infected mice.
articles.authors: Amaechi EC, Owolodun OA, Lambe KO
articles.details: In vivo study in chloroquine-resistant P. berghei-infected mice tested dichloromethane extract of Stachytarpheta jamaicensis at 200, 400, and 600 mg/kg doses. At 200 mg/kg, extract achieved significant parasite clearance by Day 11 (p<0.05) and improved survival throughout 30-day monitoring period, associated with sustained TGF-β elevation and balanced pro/anti-inflammatory responses. Phytochemical screening revealed alkaloids only.

articles.headline: Crateva adansonii extracts show strong larvicidal activity against A. gambiae
articles.title_original: Bioactive profiling, larvicidal and pupicidal activities of Crateva adansonii against two developmental stages of malaria vector, Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Ileke KD, Babatunde NA, Akeju AV
articles.details: Study tested ethanol extracts of Crateva adansonii leaf, stem, and root against Anopheles gambiae larvae and pupae at 1–20% concentrations. Root extract achieved lowest LC50 (0.04%) and LC90 (8.07%) at 24 hours with 100% larval mortality at 15–20% concentration. Leaf and bark extracts achieved complete mortality at 20% in 6 hours. GC-MS identified bioactive compounds supporting plant-based vector control development.

articles.headline: Host immune response to P. falciparum: innate and adaptive mechanisms
articles.title_original: Overview of the host immune response to P. falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Antwi-Baffour S, Mensah BT, Annison L
articles.details: Review of immunological defense against P. falciparum infection, encompassing innate immunity (macrophages, dendritic cells, NK cells, inflammatory cytokines) and adaptive immunity (T cell killing and B cell antibody production targeting parasite surface antigens). Antibodies neutralize parasites and facilitate phagocytic clearance. Protective immunity development is influenced by parasite diversity, host genetics, and prior exposure, with variable individual outcomes.

articles.headline: High asymptomatic malaria carriage in SMC-covered households in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Patterns of patent and sub-patent Plasmodium falciparum infections in household members of children under seasonal malaria chemoprevention coverage in the health district of Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Kambou SAE, Sondo P, Kabore B, Bayala IMC, Kouevi AFC, Compaore EW, Millogo KS, Ismaïla B, Rouamba T, Kazienga A, Karim D, Rouamba E, Dahal P, Otienoburu SD, Raman J, Dhorda M, Bamba S, Guérin PJ, Tinto H
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 650 household members (745 enrolled) living with SMC-eligible children in Nanoro, Burkina Faso during 2022 SMC campaign using varATS qPCR. Found 68.6% P. falciparum infections overall: 27.4% patent and 41.2% sub-patent (qPCR-positive/RDT-negative). Patent infections declined with age (37.7% in 5–14 years to 17.1% in ≥25 years); sub-patent predominated in older ages (49.2% in 15–24 years). Supports extending SMC to school-aged children and household member testing.

articles.headline: Indonesia's progress toward malaria elimination by 2030
articles.title_original: Shrinking the malaria map in Indonesia: progress of subnational control, elimination, and future strategies.
articles.authors: Herdiana H, Prameswari HD, Puspadewi RT, Fajariyani SB, Diptyanusa A, Theodora M, Supriyanto D, Hawley WA
articles.details: Subnational elimination strategy review in Indonesia. As of 2023, 389 of 514 districts and 5 of 38 provinces verified malaria-free. Papua region accounts for >90% of cases with incidence >400 per 1000 person-years. Zoonotic P. knowlesi infections increasing in some regions. Study emphasizes need for new intervention combinations to achieve 2030 elimination goal.

articles.headline: Care-seeking and malaria prevalence among Myanmar migrants in Thailand
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking behaviours and infection prevalence among short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand.
articles.authors: Aung PL, Sripoorote P, Inthitanon N, Wattanagoon Y, Hamers RL, Van Nuil JI, Cui L, Nguitragool W, Jones L, Sudewo AG, Sattabongkot J, Parker DM
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 300 short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand (mean age 34.5 years, 47.3% male). Although 60% recognized need to seek care for fever, only 35% did so within 24 hours. Care-seeking scores higher in daily wage laborers (β=0.66), prior malaria experience (β=1.08), higher knowledge (β=0.34); Karen ethnicity negatively associated (β=-0.95). Six asymptomatic P. vivax detected. Lower malaria knowledge (OR=13.5) and care-seeking scores (OR=5.86) significantly associated with infection.

articles.headline: Health education and LLIN implementation in rural Brazil
articles.title_original: Contributions of health education and social mobilization in the implementation of a malaria control programme using long-lasting insecticidal nets.
articles.authors: da Costa FM, de Souza Rodrigues MM, Scarpassa VM
articles.details: Implementation of health education and social mobilization strategies (HESMS) with 8,083 LLIN distribution in 10 communities in Porto Velho, Brazil (2010-2012). LLIN acceptance rate 99.93%. Pre-intervention monthly median malaria cases: 24 in Abunã (IQR 5-61) and 7 in Imbaúba-Jirau (IQR 1-34). 92.13% had sufficient LLINs; 57.41% reported consistent use on most nights. HESMS did not reduce early care-seeking (<24h after symptom onset).

articles.headline: Avian malaria parasite transcriptional plasticity across hosts
articles.title_original: Generalist Malaria Parasites and Host Imprinting: Unveiling Transcriptional Memory.
articles.authors: García-Longoria L, Palinauskas V, Aželytė J, Marzal A, Ovelleiro D, Hellgren O
articles.details: Experimental study of P. homocircumflexum gene expression across different bird host species. Gene expression differed significantly by host species and time post-infection. At 8 days post-infection, 2,647 differentially expressed genes associated with donor environment; at 16 dpi, only 271 genes linked to recipient host. SNP analyses showed low genetic differentiation. Data support epigenetic regulation driving shift from donor-dependent to recipient-dependent gene expression.

articles.headline: Congenital P. falciparum transmission from asymptomatic mother
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria and Congenital Acquisition in Infant, Portugal, 2024.
articles.authors: Lopes I, Dias J, Das Neves E, Morais M, Santos-Reis A, Garcia AM, Varandas L, Lopes D
articles.details: Case report of congenital malaria in Portugal. P. falciparum infection diagnosed in 3-month-old infant by optical microscopy. Mother had malaria in Angola 13 months prior, remained asymptomatic throughout pregnancy. Molecular techniques confirmed imported case with congenital transmission.

articles.headline: Response on nosocomial P. falciparum transmission in Spain
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024 (Response).
articles.authors: Romero MFL, de Pipaón MRP, Amuedo MDN, Rubio Muñoz JM, Jiménez-Hoyuela JM, Cisneros JM
articles.details: Response letter regarding nosocomial transmission of P. falciparum malaria in Spain, 2024.

articles.headline: Nosocomial P. falciparum transmission in Spain, 2024
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024.
articles.authors: Gómez Perales JL, Mendoza AG, Amares MTG
articles.details: Report of nosocomial transmission of P. falciparum malaria in Spain, 2024.

articles.headline: Diagnosis and management of imported malaria in children
articles.title_original: Diagnosis and Management of Imported Malaria in the Emergency Department.
articles.authors: McQueen A, Mannheim J
articles.details: Review article summarizing diagnosis and management of children with imported malaria in nonendemic countries. Emphasizes travel history, diagnostic challenges, and management strategies in acute/emergency department settings.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 metabolizer phenotype affects P. vivax recurrence
articles.title_original: CYP2D6 Genotype and Primaquine Treatment in Patients with Malaria, Venezuela.
articles.authors: Pacheco C, Hernández-Acosta A, Pulido N, Ceballos Y, Saavedra D, Gómez C, Moreno N, Herrera F
articles.details: Study of CYP2D6 genotypes in 96 patients with suspected malaria in Venezuela. Intermediate or poor metabolizer phenotypes found in approximately 25% of cases. Among 44 confirmed malaria patients, 9 had P. vivax recurrence. Authors recommend evaluation of increased primaquine doses to account for poor metabolizer phenotypes.

articles.headline: P. vivax microgametes diagnostic challenge in malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax microgametes in a Colombian malaria case: A diagnostic challenge.
articles.authors: Rodríguez Lugo DA, Morcillo Muñoz JA, Medina-Lozano LJ, Faccini-Martínez ÁA
articles.details: Case report of diagnostic challenge involving P. vivax microgametes in Colombian malaria case.

articles.headline: Low-cost eave screening reduces vector exposure in Cameroon
articles.title_original: A low-cost house eave screening technique complements insecticide-treated nets in reducing indoor human exposure to the bites of anopheles mosquitoes in a malaria endemic area of south forested Cameroon: results from a pilot study.
articles.authors: Metitsi Tesongang RD, Elanga-Ndille E, Cheteug Nguetsa G, Awono-Ambene HP, Fomena A, Tabue R, Ndo C
articles.details: Pilot study in forested Cameroon. Baseline malaria prevalence 38.85%, entomological inoculation rate 73 infected bites per person-year. An. gambiae susceptible to pirimiphos-methyl but resistant to bendiocarb and pyrethroids. ITN possession 89.62%, use 79%. Eave screening intervention reduced EIR from 37.70 to 6.90 ib/p/y (5.5-fold reduction), cost-effective at $23.6 per house, well-received by community.

articles.headline: IRS and BCC with LLINs reduce malaria in west Africa
articles.title_original: Efficacy of non-pyrethroid indoor residual spraying or intensive behaviour change communication in combination with long-lasting insecticidal nets for malaria control in west Africa: a pragmatic, cluster-randomised, controlled trial.
articles.authors: Moiroux N, Zongo I, Assi SB, Some A, Tchiekoi NB, Zogo B, Soma DD, Hien DFDS, Taconet P, Ahoua Alou LP, Lefevre T, Cohuet A, Fournet F, Mouline K, Koffi AA, Dabiré RK, Pennetier C
articles.details: Pragmatic cluster-RCT in 39 villages (10,750 inhabitants) in Burkina Faso and Côte d'Ivoire comparing LLIN alone, LLIN plus pirimiphos-methyl IRS, and LLIN plus intensive behavior change communication. Over 10-month follow-up (215,000 person-months, 3,612 malaria cases). LLIN plus IRS showed 23% incidence reduction (RR 0.77, 95% CI 0.64-0.93, p=0.0073) and LLIN plus BCC showed 22% reduction (RR 0.78, 95% CI 0.63-0.96, p=0.020) versus LLIN alone. No IRS adverse effects.

articles.headline: FFI framework assesses surveillance sensitivity for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Evidence-based decision making for malaria elimination applying the Freedom From Infection statistical framework in five malaria eliminating countries: an observational study.
articles.authors: Stresman G, Nelli L, Wu L, Byrne I, Surendra H, Fernandez-Camacho B, Ruiz-Cabrejos J, Arana LB, Gonçalves AALM, Monteiro DDSR, Desir L, Ureña K, Tejada Beato MJ, Dumont E, Hill M, Grignard L, Elechosa S, Bunagan R, Xuan Thang N, Huong Binh NT, Hong Ngoc NT, Tetteh KKA, Noland GS, Hamre KES, da Veiga Leal S, DePina A, Thang N, Espino FE, Carrasco-Escobar G, Matthiopoulos J, Drakeley C
articles.details: Study applied Freedom From Infection (FFI) statistical framework to routine surveillance data from 1515 facilities across five countries for P. falciparum and P. vivax, incorporating data from 12 community health workers and 10,767 individuals from cross-sectional surveys. Only 55% of facilities (841/1515 for P. falciparum, 771/1455 for P. vivax) achieved sufficient surveillance sensitivity to achieve high probability of malaria freedom. Strong surveillance systems were characterized by access to testing/treatment supplies, training within previous 12 months, and shorter travel times to facilities. Authors conclude that combining routine surveillance with community case management and active case detection improves confidence in elimination readiness.

articles.headline: Behaviour change communication potential for malaria control
articles.title_original: Can behaviour change communication improve malaria control?
articles.authors: Sherrard-Smith E, Ngufor C
articles.details: Title indicates exploration of whether behavioral interventions can improve malaria control effectiveness, though specific study details are not available in abstract.

articles.headline: Robust surveillance systems critical for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: The road to malaria elimination: why a robust surveillance system is imperative.
articles.authors: Reithinger R
articles.details: Title-only editorial/perspective piece emphasizing importance of strong surveillance systems as prerequisite for achieving malaria elimination, though specific findings unavailable.

articles.headline: Novel pfDHFR inhibitors overcome antifolate-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Integrated computational strategies for the identification of Plasmodium falciparum dihydrofolate reductase inhibitors.
articles.authors: Gogoi D, Dutta PP, Gogoi B, Baruah VJ, Paul R, Sarmah B, Baruah A, Kakoti BB, Kalita B, Dutta P, Yadav RNS
articles.details: Integrated computational study identified novel inhibitors targeting mutant Plasmodium falciparum dihydrofolate reductase (pfDHFR) associated with antifolate resistance (pyrimethamine, cycloguanil). Twenty-eight compounds showed robust binding to resistant pfDHFR variants through molecular docking and dynamics simulations. Top compound demonstrated IC50 values of 30.822 μg/mL against 3D7 strain and 126.077 μg/mL against Dd2 strain, significantly outperforming other compounds (p<0.05). Authors conclude this computational-experimental approach identifies alternatives to circumvent antifolate resistance.

articles.headline: Stearylamine lipid shows fast-acting antimalarial efficacy in vitro
articles.title_original: Cationic Lipid Stearylamine Displays Fast-Acting Antimalarial Action Against In Vitro Blood Stages of Chloroquine Susceptible and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.
articles.authors: Rajendran V, Gurukkalot K, Naveen NC
articles.details: In vitro study evaluated cationic lipid stearylamine (SA) against drug-sensitive (Pf3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (PfINDO) strains of P. falciparum. SA demonstrated rapid cytostatic action with IC50 values of 1.33–3.50 μg/mL and cytocidal effects (average IC50 1.68 μg/mL) after 1–6 hour exposure. Parasite growth arrest was maintained through 96 hours. SA induced reactive oxygen species production with minimal hemolytic effects. Authors suggest incorporation into lipid delivery systems could enhance antimalarial efficacy and reduce resistance development.

articles.headline: BioMalPar initiative reaches maturity in malaria research contributions
articles.title_original: BioMalPar at 21 - a coming of age for malaria research and interventions.
articles.authors: Aniweh Y, Dziekan J, Jore MM, Kho S, Mancio-Silva L, Merrick CJ, Nahrendorf W, Ralph SA, Vaughan AM, Verhoef JMJ
articles.details: Commentary on 21-year track record and evolution of BioMalPar in supporting malaria research and interventions, though specific study details unavailable from title alone.

articles.headline: Naphthoquinone hybrids overcome atovaquone-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: A dual In vitro and In silico approach to evaluate 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant malaria.
articles.authors: Agarwal P, N'Da DD, van der Westhuizen CJ, van Zyl RL
articles.details: Dual in vitro and in silico study evaluated ten 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant P. falciparum strain FCR3 harboring Y279S mutation in cytochrome b gene. Hybrids D12 and D13 exhibited 61-fold and 52-fold greater antiplasmodial activity than atovaquone, with strong correlation between molecular modeling predictions and in vitro results. In silico evaluation indicated favorable oral bioavailability, high GI absorption, and low severe toxicity risk. Authors propose D12 and D13 as lead candidates warranting further optimization.

articles.headline: PCR diagnostic failure in mixed P. falciparum/P. vivax co-infection
articles.title_original: Polymerase chain reaction fails to detect mixed malaria infections in siblings from Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Hall AD, Whitehurst DA, Mortensen JE, Prus K, Staat MA, Schlaudecker EP, Prasanphanich NS
articles.details: Case report of two pediatric siblings recently returned from Ethiopia diagnosed with P. falciparum/P. vivax co-infection. PCR initially failed to detect P. vivax despite later confirmation by blood smear and repeat PCR following recurrent symptoms. Case highlights limitations of PCR-based diagnostics in detecting mixed infections in returning travelers from co-endemic regions, underscoring need for high clinical suspicion and confirmatory microscopy.

articles.headline: Vector species sanitation and structural resilience for malaria control
articles.title_original: Safeguarding malaria control gains in Africa through 'species sanitation' and structural resilience.
articles.authors: Okumu F, Odero JO, Charlwood JD, Knols BGJ, Monroe A
articles.details: Perspective proposing two-tier strategy to safeguard malaria control gains in Africa: (1) aggressively pursue 'species sanitation' targeting ecological vulnerabilities of primary Anopheles vectors, (2) gradually build structural resilience through improved housing and environmental management. Authors suggest future innovations like gene-drive mosquitoes and longer-lasting vaccines could amplify impact. Strategy emphasizes human-centered policies, increased domestic funding, cross-sector partnerships, and resilient health systems anchored in longer-term planning beyond typical 5-year cycles.

articles.headline: Travel blogs provide inconsistent malaria prevention guidance to travelers
articles.title_original: Content analysis of malaria-related health information published in online travel blogs.
articles.authors: Din S, Bishay R, Asad U, Flaherty GT
articles.details: Content analysis of 100 online travel blogs revealed 92 contained malaria-related information. Ten different antimalarial drugs were mentioned; five were inappropriate for P. falciparum prevention. Pre-travel medical consultation was recommended by approximately one-third of blogs. Most blogs were supported by commercial sponsors related to travel industry or travel health products. Authors conclude that inconsistent prevention advice highlights need for travel medicine community to provide reliable resources for lay travelers.

articles.headline: Intermediate Amazon forest cover increases malaria transmission risk
articles.title_original: Intermediate forest cover and malaria risk in an Amazon deforestation frontier.
articles.authors: Laporta GZ, Valle D, Prist PR, Ilacqua RC, Santos TC, Madeira FP, Silva GM, Brilhante AF, Nolan MS, Sperança MA
articles.details: Natural experiment across 40 sites in deforestation frontier of Acre state, Brazilian Amazon examined effects of landscape structure on malaria transmission. Sites with intermediate forest cover (approximately 50%), often with high fragmentation, exhibited higher Nyssorhynchus mosquito abundance and anopheline infection rates, resulting in increased P. vivax and P. falciparum infection risk. All 16 human infections detected were asymptomatic, representing persistent transmission reservoir. Authors recommend integration of radical cure treatments with alternative vector control targeting outdoor-biting Nyssorhynchus anophelines, plus landscape-based conservation/restoration strategies.

articles.headline: Spontaneous splenic rupture: rare but serious malaria complication
articles.title_original: Case series of spontaneous splenic rupture in patients with malaria: A rare surgical emergency in non-traumatic cases.
articles.authors: Tola GG, Hemba YG, Hussen SK, Matiop MP
articles.details: Case series of four patients with spontaneous splenic rupture (SSR) due to malaria, predominantly in endemic regions. One case involved 30-year-old previously healthy male from Ethiopia presenting with acute left upper quadrant pain, dizziness, and massive hemoperitoneum requiring emergency splenectomy. Three additional patients with SSR were hemodynamically stable and managed conservatively with close monitoring and serial imaging without surgical intervention. Authors discuss pathophysiology involving vascular congestion, reticuloendothelial hyperplasia, and fragile splenic capsule, emphasizing importance of clinical suspicion and appropriate imaging in malaria patients with abdominal pain.

articles.headline: Malaria shows slower drug resistance than HIV, TB
articles.title_original: A timeline of reckoning: Tracking the historical rise of antimicrobial resistance across HIV, TB, and malaria.
articles.authors: Williamson T, Adderley J, Quinn K, Clark TG, Lewin SR, Doerig C
articles.details: Comparative analysis of antimicrobial resistance timelines across HIV, TB, and malaria. Data reveals malaria exhibits slower rate of resistance development to monotherapies compared to HIV and TB. Combination therapies reduce de novo resistance risk, but pre-existing drug resistance persists, necessitating continuous surveillance.

articles.headline: Gene drive protects Anopheles mosquitoes against malaria
articles.title_original: Driving a protective allele of the mosquito FREP1 gene to combat malaria.
articles.authors: Li Z, Dong Y, You L, Corder RM, Arzobal J, Yeun A, Yang L, Marshall JM, Dimopoulos G, Bier E
articles.details: Engineered Anopheles stephensi strains carrying FREP1Q224 allele confer robust resistance to infection by human and rodent malaria parasites with negligible fitness costs. Protective allele can be efficiently driven into wild populations using linked allelic-drive system, potentially rendering populations refractory to parasite infection.

articles.headline: Dual-dye LAMP assay detects P. knowlesi rapidly
articles.title_original: Establishment of a visual mixed-dye loop mediated isothermal amplification assay for detecting Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Lai MY, Lau YL
articles.details: Novel dual-colour LAMP assay using phenol red and hydroxynaphthol blue for P. knowlesi detection. Tested on 55 clinical samples and achieved 100% sensitivity (95% CI: 91.9–100%) and 100% specificity (95% CI: 78.2–100%) compared to nested PCR, offering rapid, cost-effective diagnostics for resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Mycosporine-like amino acids inhibit falcipain-2
articles.title_original: Screening mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) as potential inhibitors of a malaria-associated cysteine protease falcipain-2.
articles.authors: Singh VK, Sahu N, Kesheri M, Kanchan S, Kumar A, Sinha RP
articles.details: In-silico screening of 27 mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) for inhibition of falcipain-2, a cysteine protease of P. falciparum. Seven compounds showed favorable drug properties with no toxicity; top two hits (shinorine and mycosporine-hydroxyglutamicol) demonstrated favorable binding affinity and complex stability in molecular dynamics simulations.

articles.headline: RDT complements but cannot replace microscopy
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of rapid diagnostic test and peripheral smear microscopy for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Bhise M, Anandani G, Goswami P, Padhi A, Agarwal A
articles.details: Retrospective comparison of rapid diagnostic tests (RDT) and peripheral blood smear (PBS) microscopy in 150 suspected malaria cases in India. PBS confirmed 30 positives (20%), RDT detected 20 positives (13.33%). RDT failed to identify P. falciparum but detected 2 P. vivax cases missed by PBS.

articles.headline: Transmission-blocking bacterium compatible with vector
articles.title_original: Compatibility of Serratia ureylitica Su_YN1, Malaria Transmission-Blocking Bacterium, with the Anopheles aquasalis Vector.
articles.authors: Ferreira MADS, Martins ESDR, Katak RM, Campos KMM, Rocha EM, Roque RA, Lalwani PJ, Silva LA, Carmo EJD, de Andrade PP, Wang S, Moreira LA, Jacobs-Lorena M, Ríos-Velásquez CM
articles.details: S. ureilytica Su_YN1 bacterium, known to impair P. falciparum and P. berghei development in Anopheles, was evaluated for compatibility with Anopheles aquasalis (South American vector). Bacterium did not affect mosquito longevity, blood feeding, fecundity, or fertility, providing support for vector-control implementation in South America.

articles.headline: Congenital malaria with dual P. falciparum and P. vivax
articles.title_original: Congenital malaria in a neonate born in a malaria-endemic area: a case report.
articles.authors: Andarge BD, Almaw K
articles.details: Case report of 48-hour-old neonate with congenital malaria and mixed infection of P. falciparum and P. vivax (1,120 parasites/μL). Mother treated with artemether-lumefantrine one week before delivery but asymptomatic at neonate's diagnosis. Neonate recovered completely after treatment with IV artesunate followed by oral artemisinin-lumefantrine.

articles.headline: Malaria and amoebic abscess in pregnancy with fetal loss
articles.title_original: Management of a Pregnant Woman With Malaria, Stillbirth, and a 12 × 11-cm Amoebic Liver Abscess in a Burundian Hospital: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Castaldi MA, Castaldi SG
articles.details: Case report of pregnant woman at 22 weeks gestation with P. falciparum malaria and 12 × 11-cm hepatic amoebic abscess, resulting in fetal death in utero. Represents first reported case of severe malaria coexisting with amoebic liver abscess during pregnancy. Successfully managed in rural hospital with maternal survival.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine and DTG show drug interaction
articles.title_original: Drug-drug interaction between dolutegravir and artemether-lumefantrine in HIV and malaria mono- and co-infections: a pharmacogenetic analysis from Ghana.
articles.authors: Thomford NE, Kellermann T, Twum JA, Anyimadu J, Dixon C, Sappor D, Blackhurst D, Barnie PA, Ryabinina O, Nyarko SB, Biney RP, Ekor M, Kyei GB
articles.details: Pharmacogenetic analysis of 261 participants with HIV-malaria mono and coinfections in Ghana. Co-administration of artemether-lumefantrine and dolutegravir (DTG) decreased plasma concentrations of both drugs: lumefantrine Cday 7 was 741.5 ng/mL (monoinfection) versus 426.0 ng/mL (coinfection). Genetic variations in CYP3A5, CYP3A, UGT1A1, CYP2B6 contributed to altered drug disposition.

articles.headline: Bibliometric analysis reveals HIV-malaria research trends
articles.title_original: Unraveling the HIV-malaria interactions: a bibliometric analysis of global research trends and emerging insights.
articles.authors: Zhang H, Chen G, Ma P, Lai Y
articles.details: Bibliometric analysis of 4,717 articles (1995–2024) on HIV-malaria interactions using CiteSpace software. Identified primary research hotspots as epidemiological studies in Africa, pathogenic mechanisms of co-infection, prevention and treatment strategies, drug interactions between antimalarials and antiretrovirals, and malaria vaccine responses in HIV-infected populations.

articles.headline: Acute kidney injury in 49% of severe malaria patients
articles.title_original: Prevalence, Associated Factors, and Prognosis of Acute Kidney Injury in Severe Malaria Among Sub-Saharan Africans.
articles.authors: Mbengue M, Cheikh Gaye A, Tshabayembi J, Fall S, Sall I, Diouf M, Keita N, Gaye I, Seydi M, Fary Ka EH, Cristiano F, Niang A
articles.details: Multicenter retrospective study of 321 severe malaria patients in Dakar hospitals (2019–2023). Acute kidney injury (AKI) prevalence was 49.2%, with anuria in 64.3% and oliguria in 26.8%. In multivariate analysis, AKI was independently associated with increased mortality (OR 3.584; p=0.004). Associated factors included diabetes, respiratory distress, and anemia.

articles.headline: Artemether superior for cerebral malaria parasite clearance
articles.title_original: Comparison of Artemether Versus Quinine for the Treatment of Cerebral Malaria in Children: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.authors: Saeed H, Tahir D, Khan MM, Faheem MT, Malik AH, Saeed S, Azeem S, Syed M, Allami A
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 7 randomized controlled trials (1,225 patients: 618 artemether, 607 quinine) for cerebral malaria in children. Parasite clearance time significantly improved with artemether (MD –7.63 days; 95% CI –11.06, –4.21; P<0.0001). Fever clearance time, coma clearance time, and mortality showed no significant differences between treatments.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein polymorphism in Congo
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of circumsporozoite protein of Plasmodium falciparum isolates in children in Brazzaville, Republic of Congo.
articles.authors: Baina MT, Djontu JC, Ntabi JDM, Lissom A, Eouasse AGK, Elenga VA, Mapanguy CCM, Mouanga AM, Ntoumi F
articles.details: Cross-sectional sequencing of Pfcsp gene from 202 P. falciparum-infected children in Republic of Congo (March–October 2021) identified high genetic polymorphism: 30 haplotypes in N-terminal region (A98G most frequent at 25.6%), 50 in central repeat region, and 76 in C-terminal region (K317E at 87.4%, N321K at 83.9%, E357Q at 75.9%). Authors conclude these variations at vaccine-targeted PfCSP epitopes warrant further immunological assessment of vaccine efficacy.

articles.headline: Climate change and malaria transmission in Africa: systematic review
articles.title_original: Impact of climate change on malaria transmission in Africa: A scoping review of literature.
articles.authors: Chapoterera B, Naidoo K, Marume A
articles.details: Scoping review of 90 studies (2010–2024) examining climate–malaria relationships across Africa. Temperature increases significantly accelerate mosquito vector and parasite development, while rainfall pattern changes affect breeding sites and malaria incidence. Authors recommend integrating ecological data and climate-responsive policies into malaria control strategies tailored to regional variations.

articles.headline: Garlic extract shows highest insecticidal activity against malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Insecticidal Activities of Plants Extract Against Malaria Vectors in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Assemie A, Mulu D, Getahun A, Awoke N, Muluken W, Enyew T, Gebremariam A, Aynalem Y
articles.details: Laboratory evaluation of Azadirachta indica (neem) and Allium sativum L. (garlic) ethanol extracts against malaria vectors in Ethiopia found A. sativum achieved 90.66% mortality at 250 ppm concentration with lowest LC50 (39 ppm). A. sativum showed significant effects on Anopheles gambiae s.l. (χ² = 13.6, p = 0.0087) and An. pharoensis (χ² = 11.002, p = 0.0266); A. indica was significant only against An. pharoensis.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum chromatin dynamics during intraerythrocytic development
articles.title_original: Chromatin state dynamics during the Plasmodium falciparum intraerythrocytic development cycle.
articles.authors: Brown AS Jr, Llinás M, Mahony S
articles.details: Genome-wide analysis of chromatin state dynamics throughout P. falciparum's 48-hour intraerythrocytic developmental cycle integrated profiles of chromatin accessibility, histone modifications, and Heterochromatin Protein 1 occupancy. Some chromatin states remained stable while others dynamically linked to gene activation or repression. Transcription factors were grouped by chromatin preferences, revealing coordinate regulation of asexual blood-stage development.

articles.headline: Combined IPTp and ITN reduce malaria prevalence in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Do women attending antenatal clinics who use only intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) have higher Plasmodium falciparum prevalence compared to those who used both IPTp and slept under insecticide-treated nets?
articles.authors: Madukwe JC, Ahaneku EB, Onukafor OS, Edokpayi S, Madukwe PC, Enwereji NU, Santos EM
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 143 pregnant women in Nigeria found P. falciparum in 45% overall; prevalence was 61.2% among those using only IPTp versus 17.0% among those using both IPTp and ITNs. Logistic regression (adjusted for gestational week, income, education) showed those using both strategies had 86% reduced odds of infection, with ITN being the primary protective factor.

articles.headline: PfMYST epigenetic mechanism drives artemisinin ring-stage survival
articles.title_original: Epigenetically conferred ring-stage survival in Plasmodium falciparum against artemisinin treatment.
articles.authors: Yu X, He J, Wang C, Mu J, Chen X, Zhao Y, He X, Liu S, Sa JM, Platon L, Tang J, Si W, Tang R, Menard D, Wellems TE, Jiang C, Cao J, Zhang Q
articles.details: Systematic evaluation of 73 P. falciparum field isolates identified PfMYST histone acetyltransferase as key epigenetic regulator of ring-stage survival (RS) against artemisinin; knockdown and multi-omics analysis (ChIP-seq, single-cell RNA-seq, CRISPR) revealed PfMYST-targeted genes with significant interplay with PfK13 mutations. PfMYST also modulates response to amodiaquine, pyrimethamine, chloroquine, and pyronaridine.

articles.headline: Novel PI4K inhibitor shows efficacy against Plasmodium falciparum
articles.title_original: Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase IIIβ with Low Propensity for Resistance: Life Cycle Stage Activity and In Vivo Efficacy in a Humanized Mouse Malaria Infection Model.
articles.authors: Dziwornu GA, Mmonwa MM, Coertzen D, Krugmann L, Salomane N, Leshabane M, Thomas J, da Rocha S, Reader J, Masike K, Njoroge M, Sevilleno N, Coyle R, Boonyalai N, Mayville E, Lee MCS, Fidock DA, Coulson LB, Woodland JG, Wicht KJ, Ghorpade SR, Birkholtz LM, Chibale K
articles.details: Novel PI4K inhibitor (compound 18) based on anticancer ATM inhibitor AZD0156 demonstrated activity against all stages of P. falciparum life cycle with lower resistance and toxicity propensity compared to previous PI4K inhibitors. Showed efficacy in humanized mouse malaria model with ED90 of 4.6 mg/kg.

articles.headline: Plasmodium ARK1 controls atypical mitosis and mitotic progression
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.
articles.authors: Nagar A, Yanase R, Zeeshan M, Ferguson DJP, Abel S, Pashley SL, Mishra A, Eze A, Rea E, Brady D, Bottrill AR, Vaughan S, Le Roch KG, Guttery DS, Holder AA, Tromer EC, Sharma P, Tewari R
articles.details: ARK1 (Aurora-related kinase 1) regulates spindle formation and mitotic progression in P. falciparum; conditional depletion disrupts spindle biogenesis, kinetochore segregation, and cytokinesis during schizogony and gametogony. Interactome analysis revealed ARK1 as catalytic core of a non-canonical chromosomal passenger complex with divergent INCENPs but lacking Survivin and Borealin, with potential as a multistage antimalarial target.

articles.headline: Salacia debilis compounds target Plasmodium enoyl-ACP reductase
articles.title_original: Computational elucidation of Plasmodium falciparum enoyl-ACP reductase inhibition by Salacia debilis compounds: adaptive ligand binding drives antimalarial activity.
articles.authors: Manu P, Padiga Seidu M, Adomako A, Asamoah CK, Duodu O, Britwum A, Laryea MK, Darko G, Borquaye LS
articles.details: Molecular docking and 500 ns molecular dynamics simulations identified P. falciparum enoyl-ACP reductase (PfENR) as the target of three Salacia debilis-derived compounds (SD03, SD04, SD05) with binding affinities of −8.50 to −8.90 kcal/mol. Compounds engaged key active site residues (Tyr111, Tyr267, Leu315) similarly to benchmark inhibitor triclosan; SD04 showed favorable pharmacokinetic predictions as lead candidate for structure-guided antimalarial development.

articles.headline: HIV infection factors in Mozambique youth: gender-specific disparities
articles.title_original: Factors associated with HIV infection among adolescents and young people in Mozambique: sub-analysis of immunization, malaria and HIV/AIDS indicator survey in Mozambique-IMASIDA 2015.
articles.authors: Chicanequisso EM, Sabonete A, Sacarlal J, Rossetto ÉV, Cassimo MN, Baltazar CS
articles.details: Analysis of 4,247 youth (15–24 years) from 2015 Mozambique survey found 6.9% HIV prevalence (9.8% in females). In males, widower status strongly associated (AOR 52.69, 95% CI 2.07–1340.33, p = 0.02). In females, age 20–24 years, widowhood, separation, early cohabitation (<18 years), and wealth were risk factors; completing higher education was protective (AOR 0.07, 95% CI 0.01–0.58, p = 0.02).

articles.headline: Dietary L-DOPA reduces Anopheles lifespan and malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Dietary L-3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine (L-DOPA) augments cuticular melanization in Anopheles mosquitos reducing their lifespan and malaria burden.
articles.authors: Camacho E, Dong Y, Chrissian C, Cordero RJB, Saraiva RG, Anglero-Rodriguez Y, Smith DFQ, Jacobs E, Hartshorn I, Patiño-Medina JA, DePasquale M, Dziedzic A, Jedlicka A, Smith B, Mlambo G, Tripathi A, Broderick NA, Stark RE, Dimopoulos G, Casadevall A
articles.details: Dietary L-DOPA (a tyrosine derivative present in natural mosquito habitats) was incorporated into Anopheles melanin via non-canonical pathway, enhancing transcriptional immunity to P. falciparum, increasing pigmentation, and reducing mosquito lifespan. The effect was associated with enhanced electromagnetic radiation absorption affecting mosquito temperature. Authors suggest L-DOPA's potential as an environmentally friendly vector control agent.

articles.headline: Anopheles species distribution modeling in malaria-free Paraguay
articles.title_original: Unravelling Anopheles Dynamics in a Malaria-Free Paraguay: Species Distributions, Bioclimatic Niches, and Implications for Resurgence Risks.
articles.authors: Del Puerto F, Grissetti M, Ferreira L, Franco L, Herrera L
articles.details: Ecological niche modeling (DIVA-GIS/MaxEnt) of Anopheles species in malaria-free Paraguay (2016–2017) found An. albitarsis most prevalent (59.4%), followed by An. strodei (21.5%); all tested negative for Plasmodium. Bioclimatically suitable areas spanned four ecoregions, with precipitation of wettest month (80.4% contribution) and driest month (18.4% contribution) as key variables. An. darlingi (main malaria vector) was absent, though species richness indices suggest cryptic species may exist.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence rising in northern Madagascar
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria in Northern Madagascar as Exemplified by the Mampikony District.
articles.authors: Kołodziej D, Wilczyńska W, Kasprowicz D, Marchelek-Myśliwiec M, Korzeniewski K
articles.details: Cross-sectional study from 2023-2024 in Mampikony District, Madagascar enrolled 782 residents. Malaria prevalence was 4.5% by rapid diagnostic tests and 8.2% by RT-PCR in 2023; 8.4% and 12.4% respectively in 2024, with predominantly P. falciparum cases plus P. vivax and mixed infections. Results indicate need for more sensitive diagnostic tools and revised treatment strategies due to rising asymptomatic carriage.

articles.headline: Simplified mathematical model for malaria control
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.
articles.authors: Korsah MA, Johnston ST, Tiedje K, Day K, Walker CR, Flegg JA
articles.details: Study employed reduction technique on comprehensive malaria control model to derive simplified system preserving essential dynamics. Validation via Bayesian MCMC and parameter identifiability analysis showed reduced model exhibits similar transmission characteristics as full model, providing computational efficiency and flexibility for evaluating malaria control interventions.

articles.headline: Vaccine-evolved malaria parasites modulate immune responses
articles.title_original: Understanding the traits underlying vaccine-driven virulence evolution in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Jeong Y, Kamiya T, Mideo N
articles.details: Rodent malaria model experimental study using hierarchical Bayesian mathematical modeling compared vaccinated vs. sham-vaccinated control mice. Vaccine-evolved parasites elicited lower targeted immune killing and anemia-driven erythropoiesis, but showed no differences in early-infection parasite multiplication rates, ruling out evolution at vaccine target antigen AMA-1. Results highlight importance of virulence evolution through host-response modulation beyond target antigen changes.

articles.headline: Neutrophil extracellular traps drive cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: TLR9/NF-κB-mediated dendritic cell activation by neutrophil extracellular traps drives pathogenesis in experimental cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Yao S, Zhao Y, Yao C, Li Q, Sun M, Wang Q, Zheng L, Cao Y
articles.details: Murine cerebral malaria model using Plasmodium berghei ANKA revealed neutrophil mobilization and extracellular trap (NET) formation drives pathogenesis. Neutrophil depletion protected mice from neuropathology and reduced CD8+ T cell infiltration in brain, while NETs activated dendritic cells through TLR9/NF-κB signaling. NET inhibition with Sivelestat effectively impeded onset and progression of cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Yemen's malaria crisis and GCC implications
articles.title_original: Yemen's Malaria Crisis and its Implications for the GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) Countries.
articles.authors: Ebrahim SH, Al-Maslamani M, Al-Hosani F, Al-Abri S, Alqahtani M, Alahmad B, Bland S, Memish Z
articles.details: Article addresses malaria crisis in Yemen and potential implications for neighboring GCC countries.

articles.headline: Broadly inhibitory anti-PfEMP1 antibodies from child
